New H-like and He-like emission and absorption features discovered in the Fe K regime reveal a previously unknown zone of circumnuclear gas in NGC 3516 with log 4.3 and column density 10^ 23 cm^ 2 . A lower-ionization layer with log 2 and of similar column density is confirmed from previous observations, this layer has a covering fraction around 50 , and changes in covering provide a simple explanation of a deep dip in the light curve that we interpret as an eclipse of the continuum due to passage of a cloud across the sight line within half a day. These inner zones of absorbing gas are detected to have outflow velocities in the range 1000-2000 km s ^ 1 , this, and constraints on radial location are consistent with an origin as part of a disk wind in NGC 3516.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3516 as having diffuse near-UV emission unlike its [O III] morphology, with very little contribution from [Ne V] and nebular continuum.

quotes.quote: NGC 3516 (Sy1.5) After removing the Seyfert nucleus, there remains a diffuse emission around it, which shows no clumps or star clusters. For this object the morphology in the near-UV and [ OIII ] is very dissimilar. This is probably the best example in the whole sample in which [ NeV ] and NC contribute very little to the near-UV light. Simple aperture photometry shows that in the inner kiloparsec, the contribution of the ionised gas accounts for less than 10 per cent (considering a scaling factor of f =0.6) of the diffuse near-UV light.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3516 as one of five poorly fit Seyfert/BLRG spectra with high signal-to-noise Suzaku data and strong O 7/O 8 absorption requiring more detailed warm-absorber modeling.

quotes.quote: The majority of sources have spectra that are well-fit with this model. Five sources, however, are not well-fit, with reduced ^2 > 2.0 . These include NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, and MR 2251-178. Each of these sources have high signal-to-noise Suzaku spectra with exposures from 79--275 ks in length and have strong detections of O 7 and O 8 absorption. The complexity in the soft X-ray emission is not fully accounted for with the simple addition of the absorption edges. This is clear in the bottom panels of Figure fig-basemodel , where the ratio of data/model for three of these sources clearly shows structure that is not accounted for with our model.

sources.summary: NGC 3516 has warm absorbers, but the paper argues intrinsic variability and disk reflection remain important, with a broad iron line required after absorption modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 3516: NGC 3516 shows several zones of warm absorption, described by Medhipour10 , who showed that variations in the covering fraction of the warm absorbers cannot be solely responsible for large variations in the flux, and therefore that intrinsic source variability plays a large part in the the spectral variability of the source. Turner11 argue that the lack of reverberation lags in this source is evidence that the flux variations are not intrinsic to the source, however DeMarco13 found such a lag at >99 confidence, indicating reflection from the accretion disk and the presence of intrinsic variability. Turner02 found evidence of a relativistically broadened iron line in NGC 3516, and Markowitz08 found that this was still required after complex absorption is taken into account, and adding an additional absorbing component could not reproduce the spectral curvature.

We note that some of the strongest variability, potentially due to an extreme absorption event Turner11 , is seen in the 2005 Suzaku spectrum of this source, and will therefore not manifest in our results.

sources.summary: NGC 3516 has Fe K emission significantly inside the dust radius, implying an intermediate zone between the BLR and torus, with possible multi-epoch FWHM variation.

quotes.quote: The remaining 4 sources (NGC 3516, NGC 7469, Mrk 590 and Fairall 9) have \ values significantly smaller than . The ratio of / \ in these 4 ranges over 2.5 (NGC 3516) to 75 (Fairall 9).
For NGC 3516, \ lies below \ and the uncertainty estimates on \ and \ do not overlap, implying an \ origin in an intermediate zone between the BLR and the torus.

From the individual measurements presented by shu10 , there appears to be some variation in FWHM( ) for 2 of the 5 sources with multiple observations spaced by a few months to years (these 2 sources are NGC 3516 and NGC 5548).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3516's low-luminosity Seyfert 1 nucleus, using HST Balmer-line spectra and Cloudy calculations to constrain a dust-free, inflowing broad-line region around the central black hole.

quotes.quote: Spectroscopic observations of the low luminosity Seyfert 1 nucleus in NGC 3516 obtained with the Hubble Space Telescope show that the visible spectrum is dominated by the Balmer emission lines of Hydrogen (H) and a continuum luminosity that rises into the UV. The anomalous H /H emission line ratio, the Balmer emission line luminosity and the distinctive shape observed for the H emission line profile serve as important constraints in any photoionization model aimed at explaining the visible emission line spectrum of NGC 3516.

NGC 3516 is of potentially great importance in deciphering the mystery surrounding the origin of broad H emission lines because this low luminosity active galactic nucleus (LLAGN) is also time-variable allowing various independent measures of the broad line region (BLR) size.

Collectively the STIS observations constrain a Cloudy model for the BLR in NGC 3516 that consists of 500 M _ of dust-free H gas that is free-falling towards the central BH at a steady-state rate of 1 M _ /yr.

sources.summary: The paper presents the first H I spectrum for nearby NGC 3516, requiring the longest exposure in the sample and constrained Gaussian-profile fitting.

quotes.quote: NGC 3516: This is the first HI spectrum for NGC 3516, a relatively nearby galaxy, due to the extreme faintness of its HI emission. The total on-source observing time spent on this object was longer than any of our other targets by a large margin ( 15.6 hours). The HI profile of NGC 3516 exhibits a Gaussian shape, and as such the BusyFit model held the central trough amplitude, offset, and polynomial degree parameters ( , x_ p , and n ) fixed at 0. We also held the right flank slope ( b_ 2 ) fixed at 0.1 to allow the profile fit to converge.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3516 as a famous changing-look AGN with strong X-ray variation, complex spectral evolution, and broad-line disappearance at low Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: The archived data for both NuSTAR, XMM-Newton and Chandra are selected and reduced for four famous CL AGNs -- Mrk 1018, NGC 3516, Mrk 590 and NGC 2992, where these sources suffer strong X-ray variations in last 20 years.

The top-right panel of Figure 1 shows the results for NGC 3516, where the spectral evolution is not simple as that found in Mrk 1018. The positive correlation between and L_ 2-10 keV /L_ Edd is evident, while the anti-correlation is not so strong which may be caused by the quite narrow distribution of Eddington ratios. However, BELs always present in the positive part (solid triangles), and the BEL is disappear in low-Eddington-ratio part (open triangles) with a critical Eddington ratio of 10^ 3 .

sources.summary: NGC 3516 shows one occultation, and the paper places its obscuring cloud among AGN BLR-scale absorbers with Keplerian velocities typical of broad-line-region clouds.

quotes.quote: We detect three occultations in NGC 3227 and one occultation in NGC 3783, NGC 7314, and NGC 3516, together with the well-known occultations in Mrk 766.

The eclipsing clouds in Mrk 766, NGC 3227, NGC 7314, and NGC 3516 with distances from the X-ray source of (0.3-3.6) 10 ^4 R_g are moving at Keplerian velocities > 1122 km s ^ 1 , typical parameters of broad-line region clouds, while the eclipsing cloud in NGC 3783 is likely located in the dusty torus.

Furthermore, the r_ values of 9.6 10 ^ 15 cm derived for NGC 7314 and of 3.2 10 ^ 16 cm derived for NGC 3516 are consistent with those expected in BLRs of AGNs with a SMBH mass within (0.1-2) 10 ^7 M _ see 2019A&A...628A..26P . Thus, the clouds obscuring the central X-ray source in Mrk 766, NGC 3227, NGC 7314, and NGC 3516 show r_ values of (0.3-3.6) 10 ^4 R_ g , typical of BLR clouds.

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 3516 as an unresolved VLBA snapshot source with high brightness temperature, while noting its X-ray behavior and changing-look AGN classification.

quotes.quote: For the additional snapshot observation sample, the nuclei for both the detections (NGC 3147 and NGC 3516) appear unresolved, but with follow up higher sensitivity deep observations, the core for NGC 2782 and NGC 4388 appear slightly elongated.

We find brightness temperatures of 10^ 7.6 K and 10^ 6.3 K from our two VLBA snapshot detections (NGC 3147 and NGC 3516), indicating either compact jet-associated or corona-associated synchrotron radiation as their likely dominant radio emission mechanism.

NGC 3516 (obsid: 60002042004): This source, optically classified as Seyfert 1.5 galaxy, is a changing-look AGN, which is characterized by extreme changes in flux and spectrum. Since the X-ray spectral variability does not follow the standard softer-when-brighter trend typical of Seyfert galaxies, the \ value derived from the X-ray scaling method (1.4 0.5) 10^6 M_ should be not considered reliable, and indeed it is about one order of magnitude smaller than the reverberation mapping value obtained by Feng_2021ApJ...909...18F .

sources.summary: The review reports narrow Fe K reverberation in NGC 3516, with a measured lag during a faint X-ray state that may aid black-hole mass work when optical mapping fails.

quotes.quote: Another detection of reverberation in a narrow Fe K line was recently reported in archival Suzaku observations of the bright Seyfert 1 AGN NGC 3516 . Again using javelin , a lag of = 10.1^ +5.8 _ 5.6 days is measured. Importantly, the lag is only found during a period when NGC 3516 was particularly faint in X-rays, and similar to a Seyfert 2 AGN. In those cases, the BLR is typically blocked by the torus, so the fact that reverberation mapping was still possible using the Fe K line may signal that XRISM and Athena can measure lags and black hole masses in cases where optical attempts have not been successful.

sources.summary: The rms spectra for NGC 3516 show definitive broad iron-line variability, a red wing stronger than the time-averaged spectrum, and behavior inconsistent with complex absorption alone.

quotes.quote: However, a key result is that the rms spectra of objects such as NGC 3516 do not agree with complex absorption effects mimicking the broad red wing.

Of the observations with significant line variability, two (NGC 3516(2) and NGC 5506(1)) show definitive broad line variability, in that there are two adjacent bins with excess variations at >95 confidence.
For example, NGC 3516(2) and NGC 5548(2) have line variability, especially in the red wing, that is stronger than the time-averaged spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3516 as a Seyfert 1.5 with clear outflow signatures, among the strongest outflow cases, but notes that the baseline spectral model fit was inadequate.

quotes.quote: Additionally, we include NGC 3516, whose spectrum was not well-fit by our base model, but clearly has an outflow present (see 2008PASJ...60S.277M for a complete analysis of the absorption in the Suzaku observation of NGC 3516).
Finally, the four sources with the strongest outflows (NGC 3516, NGC 4151, Mrk 6, and NGC 526A) have intermediate luminosities and are all Sy 1.5s.
For NGC 3516, NGC 4051, and NGC 4151, our baseline model was not sufficient to obtain a good fit to their spectra ( ^2/dof > 2 ).

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3516 was observed in a low-flux state with a large broad Fe K equivalent width and that this work detects one warm absorber component.

quotes.quote: As a special case where captured the source NGC 3516 in a low flux state, we detected a large equivalent width of the broad Fe K emission line ( 1560 ), in agreement with the light bending scenario.
NGC 3516 - detected large range of ionisation states for WA in this source. We detected just one WA component.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3516 as an observed Seyfert outflow case, citing its high-ionization gas column, slow-component AMD, and measured best-fit AMD slope.

quotes.quote: For example, NGC 3516 has 10^ 22 ^ 2 of 1000 gas (see next section).

In objects where the absorption has several components with different velocities v (MCG-6-30-15 and NGC 3516), we plot the AMD of the slowest component (smallest |v| ), and discuss faster components below.
The AMD of NGC 3516, which was measured later, is best-fit with N_1=3 10^ 21 ^ 2 and a=0.12 .

sources.summary: NGC 3516 is highlighted as a special case with 50 percent Fe-K variability over 180 days during historically low flux, suggesting a smaller torus inner radius.

quotes.quote: One special example of significant Fe-K variability is NGC 3516, whose Fe-K line intensity varied by 50 with t=180 days. This variability was observed between May and November in 2013, when the flux of NGC 3516 was historically low in November. It is suggested that the inner radius of torus becomes smaller due to low X-ray luminosity of (0.5-2.5) 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 (15--50 keV) in 2013--2014.

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC 3516 as a Seyfert with a high cutoff lower limit and contrasts this with Panagiotou and Akylas results, including an inconsistent low cutoff measurement.
The paper studies Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 3516 with Chandra and archival X-ray data, attributing large spectral flux changes to line-of-sight absorbing gas responding to continuum variations.

quotes.quote: A new Chandra observation and archival observations by \ are used to investigate spectral variations in the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 3516 over a period of 7 years. A large change in flux (factor 50 at 1 keV) is observed between an \ observation in 1994 and the \ observation in 2000, with the source close to the all-time maximum and minimum X-ray flux states, respectively. We find the variations in the observed flux and spectra at these epochs to be consistent with a constant column density of line-of-sight material reacting to changes in the ionizing continuum.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC3516 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy whose ASCA spectrum showed a redshifted Fe absorption feature, interpreted as possible free-falling material but limited by data quality.

quotes.quote: Nandra et al. (1999) discussed the detection of a redshifted Fe resonant absorption feature in the X-ray spectrum of the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC3516. The ASCA data showed a sharp and narrow drop of counts at E 5.9 keV (corresponding, for FeXXVI, to an energy/velocity shift of 0.15 ) imprinted over a broad emission line profile, with indication of variability on time scales of 20 ks. Being the line redshifted, the authors speculated its association with material free-falling onto the BH.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3516 as a Seyfert example whose ionization cone shows Z-shaped filaments extending to kiloparsec scales and whose structure has been interpreted with bipolar outflow deflected toward the disk.

quotes.quote: Figure gal shows, as an example, two [ OIII ] emission-line images of ionization cones in the galaxies NGC 3516 (Sy 1 type) and NGC 5252 (Sy 1.9 type) and the orientation of the axes of their radio jets.
The NGC 3516 galaxy (Fig. gal a and ) shows Z--shaped filaments in the region <10-14'' ( 2 kpc) with the Northeastern part of the cone extending out to more than 6 kpc.
Mulchaey et al. (1992) interpreted the Z--shaped structure in NGC 3516 in terms of the bipolar outflow model where ejected gas is deflected toward the galactic disk.

gmw_00000896.name: NGC 4911
gmw_00000896.overview: NGC 4911 is a giant spiral galaxy in the Coma cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0301459, arXiv:2207.06624] classified as an Sb-type LINER [arXiv:2207.06624] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0301459, arXiv:2112.12244]. It exhibits ongoing star formation [arXiv:1005.3874, arXiv:2310.20417] and is undergoing ram-pressure stripping, which has produced a shrunken central gas disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0109316] and one-sided stripped tails [arXiv:2310.20417].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4911 as a giant Coma spiral undergoing ram-pressure and tidal interactions, with low-surface-brightness debris, possible induced star formation, and an active nucleus.

quotes.quote: There are two giant spiral galaxies in the core of Coma, NGC4911 and NGC4921, which are undergoing intense and stressful interactions with their environment. Gregg West (1998) documented several extended low surface brightness (LSB) objects in the Coma cluster, one of which is trailing NGC4911 into the core of Coma (Figure 1). Such LSB features are most likely transient, produced by galaxy-galaxy interactions or by stripping of galaxies by the global cluster tidal field and ICM.

The right panel shows just the PC chip to reveal the nuclear region; there appears to be a disembodied spiral arc of enhanced surface brightness on the leading side of the disk toward the cluster core, which is 0.29^ away. This arc may be an example of ram-pressure-induced star formation, perhaps a bow shock, as NGC 4911 plows through the hot X-ray emitting ICM in Coma.

We obtained a 65ks Chandra image, centered near NGC4911, a known X-ray sources. Our Chandra X-ray spectrum of NGC4911 shows that it houses an active galactic nucleus, perhaps spawned by the same interactions which have produced the LSB material.

sources.summary: GMP2374 has connected emission clouds with disk star formation, a clear spiral pattern, red color, and may be a progenitor of a passive spiral galaxy.

quotes.quote: 1 connected \ clouds with disk star formation: GMP2374, GMP3271, and GMP3816 show galaxy-wide star formation and some regions of \ emission have flown out of the galaxies entirely. GMP3271 and GMP3816 are blue, while GMP2374 is as red as early-type galaxies and lies on the tip of the color-magnitude relation. Nevertheless, GMP2374 exhibits a very clear spiral pattern with ongoing star formation at the arms. We suppose that GMP2374 could be a progenitor of a passive spiral galaxy e.g., Goto2003 .

Moreover, GMP2374, the brightest 1 galaxy, shows a red color. Although part of the red color is due to possible extinction by internal dust, the spectrum of the whole galaxy in the GOLDMINE database Gavazzi2003 indicates that it is dominated by intermediate and old stellar populations as inferred from its large D4000 and small CaHK ratio.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4911 as a bright Coma spiral with a shrunken central gas disk, H I-optical offset, high molecular-rich gas content, and normal inner rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4911 This is one of the two brightest spirals in Coma. The morphology of NGC 4911 shows a shrunken disk with two central peaks and a normal surface density in the central region as predicted by ram--pressure stripping (Fig. 22). A shift in position is shown between the and the optical, the former lying about 4 kpc south of the optical component.

NGC 4911 shows a very high total gas content, + 3.7 10 ^9 (Casoli 1996), but only a small fraction ( / =0.34) is in atomic form. Considering the CO observations by Lavezzi (1999) this fraction is much lower: / =0.22. The velocity field shows normal rotation of the inner disk (see Figs. 23) along an axis oriented roughly SE-NW.

sources.summary: The paper marks NGC 4911 as an AGN-bearing Coma source with stellar mass similar to NGC 4921 and NGC 4907, residing on the star-forming main sequence with Seyfert-like properties.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig::sfms we marked 4 sources, NGC 4921, NGC 4907, NGC 4911, and NGC 4848, with red circles, indicating they have been found to harbour an AGN in previous studies mahajan10,gavazzi11,toba14 . The two most massive ones ( (M_ / M_ ) 11.0 -- 11.5 ), NGC 4921 and NGC 4907, are quenched late-type galaxies. NGC 4911 is of similar M_ , but resides on the SFMS, while NGC 4848 ( (M_ / M_ ) 9 ) has a sSFR consistent with that of a starburst galaxy.

We note that, according to their MIR photometry, none of them have W1-W2 colours greater than 0.8, where most AGN lie stern12 . They are most likely Seyferts, whose star formation can be comparable with their AGN activity e.g. in Circinus for12 , and as a result can have MIR colours similar to regular SF galaxies. Thus, we consider their SFR estimates to be reliable within the uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4911 as an interacting Sb LINER/RPS giant spiral with nearly circular two-band radio emission around the host and a measured spectrum.

quotes.quote: 23 (s): NGC 4911 The optical host is a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region galaxy of Sb type. It is one of the two giant spirals in the Coma cluster showing a close interaction with its neighbor DRCG 27 62 KentandGunn,MillerOwen2002 . The radio emission at both bands, 250--500 MHz and 550--850 MHz, is symmetrically surrounding the optical host, and radio emission in both bands are of identical extent. The source is also classified as an RPS galaxy see Sec. tab:tab3-rps Chenetal . The source is nearly circular in shape, and the largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz band is 0 73 ( 20.5 kpc). The integrated spectral index is (400--700 MHz) = 0.98 0.04, and it agrees with S_ 1.4 GHz = 15.08 0.23 mJy, within uncertainties Chenetal .

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC4911 to the Coma ram-pressure sample because it has H and 144 MHz one-sided stripped tails and ongoing star-formation signatures despite missing a prior selection cut.

quotes.quote: NGC4911 is known to have an H stripped tail identified by yagi2010 , and also shows a clear one-sided tail in LoTSS at 144 MHz (see Fig. fig:example_imgs_NGC4911 ) in the same direction as the H tail. The reason that NGC4911 was not included in the roberts2021_LOFARclust sample, despite the radio continuum tail, is that NGC4911 has a specific star formation rate ( sSFR = SFR / M_ = 10^ 11.2 yr^ 1 ) that falls just below the star-forming galaxy selection of sSFR > 10^ 11 yr^ 1 imposed by roberts2021_LOFARclust . However, NGC4911 clearly shows signatures of ongoing star formation gregg2003,yagi2010 and we opt to include it in our sample for completeness.

gmw_00000897.name: NGC4921
gmw_00000897.overview: NGC 4921 is the brightest spiral galaxy in the Coma cluster [arXiv:1601.03047, arXiv:2112.12244], hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1601.03047, arXiv:2112.12244] and an S0 companion galaxy [arXiv:1601.03047]. It is an anemic spiral [arXiv:1601.03047, arXiv:1304.3529] with a truncated, asymmetric gas disk [arXiv:2112.12244, arXiv:astro-ph/0109316] and dust filaments [arXiv:2112.12244, arXiv:1604.01883] shaped by ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2112.12244, arXiv:astro-ph/0109316].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4921 as Coma's brightest spiral, resolving its globular clusters, spur clusters, anemic spiral properties, infall context, and transition toward an S0-like state.

quotes.quote: NGC 4921 is the brightest spiral galaxy (SB(rs)ab) in the Coma cluster dev91 . It is only 0.5 mag fainter than NGC 4889, a cD galaxy in Coma, and is as bright as M87, a cD galaxy in Virgo. It hosts an AGN, and has an S0 companion, NGC 4923 ((R)SA()0-?), at 2 6 southeast of NGC 4921.

The heliocentric radial velocity of NGC 4921, 5482 , is somewhat smaller than that of the Coma cluster, 6925 str99 . Thus it has been considered that NGC 4921 is infalling to the Coma cluster vik97,bro11,and13 . NGC 4921 is, as well as NGC 4569 (M90) in Virgo, a prototype of anemic spiral galaxies introduced in the revised DDO classification system van76 .

In this study we resolve the GCs and extended star clusters in NGC 4921 and NGC 4923, taking advantage of the high resolution ACS images produced via drizzling, and investigate their properties in detail. Second, we study the photometric properties of the star clusters forming in the spur region of NGC 4921 in comparison with those in the normal star-forming region to find any difference between the two.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4921 as Coma's brightest spiral, with a face-on truncated gas disk compressed by ram pressure, protruding dust and molecular filaments, AGN activity, and disturbed H I.

quotes.quote: NGC 4921, the brightest spiral galaxy of the Coma cluster, is an especially good example of the diverse physical mechanisms that ram pressure can trigger. It is a face-on galaxy whose truncated gas disk is compressed on its north-western quadrant, coincident with intense star formation activity cramer21 . High resolution optical and CO observations show filaments of dust and molecular gas on this side protruding from the main gas ring, pointing towards the projected direction of the ICM wind kenney15,cramer21 . These are likely remnants of the densest regions of the interstellar medium prior to the ICM interaction, after the sparser gas has been swept back to form the observed dust front downstream from the wind. This suggests that while in NGC 4921 ram pressure globally is quenching star formation from outside in, it can locally trigger star formation activity at the same time.

In Fig. fig::sfms we marked 4 sources, NGC 4921, NGC 4907, NGC 4911, and NGC 4848, with red circles, indicating they have been found to harbour an AGN in previous studies mahajan10,gavazzi11,toba14 . The two most massive ones ( (M_ / M_ ) 11.0 -- 11.5 ), NGC 4921 and NGC 4907, are quenched late-type galaxies. NGC 4911 is of similar M_ , but resides on the SFMS, while NGC 4848 ( (M_ / M_ ) 9 ) has a sSFR consistent with that of a starburst galaxy. NGC 4921, NGC 4907, and NGC 4848 have disturbed H i components.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4921 as Coma’s brightest spiral, with a shrunken asymmetric gas disk shifted southeast and evidence for ram-pressure and viscous stripping.

quotes.quote: NGC 4921 This is the brightest spiral in Coma, located 24 ( 0.5 Mpc) SE from NGC 4874. It roughly coincides with a second order X--ray peak (Vikhlinin 1997) also detected with XMM by Arnaud et al. (2001) and Briel et al. (2001). NGC 4921 presents a very peculiar picture in 21 cm: a double peaked disk (Fig. 25) which is considerably smaller than the optical one, with a strongly asymmetric distribution, with most of the neutral gas distributed along the SE spiral arm. The gaseous disk presents a striking shift in position of 25 (some 8 kpc) SE from the optical.

NGC 4921 is perhaps the best example in Coma where several mechanisms are present simultaneously. (a) The shrunken disk and the shift between optical and positions look like clear signatures of ram--pressure stripping, which is also supported by the supersonic velocity of the galaxy relative to the cluster, 1521 . (b) NGC 4921 shows the largest cross section in our sample, which could produce, by viscous stripping, a mass loss rate value up to 20 yr ^ 1 . Another feature supporting viscous stripping is the relatively low surface gas density in the central region of NGC 4921, previously classified as anemic by van den Bergh (1976).

sources.summary: NGC 4921 is a face-on stripped Coma spiral whose leading-side dust front and wind-aligned filaments help interpret leading-versus-trailing ram-pressure structures.

quotes.quote: By comparing with face-on stripped Coma galaxy NGC 4921 (this paper Figure n4921 ; kenney15 ), we can make a case that certain features are generally present only on the leading or trailing side of a stripped galaxy. NGC 4921 has a well-defined continuous dust front on its leading side that is over 20 kpc long and extends azimuthally through 90 of the galaxy (Figure n4921 ). The dust front has significant substructure---a number of filaments elongated approximatly in the wind direction connect to it, as well as v-shaped clouds with their vertices located on the upwind sides. The trailing side, however, has no large dust fronts and few structures elongated in the projected ICM wind direction (Figure n4921trail ).

The similarity between the structures in NGC 4402 and NGC 4921 and the lack of clearly analogous kpc-scale filaments and well-defined dust fronts in NGC 4522 is likely due to our viewing angle. In dust extinction, we see the leading side of NGC 4402 and the trailing side of NGC 4522. Comparison with NGC 4921 (Figure n4921 ) shows that dust filaments and fronts may form preferentially on the leading side of a galaxy experiencing ram pressure.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4921 as a Coma cluster anemic spiral, with smoother arms and H I deficiency likely caused by intracluster-medium stripping.

quotes.quote: Anemic spirals are cluster spirals deficient in H i gas and as a result have a lower amount of dust and star formation. NGC 4921 is a Coma cluster spiral that strongly resembles a normal SBb spiral like NGC 3992, but has much smoother spiral arms. The smoothness indicates gas deficiency probably caused by stripping due to the intracluster medium. According to the hydrogen index parameter given in the RC3 (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991), NGC 4921 is H i deficient compared to NGC 3992 by a factor of more than six.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4921 as an irregular-shaped RPS radio source with 13.2 kpc low-frequency extent, no 550-250 MHz detection, and steep 400-1400 MHz spectrum.

quotes.quote: (t): NGC 4921 (a.k.a. GMP 2059) The 250--500 MHz band image suggests it is an irregular-shaped radio morphology source. The largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz band is 13.2 kpc. We do not detect it in our 550-250 MHz band image, but the source was detected in the measurement at 1.4 GHz see Table tab:tab3-rps , Chenetal . Thus, the integrated spectral index is (400--1400 MHz) = 1.16 0.22, which agrees, within uncertainties with the upper limit, (400--700 MHz) 1.90 0.58.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC4921 as a giant spiral in the Coma core whose radio continuum and distorted contours indicate loss of interstellar medium during interaction with the cluster environment.

quotes.quote: The galaxies NGC 4911 and 4921 in the Coma cluster provide spectacular examples of luminous, grand design spirals passing through the hot intracluster medium of a rich cluster of galaxies. Chandra ACIS-I imaging of their environment, coupled with CFHT, HST/WFPC2, and VLA radio imaging, reveal the interactions between the hot X-ray gas and the ISM of the spirals, highlighting the linkage between galaxy and cluster evolution generated by such interactions.

The VLA observations also captured the continuum of NGC4921, a giant spiral to the east of NGC4911. Both galaxies appear to be losing their ISM, as indicated by the distorted, asymmetric, and flowing contours seen in the radio (Figure 3).

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 4921 lies close to a nonabsorbing sightline, has HI detected only to 40 kpc, and may have gas truncated by Coma’s hot ICM.

quotes.quote: The other sightline near \ galaxies, HB89 1259+281, does not have absorbers to deep limits. This is despite the fact that the galaxy NGC 4921 has an impact parameter of only 64 kpc. When we examine this galaxy with new data from the WSRT Coma mosaic (Paolo Serra, private communication), the extent of the detection ends at 40 kpc at a column density 10^ 19 2. NGC 4921 is at 5479 \ (within 1.5 _ v Coma ) and its gas may have been truncated by the hot ICM shown in Figure coma_plot.fig .

gmw_00000898.name: IC 694
gmw_00000898.overview: IC 694 is an evolved, distorted galaxy undergoing a merger with NGC 3690 [arXiv:1108.1747, arXiv:1402.5151]. It hosts a deeply embedded nuclear starburst fueled by dense molecular gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0311154, arXiv:1311.3446], a buried low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1008.4466, arXiv:1210.5354], and both hydroxyl and water megamasers [arXiv:astro-ph/0503070, arXiv:1504.07194].

sources.summary: The paper images Arp 299-A with EVN radio data and identifies its A1-A5 complex as a buried low-luminosity AGN coexisting with nuclear starburst activity.

quotes.quote: Our aim was to characterize the compact sources in the innermost few pc region of Arp 299-A, as well as to detect recently exploded core-collapse supernovae. We used the European VLBI Network (EVN) to image the 1.7 and 5.0 GHz compact radio emission of the parsec-scale structure in the nucleus of Arp 299-A with milliarcsecond resolution. Our EVN observations show that one of the compact VLBI sources, A1, previously detected at 5.0 GHz, has a flat spectrum between 1.7 and 5.0 GHz and is the brightest source at both frequencies. Our 1.7 GHz EVN image shows also diffuse, low-surface brightness emission extending westwards from A1 and displays a prominent core-jet structure. The morphology, radio luminosity, spectral index and ratio of radio-to-X-ray emission of the A1-A5 region is consistent with a low-luminosity AGN (LLAGN), and rules out the possibility that it is a chain of young radio supernovae (RSNe) and supernova remnants (SNRs).

In fact, VLBI observations of the two primary nuclei of Arp 299 have resulted in the discovery of a large population of very compact sources in the nuclear regions of Arp 299-A (=IC694) and Arp 299-B1 (=NGC 3690) pereztorres09a,ulvestad09 .

On the other hand, X-ray imaging and spectroscopy dellaceca02,zezas03,ballo04 , as well as the presence of nuclear H _2 O masers henkel05,tarchi07 , indicate that the nuclear regions of Arp 299-A and Arp 299-B1 are likely to contain an AGN, but its location has so far remained an open question.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the IC 694 nucleus with infrared source A, finds absorbed X-ray emission, and attributes its circumnuclear luminosity to a young stellar population with many high-mass X-ray binaries.

quotes.quote: The X-ray spectrum of Source 16 which is associated with the nucleus of IC 694 (infrared source A) is well fit with a heavily absorbed ( 1.2 10^ 22 ) power-law ( 1.4 ) consistent with either a population of X-ray binaries or an AGN. Its observed luminosity in the 0.1-10.0 keV band is 3.9 10^ 39 , that is 5 of the overall emission from Arp 299. The measured absorbing column density is consistent with the extinction derived from mid-IR observations ( A_ V =17 mags; Charmandaris 2002), and narrow-band NIR photometry and spectroscopy (AH00).

Although the contribution of the nuclear point source (Src 16) to the overall X-ray emission of Arp 299 is small, we detect a significant circumnuclear extended component ( 3.4 1.7 kpc). This region has an absorption corrected luminosity of 3 10^ 40 (0.1-10.0 keV; providing 8 of the observed emission Arp 299 in this band). Based on modeling of the stellar population, the dominant stellar component in nucleus A has an age of 11 Myr. Given the large young stellar population, this region may be expected to contain a significant number of High Mass X-ray Binaries (HMXB).

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 694 (Arp 299-A) as a dust-enshrouded nuclear starburst where e-EVN imaging reveals 26 compact radio sources, mostly young supernovae or remnants, supporting a recent intense starburst.

quotes.quote: We investigate the nuclear region of the starbust galaxy IC 694 (=Arp 299-A) at radio wavelengths, aimed at discovering recently exploded CCSNe, as well as to determine their rate of explosion, which carries crucial information on star formation rates, the initial mass function and starburst scenarios at work. We use the electronic European VLBI Network to image with milliarcsecond resolution the 5.0 GHz compact radio emission of the innermost nuclear region of IC 694. Our observations reveal the presence of a rich cluster of 26 compact radio emitting sources in the central 150 pc of the nuclear starburst in IC 694. The large brightness temperatures observed for the compact sources indicate a non-thermal origin for the observed radio emission, implying that most, if not all, of those sources are young radio supernovae (RSNe) and supernova remnants (SNRs).

e-EVN observations show that Arp 299-A hosts an extremely prolific supernova factory, with radio luminosities typical of Type IIb, IIp, and IIL, and yielding strong support to the scenario proposed by almudena00 , which suggests the existence of a recent (less than 10 15 Myr old) intense, instantaneous starburst. We find evidence for at least three slowly-evolving, long-lasting, non-standarnd RSNe (A0, A12, and A15), which is very suggestive of the local CSM conditions playing a main role in determining the radio behaviour of the exploding SNe.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 299-A with IC 694, describes it as the infrared/radio-bright nucleus of the system, and argues high-resolution radio monitoring revealed compact sources and a buried low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: The brightest component at infrared and radio wavelengths is IC 694 (A in the top panel of Fig. fig3 ; hereafter Arp 299-A), which accounts for 50 of the total infrared luminosity of the system almudena00 , and 70 of its 5 GHz radio emission neff04 . Numerous H II regions populate the system near star-forming regions, which implies that star formation has been occurring at a high rate for past 10 Myr almudena00 . Given that IC 694 accounts for most of the infrared emission in Arp 299, it is the region that is most likely to contain new SNe condon92 . Since optical and near-infrared observations are likely to miss a significant fraction of CCSNe in the innermost regions of Arp 299-A due to high values of extinction [ A_V 34-40 almudena09 ] and the lack of the necessary angular resolution, radio observations of Arp 299-A at high angular resolution, high sensitivity are the only way of detecting new CCSNe and measuring directly and independently of any model its CCSN and star formation rates.

The morphology, spectral index, radio luminosity, and radio-to-X ray luminosity ratio of the A1-A5 complex are consistent with that of an LLAGN, and rules out the possibility that it consists of a chain of young RSNe and SNRs in a young SSC. We therefore concluded that A1 is the long-sought AGN in Arp 299-A. Since Arp 299-A had long been thought of as a pure starburst, our finding of a buried, low-luminosity AGN in its central region, coexisting with a recent burst of starformation, suggests that both a starburst and AGN are frequently associated phenomena in mergers.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 694 as a deeply embedded nuclear source with strong PAH emission, nuclear star formation, continuum dust emission, and a possible less luminous AGN.

quotes.quote: The nuclear GTC/CC spectrum of IC 694/Arp 299-A shows 11.3 m polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) emission stemming from a deeply embedded ( A_V 24 mag) region of less than 120 pc in size. There is also a continuum-emitting dust component. If associated with the putative AGN in IC 694, we estimate that it would be approximately 5 times less luminous than the AGN in NGC 3690.

The GTC/CC nuclear spectrum of IC 694 (Fig. fig:spectra ) shows clear PAH emission at 8.6 and 11.3 m indicative of the presence of SF on nuclear scales of 120 pc. This is consistent with results from high angular resolution near-IR (AAH00) and radio PerezTorres2009 observations of this nucleus. We measured a GTC/CC nuclear EW( 11.3 m PAH )=0.12 0.01 m, which is lower than that measured from the kpc-scale Spitzer /IRS spectrum 0.23 0.01 m, see Pereira2010 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3690/IC 694 as an evolved, distorted interaction with few good matches, many poor low-mass-simulation matches, DISM-favored fits, and overestimated absorption.

quotes.quote: In systems where the DISM set yields better overall matches (e.g. NGC 3226/3227, NGC 3690/IC 694), the AISM model reproduces the observed FIR emission as well or better than the DISM model, but the MIR emission differs significantly.
In more distorted systems (e.g. NGC 3690/IC 694), its absorption is overestimated.

Systems with very few good matches (e.g. NGC 3690/IC 694, NGC 4038/4039, NGC 5394/5395) also have the largest number of worst matches, many of which originate from the interactions between the three less massive simulated galaxies. The ^2 distributions for these galaxies (see Figure chi_by_model ) is flatter than those of other systems, particularly at the low-mass (M0) end, indicating many similarly bad matches. Many of these models are not representative of these three systems in part due to a significant difference in the stellar mass, which has a broad normalizing effect on SEDs. Further, these three systems are among our most evolved and there are only a few snapshots in each interaction simulation that capture the coalescence in its most active phases during which the SED evolves rapidly and variation with viewing angles can become significant because the optical depth of the central starburst can be very high.

sources.summary: The paper finds that IC 694 dominates Arp 299's infrared emission and hosts a deeply embedded, dusty nuclear starburst fueled by dense molecular gas and cold dust.

quotes.quote: IC 694 appears to dominate the infrared emission of the system and our observations support the interpretation of a deeply embedded nuclear starburst located behind an absorption of about 40 magnitudes.

The nuclear region of IC 694 In our mid-IR images, IC 694 (source A) appears as a bright source corresponding to the radio peak observed at 6 and 20 cm by Gehrz83 . As pointed out , unlike the infrared view, images in the UV and visible do not reveal any clear optical counterpart at this position, while large quantities of high density molecular gas are concentrated within the central 500 pc Sargent91,Casoli99 . This gas fuels a massive starburst as evidenced by a supernova rate of 0.65 yr ^ 1 , nearly five times the combined rate observed in B and C Alonso00 .

Our observations are actually consistent with all these possibilities if we consider that IC 694 contains the larger fraction of molecular gas and cold dust and dominates the IR luminosity of Arp 299. This implies that the starburst in source A has been taking place for a longer period of time than in sources B and C since the former seems to be closer to dynamical equilibrium than the two sources in NGC 3690 which are even more disturbed and obviously still strongly interacting Casoli99 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 694 as Arp 299A in the merging Arp 299 system, discusses its AGN/starburst activity, and reports several tentatively detected molecular lines and line ratios.

quotes.quote: Arp 299, a well-known merging system, is composed of two galaxies: IC 694 (Arp 299A) and NGC 3690 (Arp 299B+C, 45'' to the west of Arp 299A). Here Arp 299C refers to the overlapping interface of the two merging galaxies, which is also a starburst HII region e.g., gehrz83, aalto97, casoli99 . It is believed that IC 694 and NGC 3690 both host an AGN with strong starburst ballo04,gm06 , although for IC 694 there is still some controversy gallais04 . Previous interferometry observations have revealed that the ^ 12 CO(1-0), ^ 13 CO(1-0), and HCN(1-0) emission peak mainly at these three components Arp 299A, B, and C aalto97,casoli99 .

The spectrum of IC 694 shows plenty of molecular lines. Besides the obvious HCO ^+ (1-0), HCN(1-0), and C _2 H(1-0) lines, H ^ 13 CN(1-0), HC ^ 15 N(1-0), HCO(1-0), and HNCO(4-3) lines were also tentatively detected at > 2 level (see Figure spec-full ), but H ^ 13 CO ^+ (1-0) was not detected.

sources.summary: The review discusses IC 694 within Arp 299 as hosting OH and water maser emission, a likely nuclear outflow, and evidence for a putative AGN.

quotes.quote: Another OH megamaser source that has recently attracted a lot of attention is that in Arp 299, a merger system produced by the close interaction of two galaxies, IC 694 and NGC 3690. OH megamaser emission was detected from the nuclear region of IC 694 ( ). The OH emission was found coincident in position with the radio continuum peak and confined in a 100 pc rotating structure ( ). In the same galaxy, water maser emission was also detected ( ), with a position slightly offset w..t. that of the OH maser, and associated with an expanding slab of material, seemingly a nuclear outflow ( ). While VLBI observations of the maser emission from both molecules are planned, interestingly, has found strong evidence for the presence of the long-sought putative AGN in the system, associated with the nucleus of IC 694.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 694 as an eastern component of Arp 299 with an obscured nuclear region of uncertain nature and likely OH plus H2O megamaser emission.

quotes.quote: Arp 299 is a merging system at D 40 Mpc, composed of two main sources, IC 694 and NGC 3690 (for an alternative nomenclature, see Sect. 4.2.3), that are separated by 22 \ in east-west direction (e.g. Sargent Scoville 1991). A FIR luminosity of several 10 ^ 11 L _ (Casoli et al. 1992) places Arp 299 near the boundary between luminous (LIRGs) and ultraluminous (ULIRGs) infrared galaxies. Supporting the merging scenario, two highly extended \ tails have been identified by Hibbard Yun (1999). Radio and infrared observations reveal three main regions of activity (e.g. Gehrz et al. 1983; Aalto et al. 1997; Casoli et al. 1999), the nuclear regions of IC 694 and NGC 3690 and an interface where IC 694 and NGC 3690 overlap. NGC 3690 contains a deeply enshrouded active galactic nucleus (AGN), while the situation with respect to the similarly obscured nuclear region of IC 694 is less clear (e.g. Della Ceca et al. 2002; Ballo et al. 2004; Gallais et al. 2004).

With the H _2 O emission likely originating from IC 694 and NGC 3690, Arp 299 is the fourth extragalactic system beyond the Magellanic Clouds that is known to exhibit = 18 cm OH and = 1.3 cm H _2 O maser emission (in NGC 253, NGC 1068 and M 82, such masers are also observed; see Weliachew et al. 1984; Turner 1985; Baudry Brouillet 1996; Gallimore et al. 1996; Henkel et al. 2004). In these other galaxies, however, either H _2 O or OH or both lines only reach kilomaser luminosities. IC 694 may thus be the first known galaxy with both an OH and an H _2 O megamaser (for OH, see Baan Haschick 1990).

sources.summary: The paper reports a tentative H2CO 24-23 detection in IC 694, derives intensity over a broad non-Gaussian line window, and flags possible H2CO blending.

quotes.quote: IC 694 : Previous observation only obtained upper limits of \ 12-11 For IC 694 Lindberg2011 . In our observations, a tentative detection in IC 694 ( > 2 ) is obtained. The line profile of IC 694 obviously deviates from a Gaussian, so we derive the \ intensity by integrating the line within a window of 400 \ width (Table Tab:results2 ). We note that, in NGC 1068 and IC 694, \ 24-23 is possibly blended with H _2 CO 3(0,3) -- 2(0,2) emission ( f_ = 218.22219 GHz).

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 299A as a main Arp 299 component likely hosting an AGN, with nuclear radio supernovae and intense current star formation in its innermost 150 pc.

quotes.quote: Arp 299 consists of two main components, Arp 299A and Arp 299B (sometimes referred to as IC 694 and NGC 3690, respectively), both likely to host an active galactic nucleus see e.g. dellaceca02, perez-torres10 .

The milliarcsecond resolution of very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) radio observations has proven that there is a rich population of radio supernovae (SNe) and young SN remnants in the nuclear regions of Arp 299A and Arp 299B, within only 100 150 and 30 pc from the centre, respectively perez-torres09,ulvestad09,romero11,bondi12 . In particular, bondi12 reported a lower limit of core-collapse supernova (CCSN) rate of 0.80 yr ^ 1 for Arp 299A, suggesting that the bulk of the current star formation was taking place in the innermost 150 pc.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC694 as the prior dual hydroxyl and water megamaser case, motivating tests of whether such systems mark a narrow phase of galaxy evolution.

quotes.quote: The detection of simultaneous hydroxyl and water megamaser emission toward IC694 has called this hypothesis into question but, because many megamasers have not been surveyed for emission in the other molecule, it remains unclear whether IC694 occupies a narrow phase of galaxy evolution or whether the relationship between megamaser species and galactic processes is more complicated than previously believed.
We confirm with high degree of confidence ( > 8 ) the detection of water emission toward IIZw96, firmly establishing it as the second object to co-host both water and hydroxyl megamasers after IC694.
However, water emission has since been discovered in multiple regions in Arp 299 including Arp 299-A (IC 694), a galactic nucleus also hosting an OH megamaser (T11).

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 694 as a colliding starburst galaxy pair, previously linked to the AGASA triplet and considered a luminous infrared galaxy detectable in gamma rays.

quotes.quote: IC 694 is a colliding pair of galaxies with a starburst region . A possible relation of this object to the AGASA triplet was discussed in Ref. . It enters the list of luminuos infrared galaxies with a gamma-ray flux in excess of the expected GLAST sensitivity .

gmw_00000899.name: R.A. 17h 03m 30.3830s, Dec. +45 ^ 40 47 .167
gmw_00000899.overview: R.A. 17h 03m 30.3830s, Dec. +45 ^ 40 47.167 is a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 spiral galaxy [arXiv:1701.07298, arXiv:2405.07494] that is interacting with a dwarf companion [arXiv:2011.08762, arXiv:2307.06087]. It hosts a radio jet [arXiv:1701.07298, arXiv:2006.09330] and exhibits multi-phase X-ray outflows [arXiv:1810.07714, arXiv:2405.07494] alongside a variable warm absorber [arXiv:1810.07714].

sources.summary: The coordinate-identified source is described as a bright NLS1 spiral galaxy with a radio jet and a complex ultra-fast X-ray outflow capable of feedback.

quotes.quote: Recent XMM-Newton observations have revealed that \ is a very unusual example of black hole wind-produced feedback by a moderately luminous AGN in a spiral galaxy. Since the source is known for being a radio emitter, we investigated about the presence and the properties of a non-thermal component.

\ (located at R.A. 17h 03m 30.3830s, Dec. \ +45 ^ 40 47 .167, z=0.0604 ) is an ideal target to further explore this subject. It is a bright Narrow Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) spiral galaxy with black hole mass of M_ BH 5.9 10^6 M_ Wang2001 . We have observed it in January 2014 with , obtaining a high-resolution X-ray spectrum. Our observations reveal a series of absorption lines corresponding to at least 5 outflowing components with velocity in the range of 23,000-33,000 km/s Longinotti2015 . The energetics of the wind indicate that it might be able to produce feedback on the host galaxy.
At the same time, the presence of bright and compact radio emission indicates that this source is a radio loud (RL) NLS1 and that it also has a radio jet Doi2007,Doi2011,Gu2010 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes IRAS 17020+4544 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 with ultra-fast outflow and warm-absorber components whose decade-scale variability suggests a shocked-outflow interpretation.

quotes.quote: We present the analysis of grating spectra of the Narrow Line Seyfert 1 Galaxy \ observed by \ in 2004 and 2014. In a previous work on these data, we reported the discovery of a multi-component ultra-fast outflow that is capable of producing feedback in the host galaxy. We also reported the presence of a slow, multi-phase warm absorber. In this follow-up paper, we confirm that this low velocity absorber can be modeled by four layers of ionized gas.

When crossing our line-of-sight, this gas presents peculiar changes along the 10-yr time scale elapsed between the two observations obtained by . While two of such components are almost stationary, the other two are found inflowing and outflowing with significant variations in velocity and ionization between 2004 and 2014. The luminosity and spectral shape of the central source remain practically unvaried. We propose that the presence of the fast wind and of the variable warm absorber can be interpreted in the framework of a ``shocked outflow'', where the peculiar variability pattern of the low-velocity components might arise from instabilities in the shocked gas.

sources.summary: The paper studies IRAS 17020+4544 as a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1, finding unusual X-ray variability, a soft-state epoch, reverberation changes, and a broad Fe K emission line.

quotes.quote: We present results of temporal and spectral analyses on four EPIC pn observations of IRAS 17020+4544, a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy with evidence of a radio jet. Analysis of the light curves reveals that this radio-loud source does not behave like the bulk population of its radio-quiet counterparts. A trend of spectral hardening with increased flux is found. Variability is found to increase with energy, though it decreases as the spectrum hardens. The first 40 ks of the most recent observation behave uniquely among the epochs, exhibiting a softer spectral state than at any other time.

A reverberation signature is confirmed, with the lag-frequency, lag-energy, and covariance spectra changing significantly during the soft-state epoch. The temporal analysis suggests a variable power-law in the presence of a reflection component, thus motivating such a fit for the 0.3-10 keV EPIC pn spectra from all epochs. We find an acceptable spectral fit using the timing-motivated parameters and report the detection of a broad Fe K emission line, requiring an additional model component beyond the reflection spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes IRAS 17020+4544's molecular gas, finding a central disc, northern extension, dwarf-galaxy interaction, and AGN-driven redistribution or photodissociation of nuclear molecular gas.

quotes.quote: The narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy IRAS 17020+4544 is one of the few sources where both an X-ray ultra-fast outflow and a molecular outflow were observed to be consistent with energy conservation. However, IRAS 17020+4544 is less massive and has a much more modest active galactic nucleus (AGN) luminosity than the other examples. Using recent CO(1-0) observations with the NOrthern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA), we characterised the molecular gas content of the host galaxy for the first time. We found that the molecular gas is distributed into an apparent central disc of 1.1 10^9 M_ , and a northern extension located up to 8 kpc from the centre with a molecular gas mass M_ H_2 10^8 M_ .

The molecular gas mass and the CO dynamics in the northern extension reveal that IRAS 17020+4544 is not a standard spiral galaxy, instead it is interacting with a dwarf object corresponding to the northern extension. This interaction possibly triggers the high accretion rate onto the super massive black hole. Within the main galaxy, which hosts the AGN, a simple analytical model predicts that the molecular gas may lie in a ring, with less molecular gas in the nuclear region. Such distribution may be the result of the AGN activity which removes or photodissociates the molecular gas in the nuclear region (AGN feedback).

sources.summary: The paper confirms IRAS 17020+4544's CO luminosity with IRAM 30m but finds no molecular-outflow evidence because the new data lack sufficient sensitivity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 17020+4544 The CO(1-0) emission was previously observed with the LMT Longinotti_2018 and NOEMA SalomeQ_2021 . With the IRAM 30m, we found a CO luminosity in the host galaxy, in agreement with both the LMT and NOEMA measurements. We thus confirm the conclusion of that all the CO emission was recovered by the NOEMA observations, with no spatial filtering by the interferometer. However, the data from the IRAM 30m show no evidence for the molecular outflows detected with the LMT Longinotti_2018 and NOEMA Longinotti_2023 .

The rms noise of 3.9 mJy we reached with the IRAM 30m for CO(1-0) is higher than the peak temperature of 1 mJy for the resolved outflows with NOEMA and 1.1 mJy for the broad component observed with the LMT. Therefore, the non detection of the outflow components is simply due to a lack of sensitivity.

For example, using resolved CO observations, discovered that the host galaxy of IRAS 17020 is interacting with a smaller mass companion, while the optical images do not show any evidence of interaction.

sources.summary: The paper finds clear absorption in IRAS 17020+454, modeled with edges near 0.71 and 1.15 keV and interpreted as warm-absorber evidence consistent with prior work.

quotes.quote: The residuals from a simple power law plus Galactic absorption fit show clear signs of absorption in IRAS 17020+454 (see Figure 2). The addition of an edge at an energy of E= 0.71 0.02 keVwith a depth =0.91 0.22 , improves the fit significantly( ^ 2 / =979/971 ). A second edge at E= 1.15 0.04 keV with a depth =0.20 0.08 further improves the fit ( ^ 2 / =965/969 ). A broad Gaussian absorption line gives a comparable fit as detailed in Table 4. A narrow Gaussian emission line at an energy of E= 1.06 0.02 with an equivalent width of EW= 23^ +5 _ 7 eV gives a worse fit than an absorption line ( ^ 2 / =1006/971 ).

In agreement with Leighly (1997a) we identify the 0.7 keV feature with an absorption edge from O vii . Komossa Bade (1998) present a \ spectrum of IRAS 17020+454 and also find evidence for a warm absorber, albeit a dusty one.

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 17020+4544 in its comparison sample, deriving black-hole, bolometric, X-ray wind, and CO outflow inputs while revising the inferred UFO parameters.

quotes.quote: IRAS 17020+4544 . We infer the black hole mass from the M_ BH relation of KH13 using the \ velocity dispersion 125 WangLu01 as a proxy for the stellar . We bracket the value of L_ BOL using two measurements, the first from the 5100 Hao+15 and the second (more conservative) from that assumed in Longinotti+15 , from which we take also the X-ray wind properties. The properties of the CO molecular outflow are from Lutz+20 . In Longinotti+18 , the outflow properties of this QSO were found to be compatible with an energy-driven regime (although with large uncertainties). The differences here are due to a factor of 5 lower covering factor assumed for the UFO and to a factor 20 lower gas column density, which arises as we interpret the entry (N_ H,UFO / )=23.99^ +0 _ 1.86 in Table 2 of Longinotti+15 as a truncated Gaussian distribution, leading to a median (N_ H,UFO / ) 22.73 .

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 17020+4544 as a radio-loud NLS1 with warm absorbers, a soft-X-ray UFO, molecular outflow, and compact radio emission.

quotes.quote: IRAS 17020+4544 emerges as a nearby ( z=0.0604 ) radio-loud NLS1 galaxy, known to be characterized by a WA 1997Leighly and a compact radio emission 2004Snellen . The archival multi-wavelength campaign for IRAS 17020+4544 has revealed the presence of multi-phase UFOs ( v  24000 - 27000 km/s) in the X-ray spectra 2015Longinotti,2018Sanfrutos , a galaxy-scale molecular outflow 2018Longinotti in addition to a sub-relativistic jet on parsec scales in the radio band 2017Giroletti . Moreover, a UV counterpart of the X-ray UFO outflow has been detected through Ly absorption, showing a velocity similar to its X-ray counterpart.

Collectively, these discoveries suggest that AGN-driven hot gas gives rise to large-scale shocks into the interstellar medium. The outcomes of the line scan are depicted in Fig. app:fig:gaussian-iras17020+4544 , exhibiting strong absorption and emission lines within the spectra.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 17020 as an NLSy1 galaxy whose X-ray spectrum is steep and spectrally complex, with a dusty warm absorber requiring an even steeper intrinsic continuum.

quotes.quote: IRAS 17020+4544 A dusty warm absorber was suggested to be present in the NLSy1 galaxy IRAS 17020 (Leighly et al. 1997b) on the basis of an N_ opt N_ x discrepancy and the presence of an absorption edge in the spectrum. When fit by a single powerlaw, the X-ray spectrum of IRAS 17020 is rather steep ( = 2.4 ). Checking whether (and under which conditions) a dusty warm absorber fits the X-ray spectrum, we find that an even steeper intrinsic spectrum is required to compensate for the `flattening effect' (cf. Fig. dust_seq ) of dust (Tab. 1); for details on IRAS 17020 see Komossa Bade (1998). The presence of a dusty warm absorber in IRAS 17020 particularly adds to the spectral complexity in NLSy1 galaxies. Whereas early NLSy1 models tried to explain their very steep observed X-ray spectra by only one component (either a strong soft excess, or a warm absorber, or an intrinsically steep spectrum), there is now evidence that often all three components are simultaneously present, and the additional presence of dusty material partly compensates the `steepening effect' of the other three.

sources.summary: The paper describes B3 1702+457 as an EESBH whose high radio brightness temperature, prior AGN radio interpretation, and VLBA bipolar structure indicate AGN-driven radio activity.

quotes.quote: Only two sources (B3 1702+457 and Mrk 486) have radio brightness temperatures above 10^5 K, i.e. 10^ 5.36 K and 10^ 5.10 K, respectively. Since a star-forming region cannot reach T_B 10^5 K at frequency > 1 GHz 1991ApJ...378...65C, 1992ASPC...31...79C, 1992ARA&A..30..575C , we conclude that the radio emission observed in these two sources are preferentially from AGN activities.

Among this four objects, the radio emission from B3 1702+457 has already been proven to be from AGN 2010AJ....139.2612G , while the radio emission from the remaining sources are likely dominated by the star-forming activity.

There is only one source (Akn 564) in our sample that shows such quasi-continuous radio ejection, and VLBA images show that B3 1702+457 in our EESBHs sample has a bipolar emission 2011ApJ...738..126D .

sources.summary: The paper reports JVN 8.4 GHz VLBI detection of B3 1702+457 as an unresolved radio-loud NLS1 with high brightness temperature, attributing its radio emission mainly to AGN activity.

quotes.quote: We performed phase-reference very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) observations on five radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxies (NLS1s) at 8.4 GHz with the Japanese VLBI Network (JVN). Each of the five targets (RXS J08066+7248, RXS J16290+4007, RXS J16333+4718, RXS J16446+2619, and B3 1702+457) in milli-Jansky levels were detected and unresolved in milli-arcsecond resolutions, i.e., with brightness temperatures higher than 10^7 K. The nonthermal processes of active galactic nuclei (AGN) activity, rather than starbursts, are predominantly responsible for the radio emissions from these NLS1s.

gmw_00000900.name: XID 643
gmw_00000900.overview: XID 643 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 1.51 [arXiv:2111.06024]. Observations of the source have revealed a short-lived, quasi-periodic oscillation signal with a period of 13273 seconds [arXiv:2111.06024].

sources.summary: The paper reports XID 643 as a high-redshift AGN candidate showing a 13273-second short-lived QPO signal, with supporting light curves but only modest Gregory-Loredo probability.

quotes.quote: XID 643 (R.A.=53.143475, Decl.=-27.653576 [ J2000 ] ) has a mean count rate of 6.0 10^ 4  cts s^ 1 and exhibits a periodic signal at 13273 s with a significance of 99.89 among ten observations starting with ObsID 16620 and ending with ObsID 17573, lasting for about 3 months. Light curves from a subset of these ten observations also yield a a signal around the same period, although at somewhat lower significance. As an additional check, the same light curve spanning the ten observations, when fed to the GL algorithm, gives a signal at a nearly identical period, but with a probability of only 74 . This might be partly due to its location near the CCD edge in most observations of Epoch 4, which causes reduced sensitivity. XID 643 is identified as a high-redshift ( z =1.51) AGN with an intrinsic luminosity of 9 10^ 43   erg s^ 1 from 2017ApJS..228....2L , suggesting an intrinsic quasi-period of 5288 s, if the signal was real.

Our additional search for short-lived QPOs, referring to that only detected over a small subset of all observations, results in two statistically significant signals, one in source XID 643 at a period of 13273 s and the other in source XID 876 at a period of 7065 s. The reality of these signals invite confirmation with future observations.

gmw_00000901.name: XID 876
gmw_00000901.overview: XID 876 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 3.47 [arXiv:2111.06024]. Chandra observations of the source revealed a candidate short-lived quasi-periodic oscillation with a period of 7065 seconds [arXiv:2111.06024].

sources.summary: The paper identifies XID 876 as a high-redshift AGN with a 7065-second candidate short-lived QPO detected in one Chandra observation and supported by Gregory-Loredo analysis.

quotes.quote: XID 876 (R.A.=53.209310, Decl.=-27.881104 [ J2000 ] ) has a mean count rate of 5.5 10^ 4  cts s^ 1 and exhibits a statistically significant periodic signal of 7065 s (1-FAP = 99.92 ) in only one light curve, which is from one single observation (ObsID 8592) lasting 90.1 ks. The same light curve fed to the GL algorithm finds the same periodic signal with a probability of 99.84 , leading some support to its reality. This source is identified as a high-redshift ( z =3.47) AGN with an intrinsic luminosity of 4 10^ 44   erg s^ 1 from 2017ApJS..228....2L , suggesting an intrinsic quasi-period of 1581 s.

Our additional search for short-lived QPOs, referring to that only detected over a small subset of all observations, results in two statistically significant signals, one in source XID 643 at a period of 13273 s and the other in source XID 876 at a period of 7065 s. The reality of these signals invite confirmation with future observations.

gmw_00000902.name: NGC 2654
gmw_00000902.overview: NGC 2654 is an edge-on disk galaxy [arXiv:1211.6752] that features an untruncated stellar disk out to 5.9 radial scale-lengths [arXiv:astro-ph/0609384]. The galaxy hosts a central bar structure [arXiv:1211.6752] and a strong central peak likely enhanced by stars formed from gas pushed inward by the bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0502316].

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 2654 profile cuts with simulations, noting similar features but a stronger central peak likely enhanced by centrally formed stars.

quotes.quote: Compared to the cuts of e.g. NGC 2654 (L uticke 2000), or of other similarly viewed such galaxies, they show striking similarities. There is, nevertheless, a quantitative difference, namely that the central peak in the cuts near the equatorial plane is much stronger in NGC 2654 than in the simulation. Simulations with a classical bulge show a bigger peak (right panel of Fig. fig:hcuts ), but, at least in my simulations, not as big as that observed e.g. in NGC 2654. The latter is presumably enhanced by stars formed from the gaseous material that was pushed by the bar towards the central regions, and is not present in the N body simulations, since these are dissipationless. The remaining features are found both in the observed and the simulated profiles.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2654 as an observed edge-on disk-galaxy example where cuts along and beside the major axis estimate thin-bar and boxy/peanut extents.

quotes.quote: Figure  fig:bp-length-obs allows us to get an estimate for NGC 2654. L utticke et al. (2000) made a cut along the major axis of this edge-on disk galaxy and from the projected surface density profile along it they obtained the thin bar length (confront with method (vi) from AM02). They also made cuts parallel to this and offset above or below it and from them could obtain the extent of the bar/peanut part. In this way, L utticke et al. (2000) were able to measure the ratio of extent of the thin part of the bar to the extent of the thick boxy/peanut part and show clearly that the former can be much longer than the latter.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2654 as untruncated within the observations, reporting no detected disk truncation down to J < 25 mag/arcsec ^2 and out to 5.9 radial scale-lengths.

quotes.quote: b) Untruncated galaxies: NGC 684, UGC 4906, NGC 2654 and NGC 6835. Of course we cannot assure that these are not truncated, but only state that no truncation was observed for face-on values of J < 23 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 684, for J < 24 mag/arcsec ^2 in UGC 4906, for J < 25 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 2654 or for J < 22 mag/arcsec ^2 in NGC 6835. We refer to the J filter limits since in these galaxies the J band data covers a more extended region in the object, as can be seen in Fig. 4. In consequence we did not detect truncations out to 3.5 radial scale-lengths for NGC 684, 5.0 for UGC 4906, 5.9 for NGC 2654 or 3.3 for NGC 6835.

gmw_00000903.name: NGC 4762
gmw_00000903.overview: NGC 4762 is an edge-on barred lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1903.04738, arXiv:astro-ph/0502316] featuring a boxy bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0502316], a bar-lens, ansae, and a truncated disk [arXiv:1903.04738]. The galaxy exhibits a disturbed outer disk [arXiv:1907.10706] that is possibly warped due to an ongoing tidal encounter [arXiv:1903.04738].

sources.summary: The paper decomposes NGC 4762 as a barred lenticular galaxy with a small bulge, bar-lens, bar, ansae, truncated disk, and possible warped outer disk.

quotes.quote: Figure profiler shows the multi-component fit to the surface brightness profile of NGC 4762, for both the major- and equivalent-axes. It is a barred-lenticular galaxy with a small bulge, an (oval-shaped) bar-lens, a bar, ansae, and a truncated disk. Laurikainen:2005, Laurikainen:2007, Laurikainen:2011 observed that many S0 galaxies contain bars and ovals (also known as lenses or bar-lenses ), with the inner regions of vertically-heated bars appearing as boxy/(peanut shell)-shaped structures referred to by some as pseudobulges see Combes:Sanders:1981, Athanassoula:2002, Athanassoula:2005 .

The bumps in the light profile of NGC 4762, as well as the ellipticity, B_4 , and B_6 profiles at R_ maj 30 and R_ maj 80 correspond to the perturbation of the isophotes due to the bar-lens/pseudobulge and the bar, respectively. As shown in the simulations by their Figure 7 SAHA:GRAHAM:2018 , the adjacent bump ( R_ maj 80 ) and dip ( R_ maj 120 ) in the B_6 profile suggest the presence of an ansae at R_ maj 100 , at the end of the bar. We also note that the decomposition results from e.g., their Figure 11; see also NGC 4026 and NGC 4371 in our Appendix SAHA:GRAHAM:2018 support the truncated disk model A truncated disk model has a change in slope beyond the truncation radius in NGC 4762. Also, according to Kormendy:Bender:4762:2012 , the warped disk at the outer edge is possibly due to some ongoing tidal encounter.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4762 as an edge-on barred galaxy with a boxy bulge, plateau photometry, and kinematic signatures characteristic of barred systems.

quotes.quote: NGC 4762 is an object with a structure that is similar to, yet somewhat more complicated than, the galaxies shown in L utticke (2000). Its photometry (Wakamatsu Hamabe 1984) reveals a plateau in cuts on, or near, the equatorial plane, such as those that are associated with bars in edge-on systems. A deep image (see e.g. Fig. 1b in Wakamatsu Hamabe 1984, or the image of the SDSS) shows clearly that it has a boxy bulge.

Fisher (1997) obtained kinematical data for this galaxy. Those show a number of the characteristic signatures of barred galaxies (compared with the simulation results in Bureau Athanassoula 2005 and the observations of Chung Bureau 2004), like the double hump in the velocity profile and the fact that h_3 and V correlate over the presumed extent of the bar, except for the innermost part where they anti-correlate. It is thus clearly a barred galaxy seen edge-on.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4762 with Gemini/NIFS and SAURON kinematics and finds its roughly 2.3e7 solar-mass black hole lies on the scaling relation.

quotes.quote: We present mass estimates of supermassive black holes in six nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies (NGC 4339, NGC 4434, NGC 4474, NGC 4551, NGC 4578 and NGC 4762) with effective stellar velocity dispersion around 100 km/s. We use near-infrared laser-guide adaptive optics observations with the GEMINI/NIFS to derive stellar kinematics in the galactic nuclei, and SAURON observations from the ATLAS ^ 3D Survey for large-scale kinematics. We build axisymmetric Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild dynamical models.
The black holes in NGC 4578 and NGC 4762 lie on the scaling relation with masses of (1.9^ +0.6 _ 1.4 ) 10^7 and (2.3^ +0.9 _ 0.6 ) 10^7 M _ , respectively ( 3 confidence level).

sources.summary: The paper describes VCC 2095 as an edge-on lenticular with four components, a very small bulge-to-total ratio, and a possible S0-to-spheroidal bridge role.

quotes.quote: VCC 2095 (NGC 4762). This edge-on lenticular galaxy shows an unusual internal structure that consists of four distinct components 1984ApJS...56..283W,1989ApJ...339..783H,2004ARA&A..42..603K . 1984ApJS...56..283W presumed that the four components are the bulge, bar, lens, and outer ring. The galaxy shows an extremely small bulge-to-total light ratio ( B/T=0.13 0.02 ) as a S0 galaxy. 2012ApJS..198....2K suggested that this late-type S0 galaxy bridges the gap between S0 and spheroidal galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4762 as a disturbed edge-on S0 whose HERON image shows an extended shoe-like structure likely made of interaction-heated disk stars.

quotes.quote: Fig.  4762range shows the disturbed edge-on S0 galaxy NGC 4762. Deep images have previously shown the disturbed disk in the second panel, but our HERON images show an extended ``shoe''-like structure that we suggest may consist of disk stars that were heated or disturbed during an interaction. We conclude that the outermost detectable envelopes of face-on disks consist of disk stars.
We also illustrate two cases, NGC 4762 and NGC 3628, where interactions have resulted in the disk outskirts being strongly disturbed and thickened.

gmw_00000904.name: NGC 2590
gmw_00000904.overview: NGC 2590 is a spiral galaxy with a structure that can be modeled using a single dense central Newtonian component [arXiv:astro-ph/0511756]. This simplified model fits the galaxy well despite assuming constant scale radii and lacking azimuthal dependence [arXiv:astro-ph/0511756].

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 2590 is modeled with one dense central Newtonian component and that the model fits despite simplified constant scale radii.

quotes.quote: Figs fig:fig8 and fig:fig9 , respectively, show NGC 1097 and NGC 2590 modeled with only one central dense Newtonian component and they fit the model very well. Deviations in most cases I believe can be attributed to the fact that no azimuthal dependence has been added to the model, nor does it allow for anything but constant scale radii over regions of the galaxy where the fits apply. That is clearly unphysical but seems to be a reasonable approximation.

gmw_00000905.name: A 1775
gmw_00000905.overview: A 1775 is a system that hosts a central dumbbell galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0612530]. Both components of this central system are radio galaxies, with one associated with a double radio source and the companion associated with a head-tail radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0612530].

sources.summary: The paper describes the central dumbbell system in A 1775 as two radio galaxies with double and head-tail sources, measured flux, morphology, and spectrum.

quotes.quote: A 1775 A1775 - A 1775 hosts a Dumbell galaxy at its centre. Both galaxies are radio sources: a double source (labelled as D in Fig. fig:1 ) is associated with the North--Western component, while an head--tail (HT) is associated with the companion galaxy. We measured a total flux density of S_ (HT+D) =2.04 Jy at 235 MHz. We observed the source also at 610 MHz (G06), and found a morphology in very good agreement with the 235 MHz image. Using our data, and literature data, we determined a total spectral index of =1.15 (S ^ ) between 80 MHz and 4.85 MHz.

gmw_00000906.name: A 2622
gmw_00000906.overview: A 2622 is a cD galaxy associated with a double radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0612530]. The source exhibits a very steep spectral index, with spectral index distribution measurements confirming the presence of aged radio emission along its lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0612530].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the cD in A 2622 with a double radio source whose steep spectrum and lobe steepening indicate aged radio emission.

quotes.quote: A 2622 A2622 - A double radio source is associated with the cD at 235 MHz (Fig. fig:1 ). Our 5 ^ resolution observation at 610 MHz (G06) reveals a complex structure for this source. Its spectral index is very steep ( =1.42 ) between 74 MHz and 4.5 GHz. The 235--610 MHz spectral index distribution over the source shows that 0.6-0.7 in the central region, and steepens along the lobes up to 1.8-2 (G06). This confirms the presence of aged radio emission in this galaxy.

gmw_00000907.name: MKW 03s
gmw_00000907.overview: MKW 03s is a brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:2104.07677] classified as a relic radio galaxy [arXiv:2104.07677, arXiv:1903.10768]. It features two ionized gas filaments extending 25 kiloparsecs to the south and southwest [arXiv:2104.07677, arXiv:1903.10768], alongside two radio lobes with an extremely steep spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0612530].

sources.summary: The paper studies 3CR 318.1 as the BCG radio galaxy in MKW 3s, linking its optical/X-ray filaments to low-excitation gas and likely AGN-driven outflows.

quotes.quote: During the MURALES survey, an optical filamentary structure was detected in 3CR 318.1 ( Balmaverde2019 ), the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of the galaxy cluster MKW 3s ( Morgan1975 ), in agreement with observations by Edwards2009 . This structure consists of two ionized gas filaments extending 25 kpc in the south and the south-west directions, both with almost constant velocities along their projected lengths ( 230 km s ^ 1 and 280 km s ^ 1 , respectively). The filament that points to the south presents a bright knot at its southern end.

3CR 318.1 is the radio source associated with the nearby ( z=0.0453 , which corresponds to 0.896 kpc/arcsec) galaxy NGC 5920, the BCG of the galaxy cluster MKW 3s. Based on its optical line emission, it can be classified as an extremely low-excitation galaxy (see Capetti2013 ). The central source presents a steep spectrum at low radio frequencies (i.e., ^ 150 MHz _ 1.4 GHz =0.63 ) with a luminosity of L_ 150 MHz =23.16 W Hz ^ 1 ( Capetti2020 ). The surrounding diffuse radio emission also shows an extremely steep radio spectrum ( ^ 1.28 GHz _ 235 MHz =2.42 ), leading to a classification of relic radio galaxy by Giacintucci2007 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 318.1 as a likely relic radio source with steep spectrum and two southern line-emission plumes of distinct blueshifted velocities.
