quotes.quote: 3C 318.1: a very peculiar source from the point of view of its radio properties, a likely relic source, with an extremely steep spectrum ( _ 235 MHz ^ 1.28 GHz =2.42) giacintucci07 . The line emission, beside the bright region cospatial with the host, shows two linear plumes emerging from the center and extending toward the South, at slightly different angles, for 25 kpc. One of them terminates with a bright spot, not associated with any continuum structure. The plumes show an almost constant velocity along their whole length, at slightly different velocities: the plume pointing to South is blueshifted by 230 , while that to the South-West has a larger blueshift, 280 . The line dispersion is much larger on the host than on the plumes, where it is generally consistent with the MUSE spectral resolution.

sources.summary: The paper describes MKW 03s as a cD radio source with two lobes, no detected compact nucleus at 235 MHz, and very steep relic emission.

quotes.quote: MKW 03s MKW03s - A combined GMRT 1.28 GHz/610 MHz and Chandra study of this source was presented in Mazzotta et al. . In the new 235 MHz image (Fig. fig:2 ) the source shows two lobes, the southern lobe being brighter than the northern one. We do not detect the compact component between the lobes observed at higher frequency, and associated with the cD nucleus. The source 235 MHz flux density is 8.44 Jy. The spectral index between 235 MHz and 1.28 GHz is very steep ( =2.49 ), confirming the presence of relic emission in this source.

gmw_00000908.name: A 2162
gmw_00000908.overview: A 2162 is a radio galaxy that exhibits weak radio emission at 610 MHz [arXiv:astro-ph/0612530]. This emission is characterized by two radio lobes with no detected central compact component [arXiv:astro-ph/0612530].

sources.summary: The paper reports weak 610 MHz emission from A 2162, two radio lobes without a central compact component, and a 74 MHz--4.7 GHz spectral index.

quotes.quote: A 2162 A2162 - The 610 MHz radio emission is weak, and shows two radio lobes and no hint of a central compact component (Fig.2). The source flux density at 610 MHz is 240 mJy. Using this value and literature data, we found =0.81 in the 74 MHz--4.7 GHz frequency range.

gmw_00000909.name: A 2372
gmw_00000909.overview: A 2372 is a cD galaxy that hosts a very large wide-angle tailed radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0612530]. The radio source exhibits spectral index steepening from the core and along its jets into the outer regions of its tails [arXiv:astro-ph/0612530].

sources.summary: The paper describes A 2372 as a cD hosting a large WAT radio source, observed at two GMRT frequencies with core, jet, and tail spectral steepening.

quotes.quote: A 2372 A2372 - This cD hosts a very large WAT radio source, with a total flux density of 1.02 Jy at 610 MHz (Fig. fig:2 ). We observed A 2372 also at 235 MHz (G06). Using our data and literature values, we found =1.13 between 235 and 1400 MHz. The image of the 235--610 MHz spectral index distribution shows that the 0.5 in the core, and steepens from 0.7 to 1.0 along the jets. In the outer regions of the tails 2 (G06).

gmw_00000910.name: J105400.40+034801.2
gmw_00000910.overview: J105400.40+034801.2 is a luminous quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.1 [arXiv:0808.3998]. It exhibits a high-velocity broad-line outflow that emerged between 2002 and 2006, which subsequently weakened and narrowed by 2008 [arXiv:0808.3998].

sources.summary: The paper reports a luminous z 2.1 quasar where high-velocity BAL-like outflow lines emerged, weakened later, and imply optically thick partial covering.

quotes.quote: Received 2008 August 20; in original form 2008 August 14 firstpage - lastpage 2008 We report the first discovery of the emergence of a high-velocity broad-line outflow in a luminous quasar, J105400.40+034801.2 at redshift z 2.1 . The outflow is evident in ultraviolet \ and \ absorption lines with velocity shifts v 26,300 \ and deblended widths FWHM 4000 . These features are marginally strong and broad enough to be considered broad absorption lines (BALs), but their large velocities exclude them from the standard BAL definition. The outflow lines appeared between two observations in the years 2002.18 and 2006.96. A third observation in 2008.48 showed the lines becoming 40 weaker and 10 to 15 narrower. There is no evidence for acceleration or for any outflow gas at velocities 23,000 . The lines appear to be optically thick, with the absorber covering just 20 of the quasar continuum source. This indicates a characteristic absorber size of 4 10^ 15 cm, but with a BAL-like total column density N_H ( cm ^ 2 ) 21.2 and average space density n_H 2 10^5 .

gmw_00000911.name: APM 08279+5255
gmw_00000911.overview: APM 08279+5255 is a highly luminous, gravitationally lensed broad absorption line quasar located at a redshift of z = 3.91 [arXiv:0905.2683, arXiv:1804.10038]. The system hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1512.03195] and exhibits highly variable relativistic outflows and disk winds [arXiv:1904.07341, arXiv:1609.07527].

sources.summary: The paper treats APM 08279+5255 as an old BAL quasar at z=3.91 whose Fe/O-inferred age and formation time create an age problem for CDM cosmology.

quotes.quote: APM 08279+5255 is an exceptionally luminous broad absorption line (BAL) quasar at redshift z=3.91 . From XMM-Newton observations of APM 08279+5255, Hasinger et al. (2002) have derived an iron overabundance of Fe/O of 3.3 0.9 (here the abundance ratio has been normalized to the solar value) for the BAL system. Using an Fe/O=3 abundance ratio, derived from X-ray observations, Komossa and Hasinger (2003) estimated the age of the quasar APM 08279+5255 to lie within the interval 2-3 Gyr. An age of 3 Gyr is inferred from the temporal evolution of Fe/O ratio in the giant elliptical model (M4a) of Hamann and Ferland (1993, hereafter HF93). For the `extreme model' M6a of HF93, the Fe/O evolution would be faster, and Fe/O=3 is already reached after 2 Gyr.

Based on the above discussions, we obtain the following estimates of the age of APM 08279+5255 since the initial star formation and stellar evolution in the galaxy : (1) the best estimated value is 2.1 Gyr; (2) 1 lower limit is 1.8 Gyr; (3) the lowest limit is 1.5 Gyr. We therefore conclude that the quasar APM 08279+5255 started its initial formation process at least 0.2-0.3 Gyr since the beginning of the Universe. In all these cases, we found that CDM model accommodates the total age ( 14 Gyr for z=0 ) of the Universe estimated from old globular clusters, but cannot accommodate statistically the 1 lower limit to the best-fit age of APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 .

sources.summary: The paper describes APM 08279+5255 as a lensed BAL quasar with persistent, variable iron-K X-ray absorption indicating a powerful relativistic outflow.

quotes.quote: In 2002, we proposed that a relativistic outflow was responsible for creating remarkable 8--12 keV (rest-frame) absorption features seen in a \ spectrum of the luminous and gravitationally lensed BAL quasar APM 08279+5255 ( z=3.91 ; Chartas et al. 2002). These features were best fit by absorption lines, so we proposed that they were X-ray BALs from iron K transitions. The implied (projected) velocity, even for highly ionized iron (the most conservative assumption), was remarkably large with v 0.2 - 0.4c ; this is much higher than the velocity of the UV BALs in this quasar.

The observed absorption features were detectable from APM 08279+5255 only due to its large gravitational lensing boost and high redshift, and such features could be present but undetected in the limited signal-to-noise spectra of many other distant BAL quasars. Since remarkable claims require remarkable evidence, we have been working to acquire additional spectra for APM 08279+5255 with one primary goal being to test the model outlined in the previous paragraph. We now have 2 , 3 , and 3 \ spectra of APM 08279+5255, and the relevant absorption features are consistently detected in all 8 observations (see Fig. 3; Chartas et al. 2009; Saez, Chartas, Brandt 2009).

Our multi-epoch observations also detect variability of the absorption-line energies and equivalent widths; the full rest-frame energy range over which absorption has now been seen is 7.5--17 keV . Such variability is expected given the likely small launching radius of the outflow, and it also helps to explain the somewhat different initial interpretations of the absorption features put forward by Chartas et al. (2002) vs. Hasinger, Schartel, Komossa (2002).

sources.summary: The paper describes APM 08279 as a lensed high-redshift galaxy whose magnification exposes compact AGN-region molecular gas, with CO and HCN emission useful for testing XDR/PDR models.

quotes.quote: APM 08279 In the z=3.9 galaxy APM 08279, the usual geometric effects concerning emission from the central regions are compensated by a gravitational lens. It therefore provides an ideal test case to study the emission we might see when future telescopes like ALMA can observe the central regions with higher sensitivity and higher spatial resolution. As expected for molecular clouds excited by X-rays , they find that the CO line intensity increases up to the (10-9) transition. Significant flux is also detected in the HCN(5-4) line. Based on brightness temperature arguments, the results from high-resolution mapping and lens models from the literature, Weiss07 show that the molecular lines arise from a compact ( 100-300 pc), highly gravitationally magnified (m=60-110) region surrounding the central AGN.

We consider another example, the lensed galaxy APM 08279. There, the situation is not fully clear and is currently consistent with the presence of an XDR, which should also be expected due to the high X-ray luminosity, but also with the presence of a PDR with a radiation field of G_0=10^ 4.5 . To some degree, this is due to uncertainties in the measured line fluxes.

sources.summary: The paper treats APM 08279+5255 as a 2.0-3.0 Gyr quasar at z=3.91 whose age challenges holographic dark energy, then argues interaction plus curvature can accommodate it.

quotes.quote: In particular, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 is an important one, which has been used as a ``cosmic clock'' to constrain cosmological models. Its age is estimated to be 2.0-3.0 Gyr .

It is found in Ref. that the former two OHROs, the old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55 and the old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43 , can be easily accommodated, but the last one, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 , cannot be accommodated in the model. In the present Letter, we extend the holographic dark energy model to involving the spatial curvature and the interaction, as described in the previous section, and we shall examine whether the OHRO, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 , is consistent with such a sophisticated holographic dark energy model.

In order to accommodate the old quasar APM 08279+5255 in the holographic dark energy model, we propose to consider the interaction between dark energy and matter in the model. We have shown that the quasar indeed can be accommodated in the holographic dark energy model when an appropriate interaction strength is chosen. Taking the current observational constraints into account, we have demonstrated that both interaction and spatial curvature should be simultaneously involved in the holographic dark energy model. It has been shown that if such a sophisticated case is considered the quasar APM 08279+5255 can be accommodated and the cosmic age problem can thus be avoided in the holographic dark energy model.

sources.summary: The paper treats APM 08279+5255 as an old z=3.91 quasar causing a cosmic-age problem for NADE/CDM models, then argues INADE can accommodate it while noting caveats in the age estimate.

quotes.quote: Many dark energy models fail to pass the cosmic age test because of the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 , the CDM model and holographic dark energy models being no exception.

We find that the NADE model also faces the challenge of the age problem caused by the old quasar APM 08279+5255. In order to overcome such a difficulty, we consider the possible interaction between dark energy and dark matter. We show that this quasar can be successfully accommodated in the interacting new agegraphic dark energy (INADE) model at the 2 level under the current observational constraints.

The age problem is seen in the apparent old age of a single quasar, APM 08279+5255, whose high Fe/O ratio derived from X-ray observations has been used to argue for an age of at least 2 Gyr. At the quasar's redshift of 3.91, the standard CDM model is only 1.6 Gyr old and thus apparently younger than the quasar itself. However, the age estimate of the quasar rests on the assumption that star formation and evolution proceeds in a way similar to that observed in the late universe. It is not even the absolute amount of Fe that serves as an argument, but the relative Fe/O abundance. Sub-millimeter observations show, however, that star formation seems to proceed in an atypical way in APM 08279+5255 .

sources.summary: The paper studies the lensed BAL quasar APM 08279+5255, measuring emission-line lags, a roughly 10^10 solar-mass black hole, an under-massive host bulge, and absorption-gas conditions.

quotes.quote: In this study, we take advantage of our long-term monitoring programme of high-luminosity quasars Tre07 to increase the data statistics about the bright BAL QSO APM 08279+5255, in order to update our study on its C IV absorption variability ( Tre13 , Paper I henceforth; Sat14 ) and to obtain estimates of its black hole mass through RM of Si IV and C IV emission lines. APM 08279+5255 is one of the most luminous quasars, discovered in 1998 by Irw98 . It is well known for its gravitational lensing, first confirmed case of a lens with odd number of image components Lew02 . Additionally, its redshift inferred from the high-ionisation emission lines (N V , Si IV , C IV and C III ] ) is z =3.87 Irw98 , which corresponds to an outflow velocity of 2500 km s ^ 1 with respect to the systemic redshift z = 3.911 derived from the CO emission lines Dow99 .

We find a reverberation-mapping time lag of 900 rest-frame days for both Si IV and C IV emission lines. This is consistent with an extension of the BLR size-to-luminosity relation for active galactic nuclei up to a luminosity of 10^ 48 erg s ^ 1 , and implies a black hole mass of 10^ 10 M_ . Additionally, we measure a recombination time lag of 160 days in the rest frame for the C IV narrow absorption system, which implies an electron density of the absorbing gas of 2.5 10^4 cm ^ 3 . The measured black hole mass of APM 08279+5255 indicates that the quasar resides in an under-massive host-galaxy bulge with M_ bulge 7.5 M_ BH , and that the lens magnification is lower than 8 .

sources.summary: The paper reports [CI] emission from the lensed z=3.91 quasar APM 08279+5255, tying it to dense molecular gas, an active nucleus, likely starburst activity, and strong lensing amplification.

quotes.quote: We present a detection of emission in the lensed quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 using the IRAM Plateau de Bure interferometer. The [ CI ] line velocity and width are similar to the values of previously detected high-J CO and HCN lines in this source, suggesting that the emission from all of these species arises from the same region. The apparent luminosity of the [ CI ] line is L'_ CI = (3.1 0.4) 10^ 10  K km s ^ 1  pc ^2 , which implies a neutral carbon mass, M_ CI = (4.4 0.6)m^ 1 10^ 7  M _ , where m is the lensing magnification factor. The [ CI ] line luminosity is consistent with the large molecular gas mass inferred from the nuclear CO line luminosity ( 10 ^ 11 m^ 1  M _ ). We also present an upper limit on the line luminosity in APM 08279+5255 of, L'_ H_2O < 1.8 10^ 10  K km s ^ 1  pc ^2 (3- ).

The ultraluminous quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 (Irwin et al. \ 1998) is strongly gravitationally lensed (Ledoux et al. \ 1998; Ibata et al. \ 1999; Egami et al. \ 2000) and appears to be one of the most luminous objects in the Universe, with an apparent infrared luminosity of 10 ^ 15  L _ (Lewis et al. \ 1998). Observations over a wide range of wavelengths (e.g., Downes et al. \ 1999; Ellison et al. \ 1999; Gallagher et al. \ 2002; Soifer et al. \ 2004; Wagg et al. \ 2005) have shown that APM08279+5255 contains an active nucleus and likely a starburst component. The combination of extreme intrinsic luminosity and amplification by strong gravitational lensing has allowed APM 08279+5255 to be detected in many rotational lines of CO (J=4-3 and J=9-8 Downes et al. \ 1999, hereafter D99; J=1-0 and J=2-1 Papadopoulos et al. \ 2001; Lewis et al. \ 2002 ), including the unique detection in the high excitation J=11-10 line (A. Weiss et al., in preparation).

sources.summary: The paper presents CO and HCN observations of the lensed z=3.9 BAL quasar, finding compact highly magnified molecular emission, dense gas, IR-pumped HCN, and an already assembled black hole.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of the CO 4--3, 6--5, 9--8, 10--9, and 11--10 lines in the Broad Absorption Line quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.9 using the IRAM 30 m telescope. We also present IRAM PdBI high spatial resolution observations of the CO 4--3 and 9--8 lines, and of the 1.4 mm dust radiation as well as an improved spectrum of the HCN(5--4) line. Unlike CO in other QSO host galaxies, the CO line SED of APM 08279+5255 rises up to the CO(10--9) transition.

Although the gas density for the cold gas is typical for the HCN emitting volume in local ULIRGs it is not high enough to explain the observed HCN(5--4) luminosity in APM 08279. From LVG models including IR pumping via the 14 \ bending mode of HCN we conclude that the hot dust heated by the AGN efficiently boosts the HCN excitation and that this excitation channel is more important than the collisional excitation for this particular source. The star formation rate associated with the dense gas component is only 25 yr ^ 1 . An estimate of the mass budget in the central region of APM 08279 suggests that the black hole and gas mass does not leave sufficient room for a stellar mass contribution following the local M_ BH _* relation for reasonable inclinations of the molecular disk. Hence we conclude that the super-massive black hole in APM 08279 is already in place, before the buildup of the stellar bulge is complete.

sources.summary: The paper treats APM 08279+5255 as the z=3.911 lensed quasar target, reporting a secure fine-structure-line detection, strong lensing, high molecular brightness, a 4.8 Jy line flux, and a 570 km/s line width.

quotes.quote: Here we present secure detections of the fine-structure line in two high- z sources, the lensed quasar ( z = 3.911 ) and the sub-millimeter galaxy (SMG) ( z = 3.930 ). These sources are strongly magnified by gravitational lensing (magnification factor =4--100 for and 12--20 for ; see Egami et al. 2000, Riechers et al. 2009, Lestrade et al. 2011), and represent two of the brightest molecular emitters at this redshift, with peak flux densities of 7.3 mJy weiss07 and 33 mJy lestrade10 , respectively.

Figure fig_apm shows the observed spectrum and the (continuum-subtracted) line map of . The line is detected at modest significance. We fitted the spectrum with a flat continuum plus a gaussian profile for the emission. The fitted flux is S ( )= 4.8 0.8 Jy , consistent with the tentative detection ( 7.6 5.4 Jy ) reported in bradford11 . We measure a line width of 570 110 , consistent with the weighted average of the line width values from CO transitions reported in weiss07 .

sources.summary: The paper models APM 08279+0255 as a BAL QSO whose weak X-ray relative to UV luminosity lets MHD winds reproduce its high-velocity absorption features.

quotes.quote: Such an attempt was made by FKCB1 , who employed the specific MHD winds to model the absorption features in the spectra of BAL QSOs, in particular that of APM 08279+0255 Chartas02,Chartas03 . This AGN class was chosen because the properties of absorption features of its members are most different from those of Seyferts, since they exhibit at velocities v 0.5 and at v 0.1c . Because these objects were selected on the basis of their broad UV absorption features, any such model should be able to provide an account of the combined properties of both their X-ray and UV features. The property that most clearly separates Seyferts and BAL QSOs is the relative weakness of their X-ray to their UV luminosity.

This is reflected in the corresponding value of their _ OX parameter, which in BAL QSOs has values _ OX 2 compared to 1 to 1.2 for Seyferts. To model the ionization of BAL QSO winds, FKCB1 have chosen as ionizing spectrum that shown in Fig. fig:fig3 b. This consists of a BBB component of maximum temperature 5 eV along with a power law component of photon index , normalized at E=2 keV relative to the BBB so that it results in _ OX = -2 , consistent with that of APM 08279+0255.

sources.summary: The paper monitors APM 08279+5255, a bright lensed BAL quasar at z = 3.911, using multi-epoch data to study C IV absorption variability.

quotes.quote: In the present study, we discuss the result of the monitoring of a single object, APM 08279+5255 at z =3.911, which we observed 23 times since 2003, in the framework of a monitoring campaign of medium-high redshift QSOs, carried out with the 1.82 m Copernico telescope of the Asiago Observatory and devoted to determine the mass of the central supermassive black hole (SMBH) through reverberation mapping Trev07 . Since APM 08279+5255 is one of the brightest QSOs in the sky, and shows several other interesting peculiarities, it was observed several times by other authors, photometrically and spectroscopically, soon after its discovery Irwi98 . So that it was possible to collect both spectroscopic and photometric data from the literature, which have been included in the present analysis, providing what is, to our knowledge, the best sampling of the variability of a BAL QSO available to date. In section 2 we describe the data we collected from the literature, in section 3 we describe our spectrophotometric monitoring campaign and data reduction, in section 4 we analyse the variability of the C IV absorption systems and its correlation with photometric variability and in section 5 we discuss and summarise the results.

Soon after the discovery, adaptive optics images at CFHT telescope have shown APM 08279+5255 to be a double source, likely gravitationally lensed ledo98 , as already proposed by Irwi98 , and Hubble Space Telescope (HST) and radio images suggested the existence of a long sought ``third component'', expected in the case of axi-symmetric gravitational lensing fields Ibat99 . A lens model indicating a magnification of about 100 was derived by Egam00 . Spectroscopic HST observations, at medium-high resolution ( / 4500) confirmed that the third component is in fact part of the lensed image Lewi02 .

sources.summary: The paper uses APM 08279+5255, an old quasar at z = 3.91, as the most restrictive OHRO and tests whether lower age limits alleviate LTB void-model tension.

quotes.quote: In addition, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91   is also used extensively. Its age is estimated to be 2.0 - 3.0 Gyr  . In  , by using a different method, its age is reevaluated to be 2.1 Gyr. To assure the robustness of our analysis, we use the most conservative lower age estimate 2.0 Gyr for the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 throughout the present work.

In the previous section, it is easy to find that the age problem in three LTB void models is mainly due to the OHRO at z=3.91 . In fact, this OHRO has ruled out (at least brought trouble to) most cosmological models (see e.g.  ), including the well-known concordance CDM model. Naturally, one might doubt on the validity of this quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 (we thank the referee for pointing out this issue).

sources.summary: The paper uses APM 0879+5255, an old z=3.91 quasar with estimated age 2.1 Gyr, as an age constraint that rules against simple dark-energy models and supports interacting holographic dark energy.

quotes.quote: We analyse now the old quasar APM 0879+5255, inquiring about the constraints such data can bring to the question of Dark Energy. This quasar was discovered at a redshift z=3.91 and its age has been estimated to be 2.1Gyr . Employing the WMAP determination of the Hubble parameter, the age of the quasar is in the dimensionless interval 0.148 Tg 0.162 . A viable cosmological model should predict a considerably older universe at that high redshift in order to be compatible with the existence of this object.
Therefore, the simple cosmological model with dark energy is not compatible with the age estimate of the old quasar.

The estimated age of an old quasar APM 0879+5255 at redshift z=3.91 has also been used to test the viability of the interacting holographic dark energy model. By adopting the lower and upper bounds of values of Hubble parameter and dark energy density respectively from WMAP , H_0=69.6 , _D=0.72 , we have shown in Fig. fig4a and Fig. fig4b that the interacting holographic dark energy model with appropriate coupling between dark energy and dark matter is compatible with the estimated age for the APM 0879+5255.

sources.summary: The paper reports new CO observations of APM0827+5255 that argue against an extended CO reservoir and instead find compact resolved lens images with high magnification.

quotes.quote: APM0827+5255 ( z=3.9 ): The presence of extended CO(1--0) emission in APM0827+5255 was reported by Papadopoulus et al. \ (2001). Additional CO(2--1) C--array imaging by Papadopoulus et al. \ also showed some evidence for an extended envelope around the central QSO. We have have obtained new C and B array observations for this source and do not find evidence for such an extended CO component.

In Fig. 4, we show our highest resolution data, where we detect a compact but resolved structure of the lens images. Two clearly separated images can be seen, and their morphology and brightess ratio is very similar to that of the optical QSO. We thus conclude that the CO in this source is indeed extremely compact, and that the lensing magnification is close to the optical magnification i.e., a factor of 100 rather than 7 as derived in previous models (Lewis et al. \ 2002). Further evidence against an extended CO(1--0) reservoir around APM0827+5255 comes from our multi--CO transition study of this source (Weiss et al. \ 2006, in prep., see also this volume, and recent single dish observations of this source using the GBT, Riechers et al. \ 2006, see also this volume).

sources.summary: The paper uses APM 08279+5255 as an old high-redshift quasar whose age challenges the original holographic dark-energy model unless a lower Hubble constant is adopted.

quotes.quote: In addition, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 is also used extensively. Its age is estimated to be 2.0--3.0 Gyr . In , by using a different method, its age is reevaluated to be 2.1 Gyr. To assure the robustness of our analysis, we use the most conservative lower age estimate 2.0 Gyr for the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 throughout this work.

Therefore, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 cannot be accommodated in the original holographic dark energy model. The age problem also exists, like in the other dark energy models mentioned in Sec. sec1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats APM 08279+5255 as the hardest old high-redshift quasar age constraint, adopting a 2.0 Gyr lower-limit age at z=3.91 and requiring lower Hubble parameters for compatibility.

quotes.quote: We investigate the age constraint on the agegraphic dark energy model by using two old galaxies (LBDS 53W091 and LBDS 53W069 ) and the old high redshift quasar APM 08279+5255 .

The age of APM 08279+5255 is in debate: one method shows its age between 2.0-3.0 Gyr at z=3.91 , while the other method shows that it was 2.1 Gyr old at the same redshift. We use T=2.0Gyr as the age of the APM 08279+5255( z=3.91 ). Because the T=2.0Gyr is the lowest limit of the age of APM 08279+5255 ( z=3.91 ), it is the loosest constraint of APM 08279+5255( z=3.91 ) on the age of the universe.
The most difficult one to accommodate is APM 08279+5255. In Table table2 we list the exact result for APM 08279+5255 curves in Fig. fig1 fig5 :

sources.summary: The paper treats APM 08279 + 5255 as an old high-redshift quasar whose 2.0-3.0 Gyr age at z=3.91 is hard for CDM but accommodated by a coupled YMC model.

quotes.quote: However, the recent discovery of an old high- z quasar APM 08279 + 5255 with an age (2.0 3.0) Gyr at redshift z = 3.91 declares the come-back of the cosmic age problem. With Hubble parameter h=0.72 and fractional matter density _ m =0.27 , the CDM model would give an age t=1.6 Gyr at z = 3.91 , much lower than the age lower limit 2.0 Gyr. This so-called ``high- z cosmic age problem'' have attracted a lot of attention , where various DE models are directly tested against this quasar. So far there is no DE model that can pass this test, and most of these models perform even poorer than CDM model in solving this issue .
For =0.31H_0 , YMC yields T(3.91)=0.134 and accommodates the quasar for h 0.65 . For a larger interaction =0.67H_0 , YMC yields T(3.91)=0.172 and accommodates the quasar even for h 0.83 .

sources.summary: The paper reports water emission in the z=3.91 BAL quasar APM 08279+5255, argues the line is mainly radiatively pumped, predicts further water detections, and notes puzzling non-detection of HF absorption.

quotes.quote: We report a detection of the excited 2_ 20 2_ 11 rotational transition of para- \ in APM 08279+5255 using the IRAM Plateau de Bure interferometer. At z=3.91 , this is the highest-redshift detection of interstellar water to date. From LVG modeling, we conclude that this transition is predominantly radiatively pumped and on its own does not provide a good estimate of the water abundance. However, additional water transitions are predicted to be detectable in this source, which would lead to an improved excitation model.

We also present a sensitive upper limit for the HF J=1-0 absorption toward APM 08279+5255. While the face-on geometry of this source is not favorable for absorption studies, the lack of HF absorption is still puzzling and may be indicative of a lower fluorine abundance at z=3.91 compared with the Galactic ISM.

sources.summary: The paper studies APM 08279+5255 as a luminous lensed BAL quasar with outflows, weak intrinsic host reddening, and very high inferred black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: Rest-frame UV-to-optical spectroscopy of APM 08279+5255 F. G. Saturni et al. \ is a well-known luminous broad absorption-line quasar (BAL QSO) at z = 3.911 . Serendipitously discovered in a Galactic survey for cold carbon stars Irw98 , it is archetypal to several categories of the quasar class, showing together many of the observational phenomena that can be found in such objects. In fact, beyond having evidence of both broad Sri00 and intrinsic narrow Ell04 absorption features associated with the \ 1549 emission line, it also shows an uncommon O VI 1030 BAL embedded in the Ly forest Hin99 and an X-ray ultra-fast outflow (UFO) associated to highly-ionized iron Has02,Cha02,Sae09,Hag17 .

A blueshifted emission component with v 800 km s ^ 1 , corresponding to a molecular outflow, is also detected in the CO( 4-3 ) transition by Fer17 through 3.2 mm observations with the NOEMA interferometer. htbp \ is gravitationally lensed Led98 by an unobserved galaxy at z 1 Pet00,Ell04 . The lensed image is elongated in the NE direction and consists of three components, with a maximum separation of 0''.35 0''.02 Led98 .

sources.summary: The paper studies APM 08279+5255 as a gravitationally lensed z=3.91 broad-absorption-line quasar, measuring X-ray reflection, a high-energy cutoff, and corona properties from new observations.

quotes.quote: We present new joint and observations of APM 08279+5255, a gravitationally-lensed, broad-absorption line quasar ( z=3.91 ). After showing a fairly stable flux ( f_ 2-10 4-5.5 10^ 13 erg s^ 1 ) from 2000 to 2008, was found in a fainter state in the latest X-ray exposures ( f_ 2-10 2.7 10^ 13 erg s^ 1 ), which can likely be ascribed to a lower X-ray activity. Moreover, the 2019 data present a prominent Fe K emission line and do not show any significant absorption line.

This fainter state, coupled to the first hard X-ray sampling of APM 08279+5255, allowed us to measure X-ray reflection and the high-energy cutoff in this source for the first time. From the analysis of previous and observations, X-ray reflection is demonstrated to be a long-lasting feature of this source, but less prominent prior to 2008, possibly due to a stronger primary emission. The estimated high-energy cutoff ( E_ cut =99_ 35 ^ +91 keV) sets a new redshift record for the farthest ever measured and places APM 08279+5255 in the allowed region of the compactness-temperature diagram of X-ray coronae, in agreement with previous results on high- z quasars.

sources.summary: The paper describes APM 08279+5255 as a highly luminous lensed high-redshift quasar with dense warm gas, reporting certain HNC and tentative CN detections with co-spatial dense-gas tracers.

quotes.quote: The gravitationally lensed quasar APM 08279+5255 ( z= 3.9118, Wei et al. 2006 [ We06 ] ) is a prime target for such studies. Its huge intrinsic luminosity, boosted by a large magnifying factor ( m =60-100), makes it not only the most luminous object in the Universe (apparent luminosity L_ bol =7 10 ^ 15 L_ ), but also one of the most powerful sources of CO emission. The presence of strong CO lines with rotational quantum numbers as high as J = 11--10 indicates that its gas is very dense and warm (Downes et al. 1999, We06).

We conclude that the detection of HNC in APM 08279+5255 is certain, whereas that of CN is only tentative. A fit of the component width, while keeping the intensities fixed, yields v= 480 100 kms ^ 1 a value similar, within the errors, to the HCN and HCO ^+ line widths. This is consistent with HNC, HCN and HCO ^+ being co-spatial.

sources.summary: The paper uses the old quasar APM0879+5255 at z=3.91 as an age constraint, finding its 2.1 Gyr age compatible with the interacting dark-energy scenario.

quotes.quote: In Ref. it was investigated whether this model satisfies the current Universe age constraints and allows a considerably older universe at high redshift to be compatible with the existence of some old objects such as the old quasar APM0879+5255 at redshift z=3.91 . Its age, at that redshift, was estimated as t_g=2.1 Gyr . Using the present WMAP data on the Hubble parameter, H_0 =73.4^ +2.8 _ 3.8 , the dimensionless age of the quasar T_g=H_0 t_g is seen to lie in the interval 0.148 T_g 0.162 . In our scenario, it is easy to realize that the age of the Universe at z=3.91 was T_z = _ 3.91 ^ (1+z)^ 1 H^ 1 dz= 0.152 . This is to say, at that redshift the Universe was old enough to accommodate the existence of this old quasar.

sources.summary: The paper treats APM 08279+5255 as an old z = 3.91 quasar whose 2 Gyr age and Fe/O ratio sharply constrain simplified Chaplygin gas cosmologies.

quotes.quote: Firstly, in order to limit the free parameters of both models, we discuss the age of high redshift objects with special emphasis to the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z = 3.91 . The basic finding is that this old high redshift object constrain severely the simplified Chaplygin cosmologies.

It means that any curve crossing the rectangles yields an age parameter smaller than the minimal value required by the presence of the quasar APM 08279+5255. At this point, in line with the arguments presented by Hasinger et al. (2002) , we recall that that X-ray observations show a Fe/O ratio for the quasar APM 08279+5255 compatible with an age of 2 Gyr. Naturally, we do not expect such results to be free of observational and/or theoretical uncertainties, but the analysis of the quoted reference has independently been confirmed by Fria a and collaborators .

sources.summary: The paper models APM 08279+5255 as a lensed high-redshift BAL quasar whose X-ray absorber needs two ionized components, including a 0.18c outflow and supersolar Fe/O.

quotes.quote: Physical and kinematical properties of the X-ray absorber in the broad absorption line quasar APM 08279+5255 J.M. Ram i rez Ram i rez Max-Planck-Institut f u extraterrestrische Physik, D-85741 Garching, Germany We have re-analyzed the X-ray spectra of the gravitational lensed high-redshift BAL QSO , observed with the and observatories. Previous studies (Hasinger et al. 2002; Chartas et al. 2002) detected unusual, highly-ionized iron absorption features, but differed in their interpretation of these features, regarding the kinematical and ionization structure. We seek one physical model that can be successfully applied to both observations. For the first time we have performed detailed photoionization modeling on the X-ray spectrum of . The absorbing gas in can be represented by a two-absorbers model with column densities N_H(1) 7 10^ 22 , N_H(2) 6 10^ 22 , and ionization parameters (1) 1.5 and (2) 3 , with one of them (the high-ionization component) outflowing at v 0.18( 0.01) , carrying large amount of gas out of the system.

sources.summary: The paper describes APM 08279+5255 as an apparently very bright, strongly lensed high-redshift source with extreme molecular excitation, prior carbon detection, and no detected higher-transition carbon line.

quotes.quote: APM 08279+5255 This source is one of the (apparently) brightest sources known to date, with a submillimeter flux of S _ 850 m =75 4 mJy (Lewis et al. \ 1998). The first CO detections by Downes et al. \ (1999, J =4, J =9) already indicated the extreme excitation conditions of the molecular gas (studied in detail in Wei et al. \ 2007). One of the explanations for its apparent brightness and extreme excitation is that the lensing geometry is such that the central 100 pc of this source is magnified by a large factor ( =60--100 Wei et al. \ 2007, see also Egami et al. \ 2000) We note that Riechers et al. \ 2009a derived a magnification factor of only 4 for the molecular gas phase. .
The \ line has been detected by Wagg et al. \ (2006). The source is not detected in \ emission with an upper limit of 1 Jy km s ^ 1 (Figure 7, left; upper limits are given in Table 3).

sources.summary: The paper discusses APM 08279+5255 as a lensed quasar whose Chandra spectra show two high-energy absorption features interpreted as fast Fe outflows.

quotes.quote: observed the quasar APM 08279+5255 with the Chandra Advanced CCD Imaging Spectrometer (ACIS) and noted outflows in the absorption spectra. By using lensed quasars, one is able to probe outflows which would otherwise be too faint to observe. This quasar is at a redshift of z = 3.91 . The spectra of APM 08279+5255 shows two features at 8.05 keV and 9.79 keV in the rest frame of the host galaxy Chartas02 . If these correspond to Fe XXV then the outflowing velocities would be 0.2c and 0.4c , respectively.

sources.summary: The review uses APM 08279+5255, an extremely old z=3.91 quasar with a 2.0 Gyr age lower bound, as a cosmic-age problem that mainstream, CDM, and original HDE cosmologies struggle to accommodate.

quotes.quote: For example, the existence of an old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 is still a mystery, because it is even older than the cosmic age given by almost all the mainstream cosmological models . In other words, to accommodate this anomalous object, some more complicated cosmological model should be taken into account .

As mentioned in subsection sec:2.3.4 , the existence of an extremely old quasar APM 08279+5255 is still a mystery, because it has an age lower bound t_ obj (3.91)=2.0 Gyr at z=3.91 , which is larger than the cosmic age given by almost all the mainstream cosmological models. For example, Friaca et al. demonstrated that the CDM model cannot give a result of (3.91) 1 , and thus it is fail to accommodate this extremely old quasar . In addition, Wei and Zhang found that the original HDE model cannot accommodate this quasar, too .

sources.summary: The paper treats APM 08279+5255 as a lensed high-redshift QSO, adopting magnification, black-hole and bolometric values, plus X-ray and CO molecular outflow properties.

quotes.quote: APM 08279+5255 . Unlike the other systems considered, this is a lensed QSO at z 3.9 . While lens models can not constrain very accurately the value of the magnification , on which most quantities relevant to this analysis depend, indirect methods indicate = 4 as the best possible choice Saturni+18 . We consider this value for the magnification and take M_ BH and L_ BOL from Saturni+18 . The X-ray wind properties come from Chartas+09 , using only the slowest component (see their Table 3, model 8 , absorber 1 ) which was also identified separately by Hagino+17 . The properties of the CO molecular outflow are taken from Feruglio+17 , using their model with =4 .

sources.summary: The paper reports HCN J=5-4 emission from the strongly lensed high-redshift quasar APM08279+5255, associating the line with a warm dense circumnuclear disk and enhanced nuclear HCN abundance.

quotes.quote: We detect HCN J=5-4 emission from the ultraluminous quasar APM08279+5255 at z=3.911 using the IRAM Plateau de Bure interferometer. This object is strongly gravitationally lensed, yet still thought to be one of the most intrinsically luminous objects in the Universe. The new data imply a line luminosity L'_ HCN(J=5-4) = 4.0 0.5 10^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 . The 440 km s ^ 1 full width half maximum of the HCN J=5-4 line matches that of the previously observed high-J CO lines in this object and suggests that the emission from both species emerges from the same region: a warm, dense circumnuclear disk. Simple radiative transfer models suggest an enhanced abundance of HCN relative to CO in the nuclear region of APM08279+5255, perhaps due to increased ionization, or possibly the selective depletion of oxygen.

sources.summary: The paper describes APM 08279+5255 as a strongly lensed broad-absorption-line quasar, an intense IR/submillimeter source, and the brightest high-redshift source detected at 350 microns.

quotes.quote: APM 08279+5255 This is a strongly lensed z=3.91 broad absorption line quasar with a bolometric luminosity of 10^ 14 \ L_ after correction for magnification by a factor 7 Downes1999 . It is a strong IR/submm source which was even detected by IRAS Irwin1998 . Its mid-IR spectrum was observed with the Spitzer Space Telescope , confirming a strong continuum likely due to the dust heated by the AGN Soifer2004 .

The massive reservoir of dust (a few 10^8 M_ ) and warm molecular gas ( 3 10^9 \ M_ ) associated with this quasar is distributed in a nuclear disk of radius 100-200 pc around the Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN), and traces a dynamical mass of 1.5 10^ 10 M_ Lewis2002 . With a 350 flux density of 386 32 \ mJy , APM 8279+5255 is the strongest high- z source ever detected at 350 .

sources.summary: The paper uses the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 as a high-redshift age test; its conservative 2.0 Gyr age is not accommodated by the CDM or HDE models.

quotes.quote: In addition, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 is also used extensively, whose age is estimated to be 2.0---3.0 Gyr . To assure the robustness of our analysis, we use the most conservative lower age estimate 2.0 Gyr for the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 , and the lower age estimate 1.3 Gyr for the high redshift quasar B1422+231 at z=3.62 .
So, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 cannot be accommodated (beyond 2 ). The age problem still exists in the CDM model, even when the alternative CMB data of L L has been taken into account.
So, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 cannot be accommodated (beyond 2 ). The age problem still exists in the HDE model, even when the alternative CMB data of L L has been taken into account.

sources.summary: The paper finds APM 08279+5255 has the weakest carbon spectrum, a large CO-carbon redshift offset, and an anomalous inferred F variation likely due to low spectral resolution.

quotes.quote: There are some differences in the FWHMs, particularly for H 1413+117 and APM 08279+5255, the latter of which has the poorest \ spectral resolution (150 ), although the former has the best (17 ).

This interpretation of the measured values of F would explain why the temporal variation of F as indicated by APM 08279+5255 is at odds with other sources at similar redshifts, which all exhibit positive values of F/F (Fig. CvCO , left panel). As discussed above, such a discrepancy may be expected due to spatial variation of the constants, were the sources in widely separated regions of the sky. However, MM 18423+5938 and PSS 2322+1944 are at 122 and 106 degrees from the dipole, respectively, whereas APM 08279+5255 is at 154 degrees (Fig. CvCO , middle panel), thus being in the same hemisphere as the other two sources. This supports the suggestion that the derived offset of the \ redshift in APM 08279+5255 does not accurately trace F/F .

sources.summary: The paper identifies APM 08279+5255 as a bright lensed BAL quasar missed by SDSS targeting because it was too bright despite having colors away from the stellar locus.

quotes.quote: (2) APM 08279+5255 This object is a well known, lensed broad absorption line quasar with multiple images at z=3.91 Ibata1999 . It has an SDSS i-band magnitude of m_ i =14.84 . While it does not show any fatal or non-fatal flags, it is also not selected in the high redshift inclusion regions. The colors of this quasar (see Fig. fig_cc_missed ) place it well away from the stellar locus in ug-gr color space, but it is too bright for the SDSS spectroscopic target list to avoid scattered light from nearby fibers.

sources.summary: The paper uses QSO APM 08279+5255, age about 2.1 Gyr, as an aging problem; Model 2 still leaves this quasar age largely unresolved.

quotes.quote: Model 1 With the best-fitting parameters in this model, we have: EoR ( 6 z 15 ), for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), from around 379.9 Myr (402.6 Myr) to 1.825 Gyr (1.718 Gyr), which lasts 1.445 Gyr (1.315 Gyr); The seed redshift of J0100+2802, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), z_ seed 9.74 (11.03); If the first star formed at z 20 , then the age of the universe at that time would be, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 272.8 Myr (234.1 Myr); Old globular cluster M92 (NGC 6341) would appear at z 7.53 \ (9.27) with age of 14.0 Gyr for OHD ( H(z) + BAO); For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 3.5-Gyr-old ratio galaxy 53W091 ( z 1.55 ) and 4-Gyr-old radio galaxy 53W069 ( z 1.43 ) are formed at z 4.98 \ (5.40) and z 5.41 \ (5.99) , respectively; For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), QSO APM 08279+5255 with age around 2.1 Gyr would have formed at z 9.37 \ (12.32) .

Model 2 With the best-fitting parameters in this Model, we have: EoR ( 6 z 15 ), for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), from around 478.7 Myr (534.1 Myr) to 1.371 Gyr (1.462 Gyr), which lasts 892.4 Myr (927.8 Myr); The seed redshift of J0100+2802, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), z_ seed 17.75 \ (16.46) ; If the first star formed at z 20 , then the age of the universe at that time would be, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 337.1 Myr (381.4 Myr); Old globular cluster M92 (NGC 6341) would appear at z 18.19 \ (18.98) with age of 14.0 Gyr for OHD ( H(z) + BAO) ; For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 3.5-Gyr-old ratio galaxy 53W091 ( z 1.55 ) and 4-Gyr-old radio galaxy 53W069 ( z 1.43 ) are formed at z 5.95 \ (6.20) and z 6.85 \ (7.22) , respectively; For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), QSO APM 08279+5255 with age around 2.1 Gyr would have formed at z 92.16 \ (44.83) . If we consider it to be formed after z 20 , then its age would be 1.810 \ (1.861) Gyr for OHD ( H(z) + BAO).

From panel (b) of Figure fig1 we see that the Model 2 cases have relatively larger ages during the evolution and find that other than the age of QSO APM 08279+5255, the other age issues are significantly improved.

sources.summary: The paper includes APM 08279+5255 as an ultra-luminous BAL quasar with excellent spectra, using its systemic redshift, magnitude, wavelength interval, and BAL nature in the stacked analysis.

quotes.quote: In order to improve the statistics and S/N of stacked data ( 5), we have, in some of the work presented here, included the spectrum of APM 08279+5255, recently analysed in Paper I. APM 08279+5255 is an ultra-luminous Broad Absorption Line (BAL) quasar with a systemic redshift z_ em = 3.911 and a broad band magnitude R = 15.2. The data obtained for this quasar (as presented in Paper I) are also of excellent quality, though the broad absorption line makes some portions of the spectrum less ideal for this problem than the spectrum of Q1422+231, and taken together with Q1422+231 they represent a premium data set for this work. The wavelength region used for APM 08279+5255 is 4995 < ( ) < 5720 \ (3.11 <z_ abs < 3.70) which in addition to the selection criteria applied to Q1422+231, takes into account the BAL nature of this quasar.

sources.summary: APM 08279+5255 is described as a high-redshift quasar with shifted high-energy Fe K features and an outflow independently detected by all three observatories.

quotes.quote: In the case of the high red-shift quasar APM 08279+5255, which shows evidence for absorption lines at rest-frame energies of E>10 keV (e.g., saez:2009, chartas:2009, saez:2011 ) the measure of net source counts was instead taken for the entire XIS bandpass (i.e., 0.6-10 keV in the observer frame) because the Fe K features are shifted to lower energies due to the high red-shift of the AGN.
PDS 456 and APM 08279+5255 are not fitted with a reflection component because they lack sufficient counts in the HXD/PIN..

Fe K absorption has also been reported in the spectrum of APM 08279+5255 ( chartas:2002 ) making it the only source in the sample to have its Fe K outflow independently detected in all 3 observatories at a high significance level.

sources.summary: The paper presents APM 08279+5255 as a lensed high-redshift quasar with a relativistic wind whose outflow rate and kinetic power make it a prototype feedback case.

quotes.quote: The prototypical example of such a system is the gravitationally lensed quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 , where a relativistic wind with velocities of 0.1--0.4 , or larger, is detected (Chartas et al. \ 2002, 2009). Modeling suggests a mass-outflow rate of 10--60 M_ yr ^ 1 and a kinetic luminosity of 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 . Such outflows could be present, but undetected, in many other high-redshift quasars---for APM 08279+5255, the lensing provides uniquely high \ spectral quality.

sources.summary: The review describes APM 08279+5255 as a high-redshift BAL QSO with powerful wide-angle disk winds, strong blueshifted absorption, and variable UFO features.

quotes.quote: By increasing M_ BH and m , the AGN will eventually enter the BAL QSO regime, where powerful, wide-angle LD disk winds are launched e.g., APM 08279+5255, PDS 465; see, e.g., 2009ApJ...706..644C, 2015Sci...347..860N .

More equatorial l.o.s. go deep into the well-developed LD disk wind in large M_ BH AGN, and the strongest features in terms of width, depth, and blueshift are observed: this will be the case of the moderate m mini-BAL QSOs e.g., _ out 0.1-0.3c and N_H 5 10^ 22 5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 as observed in PG 1115+080, 2003ApJ...595...85C and the high m BAL QSOs e.g., _ out 0.4c and N_H several 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 as observed in APM 08279+5255, 2002ApJ...579..169C,2009ApJ...706..644C,2011ApJ...737...91S .

Also the broad UFO features observed in the high redshift QSO APM 08279+5255 are found to be highly variable in energy and intensity on rest-frame timescales of months, implying a non-constant kinetic energy injection in the surrounding media due to the disk wind 2009ApJ...697..194S,2009ApJ...706..644C .

gmw_00000912.name: EGSD2 J142033.66+525917.5
gmw_00000912.overview: EGSD2 J142033.66+525917.5 is an early-type, red-sequence galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z=0.71 [arXiv:astro-ph/0608380, arXiv:0810.3235]. It hosts a dual active galactic nucleus with double-peaked emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0608380, arXiv:0810.3235] and displays a disturbed structure that suggests a merger remnant [arXiv:astro-ph/0608380].

sources.summary: The paper identifies EGSD2 J142033.66+525917.5 as an early-type red-sequence galaxy hosting a dual AGN, with double-peaked emission lines, disturbed HST structure, and multiwavelength AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: We present evidence for a dual Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN) within an early-type galaxy at z=0.709 in the Extended Groth Strip. The galaxy lies on the red sequence, with absolute magnitude M_B=-21.0 (AB, with h=0.7 ) and rest-frame color U-B=1.38 . Its optical spectrum shows strong, double-peaked [ O 3 ] emission lines and weak H emission, with Seyfert-like line ratios. The two narrow peaks are separated by 630 in velocity and arise from two distinct regions, spatially resolved in the DEIMOS spectrum, with a projected physical separation of 1.2 kpc. HST /ACS imaging shows an early-type (E/S0) galaxy with hints of disturbed structure, consistent with the remnant of a dissipationless merger.
These data are most readily explained as a single galaxy harboring two AGN---the first such system to be observed in an otherwise typical early-type galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes EGSD2 J142033.6+525917 as a dual AGN early-type galaxy with two Seyfert 2 emission systems separated in velocity and projected physical distance.

quotes.quote: An example of this type of galaxy is EGSD2 J142033.6+525917, an early-type galaxy at z=0.71 with two sets of Seyfert 2 lines separated by 630 km s ^ 1 and 0.84 h^ 1 kpc GE07.2 .

t The dual AGN EGSD2 J142033.6+525917 at redshift z=0.71 is shown at the top of Figure fig:dualAGN . Its spectrum features double-peaked emission lines separated by 630 km s ^ 1 in the host rest frame GE07.2 . From the two-dimensional DEIMOS spectrum of the object, the projected physical separation between the pair of emission peaks is 1.5 DEIMOS pixels, or 0 ^ .17, which corresponds to a projected physical separation of 0.84 h^ 1 kpc.

gmw_00000913.name: NGC 1265
gmw_00000913.overview: NGC 1265 is a massive elliptical [arXiv:1709.07421, arXiv:2204.05487] and cD galaxy [arXiv:2405.13501] in the Perseus cluster, recognized as the prototype narrow-angle-tail radio galaxy [arXiv:2110.13733, arXiv:1703.01740]. Its radio jets are bent by interactions with the intracluster medium as the galaxy falls toward the cluster center [arXiv:1602.03099, arXiv:1703.01740].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1265 as a massive elliptical head-tail radio galaxy in Perseus, with wide-angle jets, downstream bending, wiggles, and a low-frequency steep-spectrum extension.

quotes.quote: NGC 1265 The massive elliptical galaxy Figure all-ht 1986ApJ...301..841O that marks the head of the radio galaxy appears to be directed toward the center of the cluster. The high-resolution and low-resolution maps of NGC 1265 at 610 and 240 MHz are shown in Figure n1265 . presented high frequency images of NGC 1265 and noted that the core possesses an inverted spectrum, which explains why the core is not quite conspicuous in our low-frequency maps while it appears quite prominent in their high-frequency maps. The individual jets are clearly seen to be launched at wide angles in the high-resolution maps at 610 MHz. Farther downstream, the jets appear to be bent by a very large angle, after which the jets expand in size and show higher surface brightness.

Subsequently, the radio jets develop wiggles, which were first reported by . found extended emission to the northeastern side with low-frequency observations and coarser resolutions. This extension was found to have low surface brightness and a steep spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1265 as a radio-source classification example, emphasizing its narrow-angle-tail morphology, its apparent WAT-like inner structure, and how catalog context affects whether it is selected or excluded.

quotes.quote: Figure 1265 gives a further example of this re-conceptualization. It shows two views of the prototypical narrow-angle-tail source NGC 1265. Such tails, when seen near the AGN at high resolution, will always look like wide-angle-tails (WATs). If we follow the criteria in the defining paper for WATs ( WAT ), the ``source" on the left would be classified a WAT .

This is not a mistake -- if we follow the schema; it simply means that it passed the relevant test. A theorist, e.g., may well want to model the properties of this source as part of studying the gently bent portion of jets, and select it as part of a sample of WAT s from a catalog. On the other hand, an observer studying ICM interactions might want to exlcude this WAT based on other catalog entries, e.g., because its small linear size shows that it is still being influenced by its host's ISM.

sources.summary: The appendix reports 3C 83.1 as a CO(2-1)-only detection whose small optical dust lane may explain CO(1-0) dilution and non-detection.

quotes.quote: 3C 83.1: NGC 1265 (in Abell 426). It is one of the seven cases where the molecular gas has been clearly detected in the CO(2-1) transition and not in the CO(1-0) transition. The optical image shows a dust lane of 2.24'' length, according to . This dust lane is oriented at 171 ^ , nearly orthogonal to the radio jet. If the CO emission corresponds to the dust lane, its small size explains the CO(1-0) signal dilution and non-detection.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1265 as a head-tail radio galaxy whose radio bubble passed through the Perseus accretion shock, producing toroidal, steep-spectrum emission and constraining shock properties.

quotes.quote: We present a consistent three-dimensional model for the head-tail radio galaxy NGC 1265 that explains the complex radio morphology and spectrum by a past passage of the galaxy and radio bubble through a shock wave. Using analytical solutions to the full Riemann problem and hydrodynamical simulations, we study how this passage transformed the plasma bubble into a toroidal vortex ring. Adiabatic compression of the aged electron population causes it to be energized and to emit low surface brightness and steep-spectrum radio emission. The large infall velocity of NGC 1265---which is barely gravitationally bound to the Perseus cluster at its current position---and the low Faraday rotation measure values and variance of the jet strongly argue that this transformation was due to the accretion shock onto Perseus situated roughly at R_ 200 .

High-resolution observations of the head structure of NGC 1265 reveal two radio jets emerging from the galaxy which are at an angle of 90 to the tail where the two jets are first parallel to each other and then merge 1986ApJ...301..841O .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1265 as a head-tail radio galaxy in Perseus; MAGIC does not detect it and derives a 95 percent flux upper limit above 250 GeV.

quotes.quote: The Perseus cluster also hosts three bright radio galaxies 1968MNRS138 : NGC 1275, the central dominant galaxy of the cluster, NGC 1265, archetype of a head-tail radio galaxy, in which the jets are bent by their interaction with the ICM, and IC 310, a peculiar object that shows properties of different classifications and which could be an intermediate state between a BL Lac and a radio galaxy.

The non-detection of NGC 1265 in Figure fig:SkyMaps allows us to derive flux upper limits for this radio galaxy. Also, rays could be expected from the central AGN or from the bowshock 1998SijbringBruyn as speculated earlier for IC 310 before the flux variability was established, confirming the AGN nature of the emission 2010A&A...519L...6N . The NGC 1265 position is slightly off-centred, and the exposure at this position is about 20 lower than for NGC 1275. Assuming a point-like source with the same spectral shape as NGC 1275 (a power law with photo index =3.6 ), the upper limit of the integral flux above 250 GeV is estimated to be 5.6 10^ 13 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .
No other point-like emission is detected in the cluster, in particular from the radio galaxy NGC 1265, for which we derived a 95 .l. flux upper limit above 250 GeV of 5.6 10^ 13 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1265 as a giant ETG whose extended globular-cluster halo may contaminate CDG-1 detection but is unlikely to explain the clump.

quotes.quote: The weaker signals in Figure fig:v8_results seem to be arising from the remote halo region of the massive ETG NGC 1265. This giant galaxy is 120 kpc away, well outside the field of view of the ACS, but the globular cluster systems of giant ellipticals often extend more than 100 kpc outward. A giant galaxy with the luminosity of NGC 1265 would normally have a population of 5000 GCs for a GC specific frequency S_N 4 Harris+2013 . Though there are individual differences from galaxy to galaxy, some dozens of GCs could easily extend beyond 100 kpc galactocentric radius, a segment of which seems visible in the V8-ACS pointing.
For NGC 1265, CDG-1 is too far ( 120 kpc) from the center of the giant ETG for its GC system to have such an over-dense clump of GCs.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1265 as a massive cD galaxy in the Perseus core whose inclusion with NGC 1275 and NGC 1272 affects luminosity-function modeling choices.

quotes.quote: Robustness of the derived parameters The three cD galaxies in the core of the cluster (NGC 1275, NGC 1272, and NGC 1265) introduce a discontinuity in the LF that cannot be traced by our simple parametric function given in Eq. ( EqLF ). We thus fit the data including all galaxies with an absolute magnitude M( ) 11.33 , or excluding either NGC 1275, or the three brightest objects NGC 1275, NGC 1272, and NGC 1265. The best fit to the data derived using these three different samples are given in Table table:TabLF . pdfLF shows the probability distribution functions of the output parameters derived when the three cD galaxies are excluded. As expected, the output parameters of the Gaussian function strongly depend on the adopted sample, i.e., on the inclusion or exclusion of the brightest cD galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC1265 as a 6 cm radio-source test case, simulating its jet trajectory and comparing model output for length, perpendicular displacement, and galaxy velocity with radio-map data.

quotes.quote: The first target chosen for our simulation was the radio source NGC1265 at 6 cm. Some basic input parameters extracted from odea are reported in Table data_ngc1265 . } The result of the simulation is reported in Figure NGC1265_proj and in Figure NGC1265_proj_theo with the radio map superimposed on the simulation ; the adopted input parameters are given in the caption. The output data as obtained from the code concerning the overall jet length, the perpendicular-displacement, and the galaxy velocity of NGC1265 are reported in Table fine_ngc1265 . }

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1265 as the prototype narrow-angle tailed radio source and an example of a field galaxy falling through its cluster fast enough to bend its jets.

quotes.quote: The observed head-tail radio sources are classified into two types according to their tail morphology, i.e., the narrow-angle tailed sources NATs; NGC 1265 is known as its prototype, see Figure fig:cartoon-head-tail-sources , right panel; ryle_radio_1968, odea_multifrequency_1986, sun_small_2005 and the wide-angle tailed sources WATs; 3C 465 is known as its prototype, see Figure fig:cartoon-head-tail-sources , left panel; burns_structure_1981, eilek_what_1984, eilek_magnetic_2002, hardcastle_chandra_2005 . As the tails are bent into the same direction, it implies that the intra-cluster weather should be some regular ICM motion relative (probably transverse) to the jet, rather than turbulence. The origin of such intra-cluster weather is subtle and there are several possibilities: (1) it is plausible that NATs are usually field galaxies moving through their host cluster, e.g., NGC 1265, it is falling onto the cluster center with a speed up to 2500 km/s sun_small_2005 ; (2) WATs usually settle in the cluster center, i.e., at the bottom of the potential well, so their relative velocities to the background are thought to be low, e.g., the one of 3C 465 is supposed to be only few hundreds of km/s leahy_3c_1984 ; (3) it is also known that merger between two clusters could induce shocks, the shock passage through the radio galaxies could also affect the jet morphology.

Without loss of generality, two kinds of intra-cluster weather are studied in this paper: (1) wind (relative motion between the radio-jet host galaxy and its surrounding ICM, like the case of NGC 1265) and (2) shock (e.g. those induced by galaxy/cluster merger).

gmw_00000914.name: SBS 0846+513
gmw_00000914.overview: SBS 0846+513 is a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1205.0402, arXiv:2104.04124] with an asymmetric spiral host [arXiv:2012.00008] and a relativistic core-jet structure [arXiv:1205.0402, arXiv:1305.5449]. It exhibits blazar-like behavior [arXiv:1303.3030], including superluminal jet motion [arXiv:1503.01480], strong gamma-ray flaring [arXiv:1410.0755], and correlated multi-band variability [arXiv:2006.09411].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SBS 0846+513 as a gamma-ray NLS1 whose radio emission varies and whose VLBA morphology resembles an asymmetric core-jet source with possible extended emission.

quotes.quote: SBS 0846+513 is the most recent NLS1 detected in gamma rays by Fermi LAT donato11 . Multi-epoch studies of the radio emission from the source indicate that the flux density has some degrees of variability (i.e. a variation of 70 at 4.8 GHz during 1986-2009). The resolution provided by the VLA is not adequate to resolve the source structure (Fig. FMJ-f1 ).

When imaged with the high spatial resolution provided by the VLBA, the source shows two asymmetric components resembling a core-jet structure (Figs. FMJ-f2 and FMJ-f3 ), as also pointed out in a previous work by taylor05 . The flux density ratio between components E and W is about 19 and 14 at 5 GHz and 8.4 GHz, respectively. They are separated by about 3.5 mas ( 23 pc at source redshift z=0.5835). Although the source is unresolved with the VLA, pc-scale VLBA observations cannot recover all the VLA flux density. Such a discrepancy between the flux densities may be related to the intrinsic variability of the source. Even when VLA and VLBA observations were performed simultaneously (i.e. 1996, January 6), the VLBA flux density was only 85 of the value measured with the VLA at the same frequency. This suggests that the missing flux on parsec scale may be related to extended, low-brightness features, like a jet structure, that is resolved out by the VLBA array.

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS 0846+513 as a flaring Fermi-detected RL-NLSy1 galaxy with strong flux variability, high luminosity during its 2011 flare, and possible spectral curvature.

quotes.quote: SBS 0846+513 was in quiescence during the first two years of Fermi operation and thus was not included in the 2FGL catalog. This source was discovered in the ray band only when Fermi LAT detected a GeV flare during 2011 June 2011ATel.3452....1D . After that, many flaring activities have been observed from this source and they are shown by S1, S2, and S3 in Figure lc .

Detection of a large flare in 2011 June led to the discovery of SBS 0846+513 in the ray band. During this period of high activity, we find a maximum daily averaged flux of (8.20 0.08) 10 ^ 7 , which is almost 20 times higher than its five years averaged flux. When the data are further re-binned using six hour bins, we notice a high ray flux of (9.37 1.74) 10 ^ 7 . The apparent isotropic ray luminosity is found to be 1.44 10 ^ 48 .
A TS_ curve value of 15 for SBS 0846+513 indicates a possible curvature in its ray spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SBS 0846 + 513 as a Fermi-detected gamma-ray NLSy1 with strong flares, blazar-like gamma-ray behavior, a pc-scale jet, and superluminal motion.

quotes.quote: Five radio-loud NLSy1 galaxies have been detected at high significance by Fermi LAT so far: 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, and PKS 2004 447 , with a redshift between 0.061 and 0.585. The average apparent isotropic luminosity of these sources in the 0.1--100 GeV energy band is between 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 and 10 ^ 47 erg s ^ 1 , a range of values typical of blazars . This may be an indication of a small viewing angle with respect to the jet axis and thus a high beaming factor for the ray emission, similarly to blazars.

Several strong ray flares were observed from SBS 0846 + 513 (Fig.  Fig1 ) and PMN J0948 + 0022, reaching a peak apparent isotropic ray luminosity of 10 ^ 48 erg s ^ 1 , comparable to that of the bright FSRQ . In particular, SBS 0846 + 513 and PMN J0948 + 0022 showed a ray flaring activity combined with a moderate spectral evolution , a behaviour that was already observed in bright flat spectrum radio quasars (FSRQ) and low-synchrotron-peaked BL Lacs . Variability and spectral properties of these two NLSy1s in rays indicate a blazar-like behaviour.

The analysis of the 6-epoch data set of SBS 0846 + 513 collected by the MOJAVE programme during 2011-2013 indicates that a superluminal jet component is moving away from the core with an apparent angular velocity of (0.27 0.02) mas yr ^ 1 (Fig.  Fig4 ), corresponding to (9.3 0.6) . This apparent superluminal velocity indicates the presence of boosting effects for the jet of SBS 0846 + 513.

sources.summary: The review describes SBS 0846 + 513 as a gamma-ray NLSy1 with strong flares, moderate spectral evolution, a boosted superluminal jet, and a jet component tied to a flare.

quotes.quote: SBS 0846 + 513 was detected in rays for the first time during October 2010--August 2011, when a significant increase of activity was observed by LAT dammando12 .

Some of the ray-emitting NLSy1 do exhibit large-amplitude ray flares: SBS 0846 + 513 dammando12,dammando13a (see Figure LATflare , left panel); PMN J0948 + 0022 dammando15b,foschini11b (see Figure LATflare2 , left panel), 1H 0323 + 342 paliya14 , PKS 1502 + 036 dammando16b , PKS 2004 447 gokus19 , with an apparent isotropic ray luminosity of 10 ^ 48 erg s ^ 1 , comparable to that of bright FSRQ. This is another indication that NLSy1 are able to host relativistic jets as powerful as those in FSRQ. Variability was observed during all ray flares on a daily time-scale, and a subdaily time-scale in case of PKS 1502 + 036 dammando16b,paliya16a (see Figure LATflare , right panel). The ray flaring activity of SBS 0846 + 513 and PMN J0948 + 0022 has been associated with a moderate spectral evolution, a behaviour observed also in bright FSRQ and low-synchrotron-peaked BL Lacs (e.g., abdo10b ).

The analysis of multi-epoch Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) data of SBS 0846 + 513 collected by the MOJAVE programme https://www.physics.purdue.edu/MOJAVE/ . during 2011--2013 pointed out a superluminal jet component moving away from the core with an apparent velocity of (9.3 0.6) dammando13a (Figure MOJAVE_VLBA , left panel), in agreement with the results presented in lister16 . This apparent superluminal velocity supports the presence of boosting effects for the jet of SBS 0846 + 513. Interestingly, the superluminal jet component was ejected close in time with a radio flare observed in December 2009 by the Owens Valley Radio Observatory (OVRO) but during a quiescent activity state in rays, representing an example of knot emission not connected to any ray flaring activity. On the other hand, a new jet component for SBS 0846 + 513 has been ejected close in time with the ray flare occurred in June--July 2011.

sources.summary: Swift observations of SBS 0846 + 513 show correlated multiband variability and a broken X-ray spectrum, supporting a dominant relativistic jet with additional low-energy structure.

quotes.quote: A strong correlation between X-ray, UV, and optical emission and simultaneous flux variations have been observed in 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022 as expected in case the jet radiation is the dominant mechanism. Correlated multiband variability favours the jet-dominated scenario also in FBQS J1644 + 2619 and PKS 2004--447. The summed X-ray Telescope spectra of 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, and FBQS J1644 + 2619 are well fitted by a broken power law with a break around 2 keV.

Moreover, in order to compare the variability at different frequencies, we quantify the observed variability of 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, FBQS J1644 + 2619, and PKS 2004--447 in optical, UV, and X-rays bands through the variability amplitude, V_ amp , calculated as the ratio of maximum to minimum flux corrected for Galactic extinction. The variability amplitude may depend on the sampling of the light curves at the different frequencies. However, at least for a comparison between different bands, the simultaneity of observations in optical, UV, and X-rays guaranteed by the Swift observations helps mitigate this problem.

The variability amplitude in X-rays of the six sources varies between 2.7 and 8.5. These jetted NLSy1 are less variable than the other non-jetted NLSy1, suggesting that a different emission mechanism is driving the flux variations.

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS 0846 + 513 as a gamma-ray NLSy1 with a powerful relativistic jet, blazar-like variability, superluminal motion, and unusually high radio loudness.

quotes.quote: In particular, a strong ray flare from SBS 0846 + 513 was observed in 2011 June by Fermi LAT reaching a ray luminosity (0.1--300 GeV) of 10 ^ 48 erg s ^ 1 , comparable to that of bright flat spectrum radio quasars. Apparent superluminal velocity in the jet was inferred from 2011--2012 VLBA images, suggesting the presence of a highly relativistic jet. Both the power released by this object during the flaring activity and the apparent superluminal velocity are strong indicators of the presence of a relativistic jet as powerful as those in blazars. In addition, variability and spectral properties in radio and ray bands indicate a blazar-like behaviour, suggesting that, except for some distinct optical characteristics, SBS 0846 + 513 could be considered as a young blazar at the low end of the blazar's black hole mass distribution.

When imaged with the high spatial resolution of the VLBA SBS 0846 + 513 is resolved in two components with a core-jet structure, as also pointed out in a previous work by taylor05 . At 15 GHz the jet structure (component E in Fig. Fig1 , right panel) shows an extended low-surface brightness structure with a steep spectrum. On the other hand the core (component W) is resolved into two compact components (labelled W1 and W2 in Fig. Fig1 , right panel).

sources.summary: SBS 0846 + 513 is a Fermi-detected radio-loud NLSy1 with strong gamma-ray flaring, blazar-like radio/gamma behavior, and possible unresolved host structure.

quotes.quote: Up to now 5 radio-loud NLSy1 galaxies have been detected at high significance by Fermi LAT: PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, 1H 0323 + 342, PKS 2004 447, and SBS 0846 + 513. Here we analyze the first four years of ray observations of these sources.

A strong ray flare was observed also from SBS 0846 + 513 in 2011 June, reaching an isotropic ray luminosity (0.1--300 GeV) of 10 ^ 48 erg s ^ 1 , comparable to that of the bright FSRQs dammando12 . Variability and spectral properties of SBS 0846 + 513 in radio and rays indicate a blazar-like behaviour. In addition, from the model-fitting of 4-epoch MOJAVE data of this source in 2010-2012 we found that the jet components are separating with an apparent velocity of (10.9 1.4) . This value suggest the presence of boosting effect as well as in blazars .

Among the ray emitting NLSy1s detected by Fermi LAT only for 1H 0323 + 342 Hubble Space Telescope (HST) and Nordic Optical Telescope observations are available. These observations revealed a one-armed galaxy morphology or a circumnuclear ring, suggesting two possibilities: the spiral arms of the host galaxy or the residual of a galaxy merging . On the other hand, no significant resolved structures have been observed instead by HST for SBS 0846 + 513 , and no high-resolution observations are available for the remaining ray NLSy1s.

sources.summary: The paper applies its viewing-angle method to SBS 0846+513, estimating an 8-9 degree angle and placing its Lorentz factor at the low end of blazar-like radio-loud NLS1s.

quotes.quote: Using literature values of _ app and D_ var , we also deduce a viewing angle of 8^ - 9^ towards another radio- and ray-loud NLS1, namely SBS 0846+513. galaxies: active -- galaxies: jets -- galaxies: individual: 1H 0323+342 -- gamma-rays: galaxies L.

The viewing angle towards the NLS1 galaxy SBS 0846+513 Using the results presented by see also 2012MNRAS.426..317D we can also estimate the viewing angle towards the radio and ray-loud NLS1 SBS 0846+513, using the same method presented in Sect. sect:Combi . The authors report an apparent jet speed for this source of 9.3 . Furthermore, we estimate D_ var as in Sect. D_var from the 15 GHz variability presented in Figs. 3 and 4 of to be of the order 6 - 7 , in agreement with a value of 7.1 which we obtain from the variability characteristics given in and using an intrinsic equipartition brightness temperature limit of 5 10^ 10 K. These values for _ app and D_ var then provide a viewing angle estimate of 8^ - 9^ using Eq. eq:thetaVar .
Applying similar estimates to published results of another radio-loud NLS1 galaxy, SBS 0846+513, we estimate a viewing angle of 8^ - 9^ .

sources.summary: The paper images SBS 0846+513 as a gamma-ray NLS1 host, deriving a high-end black hole mass, finding bulge-plus-disc structure, and noting possible companions or disturbed host features.

quotes.quote: The gamma-ray emitting galaxy SBS 0846+513 has been classified as a Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 from its spectroscopy, and on that basis it was thought likely to have a small central black hole hosted in a spiral galaxy. But very few of the gamma-ray Narrow-Line Seyfert 1s have high-resolution imaging of their hosts, so it is unknown how those expectations hold up for the gamma-emitting class. We have observed this galaxy in the J band with the Large Binocular Telescope's LUCI1 camera and the ARGOS adaptive optics system. We estimate its black hole mass to lie between 7.70 M M _ 8.19 , using the correlation with bulge luminosity, or 7.96 M M _ 8.16 using the correlation with S e rsic index, putting its mass at the high end of the Narrow Line Seyfert 1 range.

These estimates are independent of the Broad Line Region viewing geometry and avoid underestimates due to looking down the jet axis. Its host shows evidence of a bulge + disc structure, both from two-dimensional modeling and isophote shape, in keeping with the expectations. Mergers and interactions appear to be common among the gamma-ray Narrow-Line Seyfert 1s, and we see some circumstantial evidence for companion galaxies or disturbed features in the host.
