The running median shows good agreement with the linear fit for NGC 3521, NGC 5068, and NGC 7793, indicating that the gradients are well-described with a single fit in these systems.

sources.summary: The best-fit NGC 7793 model needs decreased metallicity, unlike other low-mass galaxies avoids high outer molecular depletion times, and CO photodissociation lowers modeled emission.

quotes.quote: In addition, the best-fit models of NGC 7793 needed a decreased metallicity ( 2 _0 ) with respect to the leaky box model (Eq. molecule ).

Whereas the molecular depletion times of the inner disks are similar to that commonly observed in star-forming spiral galaxies ( 2008AJ....136.2846B , 2008AJ....136.2782L ), they are significantly higher in the outer disks of the low-mass galaxies, except for NGC 7793 .

Even in the CO-dim galaxy NGC 7793 CO photo-dissociation only leads to a decrease of the CO emission by 50 in the inner disk and by a factor of about two in the outer disk. This translates into an increase of the CO flux by a factor of 1.7 in the absence of CO photodissociation.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 7793 as a flocculent spiral whose changing inclination and position angle may mark a warp, with outer radial-velocity increases.

quotes.quote: NGC 7793 NGC 7793 is a flocculent spiral galaxy . The TRM analysis shows a monotonic decrease of the inclination angle by approximately 20^ from the inner to the outer disk (where R_ 25 315'' ) and a corresponding monotonic increase in the P.A. by the same amount (as seen in Fig. NGC7793 ) that may correspond to a warped geometry. As a result of these moderate changes, the transversal and circular velocities are similar, but only in the outermost region.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 7793 as an example where star counts extend the known disk profile, the outer disk appears old and red-giant dominated, and metallicity-gradient flattening is absent.

quotes.quote: The example is shown in Figure fig2 ; in NGC 7793, star counts profile significantly increases the known radial extent of the disk.
On the contrary, M33, NGC 300, and NGC 7793 seem to harbor old outer disks with red giant branch stars as a dominant stellar population barker07,vlajic09,vlajic09b .
On the other hand, an apparent lack of flattening is observed in NGC 7793 vlajic09b and possibly M33 barker07 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7793 as one of the three least successful MOND fits, but attributes the difficulty to poorly constrained inclination and rotation-curve uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Among the galaxies with the 3 least good fits (NGC 3198, NGC 7793, and NGC 2976), we do not discuss further NGC 7793 and NGC 2976, since the MOND fits present the same failures as the dark matter fits (in Newtonian dynamics), therefore we do not consider them as evidence against MOND. We just briefly note that in NGC 7793 the value of the inclination angle fitted by de Blok et al. (2008) is low and presents large variations in adjacent radii, which results in a poorly constrained rotation curve; in NGC 2976 the amplitude of the non-circular motions (Trachternach et al. 2008) is correlated with the amplitude of the fit residuals.

We find that the MOND fits with the distance ``constrained" are of very good quality (Fig. fits ), with three exceptions: two of these are galaxies that cannot give good fits using Newtonian dynamics plus dark matter (NGC 2976 and NGC 7793) either, see de Blok et al. (2008).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7793 as a Sculptor-group galaxy previously observed with deep H kinematics, showing ionized emission to the H I disk edge and a declining rotation curve.

quotes.quote: In this context, Dic2008135 carried out a deep H kinematical analysis of NGC 7793, another Sculptor group galaxy. They used the 36-cm Marseille Telescope located in La Silla, Chile, offering a large field of view. With almost 20 hours of observations, they detected ionized emission all the way to the end of the H i disc and confirmed that NGC 7793 had a truly declining rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7793 cluster candidates to compare stochastic and deterministic age estimates, finding only a slight decrease in average age separations.

quotes.quote: We utilize the full posterior PDFs of the cluster candidates in NGC 7793 and NGC 628 to investigate the implications on the t - R relation of deriving the ages stochastically compared to traditionally deterministic SED modeling.
Figure fig:sto shows the t - R relation for NGC 7793 and NGC 628 where the ages are determined both from deterministic techniques and with the stochastic modeling code SLUG krumholz15 .
There is a slight decrease in the average age separations for clusters with properties derived stochastically within NGC 7793 and no difference is observed within NGC 628.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7793 as the nearest RINGS galaxy, requiring modified sky subtraction because of its low systemic velocity and large apparent extent.

quotes.quote: The nearest galaxy in our sample, NGC 7793, required us to modify slightly our procedure for subtracting the night sky emission lines from our images. Because it is so close, its systemic velocity is small enough to be comparable to its internal motions; i.e. \ some of its gas has zero line-of-sight velocity relative to Earth. Additionally, it takes up a substantially larger fraction of the RSS field of view than do the other galaxies discussed in this work.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7793 as a flocculent spiral with nearby distance, large angular size, radial dust-to-gas analysis, and lower dust-to-gas ratios than NGC 628.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the dust--to--gas ratios in five nearby galaxies NGC 628 (M74), NGC 6503, NGC 7793, UGC 5139 (Holmberg I), and UGC 4305 (Holmberg II).
NGC 628, NGC 6503, and NGC 7793 are all spiral galaxies with a range of masses, though NGC 7793 is categorized as a flocculent.
Both bins also have significantly lower dust--to--gas ratios than NGC 628, which we might expect from NGC 7793's lower metallicity.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 7793 lacks CO emission, has SFR scale height coincident with HI, and shows a declining observed HI rotation-curve profile.

quotes.quote: However, 2017Iorio showed that its contribution is negligible in the case of DDO 154, which is the least massive galaxy in our sample. , IC 2574, and NGC 7793 were not included in 2016Frank sample as no CO emission was detected, for these we used the mass models reported in .
Clearly, in the case of DDO 154, IC 2574, and NGC 7793, h_ SFR (R) coincides with h_ HI as CO emission is not detected.
The observed HI rotation curve of NGC 7793 has a declining profile in (green points in Fig. fig:vc_n7793 ).

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 7793 is better fit with a mild inner oval distortion, leading to higher inner circular speeds and confirming its unusual rotation-curve shape.

quotes.quote: NGC 7793 The velocity map for this galaxy, Figure  fig.N7793 , is better fitted with a mild oval distortion in the inner parts, as was also found by SZS10 . As for NGC 2976, we find that fitting for an inner oval in a flat disc leads to slightly higher estimates of the circular speed over the radial range of the oval than were estimated by dB08, who attributed the inner twisted isovelocity contours to variations in the projection geometry. Otherwise we confirm the somewhat unusual overall rotation curve shape they also reported.

gmw_00000917.name: NGC 3447A
gmw_00000917.overview: NGC 3447A is a very blue irregular galaxy that is strongly interacting with its companion, NGC 3447 [arXiv:0912.4266]. This tidal interaction has distorted the galaxy's disk and arms [arXiv:0801.3173], triggering recent star formation in its young stellar population [arXiv:0912.4266].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3447A as a strongly interacting, very blue irregular in LGG 225 whose young stellar population and color profile suggest interaction-triggered recent star formation.

quotes.quote: The irregular interacting galaxy NGC 3447A has a significantly younger stellar population (few Myr old) than the average of the other irregular galaxies in LGG 225 suggesting that the encounter triggered star formation.

NGC 3447A (FUV-NUV=0.11 0.08, see next Section) is the bluest galaxy and is strongly interacting with NGC 3447. Interaction is so strong that for NGC 3447A it is even difficult to figure out whether the original morphological type was irregular or late-type spiral. Summarizing, although cases of morphological distortion/asymmetries and multiple arms are present in all groups, only in LGG 225 there is a direct evidence of tidal distortion, as in the cases of pairs NGC 3447/NGC 3447A and NGC 3454/NGC 3455.

Blue color profiles of the interacting pair NGC 3454/NGC 3455, and NGC 3447/NGC 3447A, suggest that recent star formation events could be triggered by tidal interaction. There is evidence that such episodes are triggered by on-going interaction as in the case of NGC 3447A or NGC 3454/NGC 3455.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3447A as part of an interacting pair in LGG 225, with GALEX UV emission, a visible bar, and tidally distorted disk and arms.

quotes.quote: We imaged in the Near (NUV) and the Far (FUV) GALEX bands the ultraviolet emission of NGC 3447, NGC 3447A, NGC 3454, NGC 3455 and UGC 6035 aiming at tracing the ongoing star formation (SF) and its distribution across galaxies and their intergalactic medium.

NGC 3447A and its companion NGC 3447 are members of the KPG 255 pair in the catalogue with a radial velocity difference of about 30 km s ^ 1 . The two galaxies appear strongly interacting. A bar is still visible in NGC 3447A, while both the underlying disk and the (multiple?) arms are tidally distorted.

gmw_00000918.name: NGC 3447
gmw_00000918.overview: The NGC 3447 system is a member of the LGG 225 group [arXiv:1710.07474] and is characterized by a highly distorted morphology [arXiv:0912.4266, arXiv:0801.3173]. While historically classified as an interacting pair [arXiv:1710.07474, arXiv:0801.3173], kinematic and photometric studies suggest it is a single, unstable disk galaxy [arXiv:1710.07474, arXiv:1803.03967].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3447/NGC 3447A as a disturbed single system or false pair whose patchy morphology traces disk instability from an encounter.

quotes.quote: The NGC 3447/NGC 3447A system is a member of the LGG 225 group, a nearby Local Group Analog. This system is considered a physical pair composed of an intermediate luminosity late type spiral, NGC 3447 itself, and an irregular companion, NGC 3447A, linked by a faint, short filament of matter. A ring-like structure in the NGC 3447 outskirts has been emphasised by GALEX observations.

To summarise, we do not find any kinematic signature probing that NGC 3447 and NGC 3447A have different velocity fields. The velocity field does not show any rotation pattern. The velocity gradient across the entire system is too weak and irregular to be considered as a rotation pattern, although the South West region appears slightly approaching and the North East slightly receding. Our results point towards the idea that patchy and distorted features are different regions of a single system.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3447/3447A as a false pair: UV/optical data and velocity fields support a single distorted disk shaped by instability and a distant encounter.

quotes.quote: The NGC 3447/3447A system morphology, shown in the left panel of Figure fig:4 , has been interpreted as a pair distorted by an on-going interaction. In our recent study Mazzei17 , we investigated its optical and ultraviolet surface photometry revealing that NGC 3347 has an extended disk that includes NGC 3447A. The 2D velocity field and velocity dispersion map we obtained using the Fabry-Perot PUMA@2.2m in San Pedro Martir (Mexico) suggest that neither NGC 3447 nor NGC 3447A have a coherent rotation velocity, but rather that there is a small velocity gradient between the two.

In the right panel Figure fig:4 we show the far-ultraviolet image of NGC 3447/3447A derived from the snapshot of the simulation provides an excellent match to the ultraviolet morphology and brightness, in addition to the best fit of the system SED, the B-band magnitude, and the best match of the velocity field. According to this simulation, the peculiar morphology of the system can be interpreted as a consequence of the disk instability driven by the halo instability itself, enhanced by the on-going encounter with a distant ( 1 degree) companion, 0.5 mag fainter. The simulation suggests that NGC 3447/NGC 3447A is a single object. The predicted age of the system is 12 Gyr, while its stellar populations, weighted by B-band luminosity, are 1.3 Gyr old.

sources.summary: NGC 3447 is treated as the distorted companion of NGC 3447A: its bar remains visible, its disk and arms are tidally distorted, and UV emission extends beyond the optical body.

quotes.quote: NGC 3447A (FUV-NUV=0.11 0.08, see next Section) is the bluest galaxy and is strongly interacting with NGC 3447. A bar is still visible in NGC 3447, while both the underlying disk and the (multiple?) arms are tidally distorted. Summarizing, although cases of morphological distortion/asymmetries and multiple arms are present in all groups, only in LGG 225 there is a direct evidence of tidal distortion, as in the cases of pairs NGC 3447/NGC 3447A and NGC 3454/NGC 3455.

Furthermore the UV emission in NGC 3447/NGC 3447A, in NGC 3455 and UGC 6035 extends much farther out than the respective optical images (Figure 225 ). Blue color profiles of the interacting pair NGC 3454/NGC 3455, and NGC 3447/NGC 3447A, suggest that recent star formation events could be triggered by tidal interaction. Some LGG 225 members display unambiguously interacting (NGC 3447/NGC 3447A, NGC 3454/NGC 3455) and distorted morphologies (UGC 6035) particularly evident in the UV imaging.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3447 as NGC 3447A's strongly interacting companion, observed in GALEX UV, with interaction so severe that its original morphology is difficult to infer.

quotes.quote: We imaged in the Near (NUV) and the Far (FUV) GALEX bands the ultraviolet emission of NGC 3447, NGC 3447A, NGC 3454, NGC 3455 and UGC 6035 aiming at tracing the ongoing star formation (SF) and its distribution across galaxies and their intergalactic medium.
NGC 3447A and its companion NGC 3447 are members of the KPG 255 pair in the catalogue with a radial velocity difference of about 30 km s ^ 1 . The two galaxies appear strongly interacting.
Interaction is so strong that, for NGC 3447, it is even difficult to figure out the original morphological type (irregular or late-type spiral?).

gmw_00000919.name: IRAS 08311 2459
gmw_00000919.overview: IRAS 08311 2459 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0006318]. The nucleus is surrounded by a rotating disk of star formation containing extended molecular hydrogen and low-velocity Paschen-alpha emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0006318].

sources.summary: The paper presents near-infrared spectroscopy of IRAS 08311 2459, finding an AGN, unresolved high-velocity Paschen-alpha and [Si VI] emission, and extended rotating star-forming gas.

quotes.quote: Near-infrared spectroscopy using the new Palomar Integral Field Spectrograph indicates the presence of an AGN (active galactic nucleus) in the ultraluminous infrared galaxy IRAS 08311 2459. The high-velocity wings of the Paschen- hydrogen recombination line are seen to be spatially unresolved, and with no positional offset between red and blue high velocity emission. The [ Si VI ] coronal line, with a 167 eV excitation potential, is also spatially unresolved with a velocity width comparable to that of the broad component of the Pa emission.

The low velocity component of the Pa emission is seen to be rotating, and is extended over 2 kpc. Molecular hydrogen is also extended, and elongated along the maximum velocity gradient, sharing the same rotation profile as the narrow Pa emission. The simple picture in agreement with the observations is that of an AGN surrounded by a rotating disk of star formation.

gmw_00000920.name: ESO 320-G030
gmw_00000920.overview: ESO 320-G030 is an isolated, double-barred spiral galaxy classified as a luminous infrared galaxy with a compact obscured nucleus [arXiv:1607.03674, arXiv:2403.16759]. It hosts a strong nuclear starburst and a collimated molecular outflow, with no evidence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1607.03674, arXiv:2102.13563].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS F11506 3851 with ESO 320-30, describes its LIRG OH megamaser spectrum, and finds separated 1665/1667 MHz emission but no confident magnetic-field result.

quotes.quote: IRAS F11506 3851 : Also known as ESO 320-30, this LIRG is classified as an H 2 galaxy at z = 0.0108 . A Parkes single-dish spectrum is presented by staveley-smithncawr92 showing 1667 MHz OHM emission centered at 3103 km s ^ 1 with a FWHM velocity extent of 87 km s ^ 1 and a peak flux density of 105 mJy. There is neither enough sensitivity nor bandwidth to clearly discern any 1665 MHz emission. There are no interferometric observations of this source: like IC 2545, the low declination of 11506 would hinder any attempted VLBI observations.

The top panel of Figure fig:iv_11506 shows that the 1667 MHz Stokes I emission is fitted quite well by 6 narrow Gaussian components. Gaussian components 0 and 3 have derived magnetic fields that look significant when judged by their formal errors. However, given the quality of the Stokes V spectrum ( S_ rms = 0.4 mJy), we have no confidence in either result.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA and ancillary observations of ESO 320-G030, resolving its barred spiral morphology, compact starburst nucleus, and high-velocity molecular outflow driven without AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we analyze new Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) CO(2--1) high spatial resolution ( 60 pc) observations of the local ( d=48 Mpc; scale of 240 pc arcsec ^ 1 ) LIRG ESO 320-G030 ( L_ IR = 11.3 ; also known as IRAS F11506-3851). We combine the CO(2--1) data (cold molecular gas tracer) with available near-IR integral field spectroscopy (IFS) to trace the hot molecular gas as well as the ionized gas Piqueras2012 . This galaxy is an isolated spiral galaxy with an ordered velocity field Bellocchi2013 hosting a strong starburst AAH06s . Its nuclear activity is classified as H ii from optical spectroscopy vandenBroek1991, Pereira2011 and there is no evidence of an AGN in this galaxy based on its mid-IR and X-ray emissions Pereira2010c, Pereira2011 .

We spatially resolve a high velocity ( 450 km s ^ 1 ) outflow containing 10 ^ 6.8 \ of molecular gas (assuming the ULIRG conversion factor) originating in the central 250 pc. The size of the outflow is 1.2 kpc, which corresponds to a dynamical time of 2.8 Myr. The opening angle is 30 .

sources.summary: The paper reports ESO 320-G030 as a newly identified compact obscured nucleus with vibrationally excited HCN, redshifted absorption suggesting inflow, and possible minor-axis outflow structure.

quotes.quote: In ESO 320-G030 (Fig. fig:outflow_ESO320 ) we see that the HCN emission at projected velocities between 0 and 100 km s ^ 1 and between 200 and 300 km s ^ 1 from the systemic velocity has a component that is elongated along an axis with a position angle (PA) of 40 east of north. This is almost perpendicular to the major kinematic axis which has a PA of 135 as determined from the velocity field seen outside of the most nuclear region, consistent with the value found by per16 from observations of CO J=2 - 1 . The orientation of the elongated structure is similar to that of the outflow reported by per16 which has a PA of between 10 and 40 . Interestingly, the absorption features in the HCN and HCO ^ + ground-state lines in ESO 320-G030 are redshifted with respect to the emission, possibly indicating that the absorbing gas is moving inward.

One of the other newly identified CONs in this survey, ESO 320-G030, has observations of the OH 119 m doublet reported by gon17 under the name IRAS 11506-3851. Similarly to those in the already known CONs, the 119 m absorption lines of OH in ESO 320-G030 show evidence of inflowing gas in the form of a significant redshift relative to the systemic velocity of the galaxy. The other newly identified CON, IRAS 17578-0400, had an observation of the other ground state doublet at 79 m, with the absorption lines peaking at a slightly redshifted velocity (see Appendix app:OH ). In a multitransition OH analysis of molecular outflows by gon17 it is seen (their Fig. 10) that all sources in which the 79 m doublet peaks at velocities 0 km s ^ 1 also have OH 119 m lines which peak at positive velocities. At (sub)millimeter wavelengths, all seven CONs in our survey show possible signatures of molecular outflows. At least three of these, those in Arp 220 W bar18 , ESO 320-G030 per16 , and Zw 049.057 fal18 , appear collimated.

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 320-G030 as an isolated luminous infrared galaxy with a compact obscured nucleus, regular rotation, a double bar, and an ALMA-resolved molecular wind likely powered by MHD processes.

quotes.quote: Here, we present high-resolution ALMA observations ( 30 mas, 5 pc) of ground-state and vibrationally excited HCN towards \ (IRAS 11506−3851). \ is an isolated luminous infrared galaxy known to host a compact obscured nucleus and a kiloparsec-scale molecular wind. Our analysis of these high-resolution observations excludes the possibility of a starburst-driven wind, a mechanically or energy driven active galactic nucleus (AGN) wind, and exposes a molecular MDH wind.

Falstad2021 identified \ (IRAS F11506-3851) as a host to a CON with a HCN-vib luminosity of 1.4 0.2  . \ is an isolated LIRG Sanders2003 , with regular rotation Bellocchi2013 , a double-barred structure Greusard2000 , and no signs of a recent interaction Arribas2008 . It has a luminosity distance of 36 Mpc ( z = 0.0103 ) Sanders2003 using a flat cosmology with H _0 = 75 Mpc ^ 1 , _ M = 0.3 , and _ = 0.7 .

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 320-G030 as an HII-classified barred galaxy whose photometry reveals a nuclear bar, radial stellar velocity component, and centrally peaked velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: ESO 320-G030 : This SAB()a galaxy has been spectroscopically classified as H II PereiraSantaella+11 . Our photometric analysis reveals an inner PA of 90 , being spatially compatible with the presence of the nuclear bar of 1 kpc in length discovered by Greusard+00 . The kinematic analysis reveals a radial component in the velocity map which is likely due to the presence of non-circular motions, with a similar orientation ( 85 ) to the photometric PA found in the inner regions. The velocity dispersion map presents a clear centrally peaked distribution, reaching values of 130 km s ^ 1 at the central 300 pc.

gmw_00000921.name: IRAS 17578-0400
gmw_00000921.overview: IRAS 17578-0400 is a luminous infrared galaxy containing a compact obscured nucleus [arXiv:2102.13563, arXiv:2307.07641] with a rotation-dominated velocity field [arXiv:1509.08468]. The nucleus hosts a dusty torus [arXiv:2307.07641], a rotating dense gas disk [arXiv:2307.07641], and molecular outflows aligned with its minor axis [arXiv:2102.13563, arXiv:2307.07641].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 17578-0400 as a new compact obscured nucleus, noting vibrationally excited HCN, limited velocity shifts, nearby-companion context, and possible minor-axis outflow signatures.

quotes.quote: For three of the LIRGs, ESO 320-G030, ESO 173-G015, and IRAS 17578-0400, and one of the ULIRGs, IRAS F14378-3651, this is the first detection of vibrationally excited HCN.

We do not see any signatures of misaligned high-velocity emission in IRAS 17578-0400 but there is a tentative signature at lower velocities. In Fig. fig:outflow_I17578 we see that the emission within 80 km s ^ 1 (projected) of the systemic velocity is elongated along the kinematic minor axis (PA 25 , again determined from the velocity field outside the most nuclear region), with a possible offset between the red- and blueshifted emission components along this axis. For this galaxy, we have not found any published observations of CO to compare the orientations of the kinematic axes in the nucleus and on larger scales. The absorption features in the HCN and HCO ^ + ground-state lines of IRAS 17578-0400 do not show any strong velocity shifts, although the one in the HCN line appears slightly redshifted compared to the emission.

The final CON, IRAS 17578-0400, does not have any published outflow signatures, but our HCN observations reveal a low-velocity elongation along its kinematic minor axis (Fig. fig:outflow_I17578 ). This may be a signature of a minor axis outflow which is directed almost perpendicular to our line of sight toward the galaxy. Possible signatures of noncircular motions are also seen in the ground state HCN and HCO ^ + lines in the form of asymmetric line profiles in some CONs. In most of them, the absorptions are stronger on the blueshifted side: Zw 049.057 aal15b , IRAS 17208-0014 (Fig. fig:detections ), and Arp 220 sak09,aal15b,mar16 or close to the line center: IRAS 17578-0400 (Fig. fig:detections ), and NGC 4418 sak13 .

sources.summary: ALMA observations of IRAS 17578-0400 reveal a parsec-scale compact obscured nucleus with a dusty torus, dense gas disk, molecular outflows, infalling rotating disk, and massive SMBH.

quotes.quote: We here present 0.02--0.04 \ resolution ALMA observation of the compact obscured nucleus (CON) of IRAS 17578-0400. A dusty torus within the nucleus, approximately 4 pc in radius, has been uncovered, exhibiting a usually flat spectral index at ALMA band 3, likely due to the millimeter corona emission from the central supermassive black hole (SMBH). The dense gas disk, traced by 10, spans 7 pc in radius and suggests an outflow driven by a disk wind due to its asymmetrical structure along the minor axis.

Collimated molecular outflows (CMO), traced by the low-velocity components of the 32 \ and 32 \ lines, align with the minor axis gas disk. Examination of position-velocity plots of 32 \ and 32 \ reveals a flared dense gas disk extended a radius of 60 pc, infalling and rotating at speeds of about 200 \ and 300 respectively. A centrifugal barrier, located around 4 pc from the dynamical center, implies an SMBH mass of approximately 10 ^8 , consistent with millimeter corona emission estimates.

sources.summary: The paper describes this little-studied system as low-S/N in the blue but consistent with pure star formation and a rotation-dominated velocity field.

quotes.quote: IRAS 17578-0400 There is very little information available about this system in the literature. Our data are very low S/N in the blue, though the binned nuclear spectrum is indicative of pure star formation. This is consistent with the and / line ratios and the velocity dispersion distribution, which shows little to no indication of any appreciable non- region component and a rotation-dominated velocity field.

gmw_00000922.name: ID 1
gmw_00000922.overview: ID 1 is an optically-selected dwarf galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2105.05876]. Its nuclear X-ray source and mid-infrared emission provide strong evidence for an accreting massive black hole at its center [arXiv:2105.05876].

sources.summary: The paper classifies ID 1 among BPT AGN dwarf galaxies whose nuclear X-ray source and WISE mid-infrared emission support an accreting massive black hole interpretation.

quotes.quote: The X-ray sources are almost certainly associated with the peak of the NIR emission for the four BPT AGNs (IDs 1-3,5) and one composite galaxy (ID 6) with X-ray detections. In these cases, the positions of the X-ray sources are consistent with prominent nuclei and there are no other obvious alternative optical/ NIR counterparts.

There is strong evidence that the X-ray sources detected in the five optically-selected BPT AGN and Composite galaxies (IDs 1,2,3,5,6) are indeed accreting massive BHs in the nuclei of their host galaxies. The X-ray luminosities are well above the expected contribution from XRBs, and the positions of the X-ray sources are consistent with prominent nuclei, the peak of the NIR emission in the HST images, and the centroid of the mid-IR emission from WISE (see Figure fig:rgb ). It is thus reasonable to attribute the detected mid-IR emission from WISE to these optically-selected AGNs.

gmw_00000923.name: ID 2
gmw_00000923.overview: ID 2 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2003.11412, arXiv:1908.00020] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1605.05731, arXiv:2003.11412] and a nuclear disk [arXiv:1908.00020]. The system exhibits nuclear X-ray emission [arXiv:2105.05876, arXiv:2302.06214], an optically thin radio counterpart [arXiv:2302.06214], and ionized gas outflows [arXiv:2003.11412, arXiv:2402.11665].

sources.summary: RGG 9 is a dwarf-galaxy AGN candidate with persistent broad H emission across SDSS and DIS spectra, yielding a central black-hole mass of about 3.6 x 10^5 solar masses.

quotes.quote: RGG 9 RGG denotes we are using the ID assigned by Reines, Greene, Geha (2013). (NSA 10779), is classified as an AGN on the BPT diagram based on its SDSS spectrum. We observe broad H emission in both the SDSS spectrum and the DIS spectrum, taken 13 years apart (see Figure nsa10779_main for the best fit to the DIS spectrum).

For RGG 9, we measure a BH mass from the SDSS observation of 3.5( 0.7) 10^ 5 M_ . From the DIS observation, we measure a BH mass of 3.7( 0.3) 10^ 5 M_ . Using these two measurements, we compute a mean mass of M_ BH = 3.6( 0.8) 10^ 5 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes RGG 9 as a dwarf elliptical AGN hosting an intermediate-mass black hole, broad H emission, AGN-driven ionized outflows, and compact radio emission detected by VLBI/VLA surveys.

quotes.quote: The dwarf elliptical galaxy at the redshift z=0.0465 hosts an AGN and has a stellar mass of 2.3 10^ 9 M_ source ID: 9, Reines2013 . It displays not only some narrow-line AGN signatures but also a persistent broad H emission source ID: RGG 9, Baldassare2016 . Based on high spectral resolution observations, Baldassare2016 estimated the mass of its BH as M_ bh =3.6^ +5.9 _ 2.3 10^5 M_ (including the systematic uncertainty of 0.42 dex). In the long-slit spectroscopy with the Keck I telescope, it shows some spatially extended ionized gas outflows that are most likely AGN-driven because their velocity (701 7 km s ^ 1 ) exceeds the escape velocity (303 35 km s ^ 1 ) of its halo Manzano-King2019 . It is a slightly resolved point-like source with total the flux density of 22.4 4.1 mJy in the the GMRT (Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope) 150 MHz all-sky radio survey Intema2017 and 4.7 0.2 mJy in the 1.4 GHz FIRST survey Becker1995 .
It has a flux density of 0.93 0.05 mJy at 9.0 GHz and 0.78 0.04 mJy at 10.65 GHz in the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) observations source ID: 26, Reines2020 .

sources.summary: The paper includes ID 2 among BPT AGN dwarf galaxies with nuclear X-ray emission and WISE mid-infrared emission interpreted as evidence for an accreting massive black hole.

quotes.quote: The X-ray sources are almost certainly associated with the peak of the NIR emission for the four BPT AGNs (IDs 1-3,5) and one composite galaxy (ID 6) with X-ray detections. In these cases, the positions of the X-ray sources are consistent with prominent nuclei and there are no other obvious alternative optical/ NIR counterparts.

There is strong evidence that the X-ray sources detected in the five optically-selected BPT AGN and Composite galaxies (IDs 1,2,3,5,6) are indeed accreting massive BHs in the nuclei of their host galaxies. The X-ray luminosities are well above the expected contribution from XRBs, and the positions of the X-ray sources are consistent with prominent nuclei, the peak of the NIR emission in the HST images, and the centroid of the mid-IR emission from WISE (see Figure fig:rgb ). It is thus reasonable to attribute the detected mid-IR emission from WISE to these optically-selected AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J090613.77 + 561015.2 as a dwarf AGN with an IMBH, X-ray emission, an optically thin radio counterpart, and jet-linked radio morphology.

quotes.quote: Very long baseline interferometric (VLBI) observations have also revealed some relatively compact features very likely linked to jet and outflow activity in some sources, e.g. NGC 4395 Wrobel2001, Wrobel2006, Yang2022 , Henize 2--10 Ulvestad2007, Reines2012, Nyland2017, Schutte2022 , SDSS J090613.77 + 561015.2 Yang2020RGG9 and four low-mass galaxies YangX2022 .

It is a dwarf elliptical galaxy with stellar mass M_ = 2.3 10^ 9 M_ source ID: 9, Reines2013 at the redshift z=0.0465 (scale: 0.94 pc mas ^ 1 ). Based on high spectral resolution optical observations, Baldassare2016 estimated the mass of its BH as M_ BH = 3.6^ +5.9 _ 2.3 10^5 M_ (including the systematic uncertainty of 0.42 dex). Its X-ray luminosity is L_ X = 4.5 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 Baldassare2017 .

The existing interferometric observations with the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) at 150 MHz Intema2017 and the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) at 1.4 GHz Becker1995, Reines2020, Gultekin2022 show that its radio counterpart has an optically thin power-law spectrum between 0.15 and 10.65 GHz Yang2020RGG9 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID 26 (RGG 9) as a dwarf broad-line AGN with a 2.5e5 solar-mass black hole, AGN-dominated line ratios, Seyfert-region diagnostics, and AGN-like mid-infrared colors.

quotes.quote: One of these objects, ID 26, was previously identified as a broad-line AGN in reinesetal2013 (designated RGG 9) with M_ BH 2.5 10^5 M_ .

ID 26 (RGG 9) is the only dwarf galaxy with emission line ratios clearly dominated by an AGN in all three diagrams. The other sources fall in the star-forming region of the BPT diagram baldwinetal1981 , which takes /H versus /H . However, 5 objects (IDs 25, 26, 64, 65, 83) fall in the Seyfert region of the /H versus /H diagram and 1 other object falls in the LINER region (ID 6).
ID 26 (RGG 9) is the only dwarf galaxy that falls in the jarrettetal2011 AGN selection box.

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0906 + 56 as likely late-type with spiral arms and finds clear outflow evidence from blueshifted absorption features modeled with continuum and emission-line fits.

quotes.quote: While J0906 + 56 is likely a late-type galaxy with possible spiral arms, J0954 + 47 and J1009 + 26 show no sign of spiral arms and are more consistent with early-type galaxies.

Overall, objects J0906 + 56 and J0954 + 47 show clear evidence of outflows traced by absorption features. In object J1009 + 26, the detection is tentative as the blueshift of the absorption features is modest with large uncertainties. In the following, we describe the characteristic of the absorption features and fitting details for each object. In J0906 + 56, only blueshifted \ absorption features are detected on top of the broad, \ emission lines (Fig. fig:J0906fit and Appendix A2 ). We fit the featureless continuum with a linear function and the intrinsic \ emission line doublet with two Gaussian profiles, using fitting windows with no absorption signatures.

sources.summary: RGG 9 is described as a dwarf elliptical with a visible nuclear disk; fitting the disk is difficult, so the authors use a single-Sersic elliptical model.

quotes.quote: RGG 9 (Figure fig:RGG9_Model ) appears to be a dwarf elliptical galaxy with a nuclear disk. The nuclear disk is most notable when examining the residuals seen in the third panel of Figure fig:RGG9_Model . Though the disk is clearly visible in the residuals in the F110W filter this feature is more difficult to fit in the F606W filter, and including a component to account for the disk produces a mass for the nuclear disk which is overly large ( M_ ND 10^ 9 M_ ).

gmw_00000924.name: ID 3
gmw_00000924.overview: ID 3 is an early-type dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1908.00020, arXiv:2402.11665] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2209.09890, arXiv:2105.05876] powered by a low-mass black hole [arXiv:2209.09890]. The galaxy exhibits a complex radio jet [arXiv:2209.09890] alongside blueshifted absorption outflows and broad absorption line features [arXiv:2402.11665].

sources.summary: The paper reports that SDSS J095418.15+471725.1 hosts a low-mass active black hole with complex radio morphology, extended jet-like emission, and modest X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: Of the detections, 4 were consistent with a point source, and one (SDSS J095418.15+471725.1, with black hole mass M < 10^ 5 \ ) clearly shows extended emission that has a jet morphology .

We note that one of our sources, SDSS J095418.15+471725.1, has a complex radio morphology requiring a two-component Gaussian model in IMFIT. The second (dimmer) Gaussian component is located 0 5 to the East ( PA = 83^ ) and is unresolved within the 0 43 0 31 beam. Futher deep, high-resolution multi-band radio imaging is required to fully ascertain the nature of the extended emission. The luminosity and morphology of the radio emission in the available VLA data, however, are consistent with an extended jet arising from the central AGN. This source is among the lowest-mass AGN known to have an extended radio jet nyland+17, 2019MNRAS.488..685M, 2020MNRAS.495L..71Y .

SDSS J095418.15+471725.1 had enough X-ray counts to model the total amount of absorption and was found to be a column of N_ H 10^ 21 \ cm ^ 2 2017ApJ...836...20B , which leads to a factor of a few correction to the unabsorbed hard X-ray flux (which we use in our analysis).

sources.summary: RGG 11 is modeled as an S0 galaxy with a rounded classical bulge and a large outer exponential disk component.

quotes.quote: RGG 11 (Figure fig:RGG11_Model ) appears to be an S0 galaxy with galaxy with classical structure. The inner component is rounded and has a Sersic index of n = 2.4 , indicating a classical bulge. It has a half-light radius of 0.13 kpc. The outer component has a slightly higher than average Sersic index with n=1.69 , but still closely resembles the classic exponential disk. The disk is one of the larger features in our sample of galaxies with a half-light radius of 2.57 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper treats ID 3 as a BPT AGN dwarf with nuclear X-ray emission, prominent NIR association, and WISE mid-infrared emission consistent with an accreting massive black hole.

quotes.quote: The X-ray sources are almost certainly associated with the peak of the NIR emission for the four BPT AGNs (IDs 1-3,5) and one composite galaxy (ID 6) with X-ray detections. In these cases, the positions of the X-ray sources are consistent with prominent nuclei and there are no other obvious alternative optical/ NIR counterparts.

There is strong evidence that the X-ray sources detected in the five optically-selected BPT AGN and Composite galaxies (IDs 1,2,3,5,6) are indeed accreting massive BHs in the nuclei of their host galaxies. The X-ray luminosities are well above the expected contribution from XRBs, and the positions of the X-ray sources are consistent with prominent nuclei, the peak of the NIR emission in the HST images, and the centroid of the mid-IR emission from WISE (see Figure fig:rgb ). It is thus reasonable to attribute the detected mid-IR emission from WISE to these optically-selected AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0954 + 47 as early-type, with clear blueshifted absorption outflows and broad BAL-like troughs that may be the first BAL features found in a dwarf AGN.

quotes.quote: While J0906 + 56 is likely a late-type galaxy with possible spiral arms, J0954 + 47 and J1009 + 26 show no sign of spiral arms and are more consistent with early-type galaxies.

In J0954 + 47, blueshifted absorption features with similar profiles are seen in , , , \ and possibly \ (Fig. fig:J0954fit4line and Appendix A2 ). Additionally, much broader ( 4000 ) absorption troughs are seen for both \ and \ doublets (but not for other lower ionization transitions; e.g., see Fig. fig:BAL for \ as an example). The line widths of these broad troughs fall in the typical range of those of broad absorption lines (BAL) seen in luminous quasars e.g. Weymann1991 . If these broad troughs are indeed BAL features as in luminous quasars, it would be the first BAL ever discovered in a dwarf AGN to our knowledge, with a balnicity Weymann1991 of 740 .

gmw_00000925.name: ID 5
gmw_00000925.overview: ID 5 is an isolated dwarf galaxy with a low stellar mass and active star formation [arXiv:2306.01050, arXiv:2105.05876]. It hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus powered by an accreting massive black hole, which exhibits unsettled gas kinematics [arXiv:2306.01050, arXiv:2105.05876].

sources.summary: J1332 + 2634 is described as an isolated, star-forming Seyfert 2 host with low stellar mass, modest radio power, and a multi-peak absorption profile tracing unsettled gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: The source J1332 + 2634 is an isolated galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.04701 0.00002 Ahumada2020 . The host galaxy is a star-forming galaxy with a low stellar mass of log (M_ /M_ )=9.7 and star formation rate (SFR) of 3.1 M_ \ yr ^ 1 Salim2016 . J1332 + 2634 is classified as a Seyfert 2 AGN Toba2014 and has a flux of 21.1 mJy at 1.4 GHz Condon1998 . The corresponding radio power at 1.4 GHz is log (P_ 1.4 GHz / W Hz ^ 1 )=23.04 .

The \ absorption line profile of J1332 + 2634 is shown in Figure fig:abs_profile , with the velocity Doppler corrected based on the optical redshift of the host galaxy. The detected \ absorption line presents a multi-peak profile, implying unsettled kinematics of the absorbing gas.

sources.summary: The paper places ID 5 with the BPT AGN dwarf galaxies whose X-ray source is nuclear and too luminous for X-ray binaries, supporting an accreting massive black hole.

quotes.quote: The X-ray sources are almost certainly associated with the peak of the NIR emission for the four BPT AGNs (IDs 1-3,5) and one composite galaxy (ID 6) with X-ray detections. In these cases, the positions of the X-ray sources are consistent with prominent nuclei and there are no other obvious alternative optical/ NIR counterparts.

There is strong evidence that the X-ray sources detected in the five optically-selected BPT AGN and Composite galaxies (IDs 1,2,3,5,6) are indeed accreting massive BHs in the nuclei of their host galaxies. The X-ray luminosities are well above the expected contribution from XRBs, and the positions of the X-ray sources are consistent with prominent nuclei, the peak of the NIR emission in the HST images, and the centroid of the mid-IR emission from WISE (see Figure fig:rgb ). It is thus reasonable to attribute the detected mid-IR emission from WISE to these optically-selected AGNs.

gmw_00000926.name: ID 6
gmw_00000926.overview: ID 6 is a dwarf S0 galaxy featuring a classical bulge and a small stellar ring [arXiv:1908.00020]. It hosts a composite active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1605.05731, arXiv:2105.05876] powered by a central black hole with a mean mass of approximately 2.9 x 10^5 solar masses [arXiv:1605.05731].

sources.summary: RGG 119 is a composite dwarf-galaxy AGN candidate with persistent broad H emission, a mean black-hole mass near 2.9 x 10^5 solar masses, and measured stellar velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: The second secure broad line object, RGG 119 (NSA 79874), is classified as a composite object on the BPT diagram. This object has broad H emission in the SDSS, MagE, and DIS spectra, spanning five years (see Figure nsa79874_main ). For RGG 119, we measure a BH mass of 2.1( 0.5) 10^ 5 M_ from the SDSS spectroscopy.

The MagE observation yields a BH mass of 2.9( 0.2) 10^ 5 M_ , and the DIS observation gives a BH mass of 3.6( 0.3) 10^ 5 M_ . We compute a mean BH mass of M_ BH = 2.9( 0.6) 10^ 5 M_ using the three spectroscopic observations. Using the MagE spectrum of RGG 119 we measure a stellar velocity dispersion of 28 6 from the Mg b triplet, and place this galaxy on the M _ BH _ relation (Figure msig ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies RGG 119 as a composite dwarf AGN candidate with enough Chandra counts for spectral fitting, finding a soft power law and little intrinsic absorption.

quotes.quote: Two sources (RGG 119 and 127) had enough counts (n 100 ) to warrant spectral analysis (Figure spec ). We use the Sherpa fitting package in CIAO to model the extracted spectra using an absorbed power law model. Since it is possible to have both Galactic absorption and absorption intrinsic to the galaxy itself, we fit the spectra two ways: with n_ H frozen to the Galactic value, and with n_ H constrained to be at least the Galactic value. We find that the spectrum of RGG 119 has a best fit power law index of =2.25 0.25 . The models did not prefer an n_ H greater than the galactic value, suggesting little-to-no intrinsic absorption.
Despite the general trend, RGG 119 and RGG 127, i.e. the two objects with the highest L_ bol /L_ Edd , have _ OX consistent with their ``expected" values.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID 6 as a composite dwarf galaxy with nuclear X-ray emission and WISE mid-infrared emission supporting an accreting massive black hole interpretation.

quotes.quote: The X-ray sources are almost certainly associated with the peak of the NIR emission for the four BPT AGNs (IDs 1-3,5) and one composite galaxy (ID 6) with X-ray detections. In these cases, the positions of the X-ray sources are consistent with prominent nuclei and there are no other obvious alternative optical/ NIR counterparts.

There is strong evidence that the X-ray sources detected in the five optically-selected BPT AGN and Composite galaxies (IDs 1,2,3,5,6) are indeed accreting massive BHs in the nuclei of their host galaxies. The X-ray luminosities are well above the expected contribution from XRBs, and the positions of the X-ray sources are consistent with prominent nuclei, the peak of the NIR emission in the HST images, and the centroid of the mid-IR emission from WISE (see Figure fig:rgb ). It is thus reasonable to attribute the detected mid-IR emission from WISE to these optically-selected AGNs.

sources.summary: RGG 119 is modeled as an S0 galaxy with a small stellar ring, a compact high-Sersic classical bulge, and a lower-Sersic outer component.

quotes.quote: RGG 119 (Figure fig:RGG119_Model ) is an S0 galaxy with evidence of a small stellar ring seen in the residuals. The inner component has a Sersic index of n 2.5 with a half-light radius of 0.17 kpc. The relatively high Sersic index, round profile and dominance of the inner component at small radii clearly place this feature in the classical bulge category. The outer component has a Sersic index of n 0.9 with half-light radius of 1.02 kpc.

gmw_00000927.name: IC 860
gmw_00000927.overview: IC 860 is a nearby barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2208.08379] classified as a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2208.08379, arXiv:2210.04499] in a post-starburst transition phase [arXiv:2208.08379]. It features a compact, heavily dust-obscured nucleus [arXiv:2208.08379, arXiv:2210.04499] that drives a multiphase, galaxy-wide outflow [arXiv:2208.08379] and exhibits a chemically rich molecular environment [arXiv:1101.2122, arXiv:1101.1751].

sources.summary: The paper groups IC 0860 among extreme [CII]-deficient galaxies, noting undetected major cooling lines, normal gas components, and compact radio/infrared emission.

quotes.quote: In the extreme cases of NGC 4418, IC 0860 and CGCG 1510.8+0725, [ CII ] emission is undetected at the 3 level of 2cii =5-0.5 10^ 4 . Emission in the other prominent PDR cooling line [ OI ] at 63 is undetected at similar levels, as are [ OIII ] ( 88 ) and [ NII ] ( 122 ). In Figure 1 we show 3 upper limits on the flux in the [ CII ] 158 transition. The absence of [ CII ] emission in these galaxies is very striking because the upper limits on the in these galaxies are up to 40 times smaller than the narrow range in measured for two-thirds of the sample. Yet these galaxies have normal atomic and molecular gas components.

They have among the highest / luminosity ratio and dust temperature f/ 100 in the sample, but are not ultraluminous IR sources. Their FIR luminosities vary from Log( L_ FIR / )=10.05-10.24. The mid-infrared images at 7 and 15 are unresolved at 7 resolution of ISOCAM (Silbermann et al. 1997) and they are compact in radio continuum at 1.4 GHz (Condon et al 1990): NGC 4418 measures 0.5'' 0.3''(32 ) , CGCG 1510.8+0725 measures 0.5'' 0.5'' (85 ) and IC 860 0.4'' 0.3 '' (75 ) .

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 860 as a nearby post-starburst LIRG with a dusty multiphase outflow, barred spiral structure, heavy obscuration, and outflowing gas that likely remains bound.

quotes.quote: We present a multiwavelength study of IC 860, a nearby post-starburst galaxy at the early stage of transitioning from blue and star-forming to red and quiescent. Optical images reveal a galaxy-wide, dusty outflow originating from a compact core. We find evidence for a multiphase outflow in the molecular and neutral gas phase from the CO position-velocity diagram and NaD absorption features. We constrain the neutral mass outflow rate to be 0.5 M _ / yr, and the total hydrogen mass outflow rate to be 12 M _ /yr. Neither outflow component seems able to escape the galaxy.

IC 860 is a nearby ( z = 0.0129 from SDSS DR7, Abazajian_2009 ) luminous infrared (IR) galaxy (LIRG) with L_ IR =10^ 11.14 \ L_ Chu_2017 . It is a late-type spiral galaxy with a bar at the center and appears to be heavily obscured by dust.

sources.summary: The survey identifies IC 860 as chemically rich, with the brightest HC3N emission in the sample and four detected HC3N lines, including vibrationally excited transitions.

quotes.quote: The brightest HC _3 N emission is found in IC 860, where we also detect the molecule in its vibrationally excited state.

A particularly rich chemistry is found in IC 860, whose spectrum shows bright emission lines of methanol and HC _3 N J =12--11. This detection of HC _3 N is the only one in the 112 GHz sample and will be further discussed in section sec:hc3n_ic860 .

In total we detected four emission lines of HC _3 N in IC 860, reported in Table tab:ic860 . In the 112 GHz band, we detected two transitions of the vibrationally excited state v _7 =1 ( e , f ), together with the J =12--11, v=0 line. In the 88 GHz band we detected the J =10--9, v=0 line, and we have a tentative detection of the v _7 =1 f , vibrationally excited transition.

sources.summary: The paper maps formaldehyde megamaser emission in IC 860, finding central and southeast components, continuum-linked maser structure, and velocities suggestive of interaction.

quotes.quote: The 4.83 GHz formaldehyde K _a = 1 emission in IC 860 - IRAS 13126 + 2452 has first been observed with the Arecibo Observatory with a peak flux density of 2.0 - 2.2 mJy Baan93 . A representative spectrum shows an asymmetric profile centred at 3860 with a total width of 160 as presented in Figure fig:IC860sd from Araya04 . IC 860 also exhibits HI absorption centreed at 3866 as well as a combined OH spectrum with absorption at 3850 and emission at 4000 Schmelz86 .

The formaldehyde line emission structure of IC 860 is presented in Figure fig:IC860mom0 as a zeroth-moment colour map overlaid on the radio continuum map. The moment map incorporates line emission features above a threshold of 0.8 mJy beam ^ 1 , while the rms in the line channel maps (away from the location of the source) is 0.31 mJy beam ^ 1 . The central line emission region extends 31 pc at PA = 130 with an additional extension to the east at PA = 270 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes IC 860 as a nearby compact obscured nucleus, finding ALMA molecular morphology suggesting rotation, infall, outflow, and abundant methanimine in its nucleus.

quotes.quote: Here we apply principal component analysis (PCA) tomography to high-resolution (0 06) ALMA observations at frequencies 245 to 265 GHz of the nearby CON (59 Mpc) IC 860. PCA is a technique to unveil correlation in the data parameter space, and we apply it to explore the morphological and chemical properties of species in our dataset. The leading principal components reveal morphological features in molecular emission that suggest a rotating, infalling disk or envelope, and an outflow analogous to those seen in Galactic protostars.

One particular molecule of astrochemical interest is methanimine ( ), a precursor to glycine, three transitions of which have been detected towards IC 860. We estimate the average \ column density towards the nucleus of IC 860 to be 10^ 17 cm ^ 2 , with an abundance exceeding 10^ 8 relative to molecular hydrogen, using the rotation diagram method and non-LTE radiative transfer models.
IC 860 is classified as a LIRG with log( L_ IR [ ] )=11.17, and a distance of 59 Mpc through use of H _0=75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 and adopting a flat cosmology _M=0.3 and _ =0.7 Sanders2003 .

sources.summary: The paper compares IC 860 to Zw 049.057 as a highly obscured galaxy and partial OH kilomaser, emphasizing absorption-dominated lines, high 4751 MHz optical depth, and a weaker kilomaser transition state.

quotes.quote: An interesting comparison can instead be made with IC 860, another highly obscured galaxy aal15 . It is a partial maser in which the 1665 and 1667 MHz lines are dominated by absorption sch86 but with enough emission to classify it as a kilomaser baa89b,hen90 . Together with the weak megamasers Zw 049.057 and IRAS 11506-3851, IC 860 is located in the region of OH absorbers, rather than emitters, in the plot by baa89b of infrared luminosity against the ratio of 25 and 100 m flux densities (Fig. 2 of baa89b . In the measurements reported by man13 , the apparent optical depth of the OH 4751 MHz line in IC 860 is even higher ( 0.2 ) than in Zw 049.057. This is possibly an indication that Zw 049.057 is in a transition phase between the megamasers and the kilomasers, with IC 860 on the weaker kilomaser side of this transition, and on its way to become a pure OH absorber in a process similar to the scenario suggested by hen90 .

sources.summary: The survey reports IC 860 among galaxies with vibrationally excited HC3N, first HC3N and HNC detections, and includes it as HC3N-rich because of its moderate HC3N/HNC ratio.

quotes.quote: Rotational lines of vibrationally excited HC _3 N have recently been discovered in a few galaxies ( NGC 4418 costagliola10a , Arp 220 martin10 , and IC 860 costagliola10b ), therefore showing that it is important to take both radiative and collisional excitation into consideration when interpreting HC _3 N line emission from IR luminous galaxies. New detections This is not only the first text to put together a survey of all extragalactic HC _3 N emission line data, but it also reports the first HC _3 N detections in six galaxies: Circinus , IC 860 , IRAS 17208-0014 , Maffei 2 , NGC 1068 , and NGC 3079 . Three of the HNC detections are also made in sources without earlier HNC detections: Circinus , IC 860 , and IRAS 17208-0014 . We also choose to include IC 860 , considering that its moderate HC _3 N/HNC ratio is for the HC _3 N 28 27 transition, as the higher transitions seem to be weaker than the J= 10 9 line in most galaxies where more than one line has been observed (when beam effects are compensated for).

sources.summary: The paper reports first NH3 absorption toward IC 860 and finds poorly constrained, high-temperature dense gas components.

quotes.quote: IC 860: The NH _3 absorption in this galaxy, which is to our knowledge the first reported, corresponds spectrally to the H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 emission and 2_ 11 2_ 12 absorption components Mangum2013 . The background continuum source size is estimated to be 0.5^ see and references therein Condon1990 . The LVG-derived kinetic temperature fit to our ortho- and para-NH _3 (1,1) through (7,7) measurements is poorly constrained due to the influence of multiple high-temperature components. The (5,5)/(7,7) ratio implies a kinetic temperature 250 K, while all other line ratios are best described by T _K in the range 206 79 K.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 860 as having an extremely compact submillimeter source and extended HCN emission, with nuclear infrared luminosity reaching 30-50 percent of the galaxy total.

quotes.quote: IC 860 : Recent interferometric observations have revealed an extremely compact source at (sub)millimeter wavelengths, with equivalent R_ out =(13-14.5) pc aal19 ; however, the HCN _2=1^f J=3-2 line is more extended, R_ out 20 pc, suggesting that the infrared emission has at least a similar size. We have nevertheless adopted the conservative range R_ out =(14.5-20) pc and thus =(0.8-1.5) 10^ 2 arc sec ^2 . Using the HCN flux of 4 Jy km s ^ 1 by , we infer F_ HCN 2 3-2 / =260-500 Jy km s ^ 1 arc sec ^ 2 , which is mostly consistent with _ IR 5.5 10^7 L _ pc ^ 2 (Fig. hcngreenhousecolH2 a). The infrared luminosity of the nucleus is then (4-7) 10^ 10 , 30-50 of the total infrared luminosity of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports agreement between GBT and prior 1_10-1_11 H2CO emission measurements for IC 860, adds a new 2_11-2_12 absorption result, and cites CO size constraints.

quotes.quote: IC 860: The 1_ 10 1_ 11 emission line was detected by Baan1993 and Araya2004 , and the GBT detection shows good agreement in line properties (Figure fig:NGC3628IC860M83IR15107FormSpec ). Our discovery of H _2 CO 2_ 11 2_ 12 absorption is a new result. 15^ resolution CO J= 2-1 and 1-0 emission measurements Yao2003 provide an estimate of the maximum source size.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 860 as an unusual interacting/starburst-like galaxy with two CLASS detections, but weak Chandra evidence for an AGN.

quotes.quote: SDSS J131503.51+243707.7 (IC 860, MCG +04-31-015) Morphology: S? (NED) Two CLASS detections, with the brightest (10.3 mJy) offset from the SDSS position by 160 mas. IC 860 has been found to be somewhat unusual Kazes1988 , with behaviour described as typical of both an interacting and a starburst galaxy. The weak Chandra detection ( Lehmer2010 placed an upper limit of log L_ 2-10 keV = 40.19 erg s^ 1 ) fails to support the presence of an AGN.

gmw_00000928.name: Reticulum II
gmw_00000928.overview: Reticulum II is a nearby ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1503.02320, arXiv:2203.16440] characterized by a low stellar mass [arXiv:2106.13383] and a high mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:2306.04804]. Its stellar population is highly enriched with r-process elements [arXiv:2007.00749, arXiv:2108.03814], suggesting a rare, early nucleosynthetic event such as a neutron-star merger [arXiv:2007.00749, arXiv:2302.09188].

sources.summary: The paper reports a Fermi-LAT gamma-ray excess toward the dwarf galaxy Reticulum II and discusses its dark-matter interpretation, significance, and favorable location.

quotes.quote: We present a search for ray emission from the direction of the newly discovered dwarf galaxy Reticulum II. Using Fermi-LAT data, we detect a signal that exceeds expected backgrounds between 2-10 GeV and is consistent with annihilation of dark matter for particle masses less than a few 10^2 . Modeling the background as a Poisson process based on Fermi-LAT diffuse models, and taking into account trials factors, we detect emission with p value less than 9.8 10^ 5 ( >3.7 ). An alternative, model-independent treatment of background reduces the significance, raising the p value to 9.7 10^ 3 ( 2.3 ). Even in this case, however, Reticulum II has the most significant ray signal of any known dwarf galaxy.

One of these, Reticulum II (RetII), at a distance of 30 kpc, is the nearest dwarf galaxy after Segue 1 (Seg1, 23 kpc) and Sagittarius (24 kpc). RetII is 3 times more luminous than Seg1, suggesting that its dark matter halo may be more massive than Seg1's and making RetII an attractive place to search for a dark matter annihilation signal. Reticulum II occupies a near-ideal location for ray analysis: 49.7 below the Galactic plane and far from known ray emitting sources (the closest source in the 3rd Fermi Catalog is 2.9 away).

sources.summary: The paper identifies Reticulum II as a robust southern dSph with one of the largest J-factors; it dominates the southern stack, and a minispike improves detection prospects.

quotes.quote: The choice of these two dSPhs is dictated by the robustness of the J-factor against uncertainties . Reticulum II, in addition to being robust against uncertainties has one the largest J-factors among all dSPhs listed in Tab. tab:dwarf . In the Northern sky, the largest J-factors are provided by Ursa Major II and Willman I; however these ultra faint galaxies possess either a velocity field indicating strong tidal disruption effects from the Milky Way or nonequilibrium kinematics. Given its proximity and large DM content, Reticulum II seems to be one of the most promising objects for WIMP annihilation searches.

By playing the same game with the dSPhs in the Southern hemisphere, which can give rise to up-going muons in a water detector such as KM3NeT, we see that the main contribution comes from Reticulum II (the flux from the stack of dSPhs coincides with the flux produced by Reticulum II, gray points). Since there are only a few known dSphs in the Southern Sky, the flux is rather small and below the sensitivity of KM3NeT. It should be noted however that the 68 CL J-factor uncertainty ranges from 6.71 10^ 19 GeV ^2 cm ^ 5 to 1.14 10^ 21 GeV ^2 cm ^ 5 , which implies an uncertainty in the flux of roughly a factor 2. The inclusion of a minispike in Draco or Reticulum II ameliorates the prospects for detection considerably (first two panels, bottom left).

sources.summary: Reticulum II is confirmed as a very metal-poor ultra-faint dwarf galaxy; nine member-star abundances span its metallicity range, with seven strongly neutron-capture enriched stars and unusual chemical evolution.

quotes.quote: We present chemical abundances derived from high-resolution Magellan/MIKE spectra of the nine brightest known red giant members of the ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Reticulum II. These stars span the full metallicity range of Ret II ( 3.5 < [ Fe/H ] < -2 ). Seven of the nine stars have extremely high levels of process material ( [ Eu/Fe ] 1.7 ), in contrast to the extremely low neutron-capture element abundances found in every other ultra-faint dwarf galaxy studied to date. The other two stars are the most metal-poor stars in the system ( [ Fe/H ] < -3 ), and they have neutron-capture element abundance limits similar to those in other ultra-faint dwarf galaxies. We confirm that the relative abundances of Sr, Y, and Zr in these stars are similar to those found in process halo stars but 0.5 dex lower than the solar process pattern.

Reticulum II (henceforth ) is a UFD recently discovered in the Dark Energy Survey Koposov15a,Bechtol15 . Its velocity dispersion and metallicity spread confirm it to be a galaxy, and it is one of the most metal-poor galaxies known Simon15,Walker15,Koposov15b . At only 30 kpc away, it contains stars within the reach of high-resolution spectroscopy for abundance analysis.

sources.summary: The paper treats Reticulum II as an ultra-faint, dark-matter-dominated dwarf spheroidal and reports deep radio observations aimed at diffuse emission from dark matter processes.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The total observing time amounts to 30 hours and the RetII pointing was centered at RA= 03 : 35 : 41 and DEC= 54 : 03 : 00 .

quotes.quote: The dwarf galaxy Reticulum II (RetII) was discovered in 2015 in first year Dark Energy Survey data . Spectroscopic follow-­ups confirmed that the object is an ultra­-faint dSph galaxy, satellite of the Milky Way. They found RetII to be strongly DM dominated with a measured velocity dispersion around 3.5 km/s and a mass­-to­-light ratio within its half­-light radius around 500 M_ /L_ . RetII is one of the most metal-­poor galaxies known with a mean metallicity of [ Fe/H ] <­-2.5 . Moreover, recent chemical abundance determinations for the nine brightest red giant members of RetII surprisingly indicate high levels of process material , differently from every other ultra­-faint dSph, making RetII a unique target.

We performed deep radio observations around the direction of RetII with a primary beam covering 23.7 arcmin. Data have been collected with the Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) operating at 16 cm wavelength with an rms sensitivity around 10 Jy. We adopted a compact setup, optimal to test the presence of a diffuse radio continuum signal on the scale of a few arcminutes.

sources.summary: The review highlights Ret II as an ultra-faint dwarf with extreme r-process enrichment, likely from one rare early event, with neutron-star merger enrichment favored.

quotes.quote: Rare Enrichment Events in Ultra-Faint Dwarfs Perhaps the biggest surprise from chemical abundance measurements in UFDs was the recent discovery of extreme enrichment of -process elements in Reticulum II ji16,roederer16 . Out of nine Ret II stars for which high-resolution spectra have been obtained, seven have Eu/Fe > 0.9 and Ba/Fe > 0.3 ji16 . From the full sample of previously-studied ultra-faint dwarf stars, none have Ba/Fe > 0 and only one (an s-process-enhanced binary in Segue 1) has Eu/Fe > 0 . In fact, the Segue 1 binary, which was presumably contaminated with heavy elements by a companion that went through an AGB phase, was the only previous star in which Eu had been detected at all. Compared to most stars in UFDs, Ret II is enriched in Sr and Eu by a factor of >30 , and in Ba by a factor of 100 .

The only viable explanation for the chemical abundances of Ret II is that a single nucleosynthetic event early in the history of the galaxy produced a large quantity of -process material ( 10^ 5 \ of Eu; ji16 ). Given that nine other UFDs had been observed prior to Ret II, and no sign of significant -process enrichment was detected in any of them, whatever produced the neutron-capture elements in Ret II must have been a rare occurrence. Ret II does not have a large stellar mass among UFDs, so it is very unlikely that an event would take place multiple times in Ret II and never in any other similar galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses Reticulum II as a key ultra-faint dwarf with excess r-process elements, comparing its enrichment to neutron-star-binary merger models and finding persistent tension.

quotes.quote: Among the smallest dwarfs, called ultra-faint dwarf galaxies (UFD), Reticulum II Ji2016,Roederer2016,Ji2016a and very likely also Tucana III Drlica2015,Simon2017,Hansen2017,Marshall2018 show a large excess of process elements, while for all the other UFDs robust upper limits on their process element abundances have been set Frebel2010A,Simon2010,Koch2013,Francois2016 . The presence of process elements in UFDs poses serious challenges to NSB mergers as origin of the process nucleosynthesis elements. First, these galaxies have low escape velocities Walker2015 . The kick imparted to any newly born NS by its CCSN explosion could potentially eject the NS from the galaxy, even when the stellar binary system survives the second supernova explosion.

This cause a shift of at most 0.5 dex for m_ Eu , easing the tension, but without definitively solving it. Therefore, despite the large uncertainties that affect the followed procedure, our results even in the most optimistic case (right panel of Fig. fig:mFe_mEu , x=50 case), might imply a possible tension with the scenario in which europium enrichment is only generated by NSB mergers. In Fig. fig:el_abu we provide histograms of [ Eu/Fe ] for all our four dwarf models. Once again, results for models 1,2, and 3 are systematically smaller than the values required to explain abundances in Reticulum II and in classical dwarfs, as reported in table 1 of Beniamini2016b by at least one dex, even in the most optimistic case x=50 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Reticulum II to forecast dark-matter radio and gamma-ray signals, adopting dwarf-galaxy density, gas, magnetic-field, and observing-region assumptions for MeerKAT and SKA.

quotes.quote: Here, we display a comparison between the sensitivity of the MeerKAT telescope and the predicted emissions for our constrained DM model using the Reticulum II dwarf galaxy. The choice of this target is motivated by the fact that it is in an ideal location for southern hemisphere observations and has one of the highest `J-factors' among known dwarf galaxies . In fitting with our AMS-02 constraints from Fig. fig:ams2Params_Ein , we assume V = 10^ 25 cm ^3 s ^ 1 and include uncertainties on the value of _ . We make use of a cored density profile following arguments from Ref. . This being an Einasto profile (which can be cored for certain parameter choices), given by: _ e ()= _ s 2 r_s ^ 1 , where we follow Ref. in having = 0.4 , r_s = 0.2 kpc, and _s = 7 10^7 M _ kpc ^ 3 .

Fig. fig:meerKATSpectrumEin shows that Reticulum II with the Einasto profile produces radio fluxes detectable at 2 confidence interval with MeerKAT across the mass range (although the 2 2 200 GeV case is very marginal). Only the lower masses are detectable at 5 . However, the entire uncertainty band is not covered in either case. It should noted that the 2 3 case presents slightly stronger emissions. Importantly, we also display the estimated sensitivity of the full SKA which manages to probe the entire uncertainty region for both 2 2 and 2 3 processes at 5 confidence interval within 100 hours of observing time.

sources.summary: The paper reviews Reticulum II structural measurements, explains the ACS field was too small for new fitting, and summarizes multiple spectroscopic studies supporting its dwarf-galaxy classification.

quotes.quote: Reticulum II From the DES data, in their discovery papers, measured an r_h of 3 6 ( a_ h, exp 5 7 ) and measured an a_ h, p 6 . fit slightly smaller values of a_ h, exp 5 4 and a_ h, p 5 5 to the same DES data. used deeper (by 2-3 mags) data from Megacam to fit an a_ h, exp 6 3 , in agreement with the original measurement. The a_ h, p from DES DR1 was slightly larger, at 6 6 .

We did not fit structural parameters for Ret II, as the ACS FOV covers approximately 0.25 a_h . Three independent spectroscopic studies of Ret II were published in the months following the discovery papers simon2015,walker2015,koposov2015a , all concluding that Ret II had the kinematic and spectroscopic properties consistent with being a dwarf galaxy.

This large metallicity dispersion would strongly support a galaxy classification. They note the large discrepancy with past spectroscopic studies and invite follow-up observations for better membership determination.

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs Reticulum II dark-matter annihilation and decay profiles, finding a very large J factor and emphasizing its importance for indirect dark-matter searches.

quotes.quote: In this work, we reconstruct the dark matter annihilation ( J factor) and decay profiles for the newly discovered dSph Reticulum II. Using an optimized spherical Jeans analysis of kinematic data obtained from the Michigan/Magellan Fiber System (M2FS), we find Reticulum II's J factor to be among the largest of any Milky Way dSph. We have checked the robustness of this result against several ingredients of the analysis. Unless it suffers from tidal disruption or significant inflation of its velocity dispersion from binary stars, Reticulum II may provide a unique window on dark matter particle properties.

In any case, a robust determination of Ret II's DM content is crucial in order to constrain particle nature of DM. Reticulum II was found to be a DM-dominated dSph galaxy from the independent chemodynamical analyses of kinematics , 2015arXiv150402889S and 2015arXiv150407916K . Here, we reconstruct the DM annihilation and decay emission profiles of Ret II from a spherical Jeans analysis applied to stellar kinematic data obtained with the Michigan/Magellan Fiber System (M2FS) kinematics .

sources.summary: Reticulum II is the only satellite with a 3.1-sigma 511 keV signal, which the paper argues is too bright for dark matter alone and may reflect star formation or a neutron-star merger.

quotes.quote: The strongest DSG signal that we find is from the position of Reticulum II (Ret II), with 3.1 significance. Its line flux is (17.0 5.4) 10^ 5 ph cm^ 2 s^ 1 . However, we caution that Ret II 511 keV emission may be too intense a signal to be interpreted as due to DM alone (see Discussion below).

The ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Ret II Koposov2015_dsph,Simon2015_dsph_RetII is found with a significance of 3.1 . This is tantalising evidence for a bright source of positrons in Ret II, and among the other DSGs, Ret II might be special from the perspective of two different, maybe unrelated, measurements: Ji2016_RetII measured strong enhancements of neutron-capture elements in stars of Ret II, and interpret this as the result of nucleosynthesis of heavy elements from a single enrichment event only, which then would have to be a neutron star merger. The same enrichment event could be a positron source, e.g. through evolving into an accreting black hole system, or else the existence of such neutron star binary also makes plausible the existence of a microquasar, producing s in flaring states.

The case of Reticulum II and the 511 keV signal from this galaxy cannot entirely be attributed to dark matter; other origins related to star formation or a single neutron star merger Geringer-Sameth2015_RetII,Ji2016_RetII , are thus more plausible.

sources.summary: The paper treats Reticulum II as the ultra-faint dwarf with unusually high r-process enrichment, arguing its Eu abundance points to one heavy r-process production event.

quotes.quote: Recently, and reported the first discovery of highly process enriched stars in a UFD, Reticulum II, while there are only strong upper limits on the abundances of process elements for some other UFDs frebel2010ApJ,frebel2014ApJ,roederer2014MNRAS . These measurements that show large fluctuations in the process abundance imply that only a single process event took place in Reticulum II.

It is worthwhile noting that the abundances of the process elements with atomic numbers of Z<40 , corresponding to A<90 , in Reticulum II are depleted compared to the solar ji2016 and similar to those of an extreme metal-poor star CS 22892-052 sneden2003ApJ . This suggests that there are two types of events, light and heavy process events, and only the latter took place in Reticulum II.

We find M_ Eu 10^ 5 M_ for Reticulum II. For the other UFDs there are strong upper limits, in some cases as small as 10^ 7 M_ . This led to suggest that there was only a single process production event in Reticulum II and none in the other UFDs. ht 0.9 The statistical method We aim to constrain the event rate and mass per event for both SNe and process events.

sources.summary: The paper uses Reticulum II as the observed UFD benchmark, arguing its high r-process abundances require a rare event and that centrally located neutron-star mergers can reproduce its stellar Eu pattern.

quotes.quote: Recently Ji:2016ja obtained high-resolution spectra of nine stars in the local UFD Reticulum II (Ret II), and found that SNcc are unable to account for the observed high -process element abundances in this galaxy. Instead, a rare event such as a NSM Tanvir:2013bt,Wallner:2015de or a magnetorotationally-driven supernova (SN) Winteler:2012fv,Wehmeyer:2015kl,Nishimura:2015fv is required to explain the observed high stellar abundances of Eu and Ba.

Our results show that a single NSM can lead to a distribution of stellar -process abundances similar to those observed in Ret II. In one of the two halos that we simulated, the NSM event took place at the center of the stellar distribution, leading to a high spatial correlation between the -process material and the supernovae ejecta. This not only lead to high-levels of -process enrichment such as seen in Ret II, but also the positive correlation between [ Eu/H ] and [ Fe/H ] . In the second halo, the NSM event took place away from the densest part of the galaxy, and the -process material expanded primarily into the low density ISM. In this case, the more extended and shallower -process distribution lead to stars that were on average about 2 dex under-abundant in europium as compared with Ret II stars.

sources.summary: The paper uses Reticulum II as a dwarf-spheroidal gamma-ray constraint on MSSM dark matter, adopting its total J-factor, reassessing the former Pass 7 excess, and enforcing consistency with Pass 8 limits.

quotes.quote: As has been mentioned in the introduction, the main astronomical inputs in our analysis come from ray data from the GC as well as the dwarf spheroidal galaxy Reticulum II .

For calculating the ray flux for Reticulum II, we use the value of the `total' J-factor, defined as J_ tot = J( ) d , found from the Jeans analysis . The value used is J_ tot = 10^ 19.6 GeV^ 2 ^ 5 , obtained on integration over the ROI = 2.4 10^ 4 sr corresponds to the angular radius 0.5 .

Till very recently, excess in the 1 - 10 GeV range was claimed by observations using the so-called Pass 7 data from Reticulum II . Beyond about 10 GeV, the error is too large for any meaningful fit. While such announcements generated considerable activity towards fitting such excess in terms of DM models including the MSSM , the later analyses, in terms of the Pass 8 data, disavowed such claims . Upper limits on the flux in the aforesaid range continued to exist, side by side with comparable limits from other dwarf spheroidal galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper finds the strongest weak gamma-ray excess toward Reticulum II, with rising TS over time, while noting the signal remains below statistical significance.

quotes.quote: The most significant signal appears in the direction of Reticulum II. For this dSph, in the case of ^ + ^ , the TS value of the fit peaks at m_ 16 GeV with TS_ peak 13.5 (12.2). For b b , the largest TS value is 10.9 (10.0) corresponding to the DM mass of m_ 90 GeV. The TS values in the brackets are obtained by using a point source to model Reticulum II.

Some previous works have also analyzed the gamma-ray emission from Reticulum II . Shortly after the DES Collaboration released their first group of dSph candidates, evidence of ray emission from Reticulum II was reported based on the analysis of Fermi-LAT P7REP data . However, the subsequent studies on this source with updated Pass 8 data of Fermi-LAT resulted in lower significances , and hence these authors did not claim excess from Reticulum II.

sources.summary: The paper models Reticulum II radial velocities with binary motion and dataset offsets, finding a high binary fraction, lower intrinsic dispersion, reduced annihilation J-factor, and strong evidence for dark-matter domination.

quotes.quote: We have applied a Bayesian method to model multi-epoch radial velocities in the ultra-faint dwarf Reticulum II, fully accounting for the effects of binary orbital motion and systematic offsets between different spectroscopic datasets. Our primary results are encapsulated in Figures fig:posts_offset and fig:sigposts , where we infer the intrinsic dispersion and binary fraction of Ret II.
