Despite the relatively small sample size (26 stars in total), we find that the binary fraction of Ret II is higher than 0.5 at the 90 confidence level, if the mean orbital period is assumed to be 30 years or longer. Despite this high binary fraction, the best-fit intrinsic dispersion of Ret II is 2.8 _ 1.2 ^ +0.7 km/s, consistent with Ret II having a large mass-to-light ratio. Our best-fit velocity dispersion is smaller than previous estimates and implies a weaker dark matter annihilation signal, with the J-factor reduced by a factor of 0.5 compared to the results of .

These results are thus consistent with Ret II being a dark matter-dominated galaxy to high significance, in agreement with the expectation from its large metallicity dispersion. Beyond the importance of obtaining unbiased mass estimates of ultra-faint dwarfs, binary populations in these objects are interesting in their own right as they may hold clues to star formation in extremely metal-poor environments.

sources.summary: The paper compares Ret II as an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy whose r-process-enhanced stars likely reflect pollution by one early nucleosynthetic event.

quotes.quote: The first of the DES-discovered ultra-faint dwarf galaxies to be kinematically confirmed as a dark matter-dominated ultra-faint dwarf galaxy was Reticulum II Ret II; simon_ret2, walker . The nine brightest confirmed member stars were subsequently chemically analyzed by and ; these authors showed that most of the stars in Ret II are strongly enhanced in the process elements, i.e., they are II stars. Since II stars are so rare in the Milky Way, it was particularly notable to have found a galaxy seemingly composed primarily of these types of stars. Even more interesting, the authors conclude that the high fraction of process enhanced stars must be due to Ret II's chemical history being dominated by a single nucleosynthetic event, most likely a binary neutron star merger.

The fact that so many stars in a galaxy as small as Ret II share a common chemical pattern suggests that a single nucleosynthetic event must have occurred early in the history of the galaxy, polluting future generations of stars, and that most of the stars in the galaxy were impacted by the event. The II stars in Ret II have an average enhancement in Eu of [ Eu/H ] 1 . used this level and an estimated dilution gas mass, i.e. the mass of hydrogen gas that the process material is diluted into, of Ret II of 10^6 M _ to argue that a binary neutron star merger was the most likely source of the excess of Eu detected in Ret II as other sources would not have produced enough Eu to enhance the galaxy to the level detected.

sources.summary: Reticulum II is used as the observed UFD benchmark: it has low stellar mass, many highly r-process enhanced stars, and simulations produce Ret II-like abundance systems only rarely.

quotes.quote: Given that the stellar masses of Ret II and Tuc III are M_ =2.6 0.2 10^3 ( Bechtol2015 ) and M_ =0.8 0.1 10^3 ( Drlica2015 ), respectively, such UFDs are less likely to host even a single NSM event during their SFHs. Another challenge is that some stars in select UFDs show significantly high -process element ratios ( Eu/Fe 1.0, [ Eu/H ] 1.0 ). In general, depending on the Eu/Fe ratio, -process enhanced metal-poor (MP) stars are conventionally divided into two subclasses, MP -I ( 0.3 < [ Eu/Fe ] < 1, [ Fe/H ] < -1.5 ) and MP -II ( Eu/Fe > 1, [ Fe/H ] < -1.5 ) Beers2005 . In particular, in Ret II, seven out of nine stars show extremely high levels of -process element ratios, Eu/Fe >1.7 (e.g. Ji2016b ).

Meanwhile, as shown in the upper-right panel of Figure 3, element abundances of stars in Halo 26 are in good agreement with those of Ret II. The estimated europium abundances, Eu/Fe and Eu/H , of the Pop II clusters are presented in the bottom panels of Figure 3. Out of 7 Pop II clusters, 6 (red inverse triangles) show extremely high europium abundances ( Eu/Fe 2.5, [ Eu/H ] >-0.6 ), comparable to MP -II stars in Ret II.

Adopting a simple toy model for NSMs, whose parameters are in line with those inferred from observations such as the NSM rate of a single NSM per 10^5 and the europium yield of M_ Eu 4.5 10^ 5 , we identify only one Ret II-like system ( M_ vir 4.6 10^7 , M_ 3.4 10^3 at z=0 ) out of 32 UFD analogs in LV2 . Meanwhile, no Ret II-like system is found in LV1 , where 8 UFD analogs emerge. This frequency is largely consistent with the observation that only 1 in 10 UFDs (Ret II) contains highly -process enhanced stars (e.g. Ji2016a ).

sources.summary: The paper searches HST member-star positions in Reticulum II for resolved wide binaries, finding a nearest-neighbor excess consistent with a small wide-binary fraction and constraining the IMF.

quotes.quote: By analyzing the positions of stars in Reticulum II (Ret II) from Hubble Space Telescope images, we search for angularly resolved wide binaries in Ret II. We find that the distribution of their NN distances shows an enhancement at projected separations of 1 relative to a model containing no binaries. We show that such an enhancement can be explained by a wide binary fraction of f_b 0.007^ +0.008 _ 0.003 at separations of more than 3000 AU. Under the assumption that the binary separation distribution is similar to that in the Milky Way, the total binary fraction in Ret II may be on the order of 50 . We also use the observed magnitude distribution of stars in Ret II to constrain the initial mass function over the mass range 0.34-0.78 , finding that a shallow power-law slope of 1.01 1.15 matches the data.

Ret II is located at a distance of 31.4 1.4 kpc MutluPakdil2018ApJ , and recent Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging of the galaxy provides a sample of 2600 member stars. Properties of Reticulum II Ret II is a typical UFD, with an absolute magnitude of M_ V = -3.1 ( L = 1500 L_ ) and a half-light radius of 58 pc MutluPakdil2018ApJ .

sources.summary: The paper treats Reticulum II as a prototypical ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal, using profile samples to propagate J-factor uncertainties for velocity-dependent dark-matter annihilation.

quotes.quote: For definiteness, we pick here two classical dSphs (Draco and Sculptor, respectively in the Northern and Southern sky) and one prototypical ultra-faint (Reticulum II), which were found to be among the best-ranked targets for DM annihilation in . In the latter studies, the DM profile parameters were reconstructed from a Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) engine coupled to a Jeans analysis with the CLUMPY code https://clumpy.gitlab.io/CLUMPY/ ; see for more details on the methodology. We use these chains to calculate the median profile, that we adopt as a reference for our analyses here We do not use the best-fit profile parameters, because the scarcity of data in ultra-faint dSphs make them display an unphysical behaviour (e.g., a very flat and extended profile). Using `effective' structural parameters matching the median profile cures this issue.

To properly and fully propagate the DM profile uncertainties to the generalised J factor, we start from 1000 profile parameter samples taken from the analysis of ( for Reticulum II), apply the Eddington calculation to obtain the phase-space associated to each profile (see also app:phase_space_modelling ), and then calculate the associated generalised J factor.

For s wave annihilation and in the absence of any Sommerfeld enhancement, the uncertainty on the reconstruction of the DM profile parameters leads to factors of a few for classical dSphs (Draco and Sculptor), and a factor 20 for the Reticulum II ultra-faint dSph (top-left panel of fig:JS_smooth_error_bars ). This is the same in the saturation regime, off resonance, where the Sommerfeld factor does not depend on the velocity. Yet, in the Coulomb regime, and at resonance peaks in the saturation regime, the additional velocity dependence goes in the opposite direction with respect to ^ 2 . For instance, for a given value of the scale radius r_ 2 , a larger value of _ 2 gives larger ^ 2 () but at the same time a larger typical velocity which enters the Sommerfeld factor through v ^ 1 or v ^ 2 , leading to a reduction of the generalised J factors. This leads to 68 uncertainty bands that are typically smaller in the Coulomb regime --- , less than an order of magnitude for Reticulum II --- than in the no-Sommerfeld case. For p wave annihilation (top-right panel of fig:JS_smooth_error_bars ), the uncertainty band spans about two orders of magnitude for Draco in the no-Sommerfeld regime, but `only' one for Sculptor. For Reticulum II, the uncertainty reaches almost four orders of magnitude, owing to the loose kinematic constraints that affect both ^ 2 and v_ rel ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper models Reticulum II as an ultra-faint LMC satellite selected for unusual early enrichment, comparing abundance diagrams and finding best matches for Mg/Fe and Al/Fe trends.

quotes.quote: In this work, we focus on modelling the chemical evolution of two spectroscopically confirmed UFDs -- Carina II (Car II) and Reticulum (Ret II), both satellites of the LMC Bechtol2015, patel2020, Battaglia2022 . Car II - along with Carina III - is generally believed to be very understood from the analysis in Li2018 and Ji2020 , whereas Ret II is chosen from its unique process enrichment early in its formation. We select these two satellite candidates among other UFDs for our analysis as their formation history is well recorded, as well as their chemical abundance measurements.

Ret II was discovered by Bechtol2015 from the analysis of the Dark Energy Survey (DES) data. It has an estimated stellar mass M_ 10^ 3 M _ Sales2017, sacchi2021 . By fitting the observed CMD with the PARSEC library of stellar isochrones Bressan2012 , Bechtol2015 predicted that Ret II stellar populations have an average age 13.5 Gyr and metallicity Z 10^ 4 , making this UFD galaxy one of the oldest and most metal-poor structures in the known Universe.

The best match to observational data is obtained for the [ Mg/Fe ] [ Fe/H ] and [ Al/Fe ] [ Fe/H ] abundance diagrams, in which we predict a knee at [ Fe/H ] 3 , corresponding to the time when the first SNe Ia are expected to explode in the system.

sources.summary: The paper describes Reticulum II as a nearby ultra-faint dwarf with low metallicity, high mass-to-light ratio, strong r-process enrichment, and early inhomogeneous chemical mixing.

quotes.quote: Reticulum II ( ) is one of the closest ultra faint dwarf galaxies (UFD; M _ V = -2.7 ) to the Milky Way, discovered by the Dark Energy Survey DES; Diehl2014 in the southern hemisphere (RA =53.77 , DEC= 54.05 , J2000). Low resolution spectroscopy of 17 25 members Koposov2015, Walker2015, Simon2015 found that has a low mean metallicity ( [ Fe/H ] =-2.6 In this paper we refer to both the ``absolute'' abundance, X 12 + _ 10 ( N_ X /N_ H ) , and abundances in bracket notation relative to the sun, e.g., X _ 10 ( N_ X /N_ Fe )_ * _ 10 ( N_ X /N_ Fe )_ ) with a significant metallicity dispersion ( [ Fe/H ] 0.5 ). Furthermore, examination of the radial velocities showed a small velocity dispersion ( _ V 3.5 ), resulting in a high mass-to-light ratio ( >450 M _ /L _ ), typical of the UFDs. The first examination of the chemical abundances in from high resolution spectra showed that 7 (of 9) red giants are highly enriched in -process elements Ji2016Nature , a pattern reinforced by more detailed analyses which showed the -process abundance pattern matches the well-studied metal-poor halo stars Ji2016, Roederer2016 .

As is an UFD that appears to have undergone relatively little star formation, it is a unique system that can also be used to study metal mixing and enrichment in a very low mass system at very early epochs. Detailed spectral analyses of old stars in classical dwarf galaxies and UFDs has revealed that there is inhomogeneous mixing in these systems e.g., Venn2012, Leaman2012, Norris2017 , which is also supported by in low mass galaxy simulations e.g., Revaz2012, Romano2015, Corlies2018, Applebaum2020, Applebaum2021 . Recent analyses of the two UFDs, Car II and Ret II, by Alexander2023 have incorporated inhomogeneous mixing as driven by SN explosions. Chemical enrichment from the supernova bubbles spreads in the interstellar medium, causing dispersion in the elemental abundances, and driving galactic outflows that quench the star formation activity at early times. For Ret II, they predict a fairly large average outflow mass-loading factor, 10^3 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses Reticulum II as a newly discovered dwarf galaxy with a reported gamma-ray excess, omitted from the stacked dSph analysis but used for contextual lifetime limits.

quotes.quote: We point out that recently a ray excess has been claimed for a newly discovered dwarf galaxy Reticulum II . The Fermi LAT collaboration has independently performed a similar analysis that indicates that the excess above 100 MeV is merely a statistical fluctuation of the background, since no surplus of photons is observed in the remaining dwarf galaxies . The origin of this ray emission is unclear, especially because the two groups used different datasets, and their conclusion concerning the chance of a background fluctuation mimicking the potential dark matter signal differs.

For these reasons we have omitted Reticulum II from the stacked analysis, but as a contextual note we obtained the limits on the dark matter lifetime, exhibited in Fig. Limitsreti , stemming from Reticulum II using the upper flux reported in with the J-factor presented in the Table I. It is clear that there is anomalous behavior for b b , which provides a very good fit to the excess seen in for DM masses around dozens of GeV. The limit arising from Reticulum-II is still weaker than our combined one.

sources.summary: The paper studies Reticulum 2 as a newly discovered ultra-faint dwarf galaxy, measuring bright red giants and confirming unusually high r-process material in three stars.

quotes.quote: The ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Reticulum 2 (Ret 2) was recently discovered in images obtained by the Dark Energy Survey. We have observed the four brightest red giants in Ret 2 at high spectral resolution using the Michigan/Magellan Fiber System. We present detailed abundances for as many as 20 elements per star, including 12 elements heavier than the Fe group. We confirm previous detection of high levels of process material in Ret 2 (mean [ Eu/Fe ] = + 1.69 0.05) found in three of these stars (mean [ Fe/H ] = - 2.88 0.10).

The fourth star in Ret 2 ( [ Fe/H ] = - 3.42 0.20) contains only trace amounts of Sr ( [ Sr/Fe ] = - 1.73 0.43) and no detectable heavier elements. One of these, Reticulum 2 ( ), is the nearest ( 30 kpc) ultra-faint dwarf galaxy (UFD; M_V = - 2.7) to the Milky Way in the southern hemisphere.

sources.summary: Reticulum II is analyzed with Gaia DR2 and literature radial velocities; its spectroscopic members trace the overall population and its proper motion agrees with earlier measurements.

quotes.quote: We present mean absolute proper motion measurements for seven ultra-faint dwarf galaxies orbiting the Milky Way, namely Bo o tes III, Carina II, Grus II, Reticulum II, Sagittarius II, Segue 2 and Tucana IV. For four of these dwarfs our proper motion estimate is the first ever provided. The adopted astrometric data come from the second data release of the Gaia mission. We determine the mean proper motion for each galaxy starting from an initial guess of likely members, based either on radial velocity measurements or using stars on the Horizontal Branch identified in the Gaia ( G_ BP G_ RP , G) colour-magnitude diagram in the field of view towards the UFD.

On the other hand, the VPD distribution of spectroscopic members for Carina II and Reticulum II seems to represent well the overall population as determined by our procedure. In fact, our results match within 1 those in , being ( _ , _ ) = ( 1.81 0.08 , 0.14 0.08 ) for Carina II and ( _ , _ ) = ( 2.34 0.12 , 1.31 0.13 ) for Reticulum II.

sources.summary: The review highlights Reticulum II as a DES-discovered dwarf spheroidal with comparatively secure classification, high dark-matter content, no clear stripping, and a small non-significant gamma-ray excess.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:retII shows the CMD for Reticulum II, a Milky Way satellite recently discovered by DES. Of the above dSphs with smaller data samples, the object with probably the least uncertainty in its astronomical classification is Reticulum II, which was discovered in the DES first year data. The velocity dispersion of this system is 3-4 km/s , which implies a dark matter mass 500 times larger than the stellar mass within the half light radius.

Further, there appears to be no sign of tidal stripping of the stars. Annihilation and decay factors for Reticulum II have been calculated by several groups, which are consistent for similar assumptions for theoretical priors . Reticulum II is also interesting because it has a small associated gamma-ray excess, though this is not statistically significant .

sources.summary: The paper treats Reticulum II as a highly r-process-enhanced ultra-faint dwarf whose stars preserve a single enrichment signature and show high lanthanide fractions.

quotes.quote: Another useful comparison is to the of stars in process-enriched ultra-faint dwarf galaxies. The stars in these galaxies preserve a pure signature of an individual process enrichment event Ji16b . So far, two such galaxies have been found: the highly enhanced galaxy Reticulum II Ji16b,Roederer16b and the moderately enhanced galaxy Tucana III Hansen17,Marshall18 .

We computed for 7 stars in Reticulum II Ji16c and 4 stars in Tucana III Marshall18 the same way as the halo star samples, using the Arnould07 solar pattern. We find =-1.12 0.13 for Reticulum II and =-1.50 0.10 for Tucana III. These are shown in the center panel of Figure f:histogram_samples , and they tend to have higher than halo stars with comparable [ Eu/Fe ] .

sources.summary: The paper identifies Ret 2 as likely accreted with the LMC, notes it requires the highest LMC mass among satellites, and disagrees with a previous velocity-based exclusion.

quotes.quote: We study the orbits of ultra-faint dwarf galaxies in the combined presence of the Milky Way and LMC and we find 6 dwarfs which were likely accreted with the LMC (Car 2, Car 3, Hor 1, Hyi 1, Phe 2, Ret 2), in addition to the SMC, representing strong evidence of dwarf galaxy group infall. Of all the satellites, Ret 2 requires the highest LMC mass, 9.6 10^ 10 M_ . Of their 7 highest confidence candidates ( p_ LMC > 0.7 ), we confirm that Hor 1, Ret 2, and Phe 2 are likely LMC satellites.

They also identified Ret 2, Tuc 2, and Gru 1 as inconsistent with the LMC at the 3 level in velocity. However, our analysis suggests that Ret 2 is likely to be an LMC satellite. In addition to the SMC, we identify six dwarf galaxies as being highly likely bound to the Cloud previously: Car 2, Car 3, Hor 1, Hyi 1, Phe 2, and Ret 2.

sources.summary: The paper models Reticulum II as an ultrafaint dwarf whose high europium abundance is reproduced by a central neutron-star merger followed by prolonged star formation and mixing.

quotes.quote: Recent observations show that two UFDs contain stellar populations with peculiar elemental abundances. Some stars in Reticulum II (Ret II) and Tucana III (Tuc III) show high europium (Eu) abundances, suggesting early -process enrichment events Ji16_RetII, Hansen17_TucIII , but it remains unknown why only these two galaxies show clear -process element signatures.

We show that the observed europium abundance in Reticulum II is reproduced by our inner explosion model where a NSM is triggered at the centre of the galaxy, whereas the relatively low abundance in Tucana III is reproduced if a NSM occurs near the virial radius of the progenitor galaxy. The latter case is realised only if the neutron-star binary has a large natal kick velocity and travels over a long distance of a kilo-parsec before merger. In both the inner and outer explosion cases, it is necessary for the progenitor galaxy to sustain prolonged star formation over a few hundred million years after the NSM, so that the dispersed -process elements are well mixed within the inter-stellar medium. Short-duration star formation results in inefficient mixing, and then a large variation is imprinted in the stellar europium abundances, which is inconsistent with the observations of Reticulum II and Tucana III.
Note that the [ Eu/H ] is as large as the mean abundance of Ret II (-0.82 if we exclude the stars with non-detection, and -0.93 if we include them).

sources.summary: The paper treats Reticulum II as a dwarf-galaxy dark-matter halo target, adopting a Burkert profile and comparing predicted d* decay spectra to radio and gamma-ray limits.

quotes.quote: In particular we will explore several halos as target environments: the Coma cluster of galaxies, the Milky-Way galactic centre (GC), the Reticulum II dwarf galaxy, and M31 or the Andromeda galaxy. All of these environments have been previously used/proposed as targets in indirect DM searches  in a variety of frequency bands from radio to gamma-rays. Barring the Reticulum II dwarf galaxy, all of the chosen targets have been the subject of extensive astrophysical study and thus have relatively well characterised environments in terms of gas densities and magnetic field profiles  .

Reticulum II The Reticulum II dwarf galaxy is taken to have a Burkert density profile following arguments from . This profile has r_s = 0.139 kpc  and is normalised to the annihilation J-factor 8 10^ 18 GeV ^2 cm ^ 5 found in within 0.5^ of the galaxy centre. In Reticulum II we follow in using the profiles for gas density and magnetic field strength: n_e () = n_0 r_d , and B () = B_0 r_d .

sources.summary: The paper describes Reticulum II as an ultrafaint dwarf laboratory for early enrichment, comparing metal-poor stellar abundances to inhomogeneous BBN models and noting possible neutron-capture signatures.

quotes.quote: In particular, the nearby ultrafaint dwarf galaxies Reticulum II and Tucana III are fascinating laboratories for studying the evolution of and enrichment by the first stars in the universe. Unlike typical dwarfs, its process history demonstrates a lack of complete mixing of supernovae ejecta with the ambient gas in its early gas-rich phase as compared to most other dwarfs . In Fig. fig:comparisons we highlight the comparison of abundances obtained with AlterBBN at high to metal-poor stars observed in the dwarf galaxy Reticulum II (purple areas) and in Tucana III (orange areas). In these galaxies, which are highly dark matter-dominated, there are hints of an enrichment in process elements which in the absence of the most established astrophysical explanation, namely neutron star binaries , could possibly correspond to a primordial component.

A test of this would be to look for neutron-capture signatures that anti-correlate with the most common process product, Europium, and that is relatively unenhanced in our inhomogeneous BBN models We had to specifically extract the Europium data from the complete set of AlterBBN data since its abundance does not exceed the lower cut-offs we had set. , as highlighted in Fig. fig:europium . Europium suffers from an observable bias due to its very low abundance in metal-poor stars, unless it is enhanced as in Reticulum II or Tucana III, thus the comparison between different metal-poor stars populations may be flawed.

sources.summary: The paper cites Reticulum II as an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy whose r-process-enhanced stars record early heavy-element enrichment, support neutron-star-merger nucleosynthesis, and resemble abundance patterns in r-process halo stars.

quotes.quote: Metal-poor stars enhanced in \ elements have been found in both the Milky Way halo (e.g., rpa1 , rpa2 and roederer2018a ) as well as dwarf galaxies, most notably the process ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Reticulum II (Ret II) ji2016Nat,roederer2016 . These stars formed from gas that was previously enriched by a nucleosynthesis event during which heavy process elements were made. Altogether, they thus provide the best accessible evidence for the early operation of the process, as many process elements can be detected in their spectra, and the corresponding chemical abundances be measured.

Together with chemical-evolution models of the Galaxy that implement NS-NS mergers cescutti2015,wehmeyer2015,cote2017 and the existence of Reticulum II, these lines of evidence support the hypothesis that NS mergers are likely a dominant site of \ nucleosynthesis.

This is also of interest with respect to establishing possible connections between the \ signature observed in the Ret II stars and that of \ stars found in the Galactic halo. Given their nearly identical abundance patterns, the halo stars likely originated in ancient dwarf galaxy analogs similar to Ret II roederer2018 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Reticulum II as an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy whose unusually high fraction of enhanced stars and strong enrichment favor a rare early high-yield event such as a neutron-star merger.

quotes.quote: One way in which NSMs have again gained support is through the discovery of the ultra-faint dwarf (UFD) galaxy Reticulum II (Ret II). Of nine stars observed, ji2016a and roederer2016 identified seven as \ members---a much higher \ fraction than that found in the general field populations of the Milky Way. (Only high upper limits on [ Eu/Fe ] for the remaining two stars could be determined, which does not rule them out as additional \ stars.) The formation of \ stars in dwarf galaxy analogs of Ret II may alleviate the tension with the metal-poor nature of the \ stars and the long coalescence timescales of NSMs, depending on the rate of star formation in this galaxy.

A low-mass dwarf galaxy with few nucleosynthetic events will maintain its metal-poor nature longer than the Milky Way as a whole. Furthermore, beniamini2016 and ji2016a argue that the large number of CCSNe required could not simultaneously explain both the very low metallicity ( [ Fe/H ] 2.8 ) and the strong \ enrichment of Ret II, and rather, indicate that a single high-yield event (e.g., an NSM) having occurred early in the star-formation history is more likely.

sources.summary: The paper compares Ret II with Tuc III, emphasizing its similar distance, inferred stellar mass, orbital parameters, elongated shape, and lack of observed tidal features.

quotes.quote: The same estimate yields a stellar mass of about 5,000 for Ret II. With _ los =1.5 for Tuc III (which is the estimated lower limit for this system), the ratio of the tidal and internal acceleration for Tuc III is (g_t/g_ in )=-1.5 . We show this with a vertical line in the bottom panel of Figure fig_2 . This value is comparable to the ratio of tidal over internal acceleration for Ret II. Ret II and Tuc III have similar Galactocentric distances, implying that if we see tidal features in Tuc III, we would also expect to see tidal features in Ret II as well, but no such features have been observed.

However, one can argue that the orbital motion of the two satellites is different: based on Gaia DR2, Simon2018ApJGaia estimates a pericenter and apocenter distance for Tuc III to be 3^ +1 _ 1 kpc and 49^ +6 _ 5 kpc with an orbital period of 0.7 Gyr, and the corresponding values for Ret II are derived from being 29^ +14 _ 6 kpc for pericenter approach and 91^ +91 _ 39 kpc for apocenter approach with an orbital period of 1.6 Gyr. These numbers agree with the results reported in Fritz2018 . The inferred orbital motion based on Gaia DR2 suggests that despite their current similar galactocentric distance, Tuc III has been more prone to tidal disruption as opposed to Ret II. However, these orbital motion estimates are based on cold dark matter potential for the MW and whether the same orbital dynamics would be inferred in MOND remains to be verified with detailed orbital motion simulation. Moreover, we note that although Ret II is an elongated UFD with an ellipticity of about =0.6 , there have been no signs of tidal features within our field of view MutluPakdil2018ApJ .

sources.summary: The paper treats Reticulum II as a peculiar ultra-faint dwarf whose enhanced Eu and Ba abundances require short-delay production in its chemical-evolution model.

quotes.quote: Reticulum II For the chemical evolution of Reticulum II UFD, we assume a dark matter halo of mass M_ DM =3.0 10^6 M_ and a core radius R_ DM =170 pc . The effective radius of the luminous component of the galaxy has been set at R_L =50 pc . We predict a present time stellar mass of M_ ,f =0.6 10^ 3 M_ , similar to the one observed by equal to M_ ,f =2.6 10^ 3 M_ . In panel (a) of Figure fig: SFR+MDF_Ret , we show our assumed SFR as a function of time.

Concerning the observational data, Reticulum II stands out among all the other galaxies because of its peculiar Eu and Ba abundances. The data are concentrated at low metallicities and also show strong enhancements, which is about 2 orders of magnitude higher than what is observed in the other dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses Reticulum II as an ultra-faint dwarf reference galaxy, matching its metallicity peak and interpreting its Eu detections as evidence for an early neutron-star merger and prolonged star formation.

quotes.quote: In this study, we select a UFD Reticulum II 2.6 10^3 M_ , Bechtol2015 and three dwarf spheroidals Ursa Minor, Sculptor and Fornax 2.9 10^5 M_ , 2.3 10^6 M_ and 2.0 10^7 M_ , respectively, McConnachie2012 as reference galaxies (Table tab:dwarf ). What we refer for a given galaxy is only the present-day stellar mass M_* , which specifies the SFE ( k_ SF ) for each case according to equation ( eq:sfof ) as presented in Table tab:dwarf . In this sense, our model for the satellite dwarfs is parameter free. Nevertheless, the resulting metallicities at the peak of metallicity distribution, [ Fe/H ] _ peak (last column in Table tab:dwarf ), are in reasonable agreement with the mean metallicities estimated for these galaxies (except for Fornax): 2.6 for Reticulum II Koposov2015,Simon2015,Walker2015 , 2.1 for Ursa Minor, 1.7 for Sculptor and 1.0 for Fornax McConnachie2012 .
Among the reference galaxies, the (average) cumulative number of NSMs, N_ NSM , in Reticulum II (red curve) is below 1 all the way, reaching N_ NSM 0.1 at the end of evolution.

The fact that Eu has been detected only in the stars of [ Fe/H ] 3.0 Ji2016,Roederer2016 suggests that our Reticulum II-like galaxy experiences the first NSM at t 0.7 Gyr (for cases 1--3), making a jump of the [ Eu/Fe ] value from [ Eu/Fe ] = - or a small pre-enriched value, Tsujimoto2015,Ojima2018 . In addition, the presence of Eu-enhanced stars up to [ Fe/H ] = -2.1 implies that the galaxy evolves up to t 2 Gyr (for cases 1--3). This indicates that Reticulum II has experienced prolonged star formation beyond reionization e.g., Weisz2014,Applebaum2021,Miyoshi2020 .

sources.summary: The paper derives the HST-based color-magnitude diagram and star formation history of Reticulum 2, finding early stellar-mass assembly and quenching older than 11.5 Gyr within a seven-UFD comparison.

quotes.quote: We present the color-magnitude diagrams and star formation histories (SFHs) of seven ultra-faint dwarf galaxies: Horologium 1, Hydra 2, Phoenix 2, Reticulum 2, Sagittarius 2, Triangulum 2, and Tucana 2, derived from high-precision Hubble Space Telescope photometry. We find that the SFH of each galaxy is consistent with them having created at least 80 of the stellar mass by z 6 . For all galaxies, we find quenching times older than 11.5 Gyr ago, compatible with the scenario in which reionization suppresses the star formation of small dark matter halos. However, our analysis also reveals some differences in the SFHs of candidate Magellanic Cloud satellites, i.e., galaxies that are likely satellites of the Large Magellanic Cloud and that entered the Milky Way potential only recently.

On average, we find _ 90 = 12.06 0.72 Gyr ago for Magellanic satellites and _ 90 = 12.68 0.23 Gyr ago for non-Magellanic satellites (this becomes 12.67 0.41 if we exclude Sag 2); even though they are comparable within the errors, there is a marginal difference between these values (a two-sample KS test of the average distributions gives a p-value of 5.7 10^ 10 ). Indeed, the candidate Magellanic satellites appear to have more prolonged SFHs compared to non-Magellanic ones, possibly due to the fact that they should have resided in low-density environments for most of their lifetimes; however, the uncertainties are too large to reach a definitive conclusion.

sources.summary: The paper treats Reticulum II as a high mass-to-light dwarf satellite dark-matter target, finds no INTEGRAL gamma-ray or 511 keV detection, and derives limits on PBH and particle-DM scenarios.

quotes.quote: Reticulum II (Ret II) is a satellite galaxy of the Milky Way and presents a prime target to investigate the nature of dark matter (DM) because of its high mass-to-light ratio. We evaluate a dedicated INTEGRAL observation campaign data set to obtain ray fluxes from Ret II and compare those with expectations from DM. Ret II is not detected in the ray band 25--8000 keV, and we derive a flux limit of 10^ 8 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 . The previously reported 511 keV line is not seen, and we find a flux limit of 1.7 10^ 4 ph cm^ 2 s^ 1 . We construct spectral models for primordial black hole (PBH) evaporation and annihilation/decay of particle DM, and subsequent annihilation of e^+ s produced in these processes.

Given SPI's angular resolution of 2.7^ , and the expected maximum extent of Ret II's DM halo of 1^ , we model the galaxy as point source, M_ Ret II (l,b) = (l-l_0) (b-b_0) , with the Galactic coordinates of Ret II (l_0,b_0) = (266.30, -49.74) .

sources.summary: The review describes Reticulum II as an ultra-faint dwarf/process galaxy whose many enhanced stars imply a single early, high-yield neutron-capture event, plausibly a neutron star merger.

quotes.quote: It thus came as a surprise in 2016 when the dwarf galaxy Reticulum II Ji2016b, Roederer2016 turned out to be an entire `` process galaxy" when seven of nine stars observed showed the process pattern and enhancement levels among the highest ever found. The existence of such stars in a known and quantifiable dwarf galaxy environment provided a novel constraint on the astrophysical production site of the process. Based on an estimate for the mass of Reticulum II and together with the enhancement level observed in the stars, it was concluded that likely a neutron star merger had occurred to provide copious amounts of process elements in this galaxy at very early times.

This line of reasoning was brought about by the discovery that the ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Reticulum II is process enhanced, with over 2/3 of its stars having [ Eu/Fe ] 1.7 (Ret II; Ji2016b,Roederer2016 ; see Figure fig:ufdncap for updated data). Ret II has process abundances over 10-100 higher than what is found in other ultra-faint dwarf galaxies, indicating that it preserves the chemical abundance signature of a single process event. By considering the 10 ultra-faint dwarf galaxies available at that time, Ji2016b derived strong process production constraints from inferring that the event produced 10^ 5.5 10^ 3.5 M_ of europium, and that the relative rate of process events across the ultra-faint dwarf population was 1 process event for every 1,000-2,000 core-collapse supernovae (also see Beniamini2016b ).

sources.summary: The paper finds Ret2 inside or near the LMC bow shock and suggests shock interaction could complicate interpretation of its reported gamma-ray excess.

quotes.quote: In particular, we find that the Ret2 ultra-faint dwarf is currently located inside the LMC's bow shock. Intriguingly, this galaxy has been found to harbor a gamma ray excess that has been potentially attributed as a signal of dark matter annihilation Hooper2015, Bonnivard2015, Geringer-Sameth2015, Drlica-Wagner2015 . While we do not find that the gamma ray emission from the shock itself will be substantial, it could help to augment any gamma ray signal that already exists in Ret2 owing to dark matter annihilation. Moreover, if Ret2 is interacting with the shock and harbors any residual gas, the gas could be sufficiently excited to emit gamma rays. This scenario may help to explain why the signal is particularly strong in Ret2, versus other similarly massive ultra-faint dwarfs.
We further find that at present day, the Ret2 ultra-faint dwarf is in proximity to the shock, which may complicate efforts to understand the origin of any detected gamma ray excess.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Reticulum 2 member stars, measures its velocity and metallicity dispersions, concludes it is a dwarf galaxy, and finds its LMC association uncertain.

quotes.quote: We identify 18 members in Reticulum 2 and 5 in Horologium 1. We find Reticulum 2 to have a velocity dispersion of 3.22^ +1.64 _ 0.49 km s ^ 1 , implying a M/L ratio of 500. We have inferred stellar parameters ( T_ eff , g , [ Fe/H ] , [ / Fe ] ) for all candidates and we find Reticulum 2 to have a mean metallicity of Fe/H = -2.46^ +0.09 _ 0.10 , with an intrinsic dispersion of 0.29^ +0.13 _ 0.05 dex, and is enhanced to the level of [ / Fe ] 0.4 dex. We conclude that Reticulum 2 is a dwarf galaxy.
The line-of-sight velocity of Reticulum 2 is offset by some 100 km s ^ 1 from the prediction of the orbital velocity of the LMC, thus making its association with the Cloud uncertain.

sources.summary: The paper selects Reticulum-2 as the strongest dwarf-spheroidal dark-matter target, citing its large annihilation J factor and favorable NT200 visibility.

quotes.quote: In the chosen set of dwarf galaxies the largest J_a factor is expected from ``Reticulum-2''. As it has been discussed in Ref. , this source is a very attractive target in search for DM signal.

The strongest individual upper limit comes from observation of ``Reticulum-2'' direction. It is determined by the largest annihilation J_a factor and the position of this source on the sky which allows for 100 visibility from the NT200 position. Although ``Segue-1'' has similar value of J factor with smaller astrophysical uncertainty than those of Reticulum-2, this source is visible only about 32 of the whole lifetime.

sources.summary: The paper treats Reticulum II as a uniquely r-process-rich ultra-faint dwarf and models whether a Reticulum-II-like galaxy can retain neutron-star-merger ejecta.

quotes.quote: One UFD, Reticulum II, has been found to have a large excess of process elements, while all other UFDs have strong upper limits on their process abundances ji2016,roederer2014MNRAS . The low escape velocity of UFDs Walker2015 , as well as the fact that UFDs are composed of old stellar material (formed within the first Gyr of the galaxys' evolution, Brown2014,Weisz2015 ) have initially cast some doubt on the association of a NS merger as the origin of the process material in Reticulum II Bramante2016 .

For a Reticulum II - like galaxy, we show that unless a significant ( 90 ) amount of gas has been removed from the galaxy prior to the process event and/or unless the process explosion was extremely energetic ( 10^ 52 erg), the retainment fraction of process materials is large (with typical values around 0.9 ).

It includes five UFDs: Reticulum II, Segue I, Segue II, Coma Berenices and Ursa Major II. All of these are composed of old stellar material Simon2007 and have significantly fewer stars then naively expected from their halo masses, indicating significant mass loss during their evolution.

sources.summary: The paper treats Reticulum II as the main case study for a Fermi gamma-ray excess, comparing dark-matter and astrophysical spectral explanations including blazars and pulsars.

quotes.quote: As a case study we focus on Reticulum II, the dwarf which shows the strongest evidence for a gamma-ray signal in Fermi data. Dark matter models and those with curved energy spectra provide good fits to the data, while a simple power law is ruled out at 97.5 confidence. We compare RetII's spectrum to known classes of gamma-ray sources and find a useful representation in terms of spectral curvature and the energy at which the spectral energy distribution peaks. In this space the blazar classes appear segregated from the confidence region occupied by RetII. Pulsars have similar gamma-ray spectra to RetII but we show that RetII is unlikely to host a pulsar population detectable in gamma rays.

sources.summary: The paper treats Ret II as a low-mass dwarf host whose stars trace a single enrichment event, with seven stars showing halo-like heavy-element patterns and low actinide production in its gas.

quotes.quote: If a prolific \ event enriched the original, low-mass host galaxy, such as that in Ret II, the imprints on these stars offer a window into the element production by (presumably) single \ events. roederer2018 recently found kinematic grouping among spatially unrelated \ enhanced halo stars. These kinematic groups are further evidence that \ enhanced halo stars were once members of satellite galaxies which became accreted by the Milky Way. The progenitor dwarf galaxies of these kinematic groups could resemble Ret II, where all stars belonging to each of these progenitor systems would have formed from gas enriched by single, respective \ events.

Ret II -- Although the scaled, heavy-element (between Ba and the third peak) abundance patterns of seven Ret II stars closely resemble those of \ halo stars, the only Ret II member for which a Th measurement is available DES J033523 540407; ji2018 displays a strikingly low actinide abundance compared to its lanthanides ( [ Th/Eu ] =-0.34 ). The seven \ stars of Ret II might reflect an event with low actinide production, or possibly one with a significant range. Without a complete set of Th abundances for each of the \ stars in Ret II, we assume, for simplicity, that this low actinide level reflects low actinide production in the \ event that enriched the Ret II gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes Reticulum II dSph as an ATCA and KAT-7 radio target with low foregrounds, nondetected diffuse emission, and WIMP annihilation or decay constraints.

quotes.quote: , the Reticulum II dSph was targeted with the ATCA telescope in a similar configuration to , complemented by large angular scale data from the KAT-7 array . These ATCA observations involved a 23.7^ region containing Reticulum II and attained a 10 Jy rms sensitivity, as the position of Reticulum II on the sky means that galactic foregrounds are less significant.

regis2017 found no evidence of diffuse radio emission in Reticulum II and thus derived constraints on the WIMP annihilation cross-section or decay rate from this (following a model similar to AVG above). These constraints are displayed in Figure fig:compare . What is evident in Figure fig:compare is that the limits from non-observation of diffuse emission in Reticulum II from are up to an order of magnitude stronger than those from for all displayed annihilation channels (note that the diffusion scenarios are very similar in this comparison).

sources.summary: The paper cites Reticulum II as an ultrafaint dwarf galaxy where most observed stars are r-process enhanced, using it as an observational example for neutron-star-merger enrichment in low-mass galaxies.

quotes.quote: RPE stars are also found in dwarf galaxies. The ultrafaint dwarf (UFD) galaxy Reticulum II is a notable example 2016Natur.531..610J, 2016ApJ...830...93J, 2016AJ....151...82R, 2022arXiv220703499J . Seven out of nine observed stars are classified as II stars in this galaxy. 2016Natur.531..610J argued that a nucleosynthetic event with a europium yield of 10^ 4.5 M _ could enhance the process-element abundances of gas with a mass 10 ^ 6 M _ to the level required for II stars.

In the Reticulum II UFD, seven out of nine stars observed in this galaxy are enhanced in process elements 2016Natur.531..610J . In contrast, classical dSphs tend to have smaller fractions of RPE stars than in Reticulum II e.g. 2015ARA&A..53..631F .

sources.summary: The paper uses Reticulum II as an ultra-faint dwarf benchmark, arguing its Eu-enhanced stars are hard to explain with diluted neutron-star-merger enrichment alone and may indicate rare or secondary enrichment.

quotes.quote: However, -II stars (highly enhanced -process elements stars with [ Eu/Fe ] >0.7 ) have been observed in Reticulum II, an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Ji2016Natur.531..610J, Roederer2016AJ....151...82R . In our scenario, where the enrichment in dwarf galaxies is strongly diluted, galaxies like Reticulum II could not be enriched by NSMs but by a secondary source(s) of Eu (e.g. MRD and/or CC SNe). Other works, such as Brauer2019ApJ...871..247B , Hattori2022arXiv220704110H , and Hirai2022arXiv220604060H , found that (at least a fraction) of metal-poor -II stars in the Galactic halo formed in ancient ultra-faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies (similar to Reticulum II).

That model predicts that the Eu enrichment in galaxies similar to Reticulum II is rare and not enough robust to explain the observations of Reticulum II. However is still possible that, with Reticulum II, we detect a rare member of a large population of poorly enriched UFD galaxies.

sources.summary: The review describes Reticulum II as a newly discovered dwarf galaxy with a gamma-ray excess that could match a dark-matter annihilation signal, while noting uncertainties in its dark matter content.

quotes.quote: One observed in the galactic center and other in the recently discovered dwarf galaxy known as Reticulum II . Both excesses can be explained by the same dark matter particle annihilating into b b quarks with v 10^ 26 cm^3s^ 1 , m_ 30 GeV. Which is an intriguing coincidence. Although, due to the large uncertainties concerning the dark matter content in this dwarf galaxy is unclear whether the Reticulum II anomaly is actually supporting the galactic center excess .

sources.summary: The paper uses Reticulum II as the comparison ultra-faint dwarf where most bright stars are highly r-process enhanced, with patterns explainable by inhomogeneous mixing after one neutron-star merger.

quotes.quote: Reticulum II Nine stars in Ret II have been analysed with high resolution spectroscopy, and seven of these show large enhancements in process elements ji2016,ji2016b,roederer16 . One of the process enhanced stars in Ret II also has a high Ba abundance ( [ Eu/Ba ] = 0.3 ) and does not meet our selection criteria. The remaining six stars are listed in Table tab4 .
However, the data for Ret II indicate that the same signature can be produced with inhomogeneous mixing and a single NSM ji2016 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses Reticulum II as an r-process-enriched ultra-faint dwarf whose low luminosity, small velocity dispersion, and old stars constrain neutron-star merger enrichment.

quotes.quote: In recent years, and reported on the discovery of an process enriched UFD galaxy Reticulum II. The total stellar luminosity of Reticulum II is only 10^3L_ and the velocity dispersion is 4 km/s walker2015ApJ . Based on this detection, argued against a neutron star merger origin for this material, suggesting that the kick imparted on the system during the formation of the second born neutron star, would have ejected the binary out of such a low mass galaxy. An additional issue is that UFDs are composed of very old stellar material, implying that the binary neutron star should merge within the first Gyr of its formation (before star formation ceases), in order to successfully enrich future stars with process material.

sources.summary: The paper computes Reticulum II J-Factors with both DES discovery photometries; the stellar-parameter choices change log J(0.5) by about 0.1, supporting discussion of half-light-radius scaling.

quotes.quote: For several of the recently-discovered dSphs in DES (Horologium I, Reticulum II, and Tucana II) the stellar parameters differ between the two independent discovery analyses Bechtol2015ApJ...807...50B, Koposov2015ApJ...805..130K . We compute the J-Factors for these objects with both sets of stellar parameters (results for both are listed in Table data_table ). The differences in J-Factor are _ 10 (J(0.5 )) 0.4, 0.1, 0.2 for the three galaxies respectively. The largest difference is found in Horologium I where there is a factor of two difference in r_ 1/2 between the photometric studies. This suggests that there is some J-Factor scaling with r_ 1/2 despite the lack of an apparently obvious trend in Figure fig:jfactor .

sources.summary: The paper uses Simon classifications for Reticulum II, keeps 27 members, and finds its motion agrees with Simon, Kallivayalil, Pace, and mostly Massari.

quotes.quote: Reticulum II We use the catalog of Simon_15 , giving binary membership classifications. After our cuts we have 27 member stars. Our motions agrees very well with Simon_18 , Kallivayalil_18 and Pace_18 . With agrees within 1.7/0.3 with the motion of Massari_18 .

sources.summary: The review cites Reticurum II as an ultra-faint dwarf whose stars are broadly r-process enhanced, suggesting pollution by a few efficient nucleosynthesis events before accretion into the Milky Way.

quotes.quote: The recent discovery of the ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Reticurum II (Ret. II), whose member stars ubiquitously show enhancement of -process elements , has made a hint to interpret such that these -process-enhanced stars in the halo could have been formed in a small stellar systems affected by an -process event, and then accreted into the Milky Way.
Recent discoveries of -process-enhanced stars in dwarf galaxies like Ret. II are providing useful constraints on such models (see below).

Ultra-faint dwarf galaxy named Ret. II which has just thousands of solar masses was discovered, and interestingly most stars seem to be -process-enhanced . This suggests that the progenitor gas cloud was polluted by a few but very efficient -process events, which could be NSMs, MHDJs or collapsars.

sources.summary: The paper uses Reticulum II as the prototype enhanced UFD, noting seven r-process-enhanced stars, low internal velocity dispersion, and a metallicity dispersion used to judge disrupted-dwarf remnants.

quotes.quote: Recently, and discovered that the ultra-faint dwarf galaxy (UFD) named Reticulum II contains seven II stars among nine spectroscopically observed stars in this galaxy. The discovery of this enhanced UFD favors a scenario wherein a majority of II stars in the halo were originally born in UFDs similar to Reticulum II, in which very rare process events, such as a neutron-star merger, occurred Tsujimoto2014A&A...565L...5T,Tsujimoto2014ApJ...795L..18T .

This value of red _J is also supported by the observed properties of UFDs hosting II stars, such as Reticulum II, which has an internal velocity dispersion of _ v 3 Koposov2015,Walker2015,Minor2019 . If a stellar system with a similar velocity dispersion is disrupted in the inner part of the Milky Way ( r_ disruption 10-30 ), we expect that the stars stripped from the system typically have a spread in action of _ v r_ disruption (30-90) .
Indeed, the seven stars in the Reticulum II with enhanced [ Eu/Fe ] abundance show a dispersion in [ Fe/H ] of _ [ Fe/H ] =0.35 Ji2016Natur.531..610J .

sources.summary: The paper highlights Reticulum II as an extended dSph where J-factor uncertainties broaden likelihoods and make extended-template dark-matter limits weaker.

quotes.quote: In particular, the difference between using J_ tot vs J_ 05 is more relevant for the dSphs Ursa Major II, Sculptor, Ursa Minor, Coma Berenice, Bootes I, Fornax, Tucana III and Reticulum II, which are the dSphs with the largest _ 68 (see Tab. tab:jfactors ) and for which the difference between is consequently the most significant -- the ratio of in the Ext over PS case attains at least 0.55 for Ursa Major II that indeed is the source with the largest DM template (see Tab. tab:jfactors ).

However, the ranking of the dSphs with the largest ratio between the Ext and PS scenarios is driven mainly by the objects for which the difference of _ J is the largest, i.e. Reticulum II and Coma Berenices. This is explained by the factor likelihood term, Eq. eq:Lsigma , which disfavors values of J_i much different from the observed one. Objects with _ J_ tot much larger than _ J_ 05 , such as Reticulum II, have a likelihood profile that is broader for the Ext with respect to PS scenarios.
It might seem surprising that for objects such as Reticulum II or Coma Berenices the change from _ J_ 05 to _ J_ tot is much more important than the change in the average observed J factor.

sources.summary: The paper uses Ret II as motivation: an isolated ultrafaint dwarf with r-process-enriched stars, detected by DES and studied as evidence for prompt neutron-star merger enrichment.

quotes.quote: The motivation for this work comes from the -process enriched stars in Ret II, an ultrafaint dwarf (UFD) galaxy which serves as an ideal laboratory for studying early universe star formation.
Ret II was detected by the Dark Energy Survey Bechtol15 , whose stars have since been extensively studied Simon15, Roederer16, Ji16, Ji22 .

Recently, Ji22 have found that 72 of the stars in Ret II are -process enriched, whose source could have been a prompt NSM. Since this galaxy is so isolated, it serves as an ideal case study for simulating this scenario: namely, what happens if a single NSM, produced from the remnants of Pop III stars, occurs within a galaxy in the early universe?

sources.summary: The paper uses RetII as an example satellite with high expected membership, clear proper-motion clustering, and DBSCAN/HDBSCAN candidate recovery demonstrations.

quotes.quote: In some cases, certain satellite galaxies returned high R_ cm and a low R_ cnm with ease (e.g., RetII: refers to Reticulum II; short names of satellite galaxies listed in Table table:summary ). On the other hand, a satisfactory recovery seemed difficult for the satellite galaxies with elongated structures or tidal tails (e.g., BoöI, TucIII). As an example, we show two satellite galaxies with different characteristics to demonstrate the hyperparameter selection. We chose RetII as one of the satellite galaxies , due to its proximity and the highest anticipated membership. Additionally, it has a significant number of spectroscopic members, with the clear clustering of stars in proper-motion space and well-constrained satellite parameters.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes Fermi-LAT Pass 8 data for Reticulum II, treats it as an extended source, finds no significant gamma-ray signal, and derives dark-matter annihilation limits.

quotes.quote: In this work, we reanalyze the Fermi-LAT Pass 8 data from the direction of Reticulum II, where a slight excess has been reported by some previous studies. We treat Reticulum II (DES J0335.6-5403) as a spatially extended source, and find that no significant gamma-ray signature is observed. Based on this result, we set upper-limits on the DM annihilation cross section.

sources.summary: The paper describes Reticulum II as a metal-poor ultra-faint dwarf whose stars show strong r-process enhancement, likely from a rare prolific event.

quotes.quote: The ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Reticulum II was enriched by a rare and prolific process event, such as a neutron star merger.
The UFD Reticulum II (Ret II) was discovered in the Dark Energy Survey and quickly confirmed as a metal-poor UFD galaxy [ Fe/H ] 2.5 , Bechtol15,Simon15,Koposov15a,Koposov15b,Walker15 .

Surprisingly, the majority of the stars in Ret II displayed large enhancements of elements synthesized in the rapid neutron-capture process ( process), 2-3 orders of magnitude higher than most other UFDs [ Eu/Fe ] 1.7 , where Eu is a representative process element Ji16b, Ji16c, Roederer16b , and similar to the most process enhanced stars in the Milky Way stellar halo (or II stars; Christlieb04, Beers05 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds Ret2 has an orbital pole aligned with the LMC system but velocity components inconsistent with LMC debris, making membership unlikely overall.

quotes.quote: Ret2, Tuc2 and Gru1 have velocity components that are not consistent within 3 sigma of our predictions and are therefore less favorable.

Hor1, Hyi1, Car3, and Ret2 are clearly consistent with the angular momentum direction of the LMC system, listed in descending order of significance as represented by the density of LMCa debris in the same region.
Ret2, Tuc2 and Gru1 have velocity components that are not consistent within 3 sigma of our predictions and are unlikely members of the LMC group according to our analysis.

sources.summary: The paper discusses Reticulum II as an ultra-faint dwarf whose r-process enrichment is difficult for neutron-star merger models and whose stars show depleted light r-process abundances.

quotes.quote: While the probability of such mergers might still be high enough to explain the Eu enhancement in some more massive ultra-faint dwarf galaxies such as Tucana III, the probability seems too low to explain enrichment in less massive ultra-faint dwarfs such as Reticulum II . In particular, Ref. finds that 6 and 1 of Tucana III-like and Reticulum II-like dwarfs would have an enrichment event, respectively, which is in tension with 20 of ultra-faint dwarfs showing -process enhancements.

It is interesting to note that the abundances of such light -process elements in stars of the ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Reticulum II and some extremely metal-poor stars (e.g., CS 22892-052) are significantly depleted with respect to the solar system -process abundances .

sources.summary: The paper treats Reticulum II as an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal with unusually strong 511 keV emission and heavy-element enrichment, interpreting both as evidence for a rare historical neutron-star merger.

quotes.quote: Recent observations of ultra-faint dwarf spheroidals indicate that Reticulum II shows a particularly strong emission in 511 keV . The same system also displays a significant abundance of heavy elements, typically associated with process nucleosynthesis, and it has been argued that a rare historical event is responsible . These surprising observations constitute an obvious smoking gun of our proposal, which naturally links process nucleosynthesis with subsequent emission of 511 keV radiation originating from a rare event.

sources.summary: The paper treats Reticulum II as an ultra-faint dwarf with heavy-element evidence, europium-enhanced stars, and literature-based barium trends consistent with comparable delay times.

quotes.quote: Intriguing examples are the recent discoveries of the UFD galaxies Reticulum II Ji2016a,Roederer2016 and Tucana III Hansen2017 , which provide evidence on the production site of heavy elements.
This was possible due to a large enhancement of europium in the Reticulum II stars.
Literature values of Reticulum II from Ji2016b lead to a slope of 1.11 0.37 indicating a production of elements and Ba _r on comparable delay times also in UFD.

sources.summary: The paper finds Reticulum II has the strongest weak gamma-ray excess, but its offset and high-energy behavior favor contamination by an astrophysical source rather than dark matter.

quotes.quote: In the direction of Reticulum II, the peak TS value of the excess reaches 11. However, different from the previous analysis with the 9 years of Fermi-LAT data, now the location of the gamma-ray emission is significantly away from the center of Reticulum II because of the enhancement of ``offset" rays above 10 GeV since 2017. The detected weak excess is likely due to the contamination of an astrophysical ray source with a TS value of 22 , irrelevant to the dark matter inside Reticulum II.

gmw_00000929.name: SDSS J025214.67-002813.7
gmw_00000929.overview: SDSS J025214.67-002813.7 is a candidate periodic quasar [arXiv:2008.12329] that has been studied as a potential binary active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2110.02982]. However, multiwavelength spectral energy distribution and X-ray analyses suggest its emission is more consistent with a single, reddened active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2110.02982].

sources.summary: The paper lists J0252 among the five candidate periodic quasars, citing prior work that found it the most significant case, with circumbinary accretion variability favored over Doppler boosting.

quotes.quote: They are: J024613.89 004028.2, J024703.24 010032.0, J024944.66 000036.8, J025214.67 002813.7, and J025406.26 + 002753.7 (hereafter J0246, J0247, J0249, J0252, and J0254 for short). We refer to them as ``candidate'' since there may be up to two false positives among the five due to noise (discussed below in subsec:result_significance ). tab:number_candidates summarizes the numbers of candidates that satisfy each selection criterion. tab:info lists the basic properties of the five candidate periodic quasars. Among the five candidates, Liao2020 has presented the case of J0252 as the most significant candidate and the first known case whose light curve characteristics strongly prefers hydrodynamic variability.

Based on calculating the BIC differences, Liao2020 have shown for the case of J0252 that the periodic models are strongly preferred over a purely stochastic red noise model; a more bursty model is also preferred over a smooth sinusoidal model expected from Doppler boosting. This points to circumbinary accretion disk variability as the physical origin for driving the periodicity seen in J0252.

Liao2020 have tested the Doppler boost hypothesis for J0252. They suggest that the Doppler boost model is disfavored for J0252 based on both the frequency-dependent variability amplitude ratios and the multi-band variability amplitudes.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SDSS J025214.67-002813.7 as a binary AGN candidate, finding its X-ray and multiwavelength SED evidence better supports a reddened single AGN.

quotes.quote: We present results on a multi-wavelength analysis of SDSS J025214.67-002813.7, a system which has been previously classified as a binary AGN candidate based on periodic signals detected in the optical light curves. We use available radio X-ray observations of the system to investigate the true accretion nature. Analyzing new observations from XMM-Newton and NuSTAR , we characterize the X-ray emission and search for evidence of circumbinary accretion.

Although the 0.5--10 keV spectrum shows evidence of an additional soft emission component, possibly due to extended emission from hot nuclear gas, we find the spectral shape consistent with a single AGN. Compiling a full multi-wavelength SED, we also search for signs of circumbinary accretion, such as a ``notch" in the continuum due to the presence of minidisks. We find that the radio--optical emission agrees with the SED of a standard, radio-quiet, AGN, however there is a large deficit in emission blueward of 1400 .

gmw_00000930.name: 2M163700+2221
gmw_00000930.overview: The host galaxy of the quasar 2M163700+2221 is a disk galaxy that features multiple luminous knots within 5 kiloparsecs of its nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0303184]. It also exhibits possible northwestern tidal debris and has a corrected polarization aligned with its disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0303184].

sources.summary: The paper describes the QSO host as a disk galaxy with several luminous knots, possible northwestern tidal debris, and polarization aligned with the disk.

quotes.quote: 2M163700+2221 Host galaxy possesses a disk with multiple luminous knots within 5 h^ 1 kpc of nucleus. Not evident in Figure fig_hosts1 is possible tidal debris extending from the host in the northwest direction. The corrected polarization is 7 and the polarization angle is closely aligned with the disk.

gmw_00000931.name: 2M165939+1834
gmw_00000931.overview: 2M165939+1834 is a dust-obscured red active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2005.09014] whose host galaxy exhibits merging features [arXiv:2005.09014]. The system's inner region is resolved into two distinct components [arXiv:astro-ph/0303184], and it is a candidate for hosting merging supermassive black holes [arXiv:2005.09014].

sources.summary: The paper presents 2MASS J165939.7 + 183436 as a dusty red AGN in a merging host with double-peaked broad emission lines and possible close SMBH components.

quotes.quote: 2MASS J165939.7 + 183436 is a red active galactic nucleus (AGN) that is a prospective merging SMBH candidate owing to its merging features in Hubble Space Telescope imaging and double-peaked broad emission lines (BELs). Herein, we report a Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph Integral Field Unit observation of a double-peaked broad H line of 2MASS J165939.7 + 183436. Furthermore, we confirm the existence of two BEL peaks that are kinematically separated by 3000 km s^ 1 , with the SMBH of each BEL component weighing at 10^ 8.92 0.06 M_ and 10^ 7.13 0.06 M_ , if they arise from independent BELs near the two SMBHs.

Recently, we identified a red AGN, 2MASS J165939.7 + 183436 (hereafter 1659 + 1834), that has a double-peaked BEL profile and a host galaxy with a merging feature. Here, we present a spatially resolved spectroscopic observation of the core of 1659 + 1834 using the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph (GMOS) Integral Field Unit (IFU) as an attempt to find the separation of the two BELs at 0 1 .

Not all of the AGNs selected using this NIR color criterion are dust-obscured red AGNs kim18b , but 1659 + 1834 is a dust-obscured red AGN with E(B - V) 0.5 kim18b . Similar to several other dust-obscured red AGNs, 1659 + 1834 exhibits the morphology of a galaxy merger. Figure fig:HST_Image shows the surrounding region and the central part of 1659 + 1834.

sources.summary: The paper describes this QSO host as resolved into two components, with about half the luminosity nuclear and polarization suggestive of scattering by a close companion.

quotes.quote: 2M165939+1834 Inner region clearly resolved into two distinct components. Slightly more than half of the observed luminosity comes from the nucleus which has a corrected polarization of 11 . Interestingly, the polarization angle is rotated almost exactly 90 from the axis through the two components, perhaps suggesting scattering of nuclear light by a close companion.

gmw_00000932.name: 2M234449+1221
gmw_00000932.overview: The active galaxy 2M234449+1221 is part of an interacting system that shares a common envelope with an extremely close companion object [arXiv:astro-ph/0303184]. Very little residual host galaxy flux is visible around its nucleus, and its polarization angle is rotated nearly 90 degrees from the axis connecting the two bright spots [arXiv:astro-ph/0303184].

sources.summary: The paper portrays this QSO system as interacting, with little residual host light, a misleading smooth profile, a common envelope, and polarization nearly perpendicular to the two bright spots.

quotes.quote: 2M234449+1221 Very little visible residual flux around PSF-subtracted nucleus. Well behaved surface brightness profile is misleading as it probes different objects at different radii. The system appears to be interacting. A common envelope seems to include the QSO and the extremely close companion object. The polarization angle is rotated nearly 90 from the axis connecting the two bright spots.

gmw_00000933.name: GRG 0503-286
gmw_00000933.overview: GRG 0503-286 is a giant radio galaxy hosted by the luminous elliptical galaxy ESO 422-G028 [arXiv:2204.00839]. It features highly extended radio lobes spanning up to 1.8 Mpc [arXiv:1809.09327, arXiv:2204.00839], which exhibit an inversion-symmetric bending and a mega-sized X-shaped morphology [arXiv:2204.00839].

sources.summary: ESO 422 G028 is treated as a giant radio galaxy whose two lobes show contrasting low-frequency polarised morphology, likely shaped by complex Faraday structure and lobe turbulence.

quotes.quote: The highly extended radio lobes associated with this giant radio galaxy (GRG; also known in the literature as 0503 286) are hosted by the galaxy ESO 422 G028 (hereafter ESO 422) at redshift z=0.0381 Saripalli1986,Jamrozy2005 . At this redshift, the angular diameter between hotspots (approx. 34 arcmin) corresponds to a physical separation of 1.6 Mpc, given our cosmology. We present postage stamp images of the Northern and Southern lobes (respectively GLEAM J050544 282236 and GLEAM J050535 285648) in Figure fig:gleamj0505_postage . From Figure fig:gleamj0505_postage , we detect polarised emission from both lobes of this GRG.

This emission i) appears at different FD and ii) occurs from starkly different regions. At 216 MHz, polarised emission in the North is associated with the hotspot, whereas in the South the polarised emission appears to be associated with a backflow region. In addition, the polarised emission at 1.4 GHz in the NVSS exhibits different morphology to that at 216 MHz.

sources.summary: The paper reports GLEAM evidence that GRG 0503-286 has an X-shaped, inversion-symmetric radio-lobe morphology with a strip-like gap likely caused by an intervening gaseous layer.

quotes.quote: The high surface-brightness sensitivity of the GLEAM survey image of the giant radio galaxy GRG 0503-28 at 70-230 MHz has revealed an inversion-symmetric bending of its two lobes, while maintaining between their bent portions a 200 kpc wide strip-like radio emission gap. This lends the source the appearance of a mega-sized X-shaped radio galaxy. Identifying the emission gap with the presence of a gaseous layer, probably a WHIM-filled sheet in the cosmic web, we suggest that the layer is the most likely cause of the inversion-symmetric bending of the two radio lobes.

Of these, the GRG 0503-286 (MSH05-22) hosted by the luminous elliptical galaxy ESO 422-G028 at z =0.03815 and having the radio lobe pair spanning 1.8 Mpc ( 40 ) was the largest known galaxy in the southern sky.

Further, it is noted that the GRG 0503-28, known for the exceptionally unequal extents of its two lobes, in fact extends symmetrically about the radio emission gap. This agrees with the previously reported trend for double radio sources of normal dimensions. Lastly, the reported anomalous radio spectral gradient in the northern lobe is not substantiated in this study.

gmw_00000934.name: Y358
gmw_00000934.overview: Y358 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy associated with the Coma cluster [arXiv:2211.03915, arXiv:2309.15148]. It is rich in globular clusters [arXiv:2211.03915, arXiv:2309.15148], exhibits an extremely low iron abundance with high magnesium enhancement [arXiv:2309.15148], and likely hosts a cored dark matter halo [arXiv:2211.03915].

sources.summary: The paper presents new KCWI spectroscopy for Coma UDG Y358, confirms its cluster association and UDG status, and infers a likely cored dark matter halo from its velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: We begin by publishing Keck/KCWI spectroscopy for the Coma cluster UDG Y358, for which we measure both a recessional velocity and velocity dispersion. Our recessional velocity confirms association with the Coma cluster and Y358's status as a UDG. From our velocity dispersion (19 3 km s ^ 1 ) we calculate a dynamical mass within the half-light radius which provides evidence for a core in Y358's dark matter halo.

Here we present new KCWI data for the UDG, Y358. We target this galaxy due to its rich GC system which is indicative of a massive dark matter halo. Using the GC counts for Y358 from Lim2018 ( N_ GC = 28.0 5.3) and the N_ GC - halo mass relationship of Burkert2020 , we infer a total dark matter halo mass of (1.4 0.25) 10^ 11 M_ for Y358.

sources.summary: Yagi358 is described as a Coma ultra-diffuse galaxy with very low iron abundance, high magnesium enhancement, early fast formation, GC richness, and possible failed-galaxy character.

quotes.quote: Details about each observation can be found in the original papers presenting the dynamical and kinematic analysis of those UDGs: the Virgo cluster UDG VCC 1287 Gannon2020G , NGC 5846-UDG1 in the NGC 5846 group Forbes2021 , UDG1137+16 from the UGC 6594 group Gannon2021 , several UDGs in the Perseus cluster (i.e., PUDG-R15, PUDG-S74, PUDG-R84 and PUDG-R24; Gannon2022 ), and Yagi358 in the Coma cluster Gannon2022b . UDGs mostly cover the [ Mg/Fe ] range of GCs and dwarfs at all metallicities, and only the most extreme ones (DGSAT I and Yagi358) having [ Mg/Fe ] that do not match any galaxy in the comparison sets. From our line index analysis we also measure an extremely low [ Fe/H ] but high [ Mg/Fe ] for Yagi358.

Given that they both have overall similar stellar population properties, we speculate that Yagi358 could have formed in a similar process to DGSAT I. From those, PUDG-R84 and Yagi358 (in high-density environments), and DF44 (as infall galaxy), present early and fast formation and are GC-rich, indicative of a large dark matter halo.

gmw_00000935.name: J1522 2730
gmw_00000935.overview: J1522 2730 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a BL Lac object [arXiv:astro-ph/0407010, arXiv:0901.4720]. It exhibits high variability, including intra-day radio variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0407010], and displays remarkably stable circular polarization [arXiv:0901.4720].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1522 2730 as a BL Lac object with high variability, intra-day radio variability, compact VLBI structure, and only episodic GPS-like spectra.

quotes.quote: J1522 2730 is classified as a BL Lac object, and, as noted in 2, shows the highest variability index of this group. The redshift for this object was recently reported by . Intra-Day Variability at radio wavelengths has been observed in this source ked01,jau03 and VLBI imaging confirms the core is very compact e.g., fom00 . present a composite spectrum derived from a large number of observations, and note that the source appears to have a relatively flat quiescent spectrum that is inverted during flares. The high variability and apparent lack of a persistent GHz peaked spectrum suggest this is less likely to be a bona fide GPS source.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1519-273 as a known circularly polarized AGN source whose calibrated Effelsberg measurements recover published circular-polarization levels and confirm its unusually stable CP behavior.

quotes.quote: Significant amounts of CP were detected in the two known CP sources 0743-006 and 1519-273 at levels consistent with published levels (0743-006: V=(-0.51 0.08) , Allers and V = (-0.46 0.05) , Homan , both adopting the circular RL frame and for 1519-273: V = (-0.92 0.17) Allers ). In the Effelsberg measurement of 1519-273, the source appeared unpolarized from the raw values, but the D-terms calibration revealed a level of CP in good agreement with the values reported in the literature.

Time variability in CP and changes of sign are very common Allers , 1519-273 is the most stable CP source known so far. Table t:CPTime shows a sample of CP values that we observed during 2007. Our detections also confirm the stability of the CP level of 1519-273.

gmw_00000936.name: BZB J0955+3551
gmw_00000936.overview: BZB J0955+3551 is a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac blazar [arXiv:2011.03681] located at a redshift of z = 0.557 [arXiv:2003.03634, arXiv:2003.06012]. It is a possible counterpart to the IceCube-200107A neutrino event [arXiv:2003.03634, arXiv:2003.06012], during which it exhibited a hard, variable X-ray flare [arXiv:2003.06405, arXiv:2202.03381].

sources.summary: 4FGL J0955.1+3551 is identified as a BL Lac/blazar and possible IceCube-neutrino counterpart; spectroscopy fixes z = 0.557 and imaging resolves a typical BL Lac host galaxy.

quotes.quote: The BL Lac object 4FGL J0955.1+3551 has been suggested as a possible source of ultra energetic neutrinos detected by the Icecube observatory. The target was observed in January 2020 at the Large Binocular Telescope. Our spectroscopy (4100-8500 ) yields a firm redshift z = 0.557 as deduced by the absorption lines of the host galaxy. The upper limit of the minimum equivalent width on emission lines is 0.3 . From the source image we are able to resolve the host galaxy for which we measure an absolute magnitude M(R) = -22.9 and R _ e = 8 kpc, that is values which are typical of the host galaxies of BL Lacs.

Here we consider the case of the ray source 4FGL J0955.1+3551 (3HSP J095507.9+355101, named J0955 in the following). This is a blazar (g 20.4) that is considered a possible neutrino counterpart as it was found inside the error box of the IceCube neutrino event 200107A detected on 2020 January 07 GCN- 26655, icecube2020GCN0955 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 4FGL J0955.1+3551 as a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac at z=0.557 associated with IceCube-200107A, requiring an intense external photon field in the model.

quotes.quote: More recently, the second possible association between a blazar, 4FGL J0955.1+3551, and a muon neutrino event, IceCube-200107A, is reported 2020A&A...640L...4G, 2020arXiv200306012P . 4FGL J0955.1+3551 is a high-synchrotron peaked HSP; 2010ApJ...716...30A BL Lac object at redshift z=0.557 2020MNRAS.tmpL..59P . Its X-ray flux was found in a high state with a factor of 2.5 larger than the average flux in 2012-2013 when Swift started the follow-up observation the day after the arrival of IceCube-200107A 2020ATel13394....1G . Assuming the spectral index of neutrino spectrum to be 2 ( N_ (E) E^ 2 ) and the effective area for HESE starting tracks 2019ICRC...36.1021B , calculate that the all-flavour neutrino energy flux between 65 TeV and 2.6 PeV for 30 days, 250 days and 10 years are 3 10^ 9 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 , 4 10^ 10 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 and 3 10^ 11 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 , respectively.

The GeV gamma-ray flux is at the level of 10^ 12 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 which is more than one order of magnitude lower than the neutrino flux even assuming a 10-yr emission period. argue that IceCube-200107A can only be interpreted as an upward fluctuation see also 2020ApJ...899..113P or a random event detected from a numerous population of faint sources (i.e., the Eddington bias, 2019A&A...622L...9S ) in the conventional one-zone models, given the huge contrast between the neutrino flux and the GeV gamma-ray flux. Moreover, with analytical estimations, conclude that any theoretical model on explaining IceCube-200107A from 4FGL J0955.1+3551 must involve an intense external photon field.

sources.summary: The paper studies BZB J0955+3551 as a candidate neutrino-emitting blazar with an X-ray flare, measured redshift, black-hole mass, and modeled photo-hadronic emission.

quotes.quote: After TXS 0506+056, a second blazar, BZB J0955+3551, has recently been found to be spatially coincident with the IceCube detected neutrino event IC-200107A and undergoing its brightest X-ray flare measured so far. Here, we present the results of our multi-frequency campaign to study this peculiar event that includes observations with the , , , and 10.4 m Gran Telescopio Canarias (GTC). The optical spectroscopic observation from GTC secured its redshift as z=0.55703^ +0.00033 _ 0.00021 and the central black hole mass as 10 ^ 8.90 0.16 .

The following section considers general energetic requirements for the production of a detectable IceCube neutrino flux in the jet of BZB J0955+3551. These are constrained by the observed UV -- X-ray flux just after the detection of the neutrino event, on 2020 January 8 and are similar to that reported for neutrino production in TXS 0506+056 by .
