In the following, we will further investigate the possibility of photo-hadronic neutrino production in BZB J0955+3551 with an external target photon field, and check whether the observed optical -- UV -- X-ray spectrum is consistent with constraints from electromagnetic cascades initiated by the neutrino-producing pion and muon decay processes.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3HSP J095507.9+355101 as an extreme, genuine BL Lac blazar coincident with IceCube-200107A and observed in a hard, variable X-ray flaring state.

quotes.quote: The uncertainty region of the highly energetic neutrino IceCube200107A includes 3HSP J095507.9+355101 ( z = 0.557), an extreme blazar, which was detected in a high, very hard and variable X-ray state shortly after the neutrino arrival. Following a detailed multi-wavelength investigation, we confirm that the source is a genuine BL Lac, contrary to TXS 0506+056, the first source so far associated with IceCube neutrinos, which is a ``masquerading'' BL Lac. As in the case of TXS 0506+056, 3HSP J095507.9+355101 is also way off the so-called ``blazar sequence''. We consider 3HSP J095507.9+355101 a possible counterpart to the IceCube neutrino.

The 2-10 keV emission from 3HSP J095507.9+355101 exhibited over a factor of ten variability in intensity associated with strong spectral changes following a harder-when-brighter trend (see Tab. imaginggresults , spectralresults , and Fig. fig:flvsslope ). The ToO observation of 3HSP J095507.9+355101 found this object in a flaring and hard state, with a 2 -- 10 keV X-ray flux of 5 10^ 12 a factor 2.5 larger than the average value observed in 2012 -- 2013, and with a power law spectral index =1.8 0.06 .

sources.summary: The paper models 3HSP J095507.9+355101 as an extreme blazar possibly associated with IceCube-200107A, exploring leptohadronic and alternative multimessenger scenarios for its X-ray flare and neutrino output.

quotes.quote: 3HSP J095507.9+355101 is an extreme blazar which has been possibly associated with a high-energy neutrino (IceCube-200107A) detected one day before the blazar was found to undergo a hard X-ray flare. We perform a comprehensive study of the predicted multimessenger emission from 3HSP J095507.9+355101 during its recent X-ray flare, but also in the long term. We focus on one-zone leptohadronic models, but we also explore alternative scenarios: (i) a blazar-core model, which considers neutrino production in the inner jet, close to the supermassive black hole; (ii) a hidden external-photon model, which considers neutrino production in the jet through interactions with photons from a weak broad line region; (iii) a proton synchrotron model, where high-energy protons in the jet produce rays via synchrotron; and (iv) an intergalactic cascade scenario, where neutrinos are produced in the intergalactic medium by interactions of a high-energy cosmic-ray beam escaping the jet. The Poisson probability to detect one muon neutrino in ten years from 3HSP J095507.9+355101 with the real-time IceCube alert analysis is 1 ( 3 ) for the most optimistic one-zone leptohadronic model (the multi-zone blazar-core model). Meanwhile, detection of one neutrino during the 44-day-long high X-ray flux-state period following the neutrino detection is 0.06 , according to our most optimistic leptohadronic model.

sources.summary: The review identifies 3HSP J095507.9+35510 as the likely IceCube-200107 counterpart, citing its X-ray flare and modeling constraints on jet power and target photons.

quotes.quote: It was found to be spatially coincident with the high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac object 3HSP J095507.9+35510 (= BZB J0955+3551) at a redshift of z = 0.5573 . Swift XRT ToO observations one day and NuSTAR ToO observations 4 days after the neutrino alert found the blazar in a bright X-ray flare, but exhibiting only moderate ray activity . Incidentally, there was one other ray blazar within the error region of the neutrino event, namely 4FGL J0957.8+3423, which, however, did not exhibit any significant flux enhancement in any waveband . 3HSP J 095507.9+35510 is therefore considered the more likely counterpart of IceCube-200107. Estimates of requirements on jet power in relativistic protons and on the target photon field in BZB J0955+3551 to produce a neutrino flux corresponding to an association with IceCube-200107, along with numerical simulations, were presented in .

sources.summary: The review identifies 3HSP J095507.9+355101 as an extreme blazar possibly associated with IceCube-200107A, noting an accompanying hard X-ray flare but low alert-detection probability.

quotes.quote: 3HSP J095507.9+355101 is an extreme blazar, which may be associated with a high-energy neutrino event, IceCube-200107A . This blazar showed a hard X-ray flare around the detection of the neutrino event. However, the Poisson probability to detect a single muon neutrino with the effective area for realtime alerts is as low as 1 even if 10 years of IceCube observations are assumed .

gmw_00000937.name: UGC 8838
gmw_00000937.overview: UGC 8838 is a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2309.05962]. It contains neutral hydrogen gas that has been observed and analyzed using the Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical radio Telescope [arXiv:2309.05962].

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 8838 to demonstrate FAST follow-up of ALFALFA galaxies, improving its H I spectral signal-to-noise from 7.5 to 14.8.

quotes.quote: To illustrate the advantage of using longer integration time with the FAST telescope for H i , we choose two galaxies for comparison from the ALFALFA sample: UGC 8838 and AGC 125644. The spectral profile of UGC 8838 in Figure fig:specalf exhibits a relatively low signal-to-noise ratio (S/N), while AGC 125644 displays a dubious sub-component between 6900 km/s and 7000 km/s, also with a very low S/N. Figure fig:specalf illustrates that, by utilizing a longer integration time, FAST effectively improved the signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) of UGC 8838 from 7.5 to 14.8.

gmw_00000938.name: AGC 125644
gmw_00000938.overview: AGC 125644 is a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2309.05962]. Observations with the Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical radio Telescope confirmed a previously suspicious neutral hydrogen sub-component, which expanded the galaxy's measured line width by a factor of two [arXiv:2309.05962].

sources.summary: The paper uses AGC 125644 as a FAST comparison galaxy, confirming a suspicious H I sub-component and revising its line width relative to ALFALFA.

quotes.quote: To illustrate the advantage of using longer integration time with the FAST telescope for H i , we choose two galaxies for comparison from the ALFALFA sample: UGC 8838 and AGC 125644. The spectral profile of UGC 8838 in Figure fig:specalf exhibits a relatively low signal-to-noise ratio (S/N), while AGC 125644 displays a dubious sub-component between 6900 km/s and 7000 km/s, also with a very low S/N. Figure fig:specalf illustrates that, by utilizing a longer integration time, FAST effectively improved the signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) of UGC 8838 from 7.5 to 14.8. For AGC 125644, we confirmed the existence of the suspicious sub-component, despite the S/N in ALFALFA already being as high as 7.1. This discovery led to an expansion of its line width by a factor of two compared to its measurement in ALFALFA.

gmw_00000939.name: DDO 216
gmw_00000939.overview: DDO 216 is a transition-type dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:2101.03189, arXiv:2402.06953] located in the Local Group [arXiv:1411.1875, arXiv:2101.03189] as a companion of Andromeda [arXiv:2006.01735]. It features a small neutral hydrogen disk containing three holes [arXiv:2006.01735], with its gas resolved into two overlapping, velocity-separated clouds [arXiv:astro-ph/0304266].

sources.summary: The paper treats Pegasus as a peculiar dwarf system whose Cepheids fit SMC period-luminosity templates well, giving P0 and P1 distance moduli near 24.87.

quotes.quote: The first 6 Cepheids discovered in Pegasus, a peculiar dwarf system, were measured in R magnitudes by Hoessel:etal:90 whose discussion led to a modulus of (m-M)^ 0 =26.22 , which is certainly too large. These two groups are compared in the following with the corresponding, calibrated templets of P-C and P-L relations provided by SMC in Sect. sec:2 . The P-L relations in V and I of the P0 and P1 Cepheids are shown in Fig. fig:06 b and together with the fitted SMC templets. Eye inspection shows the fits to be excellent. The scatter in V of the Pegasus Cepheids about the SMC line is essentially the same as for the SMC Cepheids.

A comparison of the individual P0 Cepheids with the calibrated SMC P-L relations leads to the individual distance moduli plotted versus P in Fig. fig:06 e f. The mean distances in V and I , as shown in the respective panels of Fig. fig:06 , give a combined distance of (m-M)^ P0 =24.86 0.06 . The eight P1 Cepheids, analyzed using the corresponding templet P-L relations of SMC, lead to the mean moduli in V and I as shown in Fig. fig:06 e f and to a combined modulus of (m-M)^ P1 =24.88 0.06 in good agreement with the P0 Cepheids.

sources.summary: Pegasus is discussed as a Local Group dIrr with substantial dark matter and ram-pressure signatures; its H I maps imply a high Mach number when compared with the plasmon model.

quotes.quote: A second example is the dIrr galaxy Pegasus. Pegasus belongs to the LG of galaxies and it is located at a Galactocentric distance of 919 kpc mcconnachie:05 . It has a total mass of M 3.3 10^8 M _ , and has a large amount of DM ( 70 ) kniazev:09 . The Pegasus dIrr also presents the characteristic signature of ram pressure stripping young:03,mcconnechie:07 . Moreover, mcconnechie:07 ( mcconnechie:07 ), conclude from the ram pressure stripping in Pegasus, and its large distance from either the Milky Way or M31, that there is evidence for the existence of a LG intergalactic medium.

We now compare the plasmon model with the Pegasus dwarf HI maps of young:03 ( young:03 ) (see their Figure 5). We take the contours N_H=4 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 and N_H=1.6 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 , and computed a very similar length-to-width ratio for both contours, L/W=1.5 0.1 and L/W=1.4 0.1 respectively. If we compare this value of L/W of Pegasus dwarf, with the predictions from our plasmon model (top panel of Figure 4), we deduce a high Mach number of M_w 9 for the LG IGM.

sources.summary: The paper uses Pegasus as a dwarf example with stellar mass like Leo A but a different inferred central density, arguing SIDM halos can host it consistently.

quotes.quote: Consider, for example, the two galaxies Pegasus ( 1 kpc) and Leo A ( 500 pc) in Figure fig:rotationcurves . Both of these galaxies have about the same stellar mass M_ 10^7 . According to the expectations of abundance matching GarrisonKimmelTBTF , each of these galaxies should reside within a V_ max 40 halo. Instead, their central densities are such that, if their dark matter structure follows the CDM-inspired NFW form, they need to have drastically different potential well depths: V_ max 30 and 12 for Pegasus and Leo A, respectively see Figure 12 of GarrisonKimmelTBTF .

That is, in SIDM, just like CDM, we would naively expect both Pegasus and Leo A to reside in halos with V_ max = 40 , like Pippin and Merry. In SIDM, unlike in CDM, the predicted density profiles allow this to happen self-consistently. In the right-hand panel of Figure fig:rotationcurves we see that both Pegasus and Leo A could be hosted by Merry with /m = 10 . In the left-hand panel, both galaxies are consistent with the /m = 50 line.

sources.summary: The paper describes Peg DIG as a transition-type Local Group dwarf with a central globular cluster, evidence debated between ram-pressure stripping and recent Local Group infall, and prior rotation measurements.

quotes.quote: The Pegasus Dwarf Irregular Galaxy Peg DIG (also known as DDO 216 and UGC 12613) is a transition-type dwarf that is host to a large, central globular cluster Cole2017,Leaman2020 , making it among the lowest mass dwarfs in the Local Group with a globular cluster (in addition to Eri II; see Zoutendijk2020 and references therein). A notable population of RR Lyrae stars in the cluster have been used to derive a distance of 24.77 0.08 Cole2017 . found an asymmetry between the gas and stellar components indicative of ram pressure stripping removing the gas from the system. However, more recent HI observations by contradict this conclusion, finding minimal evidence of interactions. These authors concluded that this dwarf has only recently joined the Local Group.

The structural parameters derived by provided a good comparison to this current work, however the SDSS observations used are significantly (> 3 magnitudes) shallower than the Solo data. They found the dwarf is more extended than previously thought and noted possible stellar extensions on the north-west end. In their HI observations, observed solid body like rotation. found rotation in the red giant branch stars as well.

sources.summary: The paper classifies PegasusDwarf as a transition galaxy whose SFH resembles dwarf irregulars and whose SFR-HI position lies between irregular and elliptical regimes.

quotes.quote: Among the remaining nine galaxies, NGC 6822, IC 1613, WLM, Leo A, Sextans A, and Sextans B, PegasusDwarf are dwarf irregulars, NGC 147 and NGC 185 belong to dwarf ellipticals. It is worth noting that PegasusDwarf is a transition galaxy (dIrr/dSph). Because its SFH is similar to dwarf irregulars, we classify it as a dwarf irregular galaxy.

PegasusDwarf, situated as a transitional galaxy, occupies a position between the regions of dwarf irregulars and dwarf ellipticals in Figure fig:SFR_HI . This suggests a connection between the morphological classification of galaxies, their star formation activities, and gas content.

sources.summary: The paper argues that Pegasus is an isolated transition-type Local Group dwarf undergoing ram-pressure stripping, with cometary H I morphology requiring a Local Group intergalactic medium.

quotes.quote: We compare the stellar structure of the isolated, Local Group dwarf galaxy Pegasus (DDO 216) with low resolution HI maps from . Our comparison reveals that Pegasus displays the characteristic morphology of ram pressure stripping; in particular, the HI has a ``cometary'' appearance which is not reflected in the regular, elliptical distribution of the stars. This is the first time this phenomenon has been observed in an isolated Local Group galaxy. The density of the medium required to ram pressure strip Pegasus is at least 10^ 5 10^ 6 cm ^ 3 . We conclude that this is strong evidence for an inter-galactic medium associated with the Local Group.

sources.summary: The paper presents PegDIG as an extended Local Group dwarf irregular with old outer stars, solid-body H I rotation, low mass, and no clear tidal or ram-pressure origin.

quotes.quote: The results indicate that the outermost regions of PegDIG are 5--10 Gyr old, while the inner kpc contains stars 1 Gyr old and younger. The colours correspond to K-stars; earlier subclasses are located in the innermost parts of the galaxy. PegDIG appears to be a relatively low-mass object, with a total dynamical mass of 3 10^8 M _ of which only 30 in stars and 2 is in neutral gas.

The extended stellar distribution, the appearance of faint light extensions, and the lack of low column density HI tails rule out a possible tidal origin or a ram pressure stripping scenario. We propose that PegDIG is a fairly recent acquisition by the Local Group, since it does not appear to be disturbed by interactions with other galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats Pegasus as one of seven isolated Local Group dwarf galaxies, measuring red-giant velocities and dispersions and comparing isolated dwarfs with Milky Way and M31 satellites.

quotes.quote: We measured velocities of \ individual red giant stars in seven isolated dwarf galaxies in the Local Group: NGC 6822, IC 1613, VV 124 (UGC 4879), the Pegasus dwarf irregular galaxy (DDO 216), Leo A, Cetus, and Aquarius (DDO 210). We also computed velocity dispersions, taking into account the measurement uncertainties on individual stars. None of the isolated galaxies is denser than the densest Local Group satellite galaxy. Furthermore, the isolated dwarf galaxies have no obvious distinction in the velocity dispersion--half-light radius plane from the satellite galaxies of the Milky Way and M31. The similarity of the isolated and satellite galaxies' dynamics and structural parameters imposes limitations on environmental solutions to the too-big-to-fail problem, wherein there are fewer dense dwarf satellite galaxies than would be expected from cold dark matter simulations.

sources.summary: The review presents Pegasus as a transitional isolated dwarf whose stars and cometary gas distribution indicate ram-pressure stripping and possible evolution from dIrr toward dE.

quotes.quote: Optical and HI observations as described in McConnachie et al. (2007) in the Pegasus isolated -but possibly associated with M3- dwarf have shown that stars and gas are distributed in a different way (see Fig. 4). While stars distribute in a disk-like structure, cold gas exhibits a cometary shape. This is interpreted as Pegasus is moving across the intra-group medium and its gas is undergoing ram pressure stripping. This would represent the best case of ram pressure stripping in the LG, and would suggest the existence of significant hot gas in it. Therefore Pegasus would represent an intermediate dwarf, say a dIrr on the verge of turning - possibly- into a dE.

sources.summary: The paper uses the Pegasus dwarf irregular to validate HB age estimates with main-sequence contamination, measuring an HB morphology age of about 10.31 Gyr.

quotes.quote: We also exclude IC1613, the Pisces dwarf (LGS3) and the Pegasus dwarf irregular, as these galaxies have had recent star formation, and contamination from bright main sequence (MS) stars makes a clean HB selection more challenging for our calibration purposes. In fact, we use the Pisces and Pegasus dwarf galaxies in sec:Recent Star Formation , to validate how our methodology performs in the presence of main-sequence contamination of the HB.

Figure fig: PiscesPeg shows the F475W/F814W CMD of the Pisces and Pegasus dwarf irregular galaxies, which are part of the M31 satellite Treasury sample. The CMDs clearly show the presence of a plume of bright MS stars in our BHB selection box.
The values of t^* we measure through the HB morphology of Pisces and Pegasus are 11.48^ +0.70 _ 0.66 Gyr and 10.31^ +0.74 _ 0.69 Gyr, respectively (assuming absolute luminosities from Savino22 ).

sources.summary: The paper fits a Cepheid period-luminosity relation for DDO 216, derives a distance modulus for the Pegasus dwarf irregular, and confirms a previous TRGB distance.

quotes.quote: DDO 216 Fitting the 1991PASP..103..933M FM PLR to P02, P03, and to P01 (the latter with a doubled period, see below) we derive a distance modulus of m-M=24.92 0.20 0.06_ ZP (Fig. o25_pegasus_plr ) for the Pegasus dwarf irregular. P01 to P04 are four out of six candidates already independently proposed by 1994ApJ...437L..27A . He could not provide any period solutions lacking a sufficient number of observed epochs. Using HST imaging data 1998AJ....115.1869G we find P04 to be artificially brightened by crowding while P01 seems to be pulsating in the 1^ st overtone mode. We confirm the TRGB distance m-M=24.9 0.1 of 1994ApJ...437L..27A .

sources.summary: The paper measures Pegasus stellar rotation larger than earlier work, offset from the major axis, and argues its significant stellar rotation likely means the H I gradient is rotating gas.

quotes.quote: Pegasus: Stellar rotation in Pegasus was first measured in Kirby2014 with a magnitude of 10 across the major axis located at a position angle of 122 , Hunter2006 . We measure a larger value that is 20 offset from the major axis: =16.25_ 2.24 ^ +2.56 , =146^ +16 _ 20 . A velocity gradient is observed in H I across the major axis. It has been suggested that this gradient could be the result of random motions Young2003 , but since the stellar rotation is detected at such high significance ( B_ rot =29.09 ), it seems likely that the gas is rotating as well.

sources.summary: Pegasus is discussed as having one luminous globular cluster but an irregular, radially limited rotation curve, so its halo mass is treated as a lower bound.

quotes.quote: Pegasus (DDO216) Recently, 2017ApJ...837...54C reported a single luminous GC with M_V = -7.14 or mass of 1.15 10^5 associated with the Pegasus galaxy. As noted in 2017MNRAS.467.2019R , Pegasus has a radially limited and highly irregular rotation curve, and so is unlikely to be dynamical equilibrium, at least in its central parts where the HI data are available. For this reason, 2017MNRAS.467.2019R classify the galaxy as a `rogue' and exclude it from further analysis. We include Pegasus in Table 2 but note that its M_ 200 should be treated with caution; it is almost certainly a lower bound on its true M_ 200 .

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 216/Pegasus Dwarf as a Local Group companion of Andromeda with a small H I disk and three Type 3 holes.

quotes.quote: DDO 216 DDO 216 (Peg DIG, Pegasus Dwarf, UGC 12613 or PGC 71538) is another Local Group dwarf at a distance of 1.1 Mpc. The galaxy is a companion of Andromeda and lies in the Pegasus constellation. It is one of the smallest galaxies in the LITTLE THINGS group with \ disk radius about 0.7 kpc. The galaxy has three Type 3 holes.

sources.summary: The paper treats Peg dIrr as a first-infall candidate near M31, notes it may be a newly arriving M31 satellite, and places it among low-mass dwarfs with mostly early stellar mass assembly.

quotes.quote: Leo T and Phoenix come closest to the MW at present day, while IC 1613 and Peg dIrr come the closest to M31 at present day. Regardless of their proximity today, the orbits shown in Figures fig:orbits and fig:xy_yz_orbits do not suggest that these galaxies can be considered satellites of the MW or M31 at present with the possible exception of Peg dIrr (see below).
Peg dIrr was within the M31 virial for 19.5 of orbits, so even if it is on first infall, it may be a (just arriving) M31 satellite.

Leo A, Leo T, Pegasus dIrr, Phoenix, Sag DIG, UGC 4879) we see a different pattern with much of the stellar mass being accumulated in the early universe, prior to the epoch of reionization, and low levels of star formation in more recent times Jacobs_2011,Weisz_2012,Weisz2014,Gallart2015 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Pegasus as a small dwarf irregular with mixed stellar populations and derives a TRGB distance from RGB/AGB features.

quotes.quote: Pegasus (DDO 216, UGC 12613) Pegasus ( 23 h  28 m  36.2 s , +14^  44'  35'' ), unlike the previous systems, is a small dwarf irregular galaxy, discovered in the late 1950's by Wilson ( holmberg1958 ). were the first to study it in detail after the introduction of CCDs to astronomy; they found a lack of any bright young stars but did infer the presence of an old and intermediate stellar population from the presence of three red star clusters. More recently, have used WFPC2 in conjunction with ground-based data to study this galaxy. Their results show an object that has a surprising mix of stellar populations for such a small galaxy and suggest that Pegasus consists predominantly of a young-to-intermediate age population.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the Pegasus dwarf as an irregular-galaxy outlier with a high N/O value and notes possible IMF or delayed-nitrogen explanations.

quotes.quote: Among the observations (Fig. f:mhinoo ) we note that apart from II Zw 40 and the Pegasus dwarf, the irregular galaxies show no clear trend with mass, but seem to scatter around a constant value.

The value for the Pegasus dwarf of 1.24 is found by Skillman et al. (1997) to be one sigma above the values of the other irregular galaxies. For this exceptional object, the authors discuss several possible explanations, including a different IMF and time delayed nitrogen production. This would remain valid in the context of episodic infall.

sources.summary: The paper describes PegDIG as a transition dIrr/dSph galaxy 410 kpc from M31 whose position agrees with the polar great plane of early-type companions despite its greater distance.

quotes.quote: This great plane comprises all dEs, M32, and also all dIrrs except for the transition-type dIrr/dSph galaxy PegDIG (located at a distance of 410 kpc to M31).
As Fig. 3 (middle panel) implies, also M31's close companions, the cE M32 and the dE NGC 147 (as well as the transition-type dIrr/dSph PegDIG), lie reasonably close to the best-fit dSph plane.

M32, NGC 147, PegDIG, and even M33's position are consistent with this great plane. When excluding three deviating early-type dwarfs (And II, NGC 185, and NGC 205) as outliers from the calculation of the statistical significance, the remaining early-type galaxies lie within a mere 16 kpc of this plane, and the resulting statistical significance is 99.7 (3 ). The plane resembles the polar great planes of satellites found around the Milky Way and includes also the more distant dIrr/dSph transition-type galaxy PegDIG and even M33.

sources.summary: DDO 216 is described as having HI in the southern optical half, little rotation in velocity-field and XV maps, and a possible discrepant-velocity HI cloud.

quotes.quote: DDO 216: The HI is located in the southern half of the (optical) galaxy (Sandage 1986, Lo et al 1993). There is little sign of rotation in the velocity field and XV maps. In fact, the velocity gradient suggested by the XV map may represent a single HI cloud at a discrepant velocity.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-sensitivity H I imaging of DDO 216, classifies it as a transition dwarf, and finds its H I split into two overlapping velocity-separated clouds with many double-peaked profiles.

quotes.quote: We present the highest sensitivity images of HI emission from the dwarf irregular galaxies UGCA 292 and GR8 and from the transition dwarfs DDO 210 and DDO 216. The HI of DDO 216 is clearly resolved into two partially overlapping gas clouds which are separated by about 20 \ in velocity. Many of the HI profiles in that galaxy are double-peaked.

gmw_00000940.name: VDES J0020 3653
gmw_00000940.overview: VDES J0020 3653 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1901.07456, arXiv:1910.04122] with an optical redshift of approximately 6.86 [arXiv:1901.07456] and a MgII redshift of 6.834 [arXiv:1901.07456]. Although not detected by standard X-ray search algorithms, aperture analysis of XMM-Newton observations estimated its X-ray flux [arXiv:1910.04122].

sources.summary: The paper confirms VDESJ0020-3653 as a high-redshift quasar from DES+VHS selection, derives optical and MgII redshifts, and includes it in near-infrared spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: Our photometric selection method is shown to be highly efficient in identifying clean samples of high-redshift quasars leading to spectroscopic confirmation of three new quasars - VDESJ \ 0244 5008 ( z=6.724 ), VDESJ \ 0020 3653 ( z=6.834 ) and VDESJ \ 0246 5219 ( z=6.90 ) - which were selected as the highest priority candidates in the survey data without any need for additional follow-up observations. The new quasars span the full range in luminosity covered by other z>6.5 quasar samples (J _ AB =20.2 to 21.3 ; M _ 1450 =-25.6 to 26.6 ). We have obtained spectroscopic observations in the near infrared for VDESJ \ 0244 5008 and VDESJ \ 0020 3653 as well as our previously identified quasar, VDESJ \ 0224 4711 at z=6.50 from Reed et al. (2017). We use the near infrared spectra to derive virial black-hole masses from the full-width-half-maximum of the MgII line.

The reduced spectra can be seen in the top two panels of Fig. optSpecs and confirms the identity of both candidates as high-redshift quasars. Based on the onset of the Ly forest we derive redshifts of 6.90 0.02 and 6.86 0.01 for VDESJ0246-5219 and VDESJ0020-3653 respectively.
From these Gaussian fits we infer MgII redshifts of 6.526 0.003, 6.724 0.002 and 6.834 0.004 for VDESJ0224-4711, VDESJ0244-5008 and VDESJ0020-3653 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper includes VDES J0020 3653 as a DES z>6.5 quasar observed by XMM-Newton; it was not detected by edetect, but aperture analysis estimated counts and flux.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present the XMM-Newton of three spectroscopically confirmed high-redshift ( z>6.5 ) quasars recently discovered using observations from the Dark Energy Survey (DES) Reed17, Reed19 though colour selection and spectral energy distribution fitting of DES optical, VISTA near-infrared and WISE mid-infrared photometry. XMM-Newton observing time were allocated through the call of proposal for period AO-17, and they were observed in 2018 May with 28.3ks, 28.0ks and 35.9ks exposure for VDES J0020 3653, VDES J0244 5008 and VDES J0224 4711 respectively.

The two other DES quasars, VDES J0244 5008 and VDES J0020 3653, are not detected using the edetect chain task. From the eregionanalyse task, VDES J0020 3653 have about 100 net counts in the 0.2 10keV energy range, corresponding to a flux of 6 10^ 15 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 .

gmw_00000941.name: VDES J0244 5008
gmw_00000941.overview: VDES J0244 5008 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately 6.724 [arXiv:1901.07456, arXiv:1910.04122]. Spectroscopic observations of the active nucleus reveal a large C IV blueshift of 3200 km/s [arXiv:1901.07456], while X-ray observations show it is not detected by standard search algorithms [arXiv:1910.04122].

sources.summary: The paper confirms VDESJ0244-5008 as a z>6.5 quasar, obtains near-infrared spectroscopy, infers an MgII redshift, and measures a large C IV blueshift.

quotes.quote: Our photometric selection method is shown to be highly efficient in identifying clean samples of high-redshift quasars leading to spectroscopic confirmation of three new quasars - VDESJ \ 0244 5008 ( z=6.724 ), VDESJ \ 0020 3653 ( z=6.834 ) and VDESJ \ 0246 5219 ( z=6.90 ) - which were selected as the highest priority candidates in the survey data without any need for additional follow-up observations. The new quasars span the full range in luminosity covered by other z>6.5 quasar samples (J _ AB =20.2 to 21.3 ; M _ 1450 =-25.6 to 26.6 ). We have obtained spectroscopic observations in the near infrared for VDESJ \ 0244 5008 and VDESJ \ 0020 3653 as well as our previously identified quasar, VDESJ \ 0224 4711 at z=6.50 from Reed et al. (2017). We use the near infrared spectra to derive virial black-hole masses from the full-width-half-maximum of the MgII line.

From these Gaussian fits we infer MgII redshifts of 6.526 0.003, 6.724 0.002 and 6.834 0.004 for VDESJ0224-4711, VDESJ0244-5008 and VDESJ0020-3653 respectively. For VDESJ0244-5008, the redshift estimate is consistent with that based on the onset of Ly but for the other two quasars, Ly is redshifted by z 0.01-0.03 relative to MgII.
These blueshifts range from 1700 km/s in VDESJ0020-3653 to 3200 km/s in VDESJ0244-5008 (Table BHMasses ).

sources.summary: The paper includes VDES J0244 5008 as a DES z>6.5 quasar observed by XMM-Newton; it was not detected by edetect, with only 45 full-band counts.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present the XMM-Newton of three spectroscopically confirmed high-redshift ( z>6.5 ) quasars recently discovered using observations from the Dark Energy Survey (DES) Reed17, Reed19 though colour selection and spectral energy distribution fitting of DES optical, VISTA near-infrared and WISE mid-infrared photometry. XMM-Newton observing time were allocated through the call of proposal for period AO-17, and they were observed in 2018 May with 28.3ks, 28.0ks and 35.9ks exposure for VDES J0020 3653, VDES J0244 5008 and VDES J0224 4711 respectively.

The two other DES quasars, VDES J0244 5008 and VDES J0020 3653, are not detected using the edetect chain task. From the eregionanalyse task, VDES J0020 3653 have about 100 net counts in the 0.2 10keV energy range, corresponding to a flux of 6 10^ 15 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 . Finally, VDES J0244 5008 have only 45 counts in the full energy range and a flux of 5 10^ 15 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 .

gmw_00000942.name: VDES J0224 4711
gmw_00000942.overview: VDES J0224 4711 is a luminous quasar at a redshift of approximately 6.5 [arXiv:1701.04852, arXiv:1901.07456] that is detected in X-rays [arXiv:1910.04122] and hosts a central black hole with an estimated mass of over one billion solar masses [arXiv:2401.04211]. It exhibits highly blueshifted emission lines, which suggest the presence of ionized outflows [arXiv:2401.04211].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VDES J0224 4711 as the only DES target clearly detected in X-rays, measures its counts, flux, soft-band detection, photon index, and high luminosity.

quotes.quote: From our three DES targets, only one has been clearly detected in the X-rays: VDES J0224 4711. Its detected net counts in the full 0.2 10 keV energy range is 174, corresponding to a flux of 12 10^ 15 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 with a detection significance of 9 ; the X-ray source is clearly visible on the image Figure fig:cutouts . It is also detected individually in the 0.5 2.0 keV energy range with 118 net counts and a detection significance of 8 (see Table tab:xmm-vdes_xray ). The separation between the optical position of this quasar and the XMM detection is only 2.52 , with the uncertainty on the X-ray position beeing less than 1 ( 0.81 ).

The EPIC spectrum for VDES J0224 4711 is shown in Figure fig:spec . We fit it using XSPEC with a single power-law model ( zpo ) modified by Galactic absorption ( tbabs ). Due to the low number of counts, we used the Cash statistic . With the Galaxy absorption component kept fix, we found a photon index on = 1.82^ +0.29 _ 0.27 with C-stat = 135.5 for 161 degrees of freedom and get a flux in the 2 10keV energy range of f_ 2-10kev =(7.38 1.09) 10^ 15 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes VDESJ0224-4711 as a bright z=6.50 quasar whose NTT spectrum confirms a strong emission feature, reddened photometry, and an uncertain extended near zone.

quotes.quote: VDESJ0224-4711 (z = 6.50) This candidate was ranked as the third most likely object to be a quasar in the candidate list with a very good fit to a reddened quasar model (E(B-V) = 0.05) at z 6.4. It is quite bright with z = 20.0 and has a very red i z colour of 3.82. Follow up of this object with the NTT showed a strong emission feature starting at 9100 giving a redshift of 6.50. The reddening fit was recalculated with the redshift fixed at the observed spectroscopic redshift of 6.50. At the spectroscopic redshift the photometry was best fit by a reddened model with E(B-V) = 0.05.

This object appears to have a very extended near zone but the modest quality of the spectral data means that this measurement has very large uncertainties. VDESJ0224 4711 has J _ AB = 19.75 is the second most luminous quasar known with z 6.5 and is 0.2mag fainter than the most luminous quasar know with z > 6.5; PSO J0226+0302 with z = 6.53 and J _ AB = 19.51 Venemans2015 .

sources.summary: The paper revisits VDESJ0224-4711 with improved GMOS and near-infrared spectra, deriving a new Ly redshift and improving its ionization near-zone estimate.

quotes.quote: For completeness we also include in Table tab:propertiesz6p75 the properties of VDESJ0224-4711, which is the other z 6.5 quasar previously identified by us using DES+VHS in R17.

Gemini South GMOS VDESJ0224-4711 was first identified in R17 using DES Y1 data and spectroscopically confirmed as a z=6.50 quasar via observations with the EFOSC2 spectrograph on the European Southern Observatory (ESO)'s New Technology Telescope (NTT). Here we present a new higher spectral resolution, higher S/N rest-frame UV spectrum of the same object taken with the GMOS spectrograph on Gemini-S. Details of the observational setup can be found in Table obsSetup .

The new GMOS spectrum for VDESJ0224-4711 can be seen in the bottom panel of Fig. optSpecs . The new spectrum allows for a better estimate of the quasar ionization near zone size compared to the NTT discovery spectrum. The analysis of near zone sizes for all our high-redshift quasars will be presented in a forthcoming paper. We also derive a new Ly redshift of z=6.514 0.005 based on this spectrum (see R17 for details on the redshift estimation method).

sources.summary: The paper presents J0224-4711 as a HYPERION QSO with extreme X-ray luminosity, high bolometric luminosity, a massive SMBH, and powerful ionized outflows.

quotes.quote: VDESJ022426.54-471129.4 hereafter J0224-4711, reed2017 at z=6.5222 , firstly discovered by reed2017 , is one of the most X-ray luminous QSOs at z>5.5 and the most X-ray luminous QSO at z>6.5 pons2020,zappacosta2023 . It belongs to the HYPERION sample, the XQR-30 sample and the ASPIRE survey yang2023 . It has a bolometric luminosity of L_ bol =3.47 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 , and BH mass of M_ BH, MgII =(1.30-2.29) 10^ 9 \ M_ from the analysis of MgII emission line reed2019,wang2021,zappacosta2023,mazzucchelli2023 and M_ BH, H =2.15 10^ 9 \ M_ , from the analysis of H emission line yang2023 . It has the most extreme broad and blueshifted [ OIII ] lines observed to date, even compared to observations of lower-redshift QSOs, with a velocity shift of 1690 km s ^ 1 relative to the narrow [ OIII ] , suggesting powerful ionized outflows yang2023 .

gmw_00000943.name: PSO J159 02
gmw_00000943.overview: PSO J159 02 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z = 6.38 [arXiv:1910.04122, arXiv:2309.04614]. It is hosted by a regular-shaped galaxy with a large radius, and a projected companion galaxy has been identified nearby [arXiv:2309.04614].

sources.summary: The paper reports PSO J159 02 as an X-ray-detected high-redshift quasar, giving its counts, flux, band detections, positional agreement, photon index, and luminosity.

quotes.quote: Among the six other quasars, only PSO J159.2257 02.5438 ( z = 6.38 ) has been detected with 127 counts in the 0.2 10 keV band, corresponding to a flux of 11 10^ 15 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 , and a detection significance of 8 . It is also detected individually in the 0.5 2.0 keV and 2.0 5.0 keV energy bands with 84 counts ( 7 significance) and 27 counts ( 4 significance) respectively. As for VDES J0224-4711, the separation between the optical and X-ray positions is quite small ( 1.30 ) and the error on the XMM position is only 0.83 . From fitting its spectrum with a single power-law modified by Galactic absorption, we get a photon index of =1.94^ +0.31 _ 0.29 ( C-stat = 117.2 for 115 degrees of freedom), similar to what we found for VDES J0224 4711, and we obtained a 2 10 keV flux of f_ 2-10kev =(5.52 1.72) 10^ 15 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper detects a regular, large-radius host for J159--02 and identifies a projected companion in the PSF-subtracted image, using two available visits.

quotes.quote: J159--02 (z=6.381) was initially reported by banados16 . The best-fit image model suggests a regular-shaped host galaxy with large radii. The PSF-subtracted image shows a projected companion galaxy. By the time of writing this paper, J159--02 has only been observed by two visits, where the quasar is located on module A.

sources.summary: The paper uses combined FIRE/X-Shooter spectra for J159-02 and identifies it as a continuum-reconstruction exception, where low predicted flux may indicate an unusual C 3 profile or training-set limitation.

quotes.quote: For three of the quasars in our sample, i.e. J1030+0524, J159--02, and J1120+0641, we use combined Magellan/FIRE and VLT/X-Shooter vernet_x-shooter_2011 spectra, while one other spectrum in our sample, i.e. J1148+5251, was observed with Keck/MOSFIRE mclean_design_2010,mclean_mosfire_2012 and Keck/ESI sheinis_esi_2002 .

A few notable exceptions are J159-02 and J1030+0524, where the predicted continuum seems to underestimate the transmitted flux around the N 5 line. The interesting thing to note is that in both of these cases, the SDSS nearest neighbor composite shown in fig:QSANNdRA_predictions also underpredicts the flux in this region -- this means that either the C 3 line that is masked by the region of telluric absorption is unusual in these two quasars, or this discrepancy shows a limitation of the training set of the neutral network.

gmw_00000944.name: PKS 0537 441
gmw_00000944.overview: PKS 0537 441 is a luminous, highly variable blazar at a redshift of approximately 0.89 [arXiv:1011.1100, arXiv:1202.2181]. It exhibits characteristics of both BL Lacertae objects and flat-spectrum radio quasars [arXiv:1102.4428, arXiv:1202.2181], displaying strong broad emission lines [arXiv:1102.4428, arXiv:1202.2181] alongside multi-month optical and gamma-ray flares [arXiv:1011.1100, arXiv:1503.00099].

sources.summary: The paper presents long VRIJHK monitoring of PKS 0537-441, finding multi-month flares, power-law jet-dominated spectra, and a 25-minute J-band variability event.

quotes.quote: We present a large collection of photometric data on the Blazar PKS 0537-441 in the VRIJHK bands taken in 2004 2009. At least three flare-like episodes with months duration, and > 3 mag amplitude are apparent. The spectral energy distribution is consistent with a power law, and no indication of a thermal component is found. We searched for short time scale variability, and an interesting event was identified in the J band, with a duration of 25 minutes.

Here we report on 2004 2009 six filters photometry of the blazar PKS 0537-441, which is unprecedented for the observing time dedicated to the source. PKS 0537-441 is a z=0.896 bright (V 13) blazar, which was studied from radio to GeV rays. The main characteristics of the broad-band spectrum are the synchrotron peak in the IR, the IC hump with a maximum in the rays, the X-ray spectrum sampling the tail of the synchrotron and the rise of the IC, and the presence of emission lines in the optical spectrum. Therefore, the object belongs to the low-frequency peaked subclass of blazars (Pian et al. 2002, 2007).

sources.summary: The paper reevaluates PKS 0537-441 as a blazar with reported high-energy QPV; unlike most targets, no possible local-significance QPV is found in its updated light curve.

quotes.quote: At high energies (HE; E>100 MeV) five blazars (PG 1553+113, PKS 2155-304, PKS 0426-380, PKS 0537-441 and PKS 0301-243) have been reported to show year-like quasi-periodic variations (QPVs) with significance >3 . As these findings are based on few cycles only, care needs to be taken to properly account for random variations which can produce intervals of seemingly periodic behaviour. We present results of an updated timing analysis for six blazars (adding PKS 0447-439 to the above), utilizing suitable methods to evaluate their longterm variability properties and to search for QPVs in their light curves. We generate ray light curves covering almost ten years, study their timing properties and search for QPVs using the Lomb-Scargle Periodogram and the Wavelet Z-transform.

(1) Comparing their probability density functions (PDFs), all sources (except PG 1553+113) exhibit a clear deviation from a Gaussian distribution, but are consistent with being log-normal, suggesting that the underlying variability is of a non-linear, multiplicative nature. (2) Apart from PKS 0301-243 the power spectral density (PSD) for all investigated blazars is close to flicker noise (power-law slope 1 ). (3) Possible QPVs with a local significance 3 are found in all light curves (apart from PKS 0426-380 and PKS 0537-441), with observed periods between (1.7-2.8) yr. The evidence is strongly reduced, however, if evaluated in terms of a global significance.

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 0537--441 as a bright, peculiar LBL with broad lines, AGILE gamma-ray detection, and SED models requiring SSC or EC components.

quotes.quote: PKS 0537--441 PKS 0537--441 is a bright and variable emitter at all frequencies from radio to rays, showing a SED typical of the LBLs. While BL Lacs usually exhibit optical featureless spectra, in the optical/UV spectrum of this source strong broad emission lines of Mg II, Ly and C IV were observed (Peterson et al. 1976, Pian et al. 2002). Its relatively high redshift (z = 0.896) and the presence of broad emission lines in its spectrum, similarly to FSRQs, make this source peculiar, and possibly bridging the gap between BL Lacs and FSRQs.

During the period 10--17 October 2008, PKS 0537--441 was detected by AGILE with an average ray flux of F _ E>100 MeV = (42 11) 10 ^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and a significant rise of the activity of the source in the second half of the observing period, as reported in Pucella et al. (2010). REM and Swift /XRT detected the source in near-infrared/optical and X-rays during a relatively low and intermediate activity states, respectively, with no signs of evident variability in the different observations. Instead, Swift /UVOT detected an increase between the first and the second part of the observing period, smaller than in rays. The SED of PKS 0537--441 in mid-October 2008 seems to require two SSC components to be modeled, to account for the near-infrared/optical bump, the X-ray data, and the ray flux level observed by AGILE.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0537 441 as a blazar/BL Lac-quasar transition object at redshift about 0.894 and reports 20 months of monitoring with strong high-energy variability and curved LAT spectrum.

quotes.quote: An increase of the detected X-ray flux with no counterpart at the other wavelengths has been observed in 2008 October, suggesting once more a complex correlation between the emission at different energy bands. galaxies: active --- galaxies: BL Lacertae objects: general --- galaxies: quasars: general -- galaxies: BL Lacertae: individual (PKS 0537 441) --- gamma rays: observations PKS 0537 441 (also known as HB89 0537 441, RX J0538.8 4405) was selected as a quasi-stellar source in the Parkes 2700 MHz radio survey Peterson1976 and identified as a blazar by Burbidge1992 . According to its spectral energy distribution the source was classified as a BL Lac object, categorized as a low-energy-peaked BL Lac by padovani95 , although the determination of the redshift of this source is based on broad emission lines and the object has been listed as a highly polarized quasar Ledden1985, sambruna94 , having radio polarization up to 3 komesaroff84 and optical polarization up to 18 impey88, impey90 . PKS 0537 441 has also been suggested to be a transition object between classical BL Lac objects and violently variable and highly polarized quasars cristiani85, maraschi85, treves93, giommi95, ghisellini11 .

We presented multiwavelength observations of PKS 0537 441 during a period of 20 months (2008 August--2010 April) including Fermi , Swift , ATOM, REM, and SMA data. Strong variability has been observed in rays, with two major flaring episodes (2009 July and 2010 March) and a harder-when-brighter spectral behaviour, quite common for FSRQs and LSP BL Lacs, in 2010 March. The average LAT spectrum accumulated over 20 months showed a significant curvature, well described both by a BPL model with E_b = (2290 390) MeV and a log-parabola model.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0537 441 as a luminous LBL at z=0.894, a bridge between BL Lac and FSRQ classes, whose multiwavelength spectrum needs SSC plus EC emission.

quotes.quote: PKS 0537 441, at a redshift of z=0.894 peterson_76 , is one of the most distant and the most luminous members of low-frequency peaked BL Lac (LBL) class, and may be part of a small galaxy cluster/group with several companions heidt_03 . Although classic/normal BL Lacs have no features in their optical spectra, this source has several kinds of broad lines pian_02 , and while the lines disappear in high states falomo_89 . Therefore, PKS 0537 441 is discussed as a bridging object between the BL Lac and FSRQ classes (e.g., ). Although no detection of this source has been claimed in the VHE gamma-ray regime, GeV gamma-ray detections have been reported by EGRET hartman_99, casandjian_08 , AGILE pittori_09 , and Fermi abdo_10a, abdo_10c at high significance levels, and GeV gamma-ray flares are often reported (e.g., ).
PKS 0537 441, a luminous LBL, was studied and we found SSC + EC model could explain the observed multiwavelength spectrum where the EC component is dominant in the gamma-ray photons.

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 0537 441 as a monitored blazar with strong gamma-ray variability, two major flares, an external-Compton SED component, and correlated millimeter/gamma-ray emission.

quotes.quote: The importance of long-term monitoring was confirmed e.g. by the observations of the blazar PKS 0537 441 obtained from microwaves through rays by SMA, REM, ATOM, Swift and Fermi during 2008 August--2010 April . Strong variability has been observed in rays, with two major flaring episodes: 2009 July and 2010 March (Fig. fig1 , left panel). The spectral energy distribution (SED) of the source cannot be modelled by a simple synchrotron self-Compton model, as opposed to many BL Lacs, but the addition of an external Compton component of seed photons from a dust torus is needed. The 230 GHz light curve showed an increase simultaneous with the ray one, indicating co-spatiality of the mm and ray emission region likely at large distance from the central engine. The low, average, and high activity SED of the source could be fit changing only the electron distribution parameters.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J 0538-440 as a BL Lac source with variable total intensity, flatter spectrum, low L-band polarization, and Faraday-complex thick components.

quotes.quote: J0538 440 is a BL Lac source Impey1988,Romero2000,Romero2002 . It has a flat, though time variable, total intensity spectrum, at cm and mm wavelengths, as can be seen in calibrator databases such as the ATCA Calibrator Database https://www.narrabri.atnf.csiro.au/calibrators/calibrator_database_viewcal.html?source=0537-441 . It has also been observed to be highly polarized and variable in the optical Impey1988,Romero2000,Romero2002,Andruchow2005 .

J 0538-440 : This source shows two relatively thick components, each composed of a number of closely adjacent minor components (Figure 0538-rmclean ). The source is classified as Faraday complex with _ = 40.8 rad m ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper combines two TESS sectors for QSO B0537 441 and finds a 6.5-day tentative QPO supported by GLSP and WWZ analyses.

quotes.quote: Fig. 3(b) shows the results of the combination of consecutive observations of QSO B0537 441 during sectors 32 and 33. So, unlike other TESS light curves, this one has a duration of 53 days. In the GLSP plot, the signal at 6.5 days is found to be at least 3 significant using both red noise models which is also confirmed with time-averaged WWZ analysis. The colour density WWZ plot shows long-lived strong concentrations of power around 6.5 and 11 days. The tentative QPO of around 6.5 days is supported by both methods with at least 3 significance.

sources.summary: The paper presents REM VRIJHK photometry of blazar PKS 0537 441 during a major 2004-2005 active state, finding strong V-band variability, no clear intranight variability, and a softer low-state SED.

quotes.quote: Multiband VRIJHK photometry of the Blazar PKS 0537 441 obtained with the REM telescope from December 2004 to March 2005 is presented. A major period of activity is found with more than four magnitudes variability in the V filter in 50 days and of 2.5 in 10 days. In intensity and duration the activity is similar to that of 1972 reported by Egg73 , but it is much better documented. No clear evidence of variability on time-scale of minutes is found. The spectral energy distribution is roughly described by a power-law, with the weaker state being the softer.

sources.summary: The paper reports a significant transient QPO candidate in PKS 0537-441, with a roughly 285-day period, high wavelet significance, and four oscillation cycles during high flux.

quotes.quote: PKS 0537-441 We identify a significant QPO candidate in this BL Lac object at z=0.892 (see Fig. fig:CWT0537 (A) and (C)). The wavelet spectrum shows a clear horizontal feature centered at period 285 67 d ( 286 73 d) in the monthly (weekly) binned light curve, at a significance above 5 in the CWT map of both time binnings. This result is consistent with , who claimed a candidate QPO at 280 d in the first few years of \ data, using the Lomb Scargle periodogram (LSP) technique. We can see four complete cycles of oscillation during a period of high ray flux starting from the beginning of \ observations till around MJD 55700 (May 2011).

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 0537-441 as an example with strong optical and gamma-ray correlation, then reports a reduced detrended correlation amplitude.

quotes.quote: An example of strong correlation between optical and gamma-ray variations on both long and short time scale is PKS 0537-441 as can be seen directly in the light curves of Figure 9. The DCCF for the same data is shown in Figure 10. By comparison the correlation amplitude for the detrended light curves is reduced from 0.9 to 0.6.

sources.summary: PKS 0537--441 showed no global color-magnitude pattern over the long monitoring campaign, though low- and high-flux states hinted at behavior amid large flux variations.

quotes.quote: PKS 0537--441: The source was observed during 160 nights in 2008--2012. The analysis of a longterm monitoring does not reveal any global CM correlation pattern; however, at low flux levels some hints for behavior can be noted, while at high flux levels trend seems to be present. Despite large-amplitude flux variations ( 3 mag), the optical slope varied only slightly between _ BR 2 and 2.5.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 0537 441 as an FSRQ TeV source whose 2010 XMM-Newton spectra are log-parabolic with significant flattening and 2.1--2.7 keV breaks.

quotes.quote: PKS 0537 441: This blazar is a FSRQ and a TeV source (Tsujimoto et al. 2015). The spectrum is well fitted by simple power law by Donato et al. (2005) with spectral index of 1.8. We observed three pointings of XMM Newton in 2010 and found the X-ray spectrum of this blazar to be well described by log-parabolic model. There is significant spectral flattening with negative curvature. All the spectra are also fitted with the broken power law model with break energy at around 2.1--2.7 keV.

gmw_00000945.name: PKS 2123 463
gmw_00000945.overview: PKS 2123 463 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1503.00099] that hosts a bent radio jet [arXiv:1101.5822]. The galaxy features a thermal disk component [arXiv:1503.00099] and exhibits X-ray emission associated with its radio structures [arXiv:1101.5822].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 2123 463 as a gamma-ray source counterpart whose optical-to-gamma activity, external-Compton SED model, thermal disk component, and inferred black-hole mass support its AGN interpretation.

quotes.quote: In particular, the strict spatial association with the lower energy counterpart together with a simultaneous increase of the activity in optical, UV, X-ray and ray bands (Fig. fig2 , left panel) led to a firm identification of the ray source with PKS 2123 463. We fit the SED with a synchrotron/external Compton model (Fig. fig2 , right panel). A thermal disc component is necessary to explain the optical/UV emission detected by Swift /UVOT. This disc has a luminosity of about 1.8 10 ^ 46 erg s ^ 1 , and a fit to the disc emission assuming a Schwarzschild (i.e., non-rotating) black hole gives a mass of about 2 10 ^ 9 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper reports X-ray detection at the southeast edge of a radio feature, additional inner X-ray emission tied to a bent radio jet, and uncertainty in the adopted redshift.

quotes.quote: 2123 463 (PKS B2123 463) The southeast edge of a radio feature 4.5 \ from the core at a PA of 110 \ is detected in X-rays. There also appears to be some X-ray emission about 1.5 \ from the core at a similar PA, associated with a small-scale and bent radio jet. The adopted redshift (1.67) may be very uncertain or even incorrect 2002A&A...386...97J , being based on a unreliable objective prism spectrum.

gmw_00000946.name: FIRST J155633.8+351758
gmw_00000946.overview: FIRST J155633.8+351758 is a radio-loud, broad-absorption line quasar at a redshift of z=1.50 [arXiv:1307.1911, arXiv:1310.3739]. It is classified as a heavily reddened FeLoBAL quasar [arXiv:1310.3739, arXiv:1010.5705] that features a weak, Doppler-boosted radio jet viewed close to its polar axis [arXiv:1307.1911, arXiv:1310.3739].

sources.summary: The paper treats FIRST J155633.8+351758 as a high-polarization, radio-loud BALQSO at z=1.50, using VLBA detections of a faint secondary component to constrain its parsec-scale jet geometry.

quotes.quote: The next best choice after Mrk 231 is FIRST J155633.8+351758 (FIRST J1556+3517, hereafter), the high optical polarization quasar at z=1.50, dubbed the first discovered radio loud BALQSO bec97 . Strangely, like Mrk 231, this is a member of the rare class of BALQSOs known as FeLoBALQSOs due to absorption in low ionization species and strong Fe II emission. Being the ``first" radio loud BALQSO discovered led to a flurry of deep optical observations bro97, naj00 . The polarization PA 153^ from deep Keck spectroscopy and it has a flat 25 to 30 mJy spectrum from 1.4 to 5 GHz bro97,bec97 . FIRST J1556+3517 is viewed close to the polar axis ( < 14.3^ ) based on high variability brightness temperatures gho07 .

A previous VLBI observation of FIRST J1556+3517 was unresolved with a flux density of 26 mJy with the EVN at 1.4 GHz jia03 . Better resolution was needed to detect a jet, so we carried out observations at frequencies of 1.6, 5.0, 8.4 and 15.3 GHz with the VLBA at epoch 2011.07 (project code BR156) -- see Table table:observations . A recording rate of 512 Mbps (the best available at the time) was used in order to achieve the high sensitivity required to detect a weak jet. This provided 64 MHz of bandwidth in 2 polarizations with 2-bit Nyquist rate sampling at each frequency. The resultant image sensitivities were 0.11, 0.08, 0.08 and 0.15 mJy/beam at 1.6, 5.0, 8.4 and 15.3 GHz respectively. The data were correlated on the VLBA correlator in Socorro, NM and calibration was carried out in the standard way using NRAO's Astronomical Image Processing System (AIPS) package via the ParselTongue interface ket06 . FIRST J1556+3517 was phase referenced to the nearby calibrator J1602+3326 to provide phase calibration and some astrometric information. Subsequent phase self-calibration of the target source was also possible at all frequencies. At 1.4 and 5 GHz, the source was unresolved with synthesized beam sizes of approximately 7 10 mas and 2 3 mas respectively. As with previous VLA and EVN observations, there is no evidence of jet re-orientation in these images in the form of resolved emission at a differing position angle from the VLBI secondary component. However, at 8.4 and 15.3 GHz, with synthesized beams of 1.0 1.8 and 0.7 1.1 mas respectively, we partially resolve a faint secondary component (see Figure 1) allowing us to determine the direction of the parsec-scale jet.

sources.summary: The paper describes FIRST J1556 + 3517 as a reddened FeLoBAL quasar with a weak beamed radio jet, X-ray absorption, and a reconstructed radio-to-X-ray SED.

quotes.quote: We combine our Chandra data with observations from the literature to construct the spectral energy distribution of FIRST J1556 + 3517 from radio to X-ray energies. We make corrections for Doppler beaming for the pole-on radio jet, optical dust reddening, and X-ray absorption, in order to recover a probable intrinsic spectrum. The quasar FIRST J1556 + 3517 seems to be an intrinsically normal radio-quiet quasar with a reddened optical/UV spectrum, a Doppler-boosted but intrinsically weak radio jet, and an X-ray absorber not dissimilar from that of other broad absorption line quasars.

FIRST J155633.8 + 351758 ( z=1.5008 0.0007 ), hereafter FIRST J1556 + 3517, was originally discovered as a red stellar object associated with a radio source becker:97 , and was identified as the first radio-loud BAL quasar based on its observed properties. We will discuss this classification later. Its spectrum is unusual, even for BAL quasars, displaying not only absorption from low-ionization species like Mg II, but also metastable Fe II species, garnering it the subclass of `FeLoBAL' quasar. It is also one of the most optically polarized BAL quasars known brotherton:97 , and is reddened by A _ V 1.6 najita:00 . Furthermore, based on its radio variability, FIRST J1556 + 3517 can be identified as a BAL quasar seen close to jet on ghosh:07 . The combination of extreme properties makes this quasar an interesting target to study at all wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper presents FIRST J155633.8+351758 as a radio-loud FeLoBAL quasar with a large Balmer decrement and reddening that likely excluded it from the FBQS sample.

quotes.quote: Becker et al. \ (1997) reported the discovery of two more objects resembling Q0059 2735 (FIRST J084044.5+363328 and J155633.8+351758), the second of which is radio-loud. We will refer to these as FeLoBAL quasars. Both of the new unusual quasars were found by making optical identifications of radio sources from the VLA FIRST survey (Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-cm, ; ).

Until the discovery of FIRST J155633.8+351758 by Becker et al. \ (1997) and several additional objects by Brotherton et al. \ (1998), it was believed that the BAL phenomenon did not occur in radio-loud quasars, i.e., those with R^* > 10 . The 29 FBQS BALs demonstrate otherwise.

Egami et al. \ (1996) presented the near-IR spectrum of Q 0059 2735, which displays a very large Balmer decrement (H /H = 7.6), almost the same as that seen in FIRST J155633.8+351758 (Dey 1998, priv. comm.). For case B recombination, H /H = 2.85; and ``normal'' blue quasars usually show H /H < 4. (While case B is not likely to apply to Balmer lines and the Balmer decrement, the empirical result is that the smallest Balmer decrements are consistent with case B and that the Balmer decrement has been shown to correlate with the continuum slope in a manner consistent with an intrinsic case B ratio and dust reddening (Baker 1997)). The observed Balmer decrements of these extreme objects then imply A_V 3 , which is consistent with the Brotherton et al. \ (1997) estimate for J155633.8+351758 based on spectropolarimetry. Because of the bright magnitude limit of the FBQS ( E=17.8 ), and the redshifts required to see BALs in the optical, modest reddening will remove BAL quasars from the sample (especially the LoBAL quasars which appear redder than other classes, e.g., Sprayberry and Foltz (1992)). If it suffered no intrinsic reddening, FIRST J155633.8+351758 would likely have been selected for inclusion in the FBQS.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS 1556+3517 as the radio-loud FeLoBAL QSO in the sample, notes extraction complications, and compares its weak silicate and negligible PAH spectrum to classical QSOs and NLS1 systems.

quotes.quote: SDSS 1556+3517 is radio-loud, while the other five are radio quiet.

Only in one case (SDSS 1556+3517) was there a significant order mismatch, between the SL1 and LL2 orders. For this object, we scaled the SL1 spectrum by a factor of 1.10 to match the blue end of the LL2 continuum There remain however several uncertainties over error propagation in the IRS reduction process.

Conversely, SDSS 1556+3517, with its weaker silicate emission feature and negligible PAHs, is a good match both to some classical QSOs, and to NLS1 systems such as PG1211+143 (which has a high velocity outflow, pou06,shi07 ) and IZw1 (which has optical and UV FeII emission and a decaying starburst mar96,sch98,sur98 ).

sources.summary: The paper cites FIRST 1556+3517 as a highly polarized, heavily reddened polar BALQSO whose Mg II absorption weakens simple equatorial BAL-wind interpretations.

quotes.quote: Secondly, two of the most polarized BALQSOs, FIRST 1556+3517, bro97 , and MRK 231, smi95 , have polarizations 13 in the near UV and are now know to be polar objects gho07,rey09 . Furthermore, both of these polar sources are heavily reddened naj00,smi95 . Thus, these two well studied examples show that one can not use the enhanced polarization of BALQSOs and reddening of the continuum as an argument for equatorial BAL winds.
However, FIRST1556+3517 has deep zero velocity absorption in MgII, bro97 , and it is polar.

gmw_00000947.name: Antlia B
gmw_00000947.overview: Antlia B is a faint dwarf galaxy located in the NGC 3109 association [arXiv:2307.13035]. It is classified as a classical dwarf analog [arXiv:2209.09262] that is gas-poor relative to field dwarfs of similar stellar mass, likely due to environmental quenching by starvation [arXiv:2209.09262].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes first MUSE-Faint spectroscopy of Antlia B, identifying member stars and using GravSphere to model its dark matter density profile, virial mass, concentration, and scalar-field dark matter constraints.

quotes.quote: We use stellar line-of-sight velocities of Antlia B, a faint dwarf galaxy in the NGC 3109 association, to derive constraints on the fundamental properties of scalar field dark matter originally proposed to solve the small-scale problems faced by cold dark matter models. We use the first spectroscopic observations of Antlia B, a distant (d 1.35 Mpc) faint dwarf (M _V = -9.7 , M _ 8 10^5 M _ ), from MUSE-Faint – a survey of ultra-faint dwarfs with the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer. Through measurement of line-of-sight velocities for stars in the 1' 1' field-of-view, we identify 127 stars as members of Antlia B, allowing us to model its dark matter density profile with the Jeans modelling code GravSphere . We implement a model for scalar field dark matter into GravSphere and use this to place constraints on the self-coupling strength of this model. We find a virial mass of M_ 200 1.66^ +2.51 _ 0.92 10^9 M _ and a concentration parameter of c_ 200 17.38^ +6.06 _ 4.20 for Antlia B.

Antlia B (Ant B), a slightly brighter dwarf galaxy (M _V = -9.7 , M _ 8 10^5 M _ ), promises more data, while at this stellar mass, it is still expected that any star formation induced core would be very small (e.g. bullock , orkney2021 ). This makes Antlia B well suited for testing models of dark matter and we here present an in-depth analysis of the kinematics of Ant B bas2022 from MUSE-Faint bas2020 , a survey of UFDs with the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) bacon10 . With these data, which cover a wide range of radii, and adopting the Jeans analysis code GravSphere read2017 , we are able to derive the dark matter density profile of Ant B and, ultimately, to place constraints on the properties of scalar field dark matter.

sources.summary: Antlia B is modeled as a classical dwarf analog around NGC 3109, gas-poor relative to field dwarfs, with likely quenching by starvation after infall.

quotes.quote: The Antlia and Antlia B dwarf satellite galaxies both have low present-day masses relative to the typical values for isolated dwarfs of similar stellar mass (e.g., Papastergis2012,Bradford2015,Scoville2017 ), suggesting that they have been affected by environmental quenching. Based on the M _* - M _ relation of , Antlia has only 2 of the mass of field dwarfs with comparable stellar mass, while Antlia B has roughly 15 of the mass of comparable field dwarfs. While the M _* - M _ relation is uncertain at the low-mass end due to the limited sample of low-mass field dwarfs, it is clear that Antlia and Antlia B are significant outliers from the field population, prompting us to examine how their could have been depleted.

For the Antlia B dwarf satellite, we adopt 6^ +4 _ 3 10^5 M _ for its stellar mass, based on the aperture photometry of . This work assumed a stellar mass-to-light ratio of =1 . This also makes Antlia B a classical dwarf analog, with a stellar mass comparable to the Draco, Ursa Minor, Sextans I, Carina, Canes Venatici I, and Leo II dwarf satellites of the MW McConnachie2012 .

In contrast, we find starvation to be highly effective, producing reasonable agreement with the observed present-day gas masses for infall times between 3 and 4 Gyr ago for Antlia and 4 to 5 Gyr ago for Antlia B, squarely in the middle of the infall time probability distributions indicated in the TNG100 simulations.

gmw_00000948.overview: The object 024254.66-072205.6 is a quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. It is classified as an iron low-ionization broad absorption line (FeLoBAL) quasar [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 024254.66-072205.6 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 024254.66-072205.6 This object is included in the farrah12 sample, and has been observed once by SDSS. It was originally classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by trump06 . It was observed using SCUBA-2 but was not detected violino16 . A six-bin tophat with two-covering model was used. All bins were constrained to have a single density parameter while other parameters, including the ionization parameter, were allowed to vary.

gmw_00000949.overview: 025858.17-002827.0 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. The object has been observed three times by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey and is included in the DR14 quasar catalog [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 025858.17-002827.0 as a BAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 025858.17-002827.0 This object has been observed three times using SDSS, and is included in the DR14 SDSS quasar catalog paris18 . It was identified as a BALQ in the DR10 SDSS quasar catalog paris14 . A combination of an eight-bin tophat (single ionization parameter and single density) and a Gaussian opacity profile were used to model the two BAL components found in the spectrum. The general reddening law was used in the continuum model.

gmw_00000950.overview: 030000.57+004828.0 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:2402.10581] classified as an iron low-ionization broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. It hosts a low-ionization outflow [arXiv:2402.10581] that has shown no variability in its optically thickest portions over multiple years [arXiv:1507.07247, arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J030000.56+004828.0 as a BAL quasar, using high-resolution spectra to constrain acceleration of its low-ionization outflow and test shock-bubble models.

quotes.quote: Our target, SDSS J030000.56+004828.0 ( sdss123 ), was observed at high spectral resolution in 2001 sb2 . We adopt the same systemic redshift of z=0.89185 used in that paper. J0300 has the highest equivalent width \ BAL trough known. Strong \ 3934,3969 absorption is seen outflowing at v 2000-4000 \ with weaker \ absorption at v 1700-2000 \ and v 4000-5660 \ (Fig. \ fig:2spec , and Figs. \ 3-6 of sb2 ). We chose this target for its outflow in which lower velocity gas is inferred to be at greater distances ( intro ) and for its existing high-resolution spectrum.

We compared new and previous high-resolution spectroscopic observations of the quasar SDSS J030000.56+004828.0 (J0300) and placed a 3 rest-frame limit of |a|<0.1 \ or <3 10^ 4 \ on the average bulk deceleration or acceleration of its \ outflow over 9.65 rest-frame years. This is the tightest limit on velocity changes in a quasar outflow reported to date. We discussed a key limitation of our model in matching the \ outflow in J0300: the prediction of too small a range of outflow velocities in the shocked ISM ( sec:vrange ). Our model predicts v_ max 4 3 v_ min , but we observe v_ max 2v_ min .

sources.summary: The paper finds no significant variability in J030000 across DR7 and MDM epochs, while noting earlier work reported a possible transient 2400--2500 feature.

quotes.quote: J030000.57+004828.0 There are three available DR7 observations for J030000; to compare these epochs we scaled the two most recent spectra to coincide with the earliest observation. We did not detect any significant variability with the 8 criterion between any two DR7 epochs so we combined the three spectra together via a weighted average to be compared to the MDM observation; no significant variability was detected. The time-scales between any two epochs ranged from 35 d to 6.0 yr. viv12 also conducted a variability study on this source, utilizing observations in 2007 and 2008 in addition to the DR7 observations. They did not detect significant BAL variability across their 4.2 yr coverage, consistent with our results, however they report a possible variation of the feature between 2400--2500 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 030000.57+004828.0 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, polarization and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 030000.57+004828.0 This bright overlapping-trough BALQ has been observed three times using SDSS, and is included in the farrah12 sample. It was first classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by hall02 , and hall03 presented an analysis of the spectacular narrow Ca 2 absorption lines. Despite the heavy absorption, near-UV emission was detected by GALEX trammell07 . dipompeo11 reported spectropolarimetry observations; the continuum is modestly polarized (2 ), but the emission and absorption lines do not show any different polarization. vivek12 obtained two additional spectra; they found no variability in the optically-thickest portions of the outflow, but reported variability that may be associated with the optically thinner portion or the underlying continuum or line emission.

gmw_00000951.overview: The object 033810.84+005617.6 is a highly reddened quasar [arXiv:1101.4928] classified as an iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. It has been detected in the near-ultraviolet [arXiv:2203.11964] and exhibits weak or absent polarization [arXiv:1101.4928, arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J0338+0056 as an uncertain BALQSO that is highly reddened, has continuum-dependent absorption identifications, lacks obvious C IV absorption, and shows weak polarization evidence.

quotes.quote: Of the seven sources found in Hall et al. (2002), four of them are uncertain in their classification as BALQSOs: FBQS J0105-0033, FBQS J2204+0031, FBQS J0127+0114, and SDSS J0338+0056.

SDSS J0338+0056 is also highly reddened, which makes determining the continuum difficult to do. Depending on where the continuum is defined, there may or may not be strong absorption from Mg 2 , Al 3 , Fe 3 , and Fe 2 . With our higher S/N spectrum, we see no obvious absorption from C 4 , though it is on the edge of the spectrum.
We measure p=0.71 in SDSS J0338+0056, while the maximum ISP along its line of sight is 0.96 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 033810.84+005617.6 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, polarization and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 033810.84+005617.6 This object is included in the farrah12 sample, and has been observed five times by SDSS. It was first classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by hall02 . It was detected in the near-UV by GALEX trammell07 . Spectropolarimetry observations dipompeo11 revealed that it is unpolarized. A model with a nine-bin tophat bins (single ionization parameter and single density) and the general reddening law was used.

gmw_00000952.overview: 080248.18+551328.8 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy and quasar [arXiv:1412.2881] that has also been classified as a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. The system contains an absorption line system located in its host galaxy [arXiv:1412.2881], which exhibits variability in its magnesium II absorption line [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J080248.18 + 551328.9 as an NLS1/quasar with an unusual unshifted absorption system, locating the absorber in the host galaxy and linking it to stellar processes.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report the first such candidate absorption line system toward the narrow line type 1 quasar SDSS J080248.18 + 551328.9 (hereafter SDSS J0802 + 5513 for brevity), and present detailed study of the system incorporating follow-up optical and NIR spectroscopy. SDSS J0802 + 5513 was initially identified as a quasar during the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS, 2000AJ....120.1579Y ) based on spectroscopic observation on 2003-03-24, and was included in the SDSS quasar catalogue with a redshift of z=0.6640 0.0005 2007AJ....134..102S, 2010AJ....139.2360S .

With narrow Balmer lines, strong optical Fe II multiplets, and weak [ O III ] doublets, its emission line spectrum is typical for that of a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy (NLS1). We have derived reliable measurements of the gas-phase column densities of the absorbing ions/levels. Photoionization modeling indicates that the absorber has a density of n_ H (1.0-2.5) 10^5  cm ^ 3 and a column density of N_ H (1.0-3.2) 10^ 21   cm ^ 2 , and is located at R 100-250 pc from the central super-massive black hole. The location of the absorber, the symmetric profile of the absorption lines, and the coincidence of the absorption and emission line centroid jointly suggest that the absorption gas is originated from the host galaxy and is plausibly accelerated by stellar processes, such as stellar winds and/or supernova explosions.

sources.summary: The paper treats 080248.18+551328.8 as a BAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 080248.18+551328.8 This object was observed twice by SDSS, and was first classified as a BALQ by gibson09 . liu15 reported He 1 * absorption in the SDSS spectrum. yi19 reported variability in the Mg 2 absorption line equivalent width with 3.4 confidence. A five-bin tophat model (single ionization parameter and single density) was used.

gmw_00000953.overview: 080957.39+181804.4 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. Observations of the object reveal the presence of helium absorption in its spectrum [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 080957.39+181804.4 as a quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 080957.39+181804.4 This object was observed twice by SDSS, and is included in the DR14 SDSS quasar catalog paris18 . liu15 reported He 1 * absorption in the SDSS spectrum. villforth19 presented near-IR imaging observations obtained using HST ; the image is dominated by the PSF. A nine-bin tophat (single ionization parameter and single density) with two-covering model was used.

gmw_00000954.overview: 081312.61+432640.1 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. It is classified specifically as an iron low-ionization broad-absorption line (FeLoBAL) quasar that hosts a single broad-absorption line outflow [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 081312.61+432640.1 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 081312.61+432640.1 This object is included in the farrah12 sample, and has been observed three times by SDSS. It was first classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by trump06 .
