Two Gaussian profiles were used to model the absorption feature, mainly following the shapes of the Mg 2 2796,2803 doublet lines. They were combined and analyzed as a single BAL outflow.

gmw_00000955.overview: 083522.77+424258.3 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. The object features helium absorption lines and has shown no broad-absorption line variability over a rest-frame period of five years [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 083522.77+424258.3 as a quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 083522.77+424258.3 This object was discovered in the First Bright Quasar Survey FBQS J083522.7+424258 white00 , and is included in the farrah12 sample. It has been observed three times by SDSS. The He 1 * absorption lines were noted by liu15 . vivek12 reported no BAL variability over 5 years in the object's rest frame. Two Gaussian profiles were used based on the shape of the Mg 2 doublet lines.

gmw_00000956.overview: The object 084044.41+363327.8 is a radio-loud broad-absorption line (BAL) quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0002470, arXiv:2203.11964]. It is highly polarized with a complex polarization structure [arXiv:2203.11964], and multi-epoch spectral observations show no conclusive variability over timescales up to 5.3 years [arXiv:1507.07247].

sources.summary: The paper describes FIRST J084044.5+363328 as an unusual FeLoBAL quasar found through FIRST radio-source optical identification, with nearby radio emission affecting NVSS flux and spectral-index measurement.

quotes.quote: Becker et al. \ (1997) reported the discovery of two more objects resembling Q0059 2735 (FIRST J084044.5+363328 and J155633.8+351758), the second of which is radio-loud. We will refer to these as FeLoBAL quasars. Both of the new unusual quasars were found by making optical identifications of radio sources from the VLA FIRST survey (Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-cm, ; ).

FIRST J084044.5+363328 -- A second radio source positioned 27 away from 0840+3633 has a FIRST flux density of 2.5 mJy and appears extended. This source is too close for the NVSS to resolve from 0840+3633 and probably explains the higher NVSS flux density. The lower B-configuration 20 cm flux density was used to determine the spectral index in Table 2.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes eight spectra of J084044 and finds possible scattered changes, but concludes they are not conclusive and agree with earlier non-variability results.

quotes.quote: J084044.41+363327.8 Our sample contains eight spectra for J084044, spanning time-scales from 10 d to 5.3 yr. No variability was detected between the HET epochs using the 8 criterion so we combined them into one weighted-averaged spectrum for comparisons (weighted averages were also employed for HET epochs of J121442 and J142703, due to lack of variability). Possible absorption line changes were detected over our available time-scales in scattered regions between 2210--2800 . The region at 2800 \ cannot be conclusively attributed to the Mg ii 2796, 2803 BAL due to contamination with the Mg ii BEL. In addition, based upon follow-up inspection, we conclude that the variability elsewhere is potentially due to systematic uncertainties in matching instrumental resolutions.

sources.summary: The paper treats 084044.41+363327.8 as a BAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, polarization and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 084044.41+363327.8 This object was identified as a radio-loud BALQ in the FIRST Survey FBQS J0840+3633; becker97 , and is included in the farrah12 sample. It was observed by SDSS three times. It is highly polarized, with complex polarization structure across the troughs brotherton97 . It was detected by Chandra , with inferred _ ox =2.11 green01 . dekool02f1 performed a heroic in-depth analysis of a Keck echelle spectrum.

gmw_00000957.overview: 091658.43+453441.1 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. The object is classified as an active galactic nucleus and is included in the SDSS DR14 quasar catalog [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 091658.43+453441.1 as a quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 091658.43+453441.1 This object was observed once using SDSS, and is included in the DR14 SDSS quasar catalog paris18 . Two Gaussian profiles were used based on the shape of the Mg 2 doublet lines. One of the Gaussian profile components was added to model the weak absorption feature seen only in Mg 2 lines, thus this component was not included in the analysis.

gmw_00000958.overview: 091854.48+583339.6 is a FeLoBAL quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. The active galactic nucleus was detected in the near-ultraviolet by the GALEX satellite [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 091854.48+583339.6 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 091854.48+583339.6 This object is included in the farrah12 sample, and has been observed once by SDSS. It was first classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by trump06 . It was detected in the near-UV by GALEX trammell07 . A single Gaussian model was used.

gmw_00000959.overview: 094404.25+500050.3 is a quasar that was observed using the Sloan Digital Sky Survey [arXiv:2203.11964]. It is classified as an iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar, or FeLoBAL quasar [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 094404.25+500050.3 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 094404.25+500050.3 This object was observed once using SDSS, and is included in the DR14 SDSS quasar catalog paris18 . It was identified as an FeLoBALQ using the convolutional neural network FeLoNET dabbieri_prep . The model included an eleven-bin tophat, the general reddening law and a template spectrum for the long-wavelength region ( 4700 ).

gmw_00000960.overview: 100605.66+051349.0 is a broad-absorption line quasar classified as an FeLoBAL quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. The active galactic nucleus was detected in the FIRST survey and exhibits an unresolved core morphology [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 100605.66+051349.0 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, radio properties and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 100605.66+051349.0 This object is included in the farrah12 sample, and has been observed once by SDSS. It was first classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by trump06 . It was detected in the FIRST survey and has an unresolved core morphology kimball11 . An eight-bin tophat model (single ionization parameter and single density) was used.

gmw_00000961.overview: 101927.37+022521.4 is a luminous active galactic nucleus classified as a low-ionization broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1710.08563, arXiv:2203.11964]. It exhibits blueshifted Balmer absorption features from its outflow [arXiv:1710.08563, arXiv:2203.11964] and has been detected in radio observations with a resolved core morphology [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1019+0225 as a luminous z~1.5 LoBAL quasar with strong Balmer absorption, blueshifted H absorption, and an inferred neutral hydrogen column density.

quotes.quote: We here present the discovery of two luminous LoBAL QSOs at z 1.5 which show strong Balmer absorption features in their near-IR spectra, namely SDSS J085910.40+423911.3 and SDSS J101927.37+022521.4, hereafter SDSS J0859+4239 and SDSS J1019+0225. Both objects have been observed as part of the sample presented in Schulze:2017 (hereafter S17). In S17 we obtained near-IR spectroscopy, covering H , H and H , for a sample of 12 LoBAL QSOs at z 1.5 and 10 at z 2.3 . The targets were selected from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) quasar catalog Schneider:2010 , showing continuous broad absorption in Mg 2 or Al 3 as measured by Allen:2011 using the balnicity index BI; Weymann:1991 . We identified the two Balmer absorption systems within these objects.
SDSS J1019+0225 shows blueshifted absorption by 1400 km s ^ 1 with an H rest-frame equivalent width of 13 .

We estimate the covering fractions and optical depth of the absorber and derive neutral hydrogen column densities, N_ H I 1.3 10^ 18 cm ^ 2 for SDSS J1019+0225 and N_ H I 9 10^ 17 cm ^ 2 for SDSS J0859+4239 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 101927.37+022521.4 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, radio properties and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 101927.37+022521.4 This object is included in the farrah12 sample, and has been observed once by SDSS. It was first classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by trump06 . It was detected in the FIRST survey and has a resolved core morphology kimball11 . schulze17 presented near-infrared observations of this object, determining that the rest-frame optical-band-based redshift is 1.364. The spectrum also shows substantial Balmer absorption from the BAL outflow schulze18 .

gmw_00000962.overview: 102036.10+602339.0 is a iron low-ionization broad absorption line (FeLoBAL) quasar [arXiv:2203.03789, arXiv:2203.11964]. Its spectrum displays overlapping iron absorption lines, Mg II broad absorption troughs [arXiv:2203.03789], and an intrinsic 2175 Å extinction bump [arXiv:2203.03789].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J1020+6023 as a FeLoBAL quasar whose SDSS spectrum, UV-to-IR SED, and Keck follow-up confirm an intrinsic 2175 A extinction bump and reveal overlapping iron and Mg II absorption troughs.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1020+6023 was selected as a candidate of the intrinsic 2175 bump feature according to the SDSS spectrum. Its broad extinction bump was confirmed by UV-to-IR broadband SED and again by a follow-up Keck spectroscopy. SDSS J1020+6023 was imaged at five photometric bands on March 3, 2000, and targeted as a quasar candidate based on its location in the u'g''i' colour cube (Richards et al. 2004) for spectroscopic observations by the SDSS survey. Two observations were taken on March 05, 2002, and the spectrum we used was extracted from the SDSS DR7. This object did not vary significantly between the SDSS photometric and spectroscopic observations, since its spectrum is in excellent agreement with photometric data.

SDSS J1020+6023 is also a typical FeLoBAL quasar, classified into several existing BAL catalogs (Trump et al. 2006; Gibson et al. 2009; Zhang et al. 2010; Shen et al. 2011). The blue band spectrum shows overlapping iron absorption lines (2300-2415 , 2550-2630 and 2700-2770 ) and Mg II broad absorption troughs. Except for iron emission/absorption multiplets and narrow absorption lines of Mg I and Ca II H/K, no other emission or absorption lines can be identified from the spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper treats 102036.10+602339.0 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 102036.10+602339.0 This object is included in the farrah12 sample, and has been observed twice by SDSS. It was first classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by trump06 . Despite the heavy absorption, near-UV emission was detected by GALEX trammell07 . liu15 reported He 1 * absorption in the SDSS spectrum. yi19 found that the Mg 2 absorption line equivalent width did not vary between the two observations.

gmw_00000963.overview: The object 102358.97+015255.8 is a quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. It is classified as an iron low-ionization broad absorption line (FeLoBAL) quasar [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 102358.97+015255.8 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 102358.97+015255.8 This object is included in the farrah12 sample, and has been observed once by SDSS. It was first classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by trump06 . A single Gaussian model was used.

gmw_00000964.overview: 103036.92+312028.8 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. The active galactic nucleus has been observed multiple times by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 103036.92+312028.8 as a quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 103036.92+312028.8 This object was observed four times by SDSS and is included in the DR14 SDSS quasar catalog paris18 . A model with a six-bin tophat (single ionization parameter and single density) and the general reddening law was used. We used a long-wavelength template spectrum to model this object to 4000 .

gmw_00000965.overview: The object 103903.03+395445.8 is a quasar that is included in the SDSS DR14 catalog [arXiv:2203.11964]. It has been identified as an iron low-ionization broad absorption line (FeLoBAL) quasar featuring two distinct broad absorption line components [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 103903.03+395445.8 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 103903.03+395445.8 This object was observed once by SDSS and is included in the DR14 SDSS quasar catalog paris18 . It was identified as an FeLoBALQ using the convolutional neural network FeLoNET dabbieri_prep . The model included two Gaussian opacity profiles, the general reddening law and a template spectrum for the long-wavelength region ( 4700 ). Each Gaussian profile modeled one of the two BAL components that had a large velocity separation.

gmw_00000966.overview: 104459.60+365605.1 is a radio-loud, low-ionization broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:0808.2105] that exhibits polarization increasing toward shorter wavelengths [arXiv:2203.11964]. High-resolution radio observations reveal a core-jet structure [arXiv:0808.2105], while optical monitoring shows likely real, localized absorption line variability [arXiv:1507.07247].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1044+3656 as a LoBAL quasar whose EVN image shows a core-jet structure, possible resolved-out weak emission, variability, and polarized central components.

quotes.quote: J1044+3656 is a LoBAL quasar. The VLA observation in A-configuration at 20 cm suggests that this source is unresolved with a flux density of about 15.6 mJy. The spectral index between 20 cm and 3.6 cm is = -0.5 . The radio loudness is logR=1.29 (Becker et al. 2000). The naturally weighted image with the EVN (Fig.1()) displays a core-jet structure at 18 cm with a total flux density of 16.4 mJy.

sources.summary: The paper treats 104459.60+365605.1 as a quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, polarization and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 104459.60+365605.1 This object was discovered in the First Bright Quasar Survey FBQS J104459.5 + 365605 becker00, white00 ; kimball11 reported that the radio emission is unresolved. It has been observed three times by SDSS. A Keck HIRES spectrum was analyzed in detail by dekool01 ; they found that the absorber is located 700 pc from the continuum source. dipompeo10 reported detection of significant polarization that increases toward shorter wavelengths. runnoe13 observed the rest-frame optical spectrum; they report that for a H FWHM of 3615 km s^ 1 and a log bolometric luminosity of 46.57 [ erg s^ 1 ] , the log black hole mass is 8.87 [ M _ ] and the Eddington ratio is 0.33.

sources.summary: The paper treats J104459 as a tentative but likely real variable absorber, with localized Mg II-related absorption changes supported by large-bin tests.

quotes.quote: J104459 shows increases in absorption line amplitude centered at 2650 \ and 2700 , although the former wavelength is the only interval that reaches 6 (see Fig. 1). Tests involving larger bin widths yield a maximum significance level of 19 using a bin width of 10500 km s ^ 1 , corresponding to 70 per cent of the width of the absorption feature at 2675 . Because the variations are localized relative to the wavelength coverage and no other intervals show variability, the tentative variations in this source are likely real. The absorption may correspond to a high velocity component of Mg ii .

gmw_00000967.overview: 105748.63+610910.8 is a FeLoBAL quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. Its narrow absorption lines show full covering of the emission source, and it exhibits a high outflow velocity of approximately 9500 kilometers per second [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 105748.63+610910.8 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 105748.63+610910.8 This object was observed once using SDSS and is included in the farrah12 sample. It was first identified as an FeLoBALQ by trump06 . This object was modeled with a single Gaussian opacity profile. The narrow absorption lines (FWHM 150 km \ s^ 1 ) show full covering of the emission source which is uncommon for quasar outflows, and an unusually high outflow velocity v_ Off 9500 km \ s^ 1 . The key spectral signatures of BAL outflows include the broad width of the absorption lines and partial coverage e.g., ganguly08 .

gmw_00000968.overview: The active galaxy 112526.12+002901.3 is a quasar [arXiv:1608.05487, arXiv:2203.11964] classified as a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1507.07247, arXiv:1606.02717]. It exhibits redshifted hydrogen Balmer and helium absorption lines [arXiv:1608.05487], which indicate dense infalling gas located approximately 4 parsecs from the central engine [arXiv:1608.05487].

sources.summary: The paper identifies redshifted Balmer and He I absorption in SDSS J112526.12+002901.3, inferring dense gas about 4 pc from the engine and interpreting it as torus-origin accretion inflow.

quotes.quote: The absorption line system in the spectra of quasar SDSS J112526.12+002901.3 presents an interesting example, in which the rarely detected hydrogen Balmer and metastable He 1 absorption lines are found redshifted to the quasar's rest frame along with the low-ionization metal absorption lines Mg 2 , Fe 2 , etc. The repeated SDSS spectroscopic observations suggest a transverse velocity smaller than the radial velocity. The motion of the absorbing medium is thus dominated by infall.

The He 1 * lines present a powerful probe to the strength of ionizing flux, while the Balmer lines imply a dense environment. With the help of photoionization simulations, we find the absorbing medium is exposed to the radiation with ionization parameter U 10^ 1.8 , and the density is n( H ) 10^9 \ cm ^ 3 . Thus the absorbing medium is located 4 \ pc away from the central engine.

sources.summary: The paper classifies J112526 as a tentative, ambiguous variable case, with DR7-to-DR12 changes possibly linked to Mg II BAL weakening or emission-line strengthening.

quotes.quote: J112526.12+002901.3 The time-scales between our three available epochs for J112526 range from 0.55--6.4 yr. The only significant variability was detected between the DR7 and DR12 comparison, a timescale of 5.8 yr, with variable regions ranging from 2770--2810 . This could correspond to a weakening in the Mg ii 2796, 2803 BAL and/or strengthening of the Mg ii BEL. hal13 came to the same conclusion after scaling the DR12 spectrum using a function expressed as a constant times a power law, different than the first order linear spline function we used to scale the DR12 epoch. Inspection of Fig. 1 shows that there are tentative decreases in absorption line strength coming from the Fe ii (UV1,2,3,62,63) multiplets.

sources.summary: The paper treats 112526.12+002901.3 as a quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, X-ray constraints and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 112526.12+002901.3 This object was featured in hall02 where they commented on the He 1 * 3889 and Ca 2 H K absorption lines, as well as the low covering fraction and high excitation Fe 2 lines. hall13 discussed the redshifted absorption found in this object and a handful of other BAL outflows. shi16 compared the SDSS and BOSS spectra, and determined that variability between the two arises from a change in broad line emission, which is not absorbed by the compact outflow in this object. zhang17 reported the X-ray detection in a 3.8 ks Chandra observation; the object is 34.1 times X-ray weaker than an unabsorbed quasar. Near-IR observations obtained using HST revealed that the image is dominated by the PSF villforth19 .

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J112526.13+002901.3 as a FeLoBAL comparison with similar spectral morphology, zero balnicity but nonzero absorptive index, He I-bearing narrow troughs, and no matching post-starburst features.

quotes.quote: BALs have a large diversity of spectral morphologies, and is most similar to SDSS J112526.13+002901.3 Hall02 with regard to the approximate shape and strength of the emission and absorption features. That SDSS quasar has zero balnicity index, but an absorptive index of almost 500 .

Hall02 also classified SDSS J112526.13+002901.3 as a many narrow troughs FeLoBAL with HeI absorption. We do not see evidence for either of these characteristics in . SDSS J112526.13+002901.3 also does not have the same post-starburst features that make unique.

gmw_00000969.overview: 112828.31+011337.9 is an FeLoBAL quasar [arXiv:2203.11964] that exhibits redshifted absorption [arXiv:2203.11964] and variability [arXiv:2203.11964]. Near-infrared observations reveal that the object has a resolved, somewhat disturbed morphology [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 112828.31+011337.9 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, X-ray constraints and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 112828.31+011337.9 This object identified as an FeLoBALQ by hall02 . hall13 discussed the redshifted absorption also found in this object. yi19 found evidence for variability among the three SDSS observations. zhang17 reported the results of a 5.4 ks Chandra observation; the object was not detected, implying that it is more than 50 times weaker than a comparable unabsorbed object. Near-IR observations obtained using HST revealed a resolved target with a somewhat disturbed morphology villforth19 .

gmw_00000970.overview: 112901.71+050617.0 is a FeLoBAL quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. The object has an unresolved core morphology in radio observations, and no variability was detected between its SDSS observations [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 112901.71+050617.0 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, radio properties and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 112901.71+050617.0 This object was first classified as an FeLoBALQ by trump06 . It was detected by the FIRST survey and has an unresolved core morphology kimball11 . No variability was detected between two SDSS observations yi19 . An eight-bin tophat (single ionization parameter and single density) model with two covering factors was used.

gmw_00000971.overview: The object 114556.25+110018.4 is a quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. It is classified as a low-ionization broad absorption line quasar containing iron absorption lines [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 114556.25+110018.4 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 114556.25+110018.4 This object is included in the farrah12 sample, and was observed once by SDSS. It was first classified as an FeLoBALQ by trump06 . A single Gaussian opacity model was used. The line emission is unabsorbed by the BAL and no reddening component was included in the model.

gmw_00000972.overview: 115436.60+030006.3 is a FeLoBAL quasar characterized by overlapping absorption troughs [arXiv:2203.11964, arXiv:1005.3540]. Its infrared spectrum shows weak silicate emission, negligible polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon features, and no detected variability across multiple observations [arXiv:2203.11964, arXiv:1005.3540].

sources.summary: The paper treats 115436.60+030006.3 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 115436.60+030006.3 This spectacular overlapping trough FeLoBALQ was first identified by hall02 . It has been observed twice by SDSS and was included in the farrah12 sample. mcgraw15 did not observe variability among three observations, including one taken at MDM observatory. A model with a seven-bin tophat and the general reddening law was used. All bins were constrained to have a single density parameter but the three highest-velocity bins were group together and allowed to have a different ionization parameter than the rest of the four lower-velocity bins.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS 1154+0300 as a FeLoBAL QSO with overlapping absorption troughs, a low-S/N IRS spectrum, an unidentified 8 micron feature, and weak silicate/negligible PAH behavior.

quotes.quote: SDSS 1154+0300 has troughs where the absorption remains significant at velocities comparable to the spacing between absorption features (so 12,000 km s ^ 1 ), causing them to overlap each other.

SDSS 1154+0300 has an apparent feature at rest-frame 8 m that we cannot find a reliable ID for, though as the spectrum is low S/N it is possible this feature arises from the juxtaposition of a declining PAH and a rising silicate feature.
SDSS 1154+0300 has a poorer quality IRS spectrum but is also consistent with QSOs with weak silicate emission and negligible PAHs.

gmw_00000973.overview: 115852.86-004301.9 is a FeLoBAL quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. It has been detected in the FIRST Survey with a jet of recognizable morphology and a 21 cm flux density of 87 mJy [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 115852.86-004301.9 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, radio properties and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 115852.86-004301.9 This object was first classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by trump06 . It has been observed twice by SDSS. It was detected in the FIRST Survey; kimball11 reported a jet with a recognizable morphology with a 21 cm flux density of 87 mJy. A five-bin tophat (single ionization parameter and single density) model was used.

gmw_00000974.overview: 120049.54+632211.8 is classified as an iron low-ionization broad absorption line (FeLoBAL) quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. Although it has been observed four times by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey, no variability has been detected [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 120049.54+632211.8 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 120049.54+632211.8 This object was first classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by trump06 , and it is included in the farrah12 sample. It has been observed four times by SDSS, but no variability among was found yi19 . A twelve-bin tophat (single ionization parameter and single density) model with two covering factors was used.

gmw_00000975.overview: 120627.62+002335.4 is a FeLoBAL quasar [arXiv:2203.11964]. The object is significantly polarized at 1.7 percent and exhibits broad absorption line outflows with both high-velocity and low-velocity components [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 120627.62+002335.4 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, polarization and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 120627.62+002335.4 This object was first classified as an FeLoBAL quasar by trump06 , and is included in the farrah12 sample. It has been observed once by SDSS. hutsemekers17 reported that the object is significantly polarized at 1.7 0.36 . The model included an eight-bin tophat that is divided into two groups (single ionization parameter and single density per group), the general reddening law and a template spectrum for the long-wavelength region ( 4400 ). The principal Fe 2 opacity was modeled by the higher-velocity group ( 6800 to 1900 \ km \ s^ 1 ) with 4 tophat bins and the lower-velocity group group ( 5600 to 300 \ km \ s^ 1 ) used the remaining 4 tophat bins.

gmw_00000976.overview: The object 120815.03+624046.4 is a quasar included in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Data Release 14 catalog [arXiv:2203.11964]. It has been identified as an iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar, or FeLoBALQ [arXiv:2203.11964].

sources.summary: The paper treats 120815.03+624046.4 as a FeLoBAL quasar, summarizing SDSS observations, outflow modeling and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 120815.03+624046.4 This object has been observed once by SDSS, and is included in the DR14 SDSS quasar catalog paris18 . It was identified as an FeLoBALQ using the convolutional neural network FeLoNET dabbieri_prep . A single Gaussian opacity model was used. A long-wavelength template spectrum was included to model this object to 4400 .

gmw_00000977.overview: 121442.30+280329.1 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1507.07247, arXiv:2203.11964] with an absorber located between 1 and 30 parsecs from its continuum source [arXiv:2203.11964]. Over a 7.6-year baseline, the object exhibits complex absorption-line variability, including large strength fluctuations in its Fe II and Mg I lines [arXiv:1507.07247].

sources.summary: The paper reports complex absorption-line variability in J121442 over 7.6 years, including Fe II, Mg I, and Mg II changes that decrease and later strengthen.

quotes.quote: J121442.30+280329.0 Observations for J121442 span time-scales from 15 d to 7.6 yr between any two spectra. We did not detect significant variability when comparing any two HET epochs from 2000, therefore we created a combined HET spectrum via a weighted average. The shortest time-scale of absorption line variability meeting the 8 criterion was 0.6 yr between 2010 and 2011, and the most significant absorption line changes, at 20 significance, occurred over the 5.9 yr period between 2000 and 2010. The observed changes can be attributed to the absorption lines because inspection of the ratios shows features that mimic the series of lines in the actual spectrum. These deviations are localized relative to the wavelength coverage and therefore cannot be explained by any intrinsic, continuum variability or systematic issues.

We conclude that across the 7.6 yr baseline for J121442 the absorption line variability is complex. The absorption lines associated with Fe ii and Mg i 2852 exhibit large, significant decreases in strength over 2.3 yr (see Fig. 1), followed by increases in depth over the next 1.8 yr (see Fig. A).

sources.summary: The paper treats 121442.30+280329.1 as a quasar, summarizing SDSS observations and modeling its BAL absorption/outflow properties.

quotes.quote: 1pc 121442.30+280329.1 This object was discovered in the First Bright Quasar Survey FBQS J1214 + 2803; becker00, white00 . It has been observed twice by SDSS. A Keck HIRES spectrum was analyzed in detail by dekool02f2 . They found that the absorber is located between 1 and 30 parsecs from the continuum source. branch02 and casebeer08 presented an alternative resonance-scattering interpretation of the spectrum.

gmw_00000978.name: 3C 95
gmw_00000978.overview: 3C 95 is a relaxed, lobe-dominated quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.616 [arXiv:1604.03631]. The galaxy's polarized flux spectrum displays a spectral feature in the Balmer edge region [arXiv:astro-ph/0408105], and its radio lobes extend more than 500 kpc from end to end [arXiv:1604.03631].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 95 as a relaxed lobe-dominated quasar with broad H emission, virial black-hole mass estimates, and radio-lobe jet power inferred from low-frequency flux.

quotes.quote: 3C 95 The quasar 3C 95 is a classic relaxed lobe dominated quasar at a redshift, z = 0.616 that extends more than 500 kpc from end to end hut88,pri93 . The H profile is very wide with a full width half maximum of the broad component equal to 9030 (see Table 1). The details required for an estimate of the central black hole mass are summarized in Table 1.

3C 95 is a large lobe dominated quasar, so the 24.8 Jy of flux at 160 MHz found in NED is due almost entirely from the radio lobes. A typical lobe spectral index of 0.8 and Equations (2) - (4) indicate that Q = 1.6 0.5 10^ 46 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C95 as having ISP-affected polarization and an ISP-corrected polarized flux with Balmer-edge features similar to Ton 202 and 4C09.72.

quotes.quote: The observed polarization for 3C95 is shown in Fig. res-3C95 . We have combined the observations with an order sorting filter GG435 (thus _ obs > 4600 ) and the ones without any filter (thus _ obs < 6400 ). There is again a PA rotation shortward of rest 4000 . This is probably due to a small ISP contamination and we show the data corrected for the ISP contamination in Fig. res-3C95-cor140 .

The polarized flux spectrum again shows a spectral feature at the Balmer edge region similar to that of Ton 202 in Paper I and 4C09.72 in the previous section: a slope down-turn at around rest 4000 \ and an up-turn at around 3600 .

gmw_00000979.name: PKS 0405-123
gmw_00000979.overview: PKS 0405-123 is a highly luminous radio-loud quasar [arXiv:1604.03631, arXiv:1811.10615] and uncertain-type blazar [arXiv:2308.10071] located at a redshift of z = 0.573 [arXiv:1604.03631]. It is hosted within a galaxy group [arXiv:1811.10615, arXiv:2308.10071] and features a compact triple radio structure with two strong lobes and a bright, flat-spectrum core [arXiv:1604.03631].

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 0405-123 as a compact classical triple radio quasar, the study’s most luminous EUV source, with strong lobes, a flat-spectrum core, and high Eddington-rate implications.

quotes.quote: References: 1. mar03 2. dec11 3. ves06 4. she12 5. sul15 PKS 0405-123 PKS 0405-123 is a classical triple radio source at a redshift of z = 0.573. It is the most luminous EUV source in this study. It has two strong radio lobes and a bright flat spectrum core. The source is rather compact only 120 kpc from end to end hut96 .

PKS 0405-123, with its powerful radio lobes (see the next section), is perhaps the most extreme counter-example to the notion that radio loud quasars are associated with low Eddington rates bor02 . This is apparently a very remarkable radio source worthy of further detailed study.
PKS 0405-123 has a strong flat spectrum radio core. The multi-frequency component decomposition from hut96 is used to subtract off the core flux density as a function of wavelength.

sources.summary: The paper finds Q0405-123 has a low overall polarization, a marginal shortward drop, possible ISP-related PA rotation, and unpolarized lines within loose constraints.

quotes.quote: Q0405-123 ISP effect ^1 A PA rotation across 4000 \ is seen (Fig. res-Q0405-123 ) which resembles that of 4C09.72 (and 3C95 or 3C323.1). This could be due to ISP since the data points in the q u plane roughly align linearly, with the alignment line displaced from the origin (Fig. res-Q0405-123-qu ), although no good polarization measurements for Galactic stars in the quasar field are available. The alignment direction suggests the intrinsic polarization is at PA 160 . The line polarization is at least consistent with this interpretation, but does not provide significant limits (Fig. res-Q0405-123-qu ; the polarization of the H + [ OIII ] 5007 line measured at 4731-5032 \ is plotted instead of H only, since excluding the telluric absorption wavelengths at around 7600 \ left a very small wavelength window for H ).

Uncorrected spectra The data without any ISP correction are shown in Fig. res-Q0405-123 . The overall polarization is very low (Fig. res-Q0405-123 ; note the small P of 0.5 even at the red side), but we marginally detected a polarization decrease shortward of 4000 \ (Table tab-pol ). The above consideration of a possible ISP effect might suggest that the intrinsic polarization of this object is at PA 160 . The lines are consistent with being unpolarized, but the constraints are not tight.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PKS0405 123 as a radio-loud QSO field with southern and eastern nebulae, finding VSF slopes that differ before and after gradient treatment.

quotes.quote: Out of the seven QSO fields, the measurements for four fields (PKS0454 22, J0454 6116, J2135 5316, and TXS0206 048) were presented in Chen2023 . The three newly included fields (PKS0405 123, HE0238 1904, and PKS0552 640) are all part of the MUSE Quasar-field Blind Emitters Survey (MUSEQuBES) program, and we use the MUSE-DEEP datacubes directly downloaded from the ESO phase-3 archive with program IDs 097.A-0089(A) and 094.A-0131(B) PI: J. Schaye; Muzahid2020 .

For TXS0206 048 at z 1.1 , the line is redshifted out of the MUSE spectral window, and therefore only results based on are presented. PKS0405 123 consists of three main nebulae that are cleanly separated in velocity-position space see Figures fig:nb and fig:PKS0405_seg1_o2 - fig:PKS0405_seg2_o3 ; also see Figure 2 of Johnson2018 . For the purpose of this paper, we analyze the southern and eastern nebulae of PKS0405 123 separately and refer to them as PKS0405 123 S and PKS0405 123 E, and we do not include the nebula immediately surrounding the QSO in this field due to its relatively small size.

PKS 0405 123 S exhibits a steeper slope but this is also a system that shows a large-scale velocity gradient across the nebula. After removing a unidirectional velocity gradient, the VSF is consistent with the Kolmogorov expectation.

sources.summary: The paper presents STIS spectroscopy of QSO PKS 0405--123, using its bright z=0.574 sight line to study low-redshift Ly forest and metal absorbers.

quotes.quote: We present results for QSO PKS 0405--123 ( z=0.574 , V=14.9 ), as part of a STIS Investigation Definition Team (IDT) key project to study weak Ly forest systems at low z . We detect 59 (47) Ly absorbers at 4.0 significance to an 80 completeness limit of column density ( N_ HI )=13.3 (13.1) for Doppler parameter V_ Dop =40 \ over 0.002<z<0.423 ( 0.020<z<0.234 ). We find 4 intervening O VI systems, useful for studies of hot intergalactic gas.

PKS 0405--123 ( V=14.9 , z=0.574 ) is one of the brightest QSOs in the sky, and illuminates a much longer span of the Ly forest than most of the other QSOs of similar or brighter magnitude. It is therefore a prime target for studies of the low z Ly forest and metal absorbers and mid-latitude ( =295^ o , b=-42^ o ) studies of Galactic absorption. We use 7 \ resolution STIS data to calculate the Ly forest redshift density, Doppler parameter, clustering and void statistics, and to cross-correlate the Ly forest and field galaxy redshifts. PKS 0405--123 was observed with HST+STIS using grating E140M for ten orbits (27208 sec) on 1999 Jan 24 and 1999 Mar 7.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 0405 123 as a powerful HFSRQ whose BLR radiation should strongly absorb 10-200 GeV gamma rays unless the emission region lies outside the BLR cavity.

quotes.quote: As examples, we estimate the photon-photon absorption optical depth of gamma-rays with energies from 10 to 200 GeV for two powerful blazars, HFSRQ PKS 0405 123 and FSRQ 3C 279. Comparing our results with GLAST observations in the future could test whether the model employed and the relevant assumptions in this paper are reliable and reasonable, and then limit constraints on the position of the gamma-ray emitting region relative to the BLR and the properties of the BLR.

A powerful HFSRQ with L_ BLR obtained in literature is PKS 0405 123, and it has a BLR luminosity L_ BLR =10^ 45.67 \ erg \ s^ 1 and redshift z=0.574 (e.g., Georganopoulos 2000). Assuming that HFSRQs have the same properties of BLRs as the classical FSRQs, we estimate the optical depth of photon-photon absorption for the strong-lined, powerful HFSRQ PKS 0405 123. In the calculations, we assume R_ r_ BLR,in and _ BLR /f_ cov =1 , and take three values of h , because it is unclear whether the parameter h equals 0.20 \ pc for PKS 0405 123. From Figure 8 we see that the two diffuse radiation fields of BLR are not transparent to gamma-rays with energies from 10 to 200 GeV in the observed spectrum for this powerful HFSRQ as long as R_ r_ BLR,in .

sources.summary: PKS 0405 123 is presented as a luminous z=0.5731 quasar in a galaxy group where MUSE reveals ionized nebulae, interaction debris, and gas linked to fueling.

quotes.quote: Here, we present the discovery of ionized nebulae on scales of 10 100 pkpc in the environment of , one of the most luminous quasars in the z < 1 Universe PKS 0405 123 at z = 0.5731 has a bolometric luminosity of L_ bol 3 10^ 47 \ erg s^ 1 and a high inferred Eddington ratio of 1 Punsly:2016 . . Joint analyses of the nebular morphologies and kinematics indicate that they arise from cool filaments and interaction related debris rather than outflows. These observations provide novel insights into galaxy and quasar fueling from IGrM to ISM scales.

With deep MUSE observations, we discovered six ionized nebulae on scales of 10 100 pkpc in the z 0.57 galaxy group hosting , one of the most luminous quasars at z < 1 . Although the nebulae are distributed over a wide range of position angles and projected distances from the quasar, all exhibit line ratios that are most consistent with quasar photoionization, requiring a large opening angle and an active lifetime of 3 10^5 yr (the light travel time for 100 kpc).

sources.summary: G4Jy 415 is described as a luminous QSO and uncertain-type blazar with X-ray hotspot emission, extended nebulae, and gas streams connected to nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: G4Jy 415: is one of the most luminous QSO at z<1 see e.g., punsly16 , optically identified by Hunstead (1971b) with a measured z= 0.5731 kinman67,bechtold02,decarli10,johnson18 . G4Jy 415 (a.k.a. PKS 0405-12) shows X-ray emission arising from the northern hotspot sambruna04 , thus being included in the XJET database massaro11a , and it is also listed in the Roma-BZCAT massaro15b classified as blazar of uncertain type (i.e., BZU J0407-1211). Recent MUSE observations revealed the presence of six spatially extended line-emitting nebulae in the galaxy group where it is harbored, suggesting a connection between large-scale gas streams and the nuclear activity johnson18 . It also shows the detection of a narrow filament extending toward the QSO consistent with a cool intragroup medium filament similar to those occurring in cool-core galaxy clusters see e.g., mcdonald10 .

sources.summary: The paper uses PKS 0405--123 as a bright z=0.574 QSO sightline, observing it with HST/STIS to study low-redshift Ly forest and metal absorbers.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we address questions concerning the redshift density of low redshift \ absorbers, especially low column density systems, their correlations and their relationship to nearby galaxies by obtaining high resolution UV spectra of the QSO PKS0405--12 ( V=14.9 , z=0.574 ) and comparing them with a galaxy sample drawn from the literature and from ground-based observations presented here. It is one of the brightest QSOs in the sky, and illuminates a much longer path in the Ly forest than most of the other QSOs of similar or brighter magnitude. PKS 0405--123 is therefore a prime target for studies of the low z Ly forest and metal absorbers and mid-latitude ( =295^ o , b=-42^ o ) studies of Galactic absorption.
STIS spectra PKS 0405--123 was observed with \ and STIS using grating E140M for ten orbits (27208 sec) on 1999 Jan 24 and 1999 Mar 7.

gmw_00000980.name: 3C 57
gmw_00000980.overview: 3C 57 is a luminous, radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.671 [arXiv:1604.03631, arXiv:1503.08295]. It exhibits a complex radio morphology with a double steep-spectrum nucleus, where one of its bipolar jets is likely frustrated by host-galaxy gas [arXiv:1604.03631].

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 57 is a luminous quasar with complex radio morphology, including a double steep-spectrum nucleus and evidence that one jet is frustrated by host-galaxy gas.

quotes.quote: In the process of this study, we use multi-frequency and multi-resolution Very Large Array radio observations to determine that one of the bipolar jets in 3C 57 is likely frustrated by galactic gas that keeps the jet from propagating outside the host galaxy.

3C 57 3C 57 is a luminous quasar at z = 0.671 with a complex radio morphology. It has a double steep spectrum nucleus (two bright components separated by 9 kpc, located within the host galaxy) and a low luminosity radio lobe rei99,hut88 . This cryptic radio structure will be analyzed in detail in the next section.
As mentioned earlier the radio morphology of 3C 57 is more complicated. Figure 2 is a previously unpublished radio image of 3C 57 at 1.52 GHz from the VLA in A-Array from October 31, 1988.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 57 as an atypical radio-loud quasar with extreme optical emission, large blueshift, stable radio behavior, compact steep-spectrum morphology, and a likely major accretion event.

quotes.quote: 3C 57 is an atypical RL quasar that shows both uncertain radio morphology and falls in a region of 4DE1 space where RL quasars are rare. We present new radio flux and optical spectroscopic measures designed to verify its atypical optical/UV spectroscopic behaviour and clarify its radio structure. The former data confirms that 3C 57 falls off the 4DE1 quasar ``main sequence" with both extreme optical \ emission ( 1) and a large \ profile blueshift ( 1500 ).

New radio measures show no evidence for flux change over a 50+ year timescale consistent with compact steep-spectrum (CSS or young LD) over core-dominated morphology. In the 4DE1 context where LD RL are usually low L/L _ Edd quasars we suggest that 3C 57 is an evolved RL quasar (i.e. large Black Hole mass) undergoing a major accretion event leading to a rejuvenation reflected by strong \ emission, perhaps indicating significant heavy metal enrichment, high bolometric luminosity for a low redshift source and resultant unusually high Eddington ratio giving rise to the atypical .

gmw_00000981.name: IRAS08339+6517
gmw_00000981.overview: IRAS08339+6517 is a compact, low-metallicity starbursting disk galaxy [arXiv:2202.00024, arXiv:2311.00856] that may host a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2001.07807]. The system features a face-on spiral morphology [arXiv:2311.00856] and exhibits strong, star-formation-driven outflows originating primarily from a central star-forming ring [arXiv:2202.04672, arXiv:2311.00856].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 08339+6517 as a high-mass merging outlier with anomalously low mass outflow rate, extremely high outflow velocity, and a flat covering-fraction profile.

quotes.quote: The curious case of IRAS 08339+6517 A curious outlier to the above trends is the high-mass merging system IRAS 08339+6517. This galaxy has an anomalously low mass outflow rate, but extremely high outflow velocity. In chisholm15 we define a group of outflows with maximum velocities greater than 750 , that have outflow velocities 32 higher than other galaxies at similar stellar mass and SFR. IRAS 08339+6517 is the only galaxy in this sample that is in that high-velocity group. What makes IRAS 08339+6517 such a strange galaxy?

The line profile of IRAS 08339+6517 does not fit the prescription in profile largely because C _f does not vary coherently with velocity, as prescribed by the power-law scaling (see fig:iras ). This is in sharp contrast to the rest of the sample (see appendix ), which have similar C _f distributions with a C _f power-law exponent ( ) of -0.88. To derive an upper limit to for IRAS 08339+6517, we set the C _f distribution by-hand to match the observations, with = 0 and C _f (R _ i ) = 1.

sources.summary: The paper studies IRAS08339+6517 with KCWI, mapping widespread star-formation-driven outflows whose mass is concentrated in the star-forming ring and whose trends favor energy-driven feedback.

quotes.quote: We study star formation driven outflows in a z 0.02 starbursting disk galaxy, IRAS08339+6517, using spatially resolved measurements from the Keck Cosmic Web Imager (KCWI). We develop a new method incorporating a multi-step process to determine whether an outflow should be fit in each spaxel, and then subsequently decompose the emission line into multiple components. We detect outflows ranging in velocity, , from 100-600 km s ^ 1 across a range of star formation rate surface densities, , from 0.01-10 \ in resolution elements of a few hundred parsec. Outflows are detected in 100 of all spaxels within the half-light radius,and 70 within r_ 90 , suggestive of a high covering fraction for this starbursting disk galaxy. Around 2/3 of the total outflowing mass originates from the star forming ring, which corresponds to <10 of the total area of the galaxy.

IRAS 08339+6517 (hereafter IRAS08) is a well studied blue-compact galaxy at z 0.0191 . IRAS08 is face-on (inclination 15^ 20^ ), bright in the UV, and has a young stellar population Leitherer2002globalFUV, lopezsanchez2006IRAS08paper, ostlin2009lymanalphamorphology, otifloranes2014physicalpropertiesIRAS08 . It is an ideal case for our study, as it has a well ordered rotation field and has a SFR roughly 10 greater than the galaxy main-sequence.

sources.summary: The paper finds IRAS 08339 + 6517 has extended X-ray emission, the largest X-ray excess in the sample, and may host an LLAGN candidate.

quotes.quote: An additional 4 sources are detected only by XMM, which has much larger PSFs that cannot resolve point sources in these galaxies: ESO 338-IG004, LARS 07, and LARS 08 are unresolved, and only IRAS 08339 + 6517 is extended.

IRAS 08339 + 6517 has the largest excess L_X (Figure f_brorby ). oti-floranes14 find that its SFR is consistent with their inferred L_X = 2 10^ 41 \ , which is lower than our result for this galaxy, but the lower L_X would still correspond to a significant excess in Figure f_brorby . oti-floranes14 estimated the SFR and expected L_X from detailed population synthesis modeling, and so it is hard to compare their conclusion with the empirical L_X SFR relation. Their analysis may demonstrate the limitations of using this relation for individual galaxies. On the other hand, this galaxy may host an LLAGN candidate.
IRAS 08339 + 6517 has significant excess L_X .

sources.summary: The paper maps IRAS 08339+6517 in CO, finding extreme radial changes in depletion time and efficiency in a compact, turbulent, starbursting disk.

quotes.quote: We report on the internal distribution of star formation efficiency in IRAS 08339+6517 (hereafter IRAS08), using 200 pc resolution CO(2-1) observations from NOEMA. The molecular gas depletion time changes by 2 orders-of-magnitude from disk-like values in the outer parts to less than 10 ^8 yr inside the half-light radius. This translates to a star formation efficiency per free-fall time that also changes by 2 orders-of-magnitude, reaching 50-100 , different than local spiral galaxies and typical assumption of constant, low star formation efficiencies. Our target is a compact, massive disk galaxy that has SFR 10 above the z=0 main-sequence; Toomre Q 0.5-0.7 and high gas velocity dispersion ( _ mol 25 km s ^ 1 ).

We find that IRAS08 is similar to other rotating, starburst galaxies from the literature in the resolved _ SFR _ mol ^N relation. By combining resolved literature studies we find that distance from the main-sequence is a strong indicator of the Kennicutt-Schmidt powerlaw slope, with slopes of N 1.6 for starbursts from 100-10 ^4 M _ pc ^ 2 . Our target is consistent with a scenario in which violent disk instabilities drive rapid inflows of gas.

sources.summary: The paper maps IRAS08 and its CGM, describing a compact low-metallicity starburst disk with strong outflows, gas accretion, metal-enriched ionized CGM, and a disk-CGM transition.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08339+6517 (hereafter IRAS08) is a starbursting, low metallicity disk galaxy with stellar mass (M_ / M_ ) 10 located at z = 0.019 ( d_ L 83 Mpc). HST optical images (Fig. fig:map ) show a face-on ( i 15^ ) spiral morphology that is compact for its mass, where 90 of the starlight is contained within a radius of r_ 90 = 2.4 kpc. Unlike normal spirals it has quite extreme properties, with a metallicity ( 12+ ( O / H )=8.45 ) is three times lower than the value typical for its mass, a star formation rate ( SFR =12.1 M _ yr ^ 1 ) that is 10 times higher than typical for its mass, and stellar populations that are dominated by very young ( 10-30 Myr) stars lopez-sanchez_2006, fisher_2022 . IRAS08 has a remarkably strong outflow with a velocity of 400 km s ^ 1 in optical emission lines reichardt-chu_2022a and 1,000 km s ^ 1 observed in UV absorption chisholm_2016 , where 2/3 of the wind mass flux is being driven by a kiloparsec-sized region located in a nuclear ring near the galaxy center reichardt-chu_2022a .

We observed IRAS08 and its CGM with the Keck Cosmic Web Imager (KCWI) kcwi in five pointings covering an area of 460 kpc ^2 and reaching distances of 30 kpc from the galaxy both on and off the filament (Fig. fig:map ). We detect optical emission lines 3727, 3729 and 5007 covering the entire area of all five pointings and 4861 covering 90 of the area. The high covering fraction of oxygen emitting gas relative to hydrogen demonstrates that stellar feedback has been efficient at populating the CGM of IRAS08 with metals out to at least a third of the galaxy's virial radius ( 0.3R_ vir ).

sources.summary: The paper maps IRAS08339+6517 at 500 pc scales, linking ionized outflow mass flux to star formation, identifying breakout outflows, and arguing high-SFR regions lose more gas to outflows than stars.

quotes.quote: We compare 500 pc scale, resolved observations of ionised and molecular gas for the z 0.02 starbursting disk galaxy IRAS08339+6517, using measurements from KCWI and NOEMA. We explore the relationship of the star formation driven ionised gas outflows with colocated galaxy properties. We find a roughly linear relationship between the outflow mass flux ( _ out ) and star formation rate surface density ( _ SFR ), _ out _ SFR ^ 1.06 0.10 , and a strong correlation between _ out and the gas depletion time, such that _ out t_ dep ^ 1.1 0.06 . Moreover, we find these outflows are so-called ``breakout" outflows, according to the relationship between the gas fraction and disk kinematics.

Assuming that ionised outflow mass scales with total outflow mass, our observations suggest that the regions of highest _ SFR in IRAS08 are removing more gas via the outflow than through the conversion of gas into stars. Our results are consistent with a picture in which the outflow limits the ability for a region of a disk to maintain short depletion times.

sources.summary: The paper uses IRAS 08339+6517 as the DUVET pilot target, noting resolved ionized outflows, a shallow velocity-SFR relation, lower metallicity, and a distinct continuum-subtraction treatment.

quotes.quote: In reichardtchu2022resolvedmaps (hereafter RC22a) we studied resolved ionised outflows in the pilot target for the DUVET sample, IRAS 08339+6517. We found a shallow correlation between the local co-located _ SFR and v_ out , consistent with trends expected from supernovae being the primary outflow driving mechanism. In our follow-up paper, reichardtchu2022spatiallyresolved (hereafter RC22b), we found a relationship between the star formation rate surface density and outflow mass flux of _ out _ SFR ^ 1.06 0.1 .

Our pilot target IRAS 08339+6517 is the exception to this, retaining its original data reduction from reichardtchu2022resolvedmaps resulting in rectangular spaxels of 0 3 1 35 ( 0.1 kpc 0.5 kpc at z=0.019 ).

Emission line ratios of the galaxies suggest that IRAS 08339+6517 has a lower metallicity than the remainder of the sample. Due to this difference, and to keep consistency with the results from reichardtchu2022resolvedmaps and reichardtchu2022spatiallyresolved , the continuum subtraction for IRAS 08339+6517 used the BPASS templates Version 2.2.1, stanway2018reevaluatingbpass , including binary systems with a broken power-law initial mass function with a slope of -1.3 between 0.1 M_ and 1.0 M_ , a slope of -2.35 above 1.0 M_ , and an upper limit of 300 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 08339+6517 as a nuclear starburst with spiral H and FUV structure, central Ly emission, a halo component, and weak dust-emission correspondence.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08339+6517   -- This nuclear starburst shows a spiral structure conspicuous on the H \ (Fig images_2 ) and FUV continuum images (the latter is not shown here). However the Ly \ image does not resemble any of them, showing a central bright emission and the ubiquitous halo component. The FUV contours exhibit much more details in the arms of the galaxy consisting in many star clusters where Ly \ is absent. The extinction map indicates a dust-free central spot. The dust distribution has no clear relationship with the emission maps.

gmw_00000982.name: NGC 1156
gmw_00000982.overview: NGC 1156 is an isolated Magellanic-type dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1009.4438, arXiv:2208.05858] undergoing intense star formation [arXiv:2208.05858, arXiv:astro-ph/9902227]. Its inner region is dominated by stars, while gas and dark matter dominate its outer radii [arXiv:2307.13738].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1156 as an isolated, star-forming irregular galaxy with LMC-like appearance and H i mass, then measures its H i flux, mass, size, and companion environment.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The fitted position of the galaxy is 02:59:52.3, 25:14:30 (J2000)

quotes.quote: NGC 1156 is a nearby, star-forming, irregular galaxy at a redshift of 375 km s ^ 1 . It is similar in both optical appearance and H i mass to the Large Magellanic Cloud. The galaxy is listed in the isolated galaxy catalogue of Karachentseva, Lebedev Shcherbanovskij (1973) as one of the most isolated galaxies in the nearby Universe, with no companion within 10 ^ . Karachentsev, Musella Grimaldi (1996) estimated its distance as 7.8 Mpc (on the basis of the brightest stars); we have adopted this estimate in this paper.

NGC 1156 was analysed in the same manner as NGC 7339 (above). This gave a beam-corrected integrated flux of 75.6 6.4 Jy km s ^ 1 , which is consistent with the values of Swaters et al. (2002) and Haynes et al. (1998). This gives an H i mass for the galaxy of 1.08 0.09 10^9 M_ . The fitted gaussian has deconvolved major and minor axes of 272 16 and 212 13 arc seconds respectively, at a position angle of 93 3 degrees (north through east), which appears consistent with the Swaters et al. WSRT map of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes VV 531 as an isolated IBm galaxy with strong emission, distance-based isolation evidence, and ionized-gas velocity swings that mask global gradients.

quotes.quote: VV 531 is a rather bright galaxy of IBm type with a strong emission spectrum. Unlike the other galaxies considered in this paper, VV 531 is classified as well isolated, being in the Karachentseva et al. ( ) list of isolated galaxies. The distance to this galaxy, estimated from its individual brightest stars, is 7.8 Mpc (Karachentsev et al. ). These authors claim that this galaxy can be considered as "the best example of an actually isolated galaxy in a general field".

Fig. VV531fig2 shows the line-of-sight velocity distributions along the PA = 42 \ and 96 . It is worth noting that the major axis orientations in optical light ( PA = 42 ) and in the H i radio image ( PA 90 ) differ significantly. For both PA s, global velocity gradients of ionized gas cannot be reliably determined: they are masked by significant velocity swings presumably due to the appearance of supershells near SF regions (see section VV131_kin for a similar case in VV 131). The velocity amplitude of such variations in VV 531 reaches 50 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1156 as a dwarf irregular Magellanic-type galaxy with high star formation, then measures a TRGB distance of 7.01 Mpc and peripheral metallicity.

quotes.quote: The NGC 1156 is a dwarf irregular Magellanic-type galaxy which has a relatively high star formation rate According to the catalog of Local Volume galaxies https://relay.sao.ru/lv/lvgdb/ : 0.3 M_ yr ^ 1 . The distance to the galaxy is known with poor accuracy, and its measurement was carried out only by a few methods: the estimates vary from 3.78 Mpc ( Bottinelli1984 , the Tully-Fisher relation) to 8.13 Mpc ( Kim2012 , the brightest stars of the galaxy). Since the accuracy of the distance measurements plays a decisive role in determining fundamental stellar parameters, we decided to estimate it ourselves by using the TRGB method (tip of the red-giant branch, Lee1993 ), which employs the luminosity of the brightest stars at the red-giant branch as a distance indicator and provides an accuracy of about 0.1 ^m ( Lee1993 ; see Sec. subsec:dist ).

The galaxy NGC 1156 is located at the centre of the HST images and occupies the entire frame. Since the presence of bright stars distorts the position of the branch of red giants, which are fainter stars, we considered only the stars at the galaxy edge for these measurements, where the number of bright stars is insignificant. Red giants are clearly visible in the HST images of the galaxy periphery, so the TRGB method Lee1993 can give reliable distance estimates.

This allows us to determine the distance to NGC 1156 and the metallicity of the red giants at the periphery of NGC 1156 based on the relations from Lee1993 : (m-M) = 29.23 ^m , D = 7.01 0.40 Mpc, [ Fe/H ] = -1.31.

sources.summary: The paper estimates NGC 1156's reddening and distance from HST photometry, deriving E(B-V)=0.35 and a weighted distance modulus of 29.39 mag, or 7.6 Mpc.

quotes.quote: We present a photometric estimation of the distance and reddening values to the dwarf irregular galaxy NGC 1156, which is one of the best targets to study the isolated dwarf galaxies in the nearby universe. We have used the imaging data sets of the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) High Resolution Channel (HRC) of the central region of NGC 1156 ( 26 29 ) available in the HST archive for this study. From the (U-B, B-V) color-color diagram, we first estimate the total (foreground + internal) reddening toward NGC 1156 of E(B-V) =0.35 0.05 mag, whereas only the foreground reddening was previously known to be E(B-V)=0.16 mag (Burstein Heiles) or 0.24 mag (Schlegel, Finkbeiner, Davis).

Based on the brightest stars method, selecting the three brightest blue supergiant (BSG) stars with mean B magnitude of B(3B) = 21.94 mag and the three brightest red supergiant (RSG) stars with mean V magnitude of V(3R) = 22.76 mag, we derive the distance modulus to NGC 1156 to be (m-M)_ 0,BSG = 29.55 mag and (m-M)_ 0,RSG = 29.16 mag. By using weights of 1 and 1.5 for the distance moduli from using the BSGs and the RSGs, respectively, we finally obtain the weighted mean distance modulus to NGC 1156 (m-M)_0 = 29.39 0.20 mag ( d = 7.6 0.7 Mpc), which is in very good agreement with the previous estimates.

sources.summary: The paper finds no confirmed NGC 1156 satellites from SBF, leaving two candidates possible and identifying one candidate as likely background.

quotes.quote: NGC 1156 The SBF results for NGC 1156 are shown in Table tab:ngc1156_sbf . We do not confirm any of the candidates to be genuine satellites. One of the candidates is likely background, and the other two are possible satellites. One of the possible satellites (dw0300p2514) was above our fiducial S/N >5 threshold and had distance consistent with NGC 1156, but due to the galactic cirrus, we could not visually confirm this signal was actual SBF. Thus, we conservatively include this galaxy into the unconfirmed/possible satellite category.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1156 as a well-isolated BCD galaxy with a nearby dwarf companion whose presence does not disturb its dynamic autonomy but yields high orbital mass-to-light estimates.

quotes.quote: An example of this situation is a well-isolated BCD galaxy NGC 1156 = KIG 121 = 2MIG 360, having apparent magnitudes of B = 12.32^m , K_s = 9.54^m and the heliocentric radial velocity of V_h = 376 km/s. Within the HI survey AGES, carried out with the Arecibo radiotelescope, a dwarf galaxy AGES J030039+254656 was discovered [ 20 ] with an apparent magnitude of B = 18.1^m and radial velocity of V_h = 308 km/s, located at an angular distance of 35^ (80 kpc) from NGC 1156. There is every reason to call this dwarf galaxy a physical companion of NGC 1156, the presence of which, however, does not distort the dynamic autonomy of this bright galaxy.

On the other hand, an example of a nearby isolated galaxy NGC 1156 and its dwarf companion AGES J030039+254656 indicates that the estimates of the orbital mass-to-K-luminosity ratio in these indisputably isolated pairs can reach values of around 200M_ /L_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1156 as a nearby irregular isolated galaxy observed by AGES, adopting a 7.8 Mpc distance and literature H I masses near 10^9 solar masses.

quotes.quote: NGC 1156 is an irregular galaxy at a recessional velocity of 379 km s ^ 1 . AGES observations of this galaxy and the surrounding region were reported in Auld (2007) and in Minchin et al. (2010). As in the earlier paper, we adopt the distance estimate of 7.8 Mpc from Karachentsev et al. (2006). Previous H i observations (Haynes et al. 1998; Swaters et al. 2002; Springob et al. 2005) give it an H i mass (at our adopted distance) of 1.0 10^9 M_ and 1.1 10^9 M_ after correction of the flux for source size (based on the optical diameter) and for self-absoprtion.

sources.summary: NGC1156 has stellar dominance in the inner radius, gas and dark matter dominance outward, and a higher-quality rotation curve than prior SPARC mass modelling.

quotes.quote: NGC1156 : For this galaxy, in the inner radius, the stellar component is dominant, and as we go to the outer radius, the stellar component becomes weaker than the gas component, and dark matter dominates in the outermost radius. sparc_mass_model also studied the mass model of this galaxy, but their rotation curve does not reach the flat part ( 4 kpc), and the quality of the rotation curve is bad (Q =3 , see sparc_rotc ), while our high-quality rotation curve goes 7 kpc and clearly reaches the flat part. sparc_mass_model used different halo profiles for doing the mass modelling, but these profiles do not give unique mass modelling of this galaxy, and the variation of different components is significant. In the inner part, our result is similar to sparc_mass_model for two cases of halo profiles and priors, DC14 DC14_halo_profile and Lucky13 sparc_mass_model with priors from CDM model.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1156 as an isolated Magellanic-type irregular with intense star formation and several massive star clusters, implying its starburst was not interaction-triggered.

quotes.quote: NGC 1156 This is a Magellanic-type irregular galaxy, currently undergoing an episode of intense star formation. Ho et. al. 1995 noted that the spectrum of NGC 1156 resembles that of the ``W-R galaxy'' NGC 4214. NGC 1156 is a completely isolated galaxy, so the starburst could not have been triggered by interaction with other galaxies. We have found a number of massive star clusters in NGC 1156.

gmw_00000983.name: Taffy
gmw_00000983.overview: The Taffy Galaxies (UGC 12914/5) are a pair of colliding spiral galaxies that recently underwent a direct, near head-on collision [arXiv:2001.03795, arXiv:astro-ph/0308476]. This interaction stripped gas from the disks to form a multi-phase bridge [arXiv:2001.03795, arXiv:1104.0956] containing abundant molecular gas [arXiv:2011.13035, arXiv:astro-ph/0308476] but very little active star formation [arXiv:2011.13035, arXiv:1104.0956].

sources.summary: The paper uses the Taffy system as the key observed splash-bridge analogue, describing its gas bridge, direct collision history, ALMA-resolved clumps and filaments, and shock velocities reproduced by models.

quotes.quote: Recent multi-wavelength observations of the Taffy galaxies, UGC 12914/15, have revealed complicated gas structures in the bridge. For example, the Taffy galaxy system, UGC 12914/15, contains a multi-phase gas bridge of material stretched between the two stellar discs. There are over 25 years of detailed observations of the Taffy ring galaxies, which have apparently recently undergone a direct collision, condon93,braine03,gao03,peterson12,appleton15,almataffy2019 .

The observational resolution required to begin to see detailed structure in Taffy-like bridges has been achieved with ALMA. Results of observations of the Taffy reveal clumps and filaments across the splash bridge.

Recent ALMA observations almataffy2019 have resolved filamentary structure and clumping of the gas across the bridge of the Taffy Galaxies. This is consistent with the observed emission of almataffy2019 , which indicates a high velocity component of 200-300 in the Taffy galaxies. As a result, most molecular material that collides experiences upstream shock velocities consistent with the 80-150 observed in the Taffy.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Taffy Galaxy system as the prototype splash bridge, summarizing radio, CO, X-ray, and ALMA evidence for disturbed bridge gas and delayed star formation.

quotes.quote: The Taffy Galaxy system (UGC 1294/5) is a prototypical example of a splash bridge system. CO observations of the Taffy revealed that its splash bridge contains a mass of equal to that of the Milky Way's mass. However, the little visible star formation occurring within the bridge highlights the need for models of direct gas-rich disk collisions.

The first splash bridge was discovered in the Taffy Galaxy system (UGC 12914/5) in a wide-view radio continuum by condon93 . The discovery of a radio continuum bridge led to further observations of the system across the electromagnetic spectrum, so that the Taffy system is now the best studied example of the phenomenon. For example, the molecular content in the Taffy bridge was observed through CO emission to be between 2- 9e9 braine03 .

The observational age estimates of the Taffy Galaxies (UGC 12914/5) place this system right before the predicted second starburst. The first wave of star formation 20-25 Myr ago, predicted by the models, would leave many low mass stars scattered throughout the bridge.

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 12914/5 as the Taffy Galaxies: a direct, near head-on spiral collision with a molecular/synchrotron bridge, counter-rotating disks, and gas stripped into dense molecular form.

quotes.quote: The Taffy Galaxies system, UGC 12914/5, contains huge amounts of molecular gas in the bridge region between the receding spirals after a direct collision. 2 - 9 10^9 M _ of molecular gas is present between the galaxies, more than the CO emission from the entire Milky WaySuch dense gas can only be torn off by collisions between dense clouds, in this case with relative velocities of about 800 , such that the remnant cloud acquires an intermediate velocity and is left in the bridge after separation of the colliding galaxies. We suggest that after ionization in the collision front, the gas cooled and recombined very quickly such that the density remained high and the gas left the colliding disks in molecular form.

The interacting pair of galaxies, UGC 12914/5, is one of the best examples of a strong ISM-ISM collision. Condon93 , hereafter C93, observed the system with the VLA in the H i line and 6 and 20 cm continuum emission. They found that the bridge between UGC 12914 and UGC 12915 emits strong synchrotron emission, with the magnetic fields being stretched as the galaxies separate, hence their naming the system the Taffy Galaxies. C93 showed that the collision was indeed close to head-on, with the nucleus of UGC 12915 passing through the more massive UGC 12914 slightly North of its center. The disks are counter-rotating and the recession velocities are nearly the same, so that the relative velocities are essentially transverse.

sources.summary: The paper models UGC 12914/5 as a face-on colliding spiral system whose gas bridge contains stripped atomic and molecular gas, shocks, and radio-emitting particles.

quotes.quote: Apart from the sychrotron emitting electrons the bridge in the system UGC 12914/5 contains also large amounts of atomic (CHS) and molecular (Braine et al. 2003) gas, where practically no star formation is taking place. This is interpreted as an essentially complete hydrodynamic removal of the more diffuse atomic and molecular gas from the galaxies. Most likely, only the dense cloud cores -- capable of forming stars -- have remained within the stellar disks together with the stars.

Since it is very similar, the discussion here will be limited to UGC 12914/5. In a face-on collision the stellar disks interpenetrate each other without being too much altered. However, the diffuse gas and part of the gas clouds interact hydrodynamically and exchange energy and momentum. The relative velocity between the galaxies, v_ coll 600 km/s, in the case of UGC 12914/5 has been derived by CHS from a analysis of the HI line and of the galaxy masses.

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 12914/5 as a head-on collision system with a gas bridge, two-component bridge profiles, molecular gas, and broad shocked components in the bridge.

quotes.quote: UGC 12914/5: This system is also known as the ``Taffy galaxies", because HI and 4.86 GHz observations by showed a joined HI and radio continuum bridge between the two galaxies. suggest that cosmic rays, magnetic fields, and HI gas have been striped from the two galaxies as the result of a nearly head-on collision, about 20 Myrs ago. In the bridge, they found double peaks in the HI profile separated by 100-300 km/s. finds a significant amount of molecular gas emission (CO) in the bridge, suggesting that 18-35 of the total gas mass in the system sits in the bridge.

Figure fig:appendix:ugc12914diff shows the velocity difference between the broad and narrow component in fibers where two component fit is preferred by the F-test. It shows that most of the fibers in the bridge region prefer a two-component fit. In most of these fibers, the narrow component meets the criteria for a normal star-forming region, and the broad component is considered as shocked.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Taffy as an interesting interacting galaxy pair, reanalyzes its radio observations, and reports bridge magnetic-field B-vectors aligned along the radio bridge.

quotes.quote: Ones of the most interesting interacting systems in our sample are Taffy and the Taffy2 galaxy pairs. The unusual radio bridges between galaxies in these objects were discovered by Condon et al. ( ) and Condon et al. ( ). We re-reduced once again their radio observations to look for remnants of magnetic field structures in the galactic disks.

We indeed found them (as in the Antennae system), they are especially well visible, in UGC 12915 and similarly in UGC 816 (Fig. 1). However, the most intriguing are the directions of B-vectors in both radio bridges of the taffies. The directions of B-vectors in the Taffy are aligned along the radio bridge, whereas in the Taffy2 they are perpendicular to the line joining both galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 12914/5 as an interesting close, separated major merger with blue-band tidal structures and a gas bridge that emits synchrotron radiation.

quotes.quote: These candidates have some interesting common characteristics: (i) all the candidates are close mergers (UGC 12914/5, UGC 813/6, ESO 303-11, VV 114) or appear to have merged already so that their nuclei lie within a massive bulge in the center of the galaxy (NGC 660, NGC 3256). (ii) The few galaxies that are separated (UGC 12914/5, UGC 813/6, ES0 303-11, VV 114) appear to be major mergers, i.e., their galaxy or bulge masses are similar. (iii) All the seven galaxies have extended tidal tails, bridges or loops of hot, magnetized gas that can be clearly distinguished in the blue (or g) band images. For example, the ``Taffy” galaxies (UGC 12914/5, UGC 813/6) have bridges of neutral and ionized gas connecting the nuclei. The gas gives out synchrotron emission, which is detected in radio observations.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 12914/5 as the prototype Taffy collision, noting strong bridge CO emission and radio/magnetic morphology, plus little star formation outside one H II region.

quotes.quote: Contrary to initial expectations (C93), extremely strong CO emission was detected throughout the UGC 12914/5 bridge Braine03,Gao03 , showing that a large quantity of molecular gas is present as well. In fact, the CO emission from the UGC 12914/5 bridge was found to be nearly 5 times that of the entire Milky Way. Due to the appearance of the synchrotron brightness contours and the magnetic field, the UGC 12914/5 system is also known as the "Taffy galaxies".

Jarrett99 find that with the exception of the single giant H ii region near UGC 12915, the UGC 12914/5 bridge is devoid of stars and star formation. From the slit spectrum of the UGC 12914/5 bridge presented by Braine03 , there is no sign of star formation (except in the H ii region) although the molecular gas surface density is nearly constant Braine03,Gao03 throughout the bridge.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 12914/5 as a Taffy colliding galaxy-pair candidate within CTA range, with recent high-velocity gas collision and shocked synchrotron-emitting bridge gas.

quotes.quote: Two of the interesting candidates are the ``Taffy" colliding galaxy pairs UGC 12914/5 and UGC 813/6 Condon_et_al_1993, Candon_et_al_2002 , located at d_ L = 60 \ Mpc and 69 \ Mpc , respectively. In both cases, gas components of 10^ 9 10 \ M_ collided with a velocity of 600 \ km \ s^ 1 a few 10 \ Myr ago. The bridge region between the galaxy pairs filled with shocked gases, and shows strong synchrotron emission which may come from non-thermal electrons accelerated by the GMS Lisenfeld_Volk_2010 .

sources.summary: The paper describes VV 254 through mapped SFR and SFE distributions, noting abundant collision-region molecular gas but no enhanced and generally low overlap-region star formation.

quotes.quote: VV 254 Figures fig_SFR (), fig_SFEmol (), and fig_SFEgas () are the maps of SFR, SFE _ mol and SFE _ gas of VV 254, respectively.
Although these pairs possess plenty of molecular gas at their colliding region, only VV 254 does not show enhanced star formation activity there.
From figures fig_SFEmol () and fig_SFEgas (), we can see that star formation activity is low even existing rich molecular gas with high molecular gas fraction in the overlap region of VV 254.

gmw_00000984.name: Taffy2
gmw_00000984.overview: Taffy2 is an interacting galaxy pair [arXiv:1201.4051] formed by a head-on collision of two spiral galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0402148].
