This major merger features a bridge of shocked, synchrotron-emitting molecular gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0402148, arXiv:1405.3262] with magnetic-field vectors oriented perpendicular to the line connecting the galaxies [arXiv:1201.4051].

sources.summary: The paper treats Taffy2 as an interacting galaxy pair with an unusual radio bridge, finding magnetic-field B-vectors perpendicular to the line joining the galaxies.

quotes.quote: Ones of the most interesting interacting systems in our sample are Taffy and the Taffy2 galaxy pairs. The unusual radio bridges between galaxies in these objects were discovered by Condon et al. ( ) and Condon et al. ( ). We re-reduced once again their radio observations to look for remnants of magnetic field structures in the galactic disks.

We indeed found them (as in the Antennae system), they are especially well visible, in UGC 12915 and similarly in UGC 816 (Fig. 1). However, the most intriguing are the directions of B-vectors in both radio bridges of the taffies. The directions of B-vectors in the Taffy are aligned along the radio bridge, whereas in the Taffy2 they are perpendicular to the line joining both galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 813/6 as a head-on colliding spiral pair whose bridge contains strong CO emission and enough molecular gas to place it among Taffy-type systems.

quotes.quote: We present further observations of molecular gas in head-on collisions of spiral galaxies, this time of the CO( J=1 0 ) and CO( J=2 1 ) lines in the UGC 813 -- UGC 816 system. UGC 813/6 are only the second known example of head-on spiral-spiral collisions, the first example being the UGC 12914/5 pair. Strong CO emission is present in the bridge between UGC 813 and 816, unassociated with stellar emission, just as in UGC 12914/5. The CO emission from the UGC 813/6 bridge, not counting the emission from the galaxies themselves, is at least that of the entire Milky Way.

The UGC 813/6 pair shares these properties and thus is a member of the class of taffy galaxies. In this work, we show that the UGC 813/6 bridge contains a lot of molecular gas, again like UGC 12914/5.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 813/6 as an interesting close, separated major merger with blue-band tidal structures and a gas bridge that emits synchrotron radiation.

quotes.quote: These candidates have some interesting common characteristics: (i) all the candidates are close mergers (UGC 12914/5, UGC 813/6, ESO 303-11, VV 114) or appear to have merged already so that their nuclei lie within a massive bulge in the center of the galaxy (NGC 660, NGC 3256). (ii) The few galaxies that are separated (UGC 12914/5, UGC 813/6, ES0 303-11, VV 114) appear to be major mergers, i.e., their galaxy or bulge masses are similar. (iii) All the seven galaxies have extended tidal tails, bridges or loops of hot, magnetized gas that can be clearly distinguished in the blue (or g) band images. For example, the ``Taffy” galaxies (UGC 12914/5, UGC 813/6) have bridges of neutral and ionized gas connecting the nuclei. The gas gives out synchrotron emission, which is detected in radio observations.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 813/6 as a Taffy colliding galaxy-pair candidate within CTA range, with recent high-velocity gas collision and shocked synchrotron-emitting bridge gas.

quotes.quote: Two of the interesting candidates are the ``Taffy" colliding galaxy pairs UGC 12914/5 and UGC 813/6 Condon_et_al_1993, Candon_et_al_2002 , located at d_ L = 60 \ Mpc and 69 \ Mpc , respectively. In both cases, gas components of 10^ 9 10 \ M_ collided with a velocity of 600 \ km \ s^ 1 a few 10 \ Myr ago. The bridge region between the galaxy pairs filled with shocked gases, and shows strong synchrotron emission which may come from non-thermal electrons accelerated by the GMS Lisenfeld_Volk_2010 .

gmw_00000985.name: NGC 45
gmw_00000985.overview: NGC 45 is a low-surface-brightness spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0609148, arXiv:astro-ph/0508677] located in an unperturbed environment [arXiv:astro-ph/0508677] with no detected satellite galaxies [arXiv:2008.08954]. It exhibits a kinematical twist of its major axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0609148] and hosts a population of metal-poor globular clusters [arXiv:0808.0922] alongside continuous star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0508677].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 45 as a background low-surface-brightness galaxy with a twisted kinematic major axis and high stellar mass-to-light ratio.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Optical parameters of NGC 45 R.A. (2000) ^ a & 00^ h 14^ m 03.2^ s DEC. (2000) ^ a & 23^ 11'01.0''

quotes.quote: These two galaxies of similar magnitude M_B 17.4 lie in the background of the Sculptor group and are low surface brightness galaxies, especially NGC 45. The distribution and kinematics are regular for NGC 24 while NGC 45 exhibits a kinematical twist of its major axis. A tilted-ring model shows that the position angle of the major axis changes by 25 . A best-fit model of their mass distribution gives mass-to-light ratios for the stellar disk of 2.5 and 5.2 for NGC 24 and NGC 45 respectively.

In this area, besides the five main members studied so far (NGC 55, 247, 253, 300 and 7793), two more galaxies, NGC 24 and NGC 45 (Figure dssimages ), fall in the same region and have similar sizes and degree of resolution as well as 20 known dwarf galaxies (C ot e, Freeman Carignan 1997; C ot e, Carignan Freeman 2000). However, when looking at the estimated distances, a mean distance of 2.4 0.6 Mpc is found for the main members (de Vaucouleurs 1978) but 6.8 Mpc (7 ) and 5.9 Mpc (6 ) for NGC 24 and NGC 45, respectively (based on a Hubble constant of 75 Mpc ^ 1 , Tully 1988). Moreover, when doing dynamical mass estimates of the group, any combination of the main members gives consistently 3 10^ 12 M _ for the group, while including NGC 24 and NGC 45 makes the estimate jump to 3 10^ 13 M _ , clearly too large for a loose group like Sculptor. All this suggests strongly that NGC 24 and NGC 45 are not members of the group but background objects.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 45 as a low-luminosity unperturbed spiral, finding young low-mass clusters, old globular clusters, and an unusually high globular-cluster specific frequency.

quotes.quote: Here we investigate the star cluster population in the low-luminosity, unperturbed spiral galaxy NGC 45, which is located in the nearby Sculptor group. Both the old (globular) and young star-cluster populations are studied. Previous ground-based observations have suggested that NGC 45 has few if any ``massive'' young star clusters. We aim to study the population of lower-mass ``open'' star clusters and also identify old globular clusters that could not be distinguished from foreground stars in the ground-based data.

We identified 28 young star cluster candidates. While the exact values of age, mass, and extinction depend somewhat on the choice of SSP models, we find no young clusters with masses higher than a few 1000 M_ for any model choice. We derive the luminosity function of young star clusters and find a slope of =-1.94 0.28 . We also identified 19 old globular clusters, which appear to have a mass distribution that is roughly consistent with what is observed in other globular cluster systems.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 45 as AT 2018bwo's host galaxy, adopting its redshift, TRGB distance, extinction, reddening, and young-cluster metallicity for the transient analysis.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy NGC 45 has a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.00156 Springob2005ApJS . The distances to NGC 45 reported in literature span a wide range of values from 4.47 Mpc Bottinelli1985AAS to 13.90 Willick1997ApJS . Given the uncertainty, we choose the method that provides the most accurate individual distances, based on the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB). In this work, we adopt one of the most recent measurements of D_L=6.64 0.10 Mpc Tully2013AJ , corresponding to a distance modulus of =29.11 0.10 .

The foreground Milky Way extinction toward NGC 45 corresponds to A_V=0.058 Schlafly2011 , which translates into a reddening of E(B-V)=0.0187 for a nominal extinction law. The average extinction within the host galaxy was derived by Mora2007AA by modelling young stellar clusters at different metallicities. The best representation of cluster colors was reached for a host-galaxy metallicity between Z=0.006 and 0.008 and a mean reddening of E(B-V)=0.04 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 45 as a low-surface-brightness spiral in the Sculptor group with an unperturbed environment and continuous star-cluster formation rather than bursts.

quotes.quote: We determined ages for 52 star clusters with masses 10^6 M_ in the low surface brightness spiral galaxy NGC 45. Four of these candidates are old globular clusters located in the bulge. The remaining ones span a large age range. The cluster ages suggest a continuous star/cluster formation history without evidence for bursts, consistent with the galaxy being located in a relatively unperturbed environment in the outskirts of the Sculptor group.

NGC 45 is an interesting galaxy: It is an outlying member of the sculptor group (m-M)=28.42 0.41 (Freedman et al.  ). It is located in an unperturbed environment and it has a low luminosity of M_B =-17.13 (Bottinelli ). In the presented work in progress, we study the star formation history of this spiral galaxy by looking at the NGC45 star cluster system.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 45 as a low-luminosity Sculptor spiral whose globular clusters are metal-poor, non-rotating, and likely formed during galaxy assembly.

quotes.quote: Past observations with the ACS have shown that NGC 45 hosts a large population of globular clusters (19), as well as several young star clusters. In this work we aim to confirm the bona fide globular cluster status for 8 of 19 globular cluster candidates and to derive metallicities, ages, and velocities. VLT/FORS2 multislit spectroscopy in combination with the Lick/IDS system was used to derive velocities and to constrain metallicities and [ / Fe ] element ratio of the globular clusters.

NGC 45 is classified as a low-luminosity spiral galaxy with B=11.37 0.11 and B-V=0.71 paturel . It is located at 5 Mpc from us, (m-M)_0=28.42 0.41 bottinelli , in the periphery of the Sculptor group. In Mora we found 19 globular clusters located in projection with the galaxy bulge, which appear to belong to the metal-poor population.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 45 as a low-surface-brightness Local Volume spiral in the survey field and reports no detected satellites around it down to the stated limits.

quotes.quote: Additionally, two Local Volume spiral galaxies reside in the field: NGC 24 ( D=7.7 Mpc; 2013AJ....146...86T ), an edge-on Sc galaxy, and NGC 45 ( D=6.7 Mpc; 2013AJ....146...86T ), a low-surface brightness Sdm galaxy. These two latter galaxies are of low mass, with total baryonic plus dark matter mass estimates of 2.8 10^ 10 M _ and 3.7 10^ 10 M _ , respectively 2006AJ....132.2527C .

All dwarf galaxy satellites of the two host galaxies NGC 24 and NGC 45 should have been identified within the M_V<-10 mag luminosity range by now. For NGC 24, we find two possible satellites while there are none for NGC 45. Is this expected? Indeed, with total masses of 2.8 10^ 10 M _ and 3.7 10^ 10 M _ 2006AJ....132.2527C , respectively, the two host galaxies are at the low-mass end of the giant galaxy population.

gmw_00000986.name: IMS J0324+0426
gmw_00000986.overview: IMS J0324+0426 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately 4.7 [arXiv:1706.08454]. The object exhibits a strong Lyman-alpha emission line alongside several strong ultraviolet and near-infrared emission lines [arXiv:1706.08454].

sources.summary: The paper presents IMS J0324+0426 as a z about 4.7 quasar with strong Ly emission, several strong UV and NIR emission lines, and a literature redshift agreement.

quotes.quote: IMS J0324+0426 (z _ Ly =4.70, z _ CIV =4.68, z _ MgII =4.73): This quasar has a strong Ly emission line. It also shows relatively strong Lyman (Ly ), , + , and emission lines, and a weak emission line. In the NIR spectrum, , , and emission lines are prominent. wang16 reported z=4.72.

gmw_00000987.name: IMS J0122+1216
gmw_00000987.overview: IMS J0122+1216 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1706.08454, arXiv:1602.02236] that exhibits deep absorption features [arXiv:1706.08454] and a strong Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:1706.08454]. Its near-infrared spectrum displays strong emission lines [arXiv:1706.08454], though its broad-band photometric redshift estimates do not align with spectroscopic measurements [arXiv:1602.02236].

sources.summary: The paper classifies IMS J0122+1216 as a BAL quasar with deep absorption, strong Ly and NIR emission lines, and literature redshifts below the photometric estimate.

quotes.quote: IMS J0122+1216 (z _ Ly =4.83, z _ CIV =4.81): We classify this as a BAL quasar because of deep absorption features bluewards of Ly , , + , and lines. It has a strong Ly emission line, and a weak Ly emission line. We are not able to identify other emission lines due to these deep absorptions.

The NIR spectrum has strong , , and emission lines. The left side (shorter wavelengths) of these lines are severely absorbed. yi15 analyzed this quasar and derived a redshift of z=4.76 while wang16 reported z=4.79.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IMS J012247.33+121623.9 as a BAL quasar with deep m775 absorption, excludes it from normal quasar SED fitting, and reports a broad-band photometric redshift inconsistent with spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: Note that IMS J012247.33+121623.9 is a broad absorption line (BAL) quasar, which shows a deep absorption feature near the wavelengths of the m775 band.
We exclude IMS J012247.33+121623.9, since it is a BAL quasar, whose spectra are known to be clearly different from normal quasar spectra.

rich09, rich15 provide z _ phot values for 3 of the 6 quasars, IMS J012247.33+121623.9, IMS J143704.82+070808.3, and IMS J222514.39+033012.6, estimated from broad-band photometry, u/g//i/z/J/H/K . Their estimate for IMS J012247.33+121623.9 (z _ phot =5.455^ +0.135 _ 0.095 from rich15 ) does not agree with our spectroscopic redshift, although IMS J143704.82+070808.3 (z _ phot =5.075^ +0.505 _ 0.455 from rich09 or 5.265^ +0.115 _ 0.505 from rich15 ) and IMS J222514.39+033012.6 (z _ phot =5.415^ +0.285 _ 0.395 from rich15 ) are in agreement with our estimates.

gmw_00000988.name: IMS J1437+0708
gmw_00000988.overview: IMS J1437+0708 is a high-redshift quasar with a spectroscopic redshift estimated between 4.94 and 4.97 [arXiv:1602.02236, arXiv:1706.08454]. Its optical spectrum, obtained with the New Technology Telescope, does not display emission lines other than Lyman-alpha [arXiv:1706.08454].

sources.summary: The paper treats IMS J143704.82+070808.3 as a z=4.97 quasar, identifies its m675 dropout, explains its band flux by Ly emission, and reports photometric redshift agreement.

quotes.quote: From Figure fig_ccdall (b), we can deduce that the m675 dropout is found for IMS J012247.33+121623.9 (z=4.86) and IMS J143704.82+070808.3 (z=4.97).
For example, IMS J143704.82+070808.3 at z = 4.97 shows a higher flux in the band than in the m675 band because the filter also covers the Lyman emission line at this redshift.

rich09, rich15 provide z _ phot values for 3 of the 6 quasars, IMS J012247.33+121623.9, IMS J143704.82+070808.3, and IMS J222514.39+033012.6, estimated from broad-band photometry, u/g//i/z/J/H/K . Their estimate for IMS J012247.33+121623.9 (z _ phot =5.455^ +0.135 _ 0.095 from rich15 ) does not agree with our spectroscopic redshift, although IMS J143704.82+070808.3 (z _ phot =5.075^ +0.505 _ 0.455 from rich09 or 5.265^ +0.115 _ 0.505 from rich15 ) and IMS J222514.39+033012.6 (z _ phot =5.415^ +0.285 _ 0.395 from rich15 ) are in agreement with our estimates.

sources.summary: The paper describes IMS J1437+0708 as a z about 4.94 quasar whose NTT spectrum has the highest optical S/N but lacks prominent lines except Ly.

quotes.quote: IMS J1437+0708 (z _ Ly =4.94): Its spectrum was obtained from NTT/EFOSC2 with R 130 and it has the highest S/N ratio among the optical spectra. However it does not show any prominent emission lines except the Ly . wang16 reported z=4.93.

gmw_00000989.name: IMS J2225+0330
gmw_00000989.overview: IMS J2225+0330 is a high-redshift quasar with a spectroscopic redshift of approximately 5.35 [arXiv:1706.08454, arXiv:1602.02236]. It exhibits a smooth Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:1706.08454] and displays red colors due to a dropout in the m725 band [arXiv:1602.02236].

sources.summary: The paper reports IMS J2225+0330 as a z about 5.35 quasar observed with KPNO and NTT, with smooth Ly emission and strong NIR lines.

quotes.quote: IMS J2225+0330 (z _ Ly =5.35 and z _ MgII =5.26): This source was observed by two telescopes, the KPNO 4-m telescope and NTT, and the two spectra were combined in average. It has a smooth Ly emission line and does not show any other emission lines. In the NIR spectrum, the , , and emission lines are strong but the emission line has a rough shape due to the strong atmospheric absorption. wang16 reported z=5.24.

sources.summary: The paper discusses IMS J222514.39+033012.6 as a z=5.35 quasar with red m725-m775 colors from an m725 dropout; published broad-band photometric redshift agrees with its spectroscopic redshift.

quotes.quote: For similar reasons, in Figure fig_ccdall (), IMS J222514.39+033012.6 (z=5.35) and IMS J015533.28+041506.8 (z=5.39) also show red m725 - m775 colors relative to IMS J032407.70+042613.3 (z=4.71), IMS J012247.33+121623.9 (z=4.86), and IMS J143704.82+070808.3 (z=4.97) due to the m725 dropout.

rich09, rich15 provide z _ phot values for 3 of the 6 quasars, IMS J012247.33+121623.9, IMS J143704.82+070808.3, and IMS J222514.39+033012.6, estimated from broad-band photometry, u/g//i/z/J/H/K . Their estimate for IMS J012247.33+121623.9 (z _ phot =5.455^ +0.135 _ 0.095 from rich15 ) does not agree with our spectroscopic redshift, although IMS J143704.82+070808.3 (z _ phot =5.075^ +0.505 _ 0.455 from rich09 or 5.265^ +0.115 _ 0.505 from rich15 ) and IMS J222514.39+033012.6 (z _ phot =5.415^ +0.285 _ 0.395 from rich15 ) are in agreement with our estimates.

gmw_00000990.name: IMS J1022+0801
gmw_00000990.overview: IMS J1022+0801 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately 5.36 [arXiv:1706.08454]. It is characterized by having a particularly weak Lyman-alpha emission line, with no other emission lines visible in its spectrum [arXiv:1706.08454].

sources.summary: The paper presents IMS J1022+0801 as a z about 5.36 quasar with the weakest Ly line in the sample, no visible other lines, and an independent discovery.

quotes.quote: IMS J1022+0801 (z _ Ly =5.36): This quasar has the weakest Ly emission line among the six observed quasars. No other emission lines are visible due to low S/N. This quasar was recently discovered independently by yang17 , reporting the spectroscopic redshift of z=5.30 .

gmw_00000991.name: IMS J0155+0415
gmw_00000991.overview: IMS J0155+0415 is a high-redshift quasar with a Lyman-alpha redshift of 5.35 [arXiv:1706.08454]. Its optical spectrum displays a weak Lyman-alpha emission line, while its near-infrared spectrum shows emission lines for CIV, CIII, and NV [arXiv:1706.08454].

sources.summary: The paper describes IMS J0155+0415 as a z about 5.35 quasar with weak Ly emission, no other optical lines, prominent NIR lines, and telluric obscuration.

quotes.quote: IMS J0155+0415 (z _ Ly =5.35, z _ CIV =5.27): The optical spectrum shows a weak Ly emission line and other emission lines are not detected. In the NIR spectrum, it has prominent + , , and emission lines. The emission line is hidden due to telluric absorption. wang16 reported z=5.37.

gmw_00000992.name: SDSS J091201.68 + 532036.6
gmw_00000992.overview: SDSS J091201.68 + 532036.6 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy at z=0.1017 that displays double-peaked emission lines [arXiv:1709.03561, arXiv:2209.02717]. The system exhibits a disturbed morphology with two optical continuum peaks, a tidal tail, a close companion galaxy, and a north-south radio jet structure [arXiv:1709.03561, arXiv:2209.02717].

sources.summary: The paper describes this z=0.1017 double-peaked AGN host as having a close companion, Chandra X-ray point source, and substantial north-south subarcsecond radio structure.

quotes.quote: SDSS J091201.68 + 532036.6 The z=0.1017 AGN contains double-peaked \ lines in its SDSS fiber spectrum (Figure  fig:J091201.68+532036.6 ), with peaks blueshifted and redshifted from the systemic velocity by 193 km s ^ 1 and 208 km s ^ 1 , respectively Liu2010b . SDSS images show that the host galaxy has a close companion to the southwest at a projected separation of 3.7 kpc ( 2.''0 ). It is unclear whether the SDSS fiber spectrum is significantly contaminated by light from the companion. We have a spectrum of the companion from MMT, but it shows no emission lines and does not have the S/N to measure a redshift.

This galaxy contains a radio source that has been detected in surveys over the frequency range 0.365--8.4 GHz bwe91,gc91,dbbtw96,Condon1998 . In addition, Chandra X-ray observations show a point source with a flux of 6.8_ 1.4 ^ +1.5 10^ 15  erg s ^ 1  cm ^ 2 0.5--7  keV , evans10 . Figure  fig:J091201.68+532036.6 shows the image resulting from our VLBA observations. The radio source has three components, approximately aligned north--south.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0912+5320 as a disturbed double-peaked AGN with two optical continuum peaks, a tidal tail, VLBA jet-like components, and tentative narrow 21-cm absorption.

quotes.quote: Out of the ten double-peaked AGN, five (J0738+3156, J0842+0547, J1203+1319, J1243-0058 and J1517+3353) appear as single, undisturbed systems in the available optical images [ Dark Energy Camera Legacy Survey DECaLS; dey2019 , PanSTARRS-1 chambers2016 and SDSS ] . The remaining AGN are either part of galaxy pairs and interacting systems or show disturbed morphology. J0009-0036 has a companion galaxy at a projected separation of 40 kpc and a velocity separation of 460 . J1526+4140 is undergoing merger with another galaxy at 5 kpc and 150 . The optical continuum image of J0912+5320 shows two peaks and a tidal tail, while that of J1352+6541 shows signatures of a possible tidal disturbance. J1356+1026 is a post-merger system that has been observed as part of our \ 21-cm absorption survey in low- z galaxy mergers dutta2018 . Long-slit spectroscopy and NIR imaging of this system show double nuclei separated by 2.9 kpc greene2011,shen2011 . Moreover, there have been observations of extended \ emission in this system, suggesting large-scale outflows powered by feedback from the obscured, radio-quiet quasar greene2012 . Three of the AGN that appear as single systems (J0738+3156, J0842+0547 and J1203+1319) have been observed with the Keck Laser Guide Star Adaptive Optics system and the NIR camera NIRC2 mcgurk2015 . Based on these imaging observations, J1203+1319 has a double spatial structure on scales of <3 arcsec, indicating it is a candidate dual AGN. J0738+3156 and J0842+0547, on the other hand, show single spatial structure with no companions within 3 arcsec. Furthermore, long-slit optical spectra have been obtained for four AGN (J0738+3156, J0842+0547, J1243-0058 and J1517+3353) by comerford2018 . Based on these spectra, J0842+0547 and J1243-0058 are classified as outflows, whereas J1517+3353 is classified as an outflow composite, i.e. an outflow that includes many gas clouds at different velocities. On the other hand, J0738+3156 has a disturbed rotation-dominated profile.

Based on high-resolution ( 0.2 arcsec) multi-band (8-12 GHz) VLA observations muller2015 , J0009-0036 shows a two-sided radio jet suggesting that a radio jet-driven outflow could produce the double-peaked emission. In addition, six of the AGN have been imaged with VLBA at milliarcsecond (mas; 10 pc) resolution liu2018a . Three of them (J0009-0036, J0738+3156 and J1356+1026) remain undetected in the VLBA 8.4 GHz images. J0912+5320 shows three components in the VLBA image, an unresolved central core and northern and southern components extending over 25 mas, indicative of a jet structure. J1243-0058 shows a compact, unresolved component in the VLBA image, and there are no other radio sources stronger than 1 mJy within 1 arcsec. J1352+6541 shows a faint component at 2.4 mas from the primary component.

The observations of two of the sources, J0738+3156 and J1352+6541, were affected by strong radio frequency interference (RFI), preventing us from reliably studying the associated \ 21-cm absorption in these cases. The \ 21-cm spectra towards the remaining eight sources, along with the double-peaked \ emission lines in the SDSS spectra, are shown in Fig. fig:sdss_gmrt_spec . Among the eight sources with reliable \ 21-cm absorption spectra, we detect absorption from three sources (J1356+1026, J1517+3353, J1526+4140). In two cases, J0009-0036 and J0912+5320, we find tentative absorption features at the expected position of the \ 21-cm line. We reobserved the source J0009-0036 using the same observational set-up for an additional 7 hours in October 2019. We combined the spectra of J0009-0036 from the two different observing runs using inverse noise weighting. The peak optical depth of the absorption feature is detected at 3 significance in the combined spectrum. In the case of the absorption towards J0912+5320, the peak optical depth is detected at 5 significance. However, the absorption lines towards both J0009-0036 and J0912+5320 are narrow (velocity width encompassing 90 of the total optical depth, 90 \ 30-40 ), whereas most associated \ 21-cm absorption lines tend to broader on average ( 100 ). Further, the absorption profiles in the stokes LL and RR spectra do not resemble each other perfectly, although they are consistent within the uncertainties. Since there could be low-level sporadic RFI affecting the absorption features, we consider these as tentative detections for our analysis. The two tentative \ 21-cm absorption lines are shown in Fig. fig:ten_hi_spec . For the remaining three sources, J0842+0547, J1203+1319, J1243-0058, where we do not detect absorption, we place 3 upper limit on the integrated optical depth for a velocity width of 100 .

gmw_00000993.name: SDSS J110851.04 + 065901.4
gmw_00000993.overview: SDSS J110851.04 + 065901.4 is a major galaxy merger hosting a confirmed kpc-scale binary active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1603.07925, arXiv:1709.03561]. The system displays double stellar bulges [arXiv:1003.3467, arXiv:1011.5246], tidal interaction features [arXiv:1603.07925, arXiv:1709.03561], and an extended low-surface-brightness component [arXiv:1603.07925].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1108 as a confirmed binary AGN merger, measures its extended host morphology, and notes low-surface-brightness structure, clumpy colors, and two detected X-ray nuclei.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1108+0659 and SDSS J1146+5110 (J1108 and J1146) are confirmed to be binary AGNs. For the other two targets, SDSS J1131-0204 and SDSS J1332+0606 (J1131 and J1332), the current data are still consistent with the binary AGN scenario, but the possibility of only one AGN ionizing both components in the merger cannot be ruled out. The four optically selected kpc-scale binary AGNs were observed using the WFC3 onboard the \ in Cycle 18 under program GO 12363, between 2011 March and June (with observing dates listed in Table tab:obs ).

J1108, J1146 and J1332 are major mergers (see discussion below) where the two stellar nuclei are comparably bright. J1131 is a minor merger which displays a clear dominant disk structure with bars and spiral arms. Tidal features are prominent in the Y band images in all cases. J1108 displays an extended low-surface brightness component to the east of the galaxy. J1131 shows features of perturbation in the spiral disk. A bar structure is observed along the line of the two nuclei. J1146 has an extended tidal tail and the bulges are elongated and twisted. J1332 also displays extended tidal features and its bulges appear to be distorted.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ 110851.04+065901.4 as one of four kpc-scale binary AGNs, with double stellar components, merger tidal features, and spatially separated active emission-line nuclei.

quotes.quote: Here we report initial results on the discovery of four binary AGNs, SDSSJ 110851.04+065901.4 , SDSSJ 113126.08-020459.2 , SDSSJ 114642.47+511029.6 , and SDSSJ 133226.34+060627.4 . Deep NIR imaging revealed double stellar bulges in SDSSJ 110851.04+065901.4 , SDSSJ 113126.08-020459.2 , and SDSSJ 133226.34+060627.4 , while SDSSJ 114642.47+511029.6 's SDSS image shows clear evidence of a recent merger.
The NIR images show two stellar bulges in each of these systems. In addition, all four systems show tidal features suggestive of mergers.

The spatial correspondence between the two stellar continuum peaks and two \ velocity peaks in all cases strongly indicates we are seeing two active galaxies (Figure fig:2dspec ). We see unambiguous spatial separations between the red and blue velocity components in all targets and in each strong emission line.

sources.summary: The paper identifies this z=0.1816 double-peaked AGN as the sample target with a Chandra-detected kpc-scale dual AGN, disturbed host morphology, and no VLBA radio detection.

quotes.quote: SDSS J110851.04 + 065901.4 This z=0.1816 AGN contains double-peaked \ lines in its SDSS fiber spectrum, with peaks blueshifted and redshifted from the systemic velocity by 95 km s ^ 1 and 114 km s ^ 1 , respectively Liu2010b . It represents our only target that hosts a kpc-scale dual AGN detected by Chandra in the X-rays Liu2013 . resolved a dual AGN in this galaxy using near-IR imaging and optical slit spectroscopy, with an approximate separation of 0 7, corresponding to a linear separation of 2.1 kpc at its redshift Liu2013 .

Both ground-based imaging in the NIR Liu2010a,Fu2012 and HST Y band imaging Liu2013 show disturbance in the galaxy surface brightness profile, suggesting tidal interactions. Liu2013 confirmed its dual AGN nature in the X-rays based on Chandra observations combined with constraints on X-ray contribution from star formation estimated from HST U band imaging. We do not detect a radio source, at a 5 limit of 1.1  .

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1108+0659 as a binary AGN candidate with two NIR nuclei coincident with the two emission-line velocity components and modeled as de Vaucouleurs bulges in a disk.

quotes.quote: J1108+0659 . This object was reported in Liu_etal_2010b . It shows two nuclei in its NIR image, separated by 0.5 . The two \ velocity components are spatially coincident with the two NIR nuclei. Its K_s band image and 2d spectrum for the \ region are shown in Fig. \ fig:1108+0659 . The pair of nuclei are well resolved in a recent NIR adaptive-optics (AO) imaging observation Fu_etal_2010 . The best-fit K_s band model consists of two de Vaucouleurs bulges embedded in an exponential disk.

sources.summary: The paper says J110851.04+065901.4 has asymmetric Gaussian spectral profiles, is difficult to separate into single or double components at low velocity offset, and shows tidal-interaction signs.

quotes.quote: For the remaining 3 sources (J110851.04+065901.4, J132323.33-015941.9, J150243.10+111557.3), we notice asymmetric Gaussian profiles in the spectral shapes that can be fitted with two Gaussian profiles.

If the velocity offset is 4 69 \ (4th panel in Fig. 1), we can still visually identify double-peaked lines for 5 out of 12 sources (J112659.54+294442.8, J114642.47+511029.6, J115822.58+323102.2, J142507.32+323137.4, J171544.05+600835.7; FWHM of them are narrower than that of the average value and their peak fluxes are comparable), but it is difficult to tell if the spectral shape is composed of a single or double Gaussian lines for 4 of them (J102325.57+324348.4, J110851.04+065901.4, J132323.33-015941.9, J150243.10+111557.3).
Signs of tidal interactions can be seen in J092455.24+051052.0, J110851.04+065901.4, and J150243.10+111557.3.

sources.summary: J1108+0659 shows evidence for two nuclei, constraining non-detection in the high-J CO SLED, and a prominent blue-wing component in the first three CO transitions.

quotes.quote: Further, J1108+0659 and J1356+1026 show evidence of hosting two nuclei.
J1108+0659 shows no sign of any detection at a very constraining level in the CO SLED, placing it already at a similar excitation to starburst and main sequence galaxies.
J1108+0659 shows a prominent blue wing component, identified in the first three CO transitions.

gmw_00000994.name: SDSS J113721.36 + 612001.2
gmw_00000994.overview: SDSS J113721.36 + 612001.2 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1709.03561] classified as a Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy [arXiv:2402.12521, arXiv:1709.03561]. It exhibits an X-shaped radio morphology [arXiv:2402.12521] and contains dual milliarcsecond-scale components that may represent a core-jet structure or a dual active nucleus [arXiv:2402.12521, arXiv:1709.03561].

sources.summary: The paper presents 4C +61.23 as an FR-II radio galaxy with X-shaped GMRT morphology and dual VLBI components that could be core-jet emission or another AGN core.

quotes.quote: 4C+61.23 was classified as an FR-II radio galaxy using the 5 GHz VLA image Lara2001 . The GMRT maps at both bands show clear `X' shaped morphology of this source. The primary and secondary lobes are visible. From visual inspection, it looks like there is a continuity in the emission from the brighter lobes to the fainter ones.

4C +61.23 have presented the 8.4 GHz VLBA image of this AGN, which shows a double structure. Our follow-up observations at 15 GHz are similar to the 8.4 GHz image but suffer from low SNR. One of the dual VLBI components appears extended and might be a core-jet structure by itself, while the south-eastern component might be another AGN core. It is also possible that either component is an AGN core, and the rest of the emission is jet-related.

sources.summary: The paper describes this z=0.1112 AGN, alias 4C 61.23, as an FR II radio galaxy whose VLBA structure likely traces a jet feeding a larger lobe.

quotes.quote: SDSS J113721.36 + 612001.2 ( 4C 61.23 ) This z=0.1112 AGN contains double-peaked \ lines in its SDSS fiber spectrum (Figure  fig:J113721.36+612001.2 ), with peaks blueshifted and redshifted from the systemic velocity by 87 km s ^ 1 and 214 km s ^ 1 , respectively Liu2010b . The SDSS images show no evidence for tidal disturbance, which is also confirmed by an HST Y band image (in preparation). This galaxy contains a radio source that has been detected in surveys at least over the frequency range 38 MHz to 5 GHz gsw67,bwe91,gc91,White1992,hwrw95,dbbtw96,Cohen2007 . classify it as a Faranoff-Riley II radio galaxy.

Their VLA image (at 5 GHz) shows it to be symmetric, with the lobes oriented at 135 \ E from N. Figure  fig:J113721.36+612001.2 shows our VLBA image. Although superficially an apparent double, with the second component approximately 2 mas to the southeast of the primary component, the orientation of the milliarcsecond structure is similar to that of the arcsecond FR II structure, suggesting that the small-scale fainter component could be a portion of the jet that is feeding the southeast large-scale radio lobe.

gmw_00000995.name: SDSS J124358.36 005845.4
gmw_00000995.overview: SDSS J124358.36 005845.4 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy at a redshift of z=0.4092 [arXiv:1709.03561] that is classified as an outflow source [arXiv:2209.02717]. Optical imaging shows a single, undisturbed system with no clear tidal disturbances [arXiv:1709.03561, arXiv:2209.02717], while radio observations reveal a compact, unresolved core [arXiv:1709.03561, arXiv:2209.02717].

sources.summary: The paper reports this z=0.4092 double-peaked AGN host has no clear tidal disturbance, an unresolved FIRST radio source, and a compact unresolved VLBA component.

quotes.quote: SDSS J124358.36 005845.4 This z=0.4092 AGN shows double-peaked \ lines in its SDSS fiber spectrum (Figure  fig:J124358.36-005845.4 ), with peaks blueshifted and redshifted from the systemic velocity by 360 km s ^ 1 and 161 km s ^ 1 , respectively Liu2010b . The SDSS images show no evidence for tidal disturbance, although both the image quality and sensitivity may be too low to put a strong constraint. This galaxy contains a radio source that has been detected in surveys over the frequency range 1.4--5 GHz gwbe95,Condon1998 , and the radio source appears unresolved in FIRST . Figure  fig:J124358.36-005845.4 shows that the radio source has a compact, unresolved component in our VLBA image. The flux density on these scales is approximately 23 mJy at 3.6 cm, whereas the FIRST flux density is approximately 40 mJy (at 20 cm).

sources.summary: The paper describes J1243-0058 as a single double-peaked AGN classified as an outflow source, compact and unresolved in VLBA imaging, with no detected 21-cm absorption.

gmw_00000996.name: SDSS J135251.22 + 654113.2
gmw_00000996.overview: SDSS J135251.22 + 654113.2 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy at a redshift of z=0.2064 [arXiv:1709.03561]. The system shows signatures of a possible tidal disturbance [arXiv:1709.03561, arXiv:2209.02717] and is currently merging with a companion galaxy located 5 kpc to its north [arXiv:2304.12061].

sources.summary: The paper describes this z=0.2064 double-peaked AGN host as tidally disturbed, radio detected from 74 MHz to 8 GHz, and showing eastward milliarcsecond components.

quotes.quote: SDSS J135251.22 + 654113.2 This z=0.2064 AGN shows double-peaked \ lines in its SDSS fiber spectrum (Figure  fig:J135251.22+654113.2 ), with peaks blueshifted and redshifted from the systemic velocity by 108 km s ^ 1 and 265 km s ^ 1 , respectively Liu2010b . The SDSS images show tentative evidence for tidal disturbance, possibly related to a companion to the southeast of the main galaxy. This galaxy contains a radio source which has been detected in surveys at least over the frequency range 74 MHz to 8 GHz hmwb90,bwe91,gc91,White1992,dbbtw96,Condon1998,Cohen2007 . The radio source is unresolved in the CLASS image, with a flux density of 79 mJy. Figure  fig:J135251.22+654113.2 shows our VLBA image.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1352+6541 as a double-peaked AGN with possible tidal disturbance, a faint VLBA component near the primary, and RFI-limited 21-cm absorption data.

sources.summary: The paper highlights J135251 as a highly asymmetric QSO host whose northern neighbour lies 5 kpc away; HST contours show the pair already merging and unresolved as separate galaxies in SDSS.

quotes.quote: The presence of neighbour galaxies in both QSOs can be seen in the HST continuum images, shown as contours in Fig. fig:qso_bruno , with the neighbour galaxies highlighted by the green circles in the figure. For J135251, the neighbour galaxy is 5 kpc north of the QSO host, and the two are already merging.

In these two cases, Figure fig:qso_bruno shows that the neighbour galaxies are so close to the QSO host that the SDSS images do not show them as separate galaxies. Thus in our analysis of these images, they are not identified as separate galaxies but, instead, as galaxies with high asymmetry: A=0.16 and A_s=0.21 and A=0.21 and A_s=0.69 for J135251 and J155019, respectively.

gmw_00000997.name: SDSS J231051.95 090011.9
gmw_00000997.overview: SDSS J231051.95 090011.9 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy at a redshift of z=0.0944 [arXiv:1709.03561]. It features double-peaked emission lines [arXiv:1709.03561] and tentative evidence of tidal disturbance [arXiv:1709.03561], with a faint companion located 4 arcseconds away [arXiv:1709.03561, arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper describes this z=0.0944 double-peaked AGN host as lacking a double stellar core, showing tentative tidal disturbance, and having a single marginal VLBA component.

quotes.quote: SDSS J231051.95 090011.9 This z=0.0944 AGN shows double-peaked \ lines in its SDSS fiber spectrum (Figure  fig:j231051.95-090011.9 ), with peaks blueshifted and redshifted from the systemic velocity by 121 km s ^ 1 and 206 km s ^ 1 , respectively Liu2010b . The SDSS images show no evidence for a double stellar core, which is further confirmed by K_s band imaging with Magellan/PANIC Shen2011 at 0.''6 resolution and by HST ACS/F606W imaging at 0.''1 resolution Fu2012 . However, there is tentative evidence for tidal disturbance, both from the SDSS images and the K_s band images presented by Shen2011 . There is an apparent companion 4'' away to the southwest at PA=73 ^ , which does not contribute to the \ emission observed in the SDSS fiber spectrum.

The FIRST image shows this galaxy to be dominated by a compact component, with what may be a faint jet extending to the southeast. Figure  fig:j231051.95-090011.9 shows our VLBA image to consist of a single component, which is either unresolved or just marginally resolved.

sources.summary: The paper keeps J2310 0900 ambiguous: a faint companion lacks a measured redshift, slit seeing was poor, and better spectra are needed.

quotes.quote: J2310 0900 . This object has a companion 4 \ away, which does not contribute to the \ emission seen in the SDSS fiber spectrum. The companion is too faint to measure its redshift in our slit spectrum. The seeing was quite poor for the slit spectroscopy observation, and the two NEL components are consistent with no spatial offset. A better quality slit spectrum and/or different slit positions are needed to distinguish the binary and the NLR kinematics scenarios.

gmw_00000998.name: SDSS J233313.17 + 004911.8
gmw_00000998.overview: SDSS J233313.17 + 004911.8 is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=0.1699 [arXiv:1709.03561]. The galaxy shows tentative evidence of tidal disturbance [arXiv:1709.03561] and has a faint companion located six arcseconds to the southeast [arXiv:1709.03561, arXiv:1011.5246].

sources.summary: The paper describes this z=0.1699 AGN, alias PKS 2330+005, as tidally disturbed with a nearby companion and a single-component VLBA radio image.

quotes.quote: SDSS J233313.17 + 004911.8 ( PKS 2330 + 005 ) This z=0.1699 AGN shows double-peaked \ lines in its SDSS fiber spectrum (Figure  fig:j233313.17+004911.8 ), with both peaks blueshifted from the systemic velocity by 480 km s ^ 1 and 36 km s ^ 1 , respectively Liu2010b . The SDSS images show no evidence for a double stellar core, which is confirmed by K_s band imaging with Magellan/PANIC Shen2011 at 0.''8 resolution and by K_p band imaging with Keck II/NIRC2 LGSAO at 0.''1 resolution Fu2012 . However, there is tentative evidence for tidal disturbance, both from SDSS images and the K_s band images presented by Shen2011 . There is an apparent companion 6'' away to the southeast at PA=117 ^ , which does not contribute to the \ emission observed in the SDSS fiber spectrum.

This galaxy contains a radio source that has been detected in surveys from 74 MHz to 5 GHz blbhm86,Wright1990,bwe91,gc91,White1992,gwbe95,dbbtw96,Condon1998,Cohen2007 . We are unaware of any previous published observations in which the radio source is resolved. Figure  fig:j233313.17+004911.8 shows our VLBA image to consist of a single component.

sources.summary: The paper describes J2333+0049 as single in NIR and AO imaging, with a faint southeastern companion and no spatial offset between narrow-line components.

quotes.quote: J2333+0049 . This object has a minor companion about 6 \ to the southeast, which is too faint to measure its redshift in our slit spectrum. The two NEL components are consistent with no spatial offset in the 2d spectrum. It appears single in our NIR imaging and in the recent NIR AO imaging Fu_etal_2010 at 0.1 \ resolution.

gmw_00000999.name: Cartwheel Galaxy
gmw_00000999.overview: The Cartwheel Galaxy is the archetype of collisional ring galaxies, featuring a star-forming outer ring, an inner ring, and connecting spokes [arXiv:2205.09150, arXiv:1709.02826]. This structure was triggered by a drop-through collision with a companion galaxy, which also created a stellar bar and a population of ultraluminous X-ray sources [arXiv:2303.15502, arXiv:2310.00584].

sources.summary: The paper uses the Cartwheel as a low-metallicity ring-galaxy comparison, finding no ULX excess over NGC 922 once normalized by star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: The Cartwheel galaxy is famous for hosting 12 ULX, most of which are in the star forming ring. This is the largest number of ULX seen in a single system, and has led to speculation that the low metallicity of the Cartwheel (0.3 ) may optimize the conditions for ULX formation. In contrast, NGC 922 has metallicity near solar. The \ observations reveal a population of bright X-ray sources, including 7 ULX. The number of ULX in NGC 922 and the Cartwheel scales with the star formation rate: we do not find any evidence for an excess of sources in the Cartwheel.

The Cartwheel has a metallicity in the range Z 0.18-0.3Z_ in the ring Fosbury1977, Vorobyov2001 . This is considerably lower than the metallicity of NGC 922, which is 0.75 Z _ Wong2006 . Figure lf shows the cumulative luminosity function for sources in NGC 922 and the Cartwheel.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Cartwheel as the archetypal collisional ring galaxy, using MUSE spectroscopy across its optical extent and interpreting ring abundances through pre/post-collisional mixing with metal-poor infall.

quotes.quote: The Cartwheel (a.k.a ESO 350-40 or A0035-324) is considered the archetype of collisional ring galaxies 1996FCPh...16..111A, 2010MNRAS.403.1516S . These type of galaxies are formed as a result of a compact galaxy plunging through a massive gas-rich disk galaxy close to its center and almost perpendicular to it 1976ApJ...209..382L and are characterized by a ring that harbours a chain of star-forming knots Marston1995, Higdon1995 .

The Cartwheel has been the target of Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) observations at the Very Large Telescope (VLT), that covers the entire optical extent of the galaxy at the seeing-limited spatial resolution of 0.6 arcsec. The spectra span a rest wavelength range from 4610 to 9075  , which includes emission lines from different ionic species suitable for the determination of abundances of commonly reported elements covering the entire galaxy.

This apparent contradiction can be understood as due to the mixing of the ISM containing the pre and post-collisional elements in the ring by the gas that is more metal-poor than that of the pre-collisional disk e.g. Koppen2005 . One way of acquiring this metal-poor gas is through gas infall from the intruder, which has to be metal-poor. In the ``expanding material-wave'' scenario proposed by Renaud2018 , the processed elements are mixed naturally with the accreted metal-poor gas at successively outer radius. Most of the gas mass in the Cartwheel is in the atomic form Higdon1996, Higdon2015 , which is concentrated in the outer ring, which supports our hypothesis that dilution of ISM from metal-poor gas is responsible for the lower than the expected metallicity in the ring regions.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Cartwheel Galaxy's X-ray source population, linking its many ULXs to a collision-triggered ring starburst and finding stable XLF shape despite individual variability.

quotes.quote: Here, we address the study of a population of ULXs in the Cartwheel Galaxy -- the archetypal collisional ring galaxy -- and in the companion galaxies G1, G2, G3 that together form a compact group of 4 members SGC 0035-3357; Iovino2002 . The redshift is z = 0.03 Amram1998 and the corresponding luminosity distance is 131 Mpc Values between 129 and 133 Mpc are indicated in NED for the distance of the Cartwheel galaxy (https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu); we adopt here an intermediate value. The Cartwheel Galaxy X-ray emission has been observed with Einstein in 1979, Rosat in 1994, \ in 2001 and 2008, \ in 2004 and 2005 ( Wolter1999,Wolter2004,Crivellari2009 ). The very high X-ray activity of the Cartwheel Galaxy is connected with the impact of 300 Myr ago, that created the ring galaxy and triggered the episode of star formation (e.g. Wolter1999, Higdon1996,mapelli2008 ). The galaxy contains the largest number of ULXs for a single galaxy ( Wolter2004, gao2003 ), with the only possible exception of NGC 2207.

Most of the Cartwheel ULXs are found in the southern part of the outer ring, co-spatial with the locus of higher star formation, in agreement with the hydrodynamical simulations of Renaud2018 . Simulations in fact predict more star formation in the portion of the ring furthest from the nucleus, as is observed: the S-SW portion of the outer ring contains also massive and luminous HII regions Higdon1995 . The large number of bright ULX sources and the fact that they have been likely created in a single burst of star formation (as assumed also in wolter2018 ), rendering them coeval, make the Cartwheel the ideal place to apply a population study of ULXs.

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE observations to study the Cartwheel inner region, finding kinematic and stellar-population evidence for a bar that survived the collision and no central AGN.

quotes.quote: To investigate the characteristics of the bar and inner disk in the collisional ring galaxy Cartwheel. We used the Integral Field Unit (IFU) observations with Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) of the Very Large Telescope (VLT) to investigate the stellar kinematics, age, and nature of ionised gas in the inner region of the Cartwheel. We produced the stellar line of sight (LOS) velocity (V), velocity dispersion ( ), h _3 velocity moment, stellar population age, and emission-line maps of the galaxy using the Galaxy IFU Spectroscopy Tool (GIST) pipeline. The observed nature of intensity, V, and profiles altogether support the existence of a stellar bar as earlier revealed from near-infrared (NIR) K_s band imaging.

A weak correlation between V/ and h _3 is found within the bar radius, providing more kinematic evidence for a stellar bar which survived the drop-through collision. The overall weak anti-correlation between V/ and h _3 in the disk implies that the stellar orbits in the disk are less stable, which might be due to the impact of the collision. The mass-weighted age map of the galaxy shows that the stellar populations in the bar region are relatively older, with an increasing gradient from the bar edge to the centre, another evidence to signify that the bar was present before the galaxy underwent collision. We do not find an active galactic nuclei (AGN) from the BPT analysis of a central unresolved source reported earlier using NIR imaging.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Cartwheel as a dynamically perturbed ring galaxy whose star-forming ring hosts many ULXs, including the extremely luminous variable source N.10 observed with Chandra and XMM-Newton.

quotes.quote: The Cartwheel is one of the most outstanding examples of a dynamically perturbed galaxy where star formation is occurring inside the ring--like structure. In previous studies with Chandra , we detected 16 Ultra Luminous X-ray sources lying along the southern portion of the ring. Their Luminosity Function is consistent with them being in the high luminosity tail of the High Mass X-ray Binaries distribution, but with one exception: source N.10.

Recently, we have observed the Cartwheel with XMM-Newton in two epochs, six months apart. After having been at its brightest for at least 4 years, the source has dimmed by at least a factor of two between the two observations. This fact implies that the source is compact in nature. Given its extreme isotropic luminosity, there is the possibility that the source hosts an accreting intermediate--mass black hole. Other sources in the ring vary in flux between the different datasets.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Cartwheel galaxy as an interacting ring system with an uncertain companion, a G3-linked plume, gas-rich star-forming ring, spokes, inner ring, and nucleus.

quotes.quote: Discovered by Zwicky1941 , the Cartwheel galaxy is part of the compact group of 4 galaxies SCG 0035-3357 (see Iovino2002 and e.g. the figure 1 of Bosma2000 for identification). The companion responsible for the formation of the ring has not yet been unambiguously identified, but the presence of a long plume between the ring galaxy and the companion G3 Higdon1996 is a robust hint of tidal interaction.

The long plume in the Cartwheel system would thus be the bridge originating from G3 and extending up to the ring galaxy itself. Ram pressure stripping could also play a role in the formation of this plume, as a fraction of the G3 gas would be stripped as the galaxy flies in the hot halo of the Cartwheel.

The Cartwheel galaxy encompasses a wide range of physical conditions (ring, spokes, inner ring, nucleus), each harbouring a different star formation regime. For instance, the ring structure contains most of the gas of the galaxy Higdon1996 , hosts star forming regions Marcum1992, Fosbury1977, Higdon1995, Amram1998, Charmandaris1999, Wolter1999, Higdon2015 and ultra-luminous X-ray sources Gao2003, Wolter2004 . These points indicate an enhanced star formation activity in the form of massive stars and star clusters in this region.

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE data to study the metal-poor Cartwheel ring galaxy, its He II emitting regions, star-forming ring, metallicity, and unusually numerous ULX sources.

quotes.quote: We here report the detection of the nebular \ line in 32 \ regions in the metal-poor collisional ring galaxy Cartwheel using the Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) dataset. The measured I( )/I( ) ratio varies from 0.004 to 0.07, with a mean value of 0.010 0.003. Ten of these 32 \ regions are coincident with the location of an Ultra Luminous X-ray (ULX) source.

The Cartwheel is considered as the archetype of the class of collisional ring galaxies 1996FCPh...16..111A, 2010MNRAS.403.1516S . Ring galaxies are characterized by a ring that harbours a chain of star forming knots. The star formation in the ring is believed to be triggered by a radially expanding density wave that was formed as a result of a compact galaxy plunging through a massive gas-rich disk galaxy close to its center and almost perpendicular to it 1976ApJ...209..382L . Higdon1995 found that the \ emission in the Cartwheel, a tracer of current star formation, is distributed along a ring as predicted by the collisional scenario of the formation of ring galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper models the Cartwheel galaxy as a collisional ring system, deriving rapid outer-ring propagation and linking color gradients, surface-brightness offsets, and disk geometry to ring dynamics.

quotes.quote: Modeling of the Cartwheel galaxy indicates that the outer ring is currently propagating at v_ ring 100 km s ^ 1 , while the maximum radial expansion velocity of gas in the outer ring is currently v_ gas 65 km s ^ 1 . The latter value is in marginal agreement with the measurements of Higdon ( ) based on HI kinematics. Modeling of the radial B-V/V-K color gradients of the Cartwheel ring galaxy also indicates that the outer ring is propagating at v_ ring 90 km s ^ 1 for the adopted distance to the galaxy of 140 Mpc.

We considered other propagation velocities of the outer ring and found that the Cartwheel's B-V/V-K colors and Q_ BVK color indices could be acceptably reproduced for 55 km s ^ 1 v_ ring 120 km s ^ 1 . The best-fit value is v_ ring =90 km s ^ 1 . Stellar populations born in each annulus by a slower wave are older than those born by a faster wave. Strong dependence of B-V/V-K colors on the age of young stellar populations allows us to constrain the propagation velocity of the Cartwheel's outer ring.

sources.summary: The paper reviews ISO observations of the Cartwheel, finding warm dust in its ring, wavelength-dependent mid-infrared structure, central molecular gas, and unexpected inner-region MIR emission.

quotes.quote: Figure 3 shows some early UKIRT J, H and K-band maps of the Cartwheel ring from Marcum, Appleton Higdon (1992). Figure 4 shows the ISO detection of warm dust from the ring (from Charmandaris et al. 1999), and emphasizes how different the near and mid-IR distributions are. In most cases, K- band imaging traces the stellar component (the recently formed star clusters) whereas the MIR emission is sensitive to warm dust heated by the uv from hot stars. The ISO observations show that the outer ring is more easily detected at shortern MIR wavelengths (where the emission is most likely dominated by UIB features around 6-7 microns), and is rather weak at longer wavelengths.

One initial surprise was the detection of significant MIR flux from the inner regions of the Cartwheel where star formation activity is believed to be minimal. Horellou et al. (1998) have recently obtained clear evidence for a large concentration of molecular material in the central regions of the Cartwheel. It now seems likely that low-level, but quite extended star formation may be occurring over the inner ring regions of the Cartwheel, perhaps a precursor to much more powerful activity in the near future.

sources.summary: The paper studies Cartwheel as a highly evolved ring galaxy with HI concentrated in its ring, gas-poor interior regions, and peculiar star-formation laws.

quotes.quote: Atomic Hydrogen L-S and Cartwheel have been mapped in HI (Figure 1). In such large systems 95 of the atomic ISM is concentrated in the rings, resulting in high HI surface densities: _ HI = 30-120 M _ pc ^ 2 . At the same time, their interiors are very gas poor, with _ HI 2 M _ pc ^ 2 .

We show star formation laws derived for the Cartwheel and L-S in the same figure. Both are peculiar. Unlike M 51, atomic gas in the Cartwheel correlates with _ SFR in most of the ring, though with a small N .

sources.summary: The paper reports a near-infrared discovery of a bar, pseudobulge, and point source in the Cartwheel, arguing the bar and pseudobulge predate its collision.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a bar, a pseudobulge and unresolved point source in the archetype collisional ring galaxy Cartwheel using careful morphological analysis of a near-infrared (NIR) K _s band image of excellent quality (seeing=0.42 ) at the ESO archive. The bar is oval-shaped with a semi-major axis length of 3.23 \ ( 2.09 kpc), with almost a flat light distribution along it. The bulge is almost round (ellipticity=0.21) with an effective radius of 1.62 \ ( 1.05 kpc) and a Sersic index of 0.99, parameters typical of pseudobulges in late-type galaxies.

The newly discovered bar is not recognisable as such in the optical images even with more than a factor of two higher spatial resolution of the Hubble Space Telescope, due to a combination of its red colour and the presence of dusty features. The observed bar and pseudobulge most likely belonged to the pre-collisional progenitor of the Cartwheel. The discovery of a bar in an archetype collisional ring galaxy Cartwheel is the first observational evidence to confirm the prediction that bars can survive a drop-through collision along with the morphological structures like a central bulge (pseudo).

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs the Cartwheel galaxy stellar populations, concluding the outer ring is dominated by a recent impact-driven starburst while inner regions retain spiral-galaxy properties.

quotes.quote: The paradigm RiG is the Cartwheel galaxy at z = 0.03 . This galaxy has been the object of both theoretical and observational studies, but the details of the mechanisms that lead to its peculiar morphology of double rings with connecting spokes and to its physical properties are still far from clear. To shed light on the history of the Cartwheel galaxy, we performed a spatially resolved analysis as a function of galactocentric distance, exploiting spectroscopic data from VLT/MUSE observations combined with photometric data covering a large wavelength range, from the UV GALEX to the IR JWST/MIRI.

We find that, apart from the peculiar morphology, a large fraction of the Cartwheel galaxy is not affected by the recent impact from the companion bullet, and retains the characteristics of a typical spiral galaxy. On the contrary, the outer ring is strongly affected by the recent impact, and is completely dominated by stars formed not earlier than 400 Myr ago. Our picture suggests that the collision shock wave, while moving forward to the external region of the galaxy, drags the already formed stars, sweeping the inner areas outwards, as proposed by recent collision models.

sources.summary: The paper maps mid-infrared dust emission in the Cartwheel, showing a star-forming outer ring, unexpected inner-region emission, and collisional-ring structure.

quotes.quote: The Cartwheel Galaxy in the Mid-IR We present mid-infrared images at 6.7 m and 15 m of ``The Cartwheel'' (AM 0035-33), the prototypical collisional ring galaxy. The observations, taken with ISOCAM, reveal the distribution of hot dust in the galaxy and its two companions in the north-east. The intensity of the mid-IR emission from the outer star forming ring of the Cartwheel shows considerable azimuthal variation and peaks at the most active H region of the ring.

A surprising result of our observations is the discovery of significant emission from the inner regions of the galaxy, including the inner ring, spokes and nucleus, where previously only low level H emission had been reported. At 6.7 m , this emission is stronger than the one from the outer star forming ring, and at 15 m , it represents 40 of the emission from the outer ring. The H to mid-IR flux ratios from the inner regions are consistent with the heating of grains from weak star formation activity.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Cartwheel as a collision-shaped galaxy with ring star formation and an unusually rich ULX population, providing the setting for its analysis of the bright ULX N10.

quotes.quote: The Cartwheel galaxy is a peculiar nursery of ULXs. This galaxy underwent a collision with a smaller galaxy about 10^8 ago Hig96, Map08 . This episode not only conferred the galaxy its peculiar shape, but also triggered a massive star formation in its ring. The Cartwheel harbours the largest number of ULXs than any other galaxy: 15 sources are more luminous than 10^ 39 Wol04 , and at least some of them are known to be variable Cri09 .

sources.summary: The paper uses JWST images of the Cartwheel Galaxy/PGC 2248 as a complex mock source, emphasizing its intricate surface-brightness structure for testing curved-arc lensing reconstruction.

quotes.quote: In order to do this, we use the multiple images of the Cartwheel Galaxy (PGC 2248) taken by JWST as our mock source, since it is a galaxy with a very complex surface brightness distribution. We are motivated to use this complex shape as such a beaded, cartwheel shaped multiply imaged source has been detected in the HST image of the cluster lens CL0024+16 Colley_1996 . Here we use the higher-resolution JWST image of PGC 2248 to demonstrate the power of the curved arc basis method. We create a mock data set by lensing this mock source with four different curved arc bases, forming four different distorted images, as shown in the first row of Fig. fig:comp_source .

sources.summary: The review describes the Cartwheel as the prototype collisional ring galaxy, citing its high star formation and contradictory expansion-speed measurements for the external ring.

quotes.quote: The prototype is the Cartwheel galaxy.

Significant part of all young stars in such ring galaxies is observed in their star forming rings, and the galaxies in a total demonstrate moderately high star formation rates, like 18 M _ yr^ 1 in the Cartwheel mayya05 , which is often called a prototype for this class of objects.
They gave out rather contradictory results on the expansion speed of the Cartwheel external ring: 13-30, 53, and 89 km/s from the IFP H , VLA HI and long-slit spectroscopy, respectively.

sources.summary: The simulation paper uses the Cartwheel as a well-studied collisional ring galaxy, noting models of its formation, absent gas emission in spokes, and a red gas-poor secondary ring.

quotes.quote: This information is used to constrain N body/hydrodynamical models reproducing the formation process of well studied CRGs such as the Cartwheel galaxy (e.g. hernquist+93 ; struck-marcell+93 ; horellou+01 ; mapelli+08 ) and Arp 147 (e.g. gerber+92 ; mapelli+12 ).
Observations of the Cartwheel galaxy do not support our finding that spokes are mainly composed of gas: no HI or H emission is found in the spokes and in the inner ring ( higdon+95 , higdon+96 ).
This is in partial agreement with observations: for example, the Cartwheel galaxy shows a red but gas-poor secondary ring ( higdon+95 , higdon+96 ).

sources.summary: The paper cites the Cartwheel as a ULX-rich starburst ring galaxy, with point sources in the ring and a propagating star-formation wave triggered by a smaller galaxy.

quotes.quote: The Cartwheel galaxy reveals a substantial number of resolved as well as some unresolved point sources in a ring coinciding with starburst activity and punctuated by numerous HII regions (Wolter Trinchieri 2003, 2004; Gao et al. \ 2003). This ring of star formation is apparently propagating outward at 50 km s ^ 1 , and the original disturbance was presumably triggered by the penetration of a smaller galaxy some 5 10^8 years ago. The Chandra sensitivity limit at the distance of the Cartwheel ( 120 Mpc) is L_x 5 10^ 38 ergs s ^ 1 .

gmw_00001000.name: NGC 7250
gmw_00001000.overview: NGC 7250 is an irregular galaxy located at a distance of approximately 20 Mpc [arXiv:2308.02474, arXiv:2312.02282]. In 2013, the galaxy hosted the Type Ia supernova SN 2013dy, making it a valuable calibrator for measuring the Hubble constant [arXiv:2308.02474, arXiv:2312.02282].

sources.summary: The paper presents preliminary JWST Cepheid results for nearby SN host NGC 7250, showing crowding in HST data and a factor-of-two smaller F115W Leavitt-law scatter.

quotes.quote: Here, we present some preliminary results from our program for the nearby galaxy NGC 7250, host to SN2013DY, located at a distance of about 20 Mpc. Figure fig:CephPost illustrates cut-out images of four Cepheids in NGC 7250 from (Owens et al. 2023). Shown are images at F115W (from JWST ; top panel) and F160W (from HST ; bottom panel), which illustrate the superb resolution and the power of JWST . As can be seen in the HST data, many of the Cepheid candidates are crowded by nearby neighbors.

The scatter in the JWST F115W data for NGC 7250 is a factor of two smaller than in the SHoES F160W data; i.e., the improved resolution and higher signal-to-noise ratio of the JWST data results in a lower variance ( ^2 ) for the F115W relation by almost a factor of four. This is all the more remarkable since the J band data are single-phase observations only, while the HST observations have been corrected to mean light.

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST JAGB-star measurements for NGC 7250, an irregular SN Ia host observed by JWST, using selected JAGB stars for a fiducial mode.

quotes.quote: NGC 7250 The irregular galaxy NGC 7250 (also known as PGC 68535 or UGC 11980) was the first SN Ia host galaxy in this program to be observed by JWST in November 2022.
In 2013, NGC 7250 was host to the type 1a SN 2013dy explosion, therefore uniquely positioning it as a SN Ia calibrator for measuring the Hubble constant.
In NGC 7250, the fiducial mode was measured using JAGB stars with colors between 2.6<(F115W-F444W)<3.2 mag and an LF smoothed with a smoothing parameter of _s=0.25 mag.

gmw_00001001.name: SSA22-galDLA1
gmw_00001001.overview: SSA22-galDLA1 is a high-redshift damped Lyman-alpha absorber located at z = 3.335 [arXiv:1512.04625]. It has a high neutral hydrogen column density, but lacks a counterpart host galaxy with a star formation rate larger than a few solar masses per year [arXiv:1512.04625].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SSA22-galDLA1 as a z=3.335 DLA along a galaxy sight-line, constraining its column density, covering fraction, area, and lack of a bright host counterpart.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of an intervening z = 3.335 0.007 DLA along a galaxy sight-line identified among 80 Lyman Break Galaxy (LBG) spectra obtained with our Very Large Telescope/Visible Multi-Object Spectrograph survey in the SSA22 field. The measured DLA neutral hydrogen (H i ) column density is (N_ HI / cm^ 2 ) = 21.68 0.17 . The DLA covering fraction over the extended background LBG is > 70 ( 2 ), yielding a conservative constraint on the DLA area of 1 kpc ^2 . Our search for a counterpart galaxy hosting this DLA concludes that there is no counterpart galaxy with star formation rate (SFR) larger than a few M _ yr ^ 1 , ruling out an unobscured violent star formation in the DLA gas cloud. We also rule out the possibility that the host galaxy of the DLA is a passive galaxy with M_* 5 10^ 10 M_ or a heavily dust-obscured galaxy with E(B-V) 2 .

galaxies: high-redshift --- intergalactic medium --- galaxies: individual (SSA22-galDLA1) Damped Ly Absorbers (DLAs) are neutral hydrogen (H i ) gas clouds with a high column density ( N_ HI > 2 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ; Wolfe+86 ) typically identified in the spectra of bright background objects.

gmw_00001002.name: S5 1803+784
gmw_00001002.overview: S5 1803+784 is a low-synchrotron peak BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2204.04739, arXiv:2102.09779] that exhibits rapid variability across optical, radio, and gamma-ray wavelengths [arXiv:1110.5306, arXiv:2204.04739]. Its one-sided radio jet features mostly stationary inner components [arXiv:1305.5356, arXiv:1001.1973] that undergo strong variations in position angle and apparent width [arXiv:1110.5306, arXiv:1206.4127].

sources.summary: The paper describes S5 1803+784 as a BL Lac active galaxy whose inner jet components appear stationary while position angles, ridge-line shape, and apparent width vary quasi-periodically.

quotes.quote: S5 1803+784 is an active galaxy at a moderate redshift of z=0.68 ( Hewitt1989 ). It has been classified as a BL Lac object. Being a member of the complete S5 sample ( Witzel1987 ) it has been extensively studied in the radio, at different wavelengths and with different instruments (see Britzen2010 for a detailed recounting of the source's radio observations history). Typical for this class of objects, 1803+784 has been observed to be variable in the radio and the optical on both long and short-timescales (e.g., Wagner1995 ; Heidt1996 ; Nesci2002 ; Fan2007 ).

Britzen2010 , by (re-)analyzing more than 90 epochs of global VLBI and VLBA data, reveal a new kinematic scheme for 1803+784. All components in the inner part of the jet (up to 12 mas) appear to remain stationary with respect to the core. This behavior is seen at all frequencies studied by the authors (1.6 - 15 GHz). In contrast to this, the components show strong changes in their position angles, implying a prevailing movement perpendicular to the jet axis.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes S5 1803+784 as a BL Lac object whose VLBI jet ridge line oscillates, varies in Doppler boosting, and may reflect binary-black-hole orbital motion.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed analysis of 30 very long baseline interferometric observations of the BL Lac object S5 1803+784 ( z=0.679 ), obtained between mean observational time 1994.67 and 2012.91 at observational frequency 15 GHz. The long-term behaviour of the jet ridge line reveals the jet experiences an oscillatory motion superposed on its helical jet kinematics on a time-scale of about 6 years. The excess variance of the positional variability indicates the jet components being farther from the VLBI core have larger amplitude in their position variations. The fractional variability amplitude shows slight changes in 3 year bins of the component's position. The temporal variability in the Doppler boosting of the ridge line results in jet regions behaving as flaring "radio lanterns".

In this paper we analyse the calibrated uv data of the jet of blazar S5 1803+784 z=0.679 , Lawrence1996 , employing MOJAVE observations. In contrast to what has been observed in the majority of VLBI jets, its components do not exhibit outward motion, but rather are oscillating about certain positions of the jet Britzen2005b,Britzen2005a,Britzen2010 . S5 1803+784 exhibits a 76 difference in position angle between the parsec and kiloparsec-scale jet and references therein Britzen2005a .

sources.summary: S5 1803+78 is analyzed as a BL Lac/LSP blazar; the paper reports multi-wavelength monitoring, three gamma-ray flaring states, day-scale variability, and prior radio periodicity linked to helical jet motion.

quotes.quote: Here, we report about the multi-wavelength study for blazar S5 1803+78 between MJD 58727 to MJD 59419. We analysed ray data collected by Fermi-LAT, X-ray data collected by Swift-XRT NuSTAR, optical photons detected by Swift-UVOT TUBITAK observatory in Turkey. Three flaring states are identified by analysing the ray light curve. A day scale variability is observed throughout the flares with the similar rise and decay times suggesting a compact emission region located close to the central engine.

Blazar S5 1803+78 is located at redshift, z=0.684 ( Lawrence_1996 ) and classified as BL Lac type object ( Biermann1981 ). Under the AGN unification scheme by Urry1995 it is identified as low-synchrotron peak (LSP) blazar. Long optical and radio variations are studied in 2002AJ....124...53N, Nesci . Based on 30 years long VLBI observations, kun2018 have detected a periodicity of 6-years which is linked to the helical jet motion.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1803+784 as the largest 1-Jy sample object, with WAT-like large-scale emission, a VLBI jet that loses collimation, and an unlikely helical-jet interpretation.

quotes.quote: 1803+784 : this is the largest object of the 1-Jy sample, with total projected linear size of 444 kpc. In the VLA image a low-brightness region is evident at about 9 arcseconds toward the SW direction with respect to the core; the total radio structure resembles a Wide Angle Tailed (WAT) source. Its PA is 70 and belongs to the second group of Fig. fig1 . The VLBI image (Fig. 1803e ) shows a collimated jet about 10 mas long which abruptly loses collimation and ends in a diffuse halo slightly bending toward the South, in the direction of the large scale structure. The MERLIN image (Fig. 1803m ) does not reveal any further emission.

The extended structure in the image of Fig. 1803e could likely be originated by a shock with a denser medium, after which the jet loses collimation and changes its direction towards the arcsecond-scale structure. The enhancement of the apparent bending angle can be due to the projection onto the plane of the sky. The presence of a helical jet for this source is unlikely.

sources.summary: The paper presents S5 1803+784 as the motivating BL Lac with quasi-stationary jet components, strong transverse motions, and almost periodic ridge-line and width evolution.

quotes.quote: Through the (re-)analysis of more than 90 epochs of global VLBI and VLBA data of the BL Lac source S5 1803+784, a new kinematic scheme for that source was revealed ( Britzen2010 ). All components in the inner part of the jet (up to 12 mas) appear to be quasi-stationary in terms of their core separation. This behavior is seen at all frequencies studied by the authors (1.6 - 15 GHz). In contrast to this, the components show strong changes in their position angles, implying a dominant component of motion perpendicular to the jet axis.

The jet ridge line of S5 1803+784 is found to change in an almost periodic manner, starting by resembling a straight line, evolving into a sinusoid-like pattern, and finally returning to its original linear pattern, although slightly displaced from its original position. The authors calculate a period of 8.5 years for the evolution of the jet ridge line. Finally, the authors find that the jet changes its apparent width (in a range between a few and a few tens of degrees) in an almost periodic way, with a timescale similar to the one found from the evolution of the jet ridge line.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1803+784 as one of five AGN targets observed by KVN at 22-130 GHz for multi-frequency VLBI astrometry, map registration, position shifts, and jet-related alignment tests.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:source_pos shows the distribution in the sky of the five selected sources (1803+784, 1807+698, 1842+681, 1928+738 and 2007+777) along with their angular separations, which range between 3.6^o and 11^o . None of the sources had been observed previously with VLBI at 130 GHz. The observations consisted of 3 minutes long scans alternating between the sources in each of the two triangles shown in Figure fig:source_pos , and between the triangles ca. every hour and a half, at the four bands simultaneously. Alternating between multiple sources allowed us to develop a strategy to decompose the relative astrometric measurements into single source position shifts that allow, for example, the registration of the images at different frequencies.

Figure fig:sc shows self-calibration images for the five AGN sources in the multi-frequency KVN observations, including the first images of these sources at 130 GHz. The visibility datasets were modelfitted and imaged and show little divergence from point-source core dominated images; in some cases there appears to be some elongation aligned with the jet direction as seen in higher resolution maps from MOJAVE mojave . Table tab:flux lists the total flux values as measured from the maps, at all frequency bands.

sources.summary: The paper includes S5 1803+784 among extreme high-polarization RA blazars, emphasizing its redshifted MgII broad-line emission and extreme superluminal blazar properties.

quotes.quote: There are three very redward asymmetric (RA) examples of a MgII BEL, 3C 279 (z = 0.536), 0954+556 (z = 0.899, 4C +55.17) and S5 1803+784 (z = 0.684). They are very high polarization quasars (HPQs) that have been detected with 10 optical polarization imp90,lis16 . The MgII profiles are shown in Figure 1 in order to demonstrate the extreme circumstance under consideration. This is not a subtle effect nor a consequence of FeII multiplets. The preponderance of the line shape is redshifted emission from, ostensibly, receding gas even though these objects have powerful jets relativistic of plasma beamed toward Earth.
Both 3C 279 and 1803+784 are extremely superluminal. All three are part of the ghi10 bright gamma ray blazar sample.

sources.summary: The paper uses 1803+784 to show that some low-speed AGN components at 15 GHz can hide faster 43 GHz motion, so the reported component speed may not trace the beam speed.

quotes.quote: In support of comment (iii), we note that one of the slow objects, 1803+784 , was also observed by J05 at 43 GHz. They find _ app =15.9 1.9 , whereas, at 15 GHz, we found _ app =-0.6 0.6 . The higher resolution at 43 GHz is crucial in detecting fast components in sources like this, because they are within 1 mas of the core, at or below the resolution limit at 15 GHz.
